School of Global Pathways - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login School of Global Pathways Your gateway to a global future School of Global Pathways Your gateway to a global future ABOUT About School of Global Pathways The School of Global Pathways opens its doors to ambitious minds with global aspirations, inviting students to embark on a transformative academic journey that spans continents and cultures. With a mission to redefine the boundaries of higher education, the school promotes cross-cultural dialogue, challenges geographic limitations, and fosters an environment where academic excellence meets a global perspective. In line with this vision, the School of Global Pathways aims to equip future professionals with the international exposure and adaptability required to thrive in an interconnected world through meaningful, immersive, and career-expanding opportunities. The School of Global Pathways is established to address the growing demand for global exposure, international credentials, and cross-cultural competencies. It offers structured educational pathways that combine the foundation of home-country learning with the enriching experience of overseas education. Through multi-country programs, student mobility initiatives, and internship and exchange opportunities, the school enables students to progress to world-ranked partner universities across the UK, USA, Europe, and other global destinations. Through these offerings, the School of Global Pathways prepares graduates with diverse educational experiences, cultural intelligence, adaptability, and a strong global mindset, empowering them to build successful careers without borders. Gain the Global Edge International-Degree Advantage Earn degrees from both Bennett University and international partners. Global Career Mobility Access job markets across India and your host country. Curated University Partnerships Study at prestigious partner institutions with proven academic excellence. Cost-Effective International Education Access world-class international education at a fraction of the cost of a complete four-year overseas program. Learning Beyond Boudiaries Dean’s Message Prof. (Dr.) Abhay Bansal Dean, School of Global Pathways Dean, Office of International Affairs Dean, School of Computer Science Engineering & Technology On behalf of Bennett University, it is my privilege to welcome you to the School of Global Pathways, a transformative new venture designed to centralise and elevate the international aspirations of our student body. As Dean, I am proud to lead this initiative, which now serves as the unified umbrella for all foreign exchange and global mobility programs across every existing school at the university. The School of Global Pathways operates with a singular mission: to facilitate the process for students who wish to pursue or continue their education abroad from the very start of their academic journey. We recognise that true holistic development often requires exposure to diverse educational methodologies and cultures. Leveraging our robust foundation of over 120 collaborations with world-ranked institutions—including Georgia Tech, UC Berkeley, the University of British Columbia, and the National University of Singapore – we provide a structured ecosystem for credit transfers, exchange programs, and joint research projects. By integrating the best practices of the New Education Policy with deep industry and academic interaction, we aim to mould our students into "world citizens" ready to contribute to society's growth on a global scale. We are here to support your ambitions every step of the way. The School of Global Pathways is your gateway to a successful international career, ensuring your time at Bennett University is both rewarding and the launchpad for a borderless future. B.A (Hons.) Political Science & International Relations - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login B.A (Hons.) Political Science & International Relations The Political Science & International Relations major at the School of Liberal Arts, Bennett University, offers an exceptional and transformative educational journey. Political Science & International Relations (IR) is the study of governance, political systems, diplomacy, and global interactions. It provides insight into power structures, public policies, international conflicts, and political ideologies. The discipline equips students with the ability to analyse political developments and global affairs critically. The program is designed to: (i) introduce key political theories and international relations frameworks in the first two semesters; (ii) explore comparative politics, public policy, and international diplomacy in the following semesters: and (iii) offer specialized courses in global security, human rights, and political economy in the final year. General Information Students wishing to major, minor, or pursue a concentration in Political Science & IR should develop critical thinking and research skills. Courses will involve qualitative and quantitative analysis of political events and global interactions. Theory courses – Two lectures a week, each lasting 1.5 hours. Research projects – Analysis of case studies, policy papers, documentary analysis and political trends. Fieldwork projects – Political-cultural trips. The curriculum is designed to offer a strong academic foundation through Major-Core courses , complemented by Major-Electives for added specialization. Major-Core At the heart of the program, the Major-Core (MJ) curriculum delivers essential and advanced coursework, with students required to complete a minimum of 45 credits, up to a maximum of 60 credits. International Relations Political Theory Indian Constitution Nationalism in India Indian Political Thought Human Rights: Theory and Indian Context Western Political Thought Political Parties and changing political landscape Gender, Power and Politics India’s Foreign Policy Public Administration India and World Politics Peace and Conflict Resolution Comparative Politics Major-Elective To further enrich their learning experience, students can select from Major-Electives (MJ-Elec), offering additional flexibility and breadth, with credit options ranging from a minimum of 27 to a maximum of 36 credits. NRI Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score). Student Testimonials This course has taught me many things, like the society we live in, whereas subjects like Sociology and Psychology have helped me grasp various concepts Priyansha BA Liberal Arts Batch: 2020-23 The faculty and coordinators of Bennett University have given it all to make the virtual transition a more effortless process. Tanvi BA Liberal Arts Batch: 2020-23 Events BA Honours Programmes Frequently Asked Questions When was the B.A Liberal Arts program initiated under the School of Liberal Arts at Bennett University? The Liberal Arts program started with its first batch in 2020. It offers the opportunity to learn from highly qualified and experienced teachers. The program's foundation rests on two pillars -- the development of all-around personality and life skills. What are the majors available in the BA Liberal Arts course at Bennett University? The students can choose Majors from seven domains: Psychology, Political Science & IR, Economics, English, Business Studies, Philosophy & Sociology How is the BA Liberal Arts curriculum structured? In the first semester of the program, the students will be introduced to all the majors to select the Major which suits them the most. The choice of Majors and Electives is given from the second semester onwards. How do electives benefit a student's career? An elective is a combination of courses taken through the duration of university education. The courses taken as electives allow students to understand the world with exposure to a wide range of subjects and areas of practice. This broader understanding is a critical requirement to succeed in the world of the future. Can an undergraduate student take part in research activities? Bennett University focuses on research, experiential, and applied learning. Undergraduate students are encouraged to work with members of the faculty in their live research projects. Such exposure makes students understand the intricacies of the research process and deepens their knowledge Do you assist students in placements, summer internships, and industry tours? Bennett University has a dedicated Career Services Centre that works closely with the industry to participate in guest lectures, curricular inputs, joint program development, internships, and placements. What are the future career options after pursuing BA Liberal Arts? BA Liberal Arts graduates can look for rewarding careers in advertising, public relations, financial analysis, design, journalism, publishing, corporate communications, marketing, etc., or start a successful entrepreneurial journey of their own. What kind of industry exposure do students have access to? This is one of the unique hallmarks of the BA Liberal Arts program as it benefits from faculty members teaching courses in management, which in turn, provides students an opportunity to continuously interact with industry experts from India and abroad. Best B. Tech Engineering Physics Course, Fee, Eligibility, Admission - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login B.Tech Engineering Physics Explore dynamics of movement through innovation Apply Now B.Tech Engineering Physics Explore dynamics of movement through innovation Apply Now Why B.Tech Engineering Physics at Bennett University Research-oriented faculty Having experience of working in research laboratories in Germany, France, Netherlands, Sweden, Finland, Italy, USA, South Korea, Israel and Turkey. Live Projects 4 externally funded (DST and DBT) projects running, worth more than ₹ 6 million. Emerging Technologies Familiarity with advanced software and skills. Specialisations Six Specialisations under B.Tech Engineering Physics. ABOUT About B.Tech Engineering Physics B.Tech. Engineering Physics (EP) students at Bennett University (BU) build strong foundations in Physics, Mathematics, and engineering with the help of the fundamental courses. The curriculum exposes the students to advanced courses in material science, photonics, and quantum physics, which find diverse applications in many technological sectors such as nanotechnology, optical fiber communication, quantum technology, to name a few. B. Tech EP program aims to offer a four-dimensional (4D) teaching and learning experience to the students. In a 4D approach, the students get the opportunity to develop knowledge, skill, character as well as metacognition mindset. The curriculum is divided into three broad sections of Core, Elective and Project. The core curriculum is bifurcated in such a way that the basic science is in harmony with engineering science and social science courses. Strong foundation in fundamental science, is the heart of the EP program. Apart from Physics, the curriculum has courses from other fundamental branches with an emphasis on Mathematics. The humanities and social science courses in the curriculum, cater for soft skill and character development. Additionally, it offers insights into the mental make-up of entrepreneurs. The courses in engineering science have elements of computer science, mechanical engineering along with electronics and communication engineering. The curriculum allows a unique opportunity to students to choose between a computer science and electronic courses, apart from the mandatory foundation level subjects. There is enough scope for students to be exposed to cutting-edge research topics as reflected in the curriculum:- B Tech. Curriculum Engineering Physics 2024-28 B Tech. Curriculum Engineering Physics 2023-27 B Tech. Curriculum Engineering Physics 2022-26 B Tech. Curriculum Engineering Physics 2021-25 B.Tech. Curriculum Engineering Physics 2020-24 B.Tech. Curriculum Engineering Physics 2019-23 B.Tech. Curriculum Engineering Physics 2018-22 Key Highlights Minor To obtain a Minor in Engineering Physics, the B. Tech Students from other branches need to take 4 courses from Department of Physics with a total of 16 credits. Similarly, EP student may choose a Minor from other engineering branches. Minor in engineering physics Minor in quantum technology Honours To earn an Honours degree in specialised domain in any of the following areas, an EP student needs to earn 18 additional credits, apart from a regular degree from the Physics Department. (i) Nanoscience and Nanotechnology (ii) Quantum Science and Technology Specialisation The B.Tech EP curriculum has 5 elective courses and two major projects. The department offers six Specialisation on the following topics: Nanotechnology Quantum Technology Complex Systems Biophysics Renewable Energy Astronomy & Astrophysics If a student wants to get specialization in his/her degree, he/she must complete at least 4 electives from the corresponding specialization basket and he/she must also do Major Project II in the same field. Each of these Specialisations contains courses offered by the Physics department and other departments of SEAS. A student can choose a maximum of two courses offered by other departments. Electives Renewable Energy Engineering Advanced Statistical Mechanics with Biophysical Applications Quantum Electronics Elements of Quantum Computation and Quantum Information Nonlinear Dynamics and Chaos Nuclear and Particle Physics Computational Modeling of Materials Superconductivity and Condensate Particle Physics Skills During fours years, students learn and acquire: Analytical ability Experience of coding physical problems Knowledge of different computing languages such as Python, C++, Fortran Ability to handle advanced computation and lab automation tools such as, Matlab, Mathematica, LabView. Experience of handling state of art experimental instruments, such as Sputtering unit, XRD, Electrochemical workstation, Fourier Transform Infrared Spectroscopy, surface profilometer etc. along with experimental automation Acquaintance with advanced softwares widely used in Material Science and Molecular Dynamics research. Projects Apart from the 12 credit Industrial R&D Project in the 8th semester, there are two mandatory projects in the 6th and 7th semester, each for 2 and 3 credits, respectively. In the past, students have actively engaged in projects related to Nanotechnology, Photovoltaics, Soft condensed matter physics and Biophysics, Computational condensed matter physics, Ultracold atoms, Quantum optics, and quantum information science. Students have also participated in international startups in the industrial project, earning remuneration based on their performances. They have also been selected for fully funded final semester R&D project in eminent universities in Europe and top tier research labs in India. Live Projects Some of the recently Completed Projects / Internship offered by the department Photonic Quantum Walks in Waveguide Lattice Study of quantum correlations and their quantification Quantum Simulations Solid state dye sensitized solar cells Resistive Switching of Vanadium Oxides Development of simulation software based on Tight binding method for computing physical properties of real materials. Learning Molecular Dynamics simulation and its applications Study of Supersolid Phase. Internships As a part of the curriculum, all B.Tech Engineering Physics students must take up a summer internship at the end of the 6th semester. In the past, students have attended a summer school at IISER-Kolkata and have carried out winter internships at IIT Guwahati, CDAC Pune. Students have also been selected for summer internships in European Universities with competitive salaries. Students have also qualified for jobs with competitive salaries from university placement drive. Laboratories Theory and experiment are blended in the B.Tech curriculum. Currently, the department has nine laboratories. Two 1st-year laboratories in Electromagnetics and Mechanics Dedicated darkroom facility with advanced optics experiments (Advanced Physics Lab-I) such as Kerr Effect, Magnetostriction Effect and so on. Computational Physics laboratory with an HPC facility and state of art computational and graphical software such as Mathematica, Matlab, Labview, Origin. The condensed matter laboratory is well equipped with advanced experiments (Advanced Physics Lab-II) such as Quantum Analog, UV-Vis-NIR Spectrophotometer, Dual Channel Source Measurement Unit, Solar Cell etc. Dedicated Nanotechnology lab with DC/RF Magnetron Sputtering Unit for the fabrication of nanostructured materials, Electrochemical workstation etc. FTIR Plasma Quantum Efficiency Source Meter Unit Spin Coater Sputtering Surface Profilometer UV-Vis-NIR Spectrophotometer Advanced Optics and Magnetic Resonance Lab XRD Solar Simulator COMPUTATION LAB BU PROUD 320+ Companies for placements 10 LPA Highest Package 7.5 LPA Avg package 700 euros/PM Highest internship stipend BU International Stars Internships Startups Awards Research Work & Patents Placements Intellectual rigor in academics, intense industry exposure, and imparting Life Skills with a structured approach enhance our students' abilities, making them truly industry-ready. ELIGIBILITY B.Tech Engineering Physics Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process Eligibility Domestic Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with Physics and Mathematics & any other third* subject with minimum 55% marks OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI Pass with 55% aggrigate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third* subject with highest score) * Third subject should not be vocational- Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc PROCESS B.Tech Engineering Physics Admission Process for 2025 The selection of a student at Bennett University will be finalized after series of evaluative steps: Submission of the application form with required details by paying non-refundable application fees of Rs. 1500. Merit-based assessment of candidates and release of provisional offer letters. Selected Students to pay advance fee within deadline given. Submit documents and pending fee payment to complete registration as per timelines shared. Aakash Parents From being an active member of the Robotics team, Theatre Club to holding a rank in Hult Prize and representing the official football team, Bennett University has provided me the platform to experience is full of personal growth. Raunak B.Tech. Engineering Physics Batch: 2019-23 OUR ASK B.Tech Engineering Physics Frequently Asked Questions How is EP different from other branches? Engineering physics blends courses from engineering, physics, and math to build an understanding of how these areas interact and support each other. It is expected to boost one’s knowledge of the physical environment while discovering how physics is applied to problem-solving in our rapidly changing high-tech world. Therefore, the horizon of EP graduates expands many folds compared to other branches. What makes Engineering Physics at Bennett University unique? The Engineering Physics course is only offered by a handful of IITs across India, besides a few Universities (Private as well as Public). Bennett University is among the few places where the course is available. The university atmosphere also allows a unique blending of science, arts and culture which is essential for holistic development. The freedom to choose courses from Law, Liberal Arts, Management or Media in the form of open elective and Minor gives an extra edge. How can I check if B.Tech. in EP is the right fit for me? If you have an inherent passion for physics and mathematics and if you wish to know how the modern technologies which you encounter frequently work, then you will enjoy B. Tech EP course. As a B.Tech engineering physics student, you’ll study the fundamentals and the intricacies of both engineering and physics, such as electromagnetism, thermodynamics, quantum mechanics and signals and systems. Some programs include research in superconductivity, optical communication, and nano-science and technology as well. You’ll also take courses in calculus and differential equations. Laboratory work will teach you how to use experimental techniques and will give you hands-on experience with high-tech equipment. What are the major career profiles after completing B.Tech. in EP? All India trend in placement reveals that financial companies such as America Express, Goldman Sachs, HSBC show deep interest in Engineering Physics graduates. The fundamental reason is that the rigorous training in Physics and Mathematics allows the student to build a sound reasoning ability and deep analytical skills. Apart from that, graduates also opt for IT jobs in well-known IT companies or R&D jobs in places like ISRO, DRDO, BARC. They may also be able to find positions in industries engaged in the design and manufacture of semiconductors, microelectronics or photonics components and systems. The scientific acumen acquired through the rigorous scientific training would also allow them to take up positions in the scientific software development industry such as Mathworks (Matlab) and Wolfram (Mathematica). In which type of companies would EP students be offered internships? We are in discussion for a summer internship with the University of Nebraska, Omaha. Apart from that, the engineering physics students may have summer internships in institutions of national importance such as ISRO, GMRT, DRDO. They may also eligible for an internship in institutions of international repute such as TIFR, IISc, IISERs, IITs. Apart from these, the university placement cell works tirelessly to arrange a good internship in the industry. What kind of projects and internships are existing EP students doing? The students are engaged in academic as well as industry projects related to the field quantum technology, material science, stochastic processes and their application in finance, decentralised finance in the internationally acclaimed institutions/companies such as Aalto University, Finland; Gutenberg University Germany; Instadapp, USA; IIT Tirupati, India; to name a few. Which are the companies/institutes where B. Tech EP students have been placed/got offers? As B. Tech EP started only in 2017, till date only one batch has passed out. In that batch, students got offers for higher studies and jobs from the likes of the University of Heidelberg, University of Bologna, University of Cologne, University of Leipzig, University of Tubingen, University of Freiburg, University of Nurnberg and Byju’s. In which companies’/institutions students have gone for industry visits? Optik Karkhana, Havells, Inter-University Accelerator Center are a few multinational companies/R&D Centres where our students have visited to experience hands-on practical training. What kind of workshops and guest lectures are organised for students? Since the inception of the university, the department of physics has been organising full-day workshops on the application of information technology, where eminent speakers of international repute shared their views, experience and skills. The program was supported by the National Academy of Science, India (Delhi Chapter). The department has also celebrated International Day of Light with a couple of colloquiums from Prof. Ajoy Ghatak and Dr Amitava Sengupta. The department of Physics has organised two days’ workshop on Mathematica with hands-on training, in association with Wolfram and Scube. Apart from these, the department invites eminent researchers from across the globe for seminars and colloquiums. Prof. Vasudevan Lakshminarayanan from University of Waterloo; Prof. Dieter Suter from TU Dortmund; Prof. Konrad Banaszek from University of Warsaw; Prof. Anirban Chakraborty from JNU; Prof. Raghavan Rangarajan from Ahmedabad University are a few to name. What is the faculty profile of BTech Engineering Physics? All the faculty members in the Department of Physics have done their Ph.D and/or have had postdoctoral experience from foreign universities namely, City University New York (USA), Louisiana State University (USA), Max Plank Institute (Germany), Technical University Dortmund (Germany), University of Gottingen (Germany), University of Helsinki (Finland), Uppsala University (Sweden), AMOLF (Netherlands), Ecole Polytechnique, (France), University of Camerino (Italy), Tel Aviv University (Israel), Bilkent University (Turkey), Seoul National University (S. Korea), Pusan National University (S. Korea), and Kyungpook National University (S. Korea). After B. Tech Engineering Physics, how can a student opt for PhD in India? What is the advantage of doing a PhD? Currently, few IITs and some leading research institutes take Ph.D students right after B. Tech. However, the recent National Educational Policy has opened avenues for direct entry to Ph.D for 4-yr degree programmes. There are several scholarship exams in India for Ph.D, such as CSIR-NET, GATE, JEST, through which financial assistance is provided to pursue a Ph.D. Apart from these, various IITs and IISERs also take entrance for the intake of Ph.D students. Govt of India has put a strong emphasis on indigenous research, and as a result, the scholarship amount and number of scholarships increase every year. The advantage of doing a Ph.D is the students put themselves on a higher-level career path towards a leadership role and can get academic faculty positions in various institutes in India or abroad. How can I go abroad after B.Tech Engineering Physics for Masters (MS) or PhD? It is strongly encouraged in the Department of Physics that B.Tech students take research projects during their studies at Bennett University which plays a crucial role in getting admission to Universities abroad. It is often found that research project outcomes (such as patents or papers) are more helpful than even CGPA, for getting into good institutions for MS/Ph.D. Is it possible to do a PhD right after B.Tech Engineering Physics at abroad universities? Yes, some universities do have direct entry from B.Tech to Ph.D programs for bright students. For the others, the students are usually taken at an MS program. If their training and aptitude are found suitable for a Ph.D, they are usually transitioned into a PhD after 1 year. It is observed that 50% of the Engineering Physics students tend to go abroad for Ph.D programs. Does B.Tech Engineering Physics at Bennett University have foreign exchange/semester exchange programs? Yes, the final semester is left to the student’s choice of whether he wishes to pursue an industry internship or a semester abroad. If the student is ambitious enough, they are encouraged to go for the semester abroad, and then pursue a Master’s (MS) there. This way, the student gets a chance to pursue MS with scholarship support. This is aside from the existing faculty-level research collaborations. What are the infrastructures that you have at Bennett university Physics Department? The available high-end instruments are but not limited to UV-Vis-NIR Spectrometer, Source Measurement Unit, Electrochemical Workstation, Spin Coating Unit, Fourier Transform Infrared Spectrometer, Single-Photon Detector, DC/RF Magnetron Sputtering Unit, Gas Sensing Chamber, Surface Profilometer, Solar Simulator, and Computing Cluster. The department also possesses a dedicated darkroom for advanced optics experiments along with an optical table and optical spectroscopy. Recently the department has purchased the licence for ahigh-end software to support advanced learning such as Mathematica, Origin and Labview. (Hons.) B.B.A LL.B. (Hons.) is a five-year integrated program, spread over 10 semesters, consisting of Law and Business administration courses. The program covers major domains of business education and key law courses, to help students learn the crucial interface of business and law in India and abroad being the best UGC approved college for BBA LLB in India As per regulations of the Bar Council of India, BBA LLB (Hons) will lead to a professional Law degree with a seamless integration of Business Administration as the first degree. It will aid the learners to become accomplished professionals understanding the interface of Business and Law and will build the requisite capacity, which is in short in such space as it is the best UGC approved college for BBA LLB in Delhi NCR Duration 5 years Credits 222 Total Papers 56 Start Date August 2026 BU PROUD 120+ Companies for placements 28.17 LPA Highest Salary 6.7 LPA Avg. Learn by doing and by interacting with students from diverse backgrounds. BBA LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2025-30 BBA LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2024-29 BBA LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2023-28 BBA LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2022-27 BBA LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2021-26 BBA LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2020-25 BBA LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2019-24 BBA LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2018-23 The program structure will aid law aspirants to dive deep into the fundamentals of legal education which consists of building interpersonal communication and should have sound knowledge of industry acts and laws. The Law courses beginning right from first semester covers 20 compulsory papers, 4 clinic papers, 6 optional papers and 8 papers of specialization. The curriculum of the Law papers has been designed to incorporate emerging areas of Artificial Intelligence & Law, Entertainment Law, Advanced Intellectual Property Law, Internet Law & Governance, Privacy Law and other laws. However, the first degree will not be awarded separately, and one has to complete the integrated course for the combined degrees. The first degree under the integrated program will have 20 non-law papers. The first degree covers the major domains of the business education like the Management Theory, Business Analytics, Business Communication, Critical Thinking, Business Accounting and other business subjects. The law school will formalize further student exchange programs in Europe, US, Canada, China, Japan and Singapore in the coming semesters allowing a global exposure for the students. Core Law Papers Management Papers Clinical Papers Mooting Interdisciplinary work The BBA LL.B. (Hons.) course offers 26 core law papers including subjects like Legal Methods, Law of Torts, Constitutional Law, Family Law, Company Law, Property Law, Labour & Industrial Law, Public International Law, among others. The program offers 13 papers as first degree papers. The compulsory papers include Business Accounting, Strategic Management, International Business, Corporate Finance, among others. The BBA LL.B. (Hons.) syllabus includes 4 clinical papers for students to apply theoretical knowledge into practical experience. Moot Court Clinic, Alternate Dispute Resolution, Professional Ethics, and Drafting, Pleading, and Conveyance are Clinical papers that are offered in the curriculum. Gain from the faculty and student-administered Moot Court Committee, which selects in-house teams for external participation. Moot court is a compulsory 1 credit course which begins from 1st academic year of the Law program. Gain knowledge and experience in an inter-disciplinary university environment e.g. artificial intelligence, entertainment, the Internet, surveillance, air and space, and privacy. Resources Avail a state-of-the-art library, Moot Court Hall, and Legal Aid Cell. Workshops, Socratic Club and Guest Lectures Invite thinkers to deliberate on public policy issues. Research Have our own research journal (Bennett Journal of Legal Studies). Five major research centres will allow you to work outside the classroom. Participate in legal conferences and competitions. Internships & Placements Gain hands-on experience through the mandatory 4-week internship every year. BU-SOL has constituted an Internship and Placement Committee (IPC) which oversee the responsibility of the internships and placement for the students of law. IPC enhances the prospects of internship in various legal institutions and is oriented towards placing the students in institutions of repute. BU-SOL has set in motion of establishing a network of law firms, corporates, policy think-tanks and other stakeholders of legal fraternity to facilitate the internships spread across geographical zones. Such internships will shape the students’ aspirations of the type of practice and connect with potential partners for final placement. Avenues of Placement Law Firms, Senior Law Practitioners, Judicial Offices, Civil Society Organisations, Parliament and State Legislature committees Government and other Local Bodies, Market Regulatory Agencies, Legal Regulatory Authorities, Corporate, International Organisations Entrepreneurship Participate in activities and hone that special idea at Centre for Innovation & Entrepreneurship at Bennett University. International Collaboration Avail international exposure to leading law schools in North America, Europe, Australia, Asia, and Africa. (Hons.) Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process Eligibility Domestic Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with minimum 60% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score) + Personal interview. PROCESS BBA LL.B. (Hons.) Admission Process for 2025 The selection of a student at Bennett University will be finalized after series of evaluative steps: Submission of the application form with required details by paying non-refundable application fees of Rs. 1500. Merit-based assessment of candidates and release of provisional offer letters Counseling by the Bennett University’s admissions team Start My Application Program Infrastructure Spacious aesthetically designed Law library State of Art Moot Court Hall for real life court room experience E-learning resources: Westlaw; Manupatra; SCC online; Lexis Nexis; HeinOnline ; JStor etc. Language Lab OUR ASK BBA LL.B. (Hons.) Frequently Asked Questions What is the faculty profile in the school of law and how many faculty members are there? The faculty members are drawn from various National Law Universities, Delhi University, and other top universities and have international exposure. The faculty members are hired from diverse regions with a strong commitment to Bennett University. They adopt modern methods of pedagogy and learning resource tools. They have a strong research background and are networked with National and International Legal Academia. The Social Sciences and Management divisions have qualified teachers who can interface legal education requirements. What percentage of the curriculum is practical/theoretical? The Bar Council of India decides almost 75% of the papers that are taught, but the quality of the syllabus, inputs in the class and other electives of Bennett will be creative and focused. Curriculum for the School of Law and the BBA LL.B. (Hons.) course is designed jointly by in-house faculty members with a review from eminent law professors holding office either as Senior Professor, Vice-Chancellors or Directors of prominent National Law Universities. Along with Academicians, our curriculum is also reviewed by Practitioners, Industry Consultant and Management Experts to see the proper balance between the theoretical and practical aspects. Moot Court, Legal Aid, Client Counselling, Negotiation, and other practical components are also included in the curriculum. Further, internship is a credit course designated as a practical-clinical course by Bar Council of India, to be done during the summer and winter vacations. A student should undergo internship for a minimum of 4 weeks in an academic year, starting from year 1 to year 5 with the non-governmental organization, trial courts, the appellate court, tribunals, international organizations, law firms, and a single internship should not exceed more than four weeks. What kind of jobs can I expect after completion of a law degree and what will be the starting salary? One can expect the following career options on the completion of the law degree: A BBA LL.B. (Hons.) degree qualifies a Law Graduate to appear in All India Bar Exam, and after successfully qualifying the same he/she can practice in Indian Courts, Law Firms (National and International), Corporate Legal Counsels, Independent practice, Judiciary, Civil Rights Groups, Academic opportunities (after post-graduation in law), International regulatory organizations (WIPO/WTO/WHO etc), Clerkships with Higher Judiciary, Civil Services exam, National Regulatory organizations (Competition Commission /Law commission etc). The Career Services team will actively assist the students in internships during their studies and final placements on completion of the program. A strong internship program for five years will convert as opportunities for placement. All of this depends on the choice of the student after they shape up their preferences in the fourth and fifth year and the quality of placement will depend on the performance of the student. The average placement package depends on the student’s academic credentials, performance in the interview, and how well they have used the University’s teaching and facilities to prepare themselves in the five years they spend here. In which law firms have law students gone for internships? Law students got the opportunity to complete their summer internships in law firms, NGOs, and corporates like Legitquest, Akshaya Patra, Mint Advise, BSK Legal, Zaid Ansari & Associates, Goodwin Law firm-Abu Dhabi, CRY, Goonj, ICADR-Hyderabad, NALSAR, Help Age India, International Bridges to Justice, Law Legion Bros, ICADR – Hyderabad, Center for Air and Space Law – NALSAR, ONGC Corporate Law Department, Central Information Commission, Patiala House Court, WWF- India, LegitQuest, HSA Advocates among others. How do law students get benefited by Times of India group backing? The School of Law and Bennett University is part of the Times of India Group which also publishes Economic Times, runs the Times Now channel and Radio Mirchi. As the largest and professionally run media group, it is well connected with legal professionals and industrial houses. Various members of the Times of India group visit occasionally to have an exchange of ideas and lectures on contemporary developments. Tech (Biotech) (Dual Degree) Fuel, Feed & Fine-Tune Human & Planet Lives Apply Now B.Tech + M. Tech (Biotech) (Dual Degree) Fuel, Feed & Fine-Tune Human & Planet Lives Apply Now Why B.Tech + M. Tech (Biotech) (Dual Degree) Program at Bennett University Industry-oriented curriculum Curriculum curated under the supervision of the Board of Studies with the Director & the R&D Heads from Biocon, Evolva Biotech, Bharat Biotech, IIT Delhi and Department of Biotechnology, Ministry of Science & Technology, Government of India State-of-the-art Research The department has academic collaborations with the researchers from the following reputed institutions: Institute National de La Recherche Scientifique, Canada, York University, Canada, University of Calgary, Canada, POSTECH, South Korea, University of Oklahoma, USA, University of Nebraska, USA, University de Lorraine, CNRS, LIEC, Nancy, France and Florida International University, USA. Research Based Live Projects Students are engaged in research-enabled project-based experiential learning. Interaction with Vigorous Industry Experts Focus on making students industry ready. Biotech students have interacted with CXOs of Emami Biotech, Patanjali, Biocon, Bharat Biotech, Serum Institute, Ayurvet Ltd. ABOUT About B.Tech + M. Tech (Biotech) (Dual Degree) The B.Tech + M. Tech (Biotech) (Dual Degree) is the field of modern medical practice, where students study the organisms and bioprocesses involved in medicine, engineering, and technology. The course offers biotechnological skills in variegated areas such as microbial, animal, plant biology, health, agriculture, and the environment. The scope of Biotechnology is expanding and steadily growing in India and the world. Bennett University offers B.Tech + M. Tech (Biotech) (Dual Degree) under the supervision of experienced faculty. These faculty members have a rich research background in academia and industry. The curriculum is curated to impart skills evolving technology and industry needs. It offers core subject knowledge with professional development. It aims to focus on research skills, analytical skills and entrepreneurship. Students would be able to earn significant knowledge about genetics, plant and animal tissue culture, molecular biology, genetic engineering. Also, students will know more about courses such as immune technology, nanotechnology and biosensors, and as genetic screening and personalised medicine, bioprocess engineering and industrial microbiology. MBA (Logistics & Supply Chain) | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login MBA Logistics & Supply Chain Apply Now MBA Logistics & Supply Chain Apply Now About MBA Logistics & Supply Chain Management The Logistics & Supply Chain Management specialisation under the MBA program is designed to prepare students to manage, optimise, and transform end-to-end supply chain operations in a globalised and technology-driven business environment. The specialisation focuses on procurement, inventory management, warehousing, transportation, multimodal logistics, logistics documentation systems, and strategic logistics planning. Students develop strong analytical, managerial, and decision-making capabilities while learning to apply emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Internet of Things (IoT), Automation, and Blockchain to improve efficiency, reduce costs, and enhance supply chain resilience. The program blends theory with hands-on applications, case studies, simulations, and industry-oriented projects. Duration 2 Years Specialisations - Credits - Start Date Aug/Sep. 2026 (Tentative) Curriculum Highlights The curriculum builds a strong foundation in operations and supply chain management with a focus on logistics and transportation. It covers procurement, inventory, warehousing, transportation, multimodal logistics, and distribution, while integrating analytics, sustainability, and strategic planning to prepare students for data-driven, real-world logistics roles. Core Courses Operations and Supply Chain Management Project Management Electives Supply Chain Management Purchasing and Inventory Management Warehousing and Distribution Facilities Management Transportation and Distribution Management Multimodal Transportation Management Logistics Documentation System Logistics Strategic Planning Analytics for Sustainable Supply Chains Tools, Software & Certifications Students gain hands-on exposure to industry-relevant tools and platforms, including: MS Excel and Solver LINGO and LINDO SPSS Power BI Tableau Coursera certifications across logistics, analytics, and supply chain management Why MBA (Logistics & Supply Chain) from Bennett University Meets the rising global demand for supply chain professionals. Hands-on training in logistics tools, forecasting, and simulation. Expert faculty from top B-Schools, IITs, and IIMs. Builds strong skills in analytics, technology, and operations. Offers excellent career opportunities across major industries. Program Objectives The MBA (Logistics & Supply Chain) program aims to provide students with a strong understanding of logistics, procurement, warehousing, and transportation processes, while developing analytical and decision-making skills for planning, coordinating, and optimising supply chain operations. It also trains students in forecasting, network design, simulation, and the use of digital tools applied in modern supply chains, preparing them for strategic and managerial roles in logistics, operations, and supply chain management. Learning Outcomes By the end of the MBA in Logistics & Supply Chain Management programme, students will be able to: Optimise supply chain and logistics networks using analytical and decision-support tools Manage procurement, inventory, warehousing, and transportation operations effectively Evaluate supplier performance and formulate appropriate global sourcing strategies Apply emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), the Internet of Things (IoT), and blockchain to address real-world logistics and supply chain challenges Career Pathways Graduates of MBA Logistics and Supply Chain specialisation are prepared for specialist and managerial roles like: Supply Chain Manager Logistics Manager Facilities Manager Procurement Specialist Warehouse and Inventory Manager Transportation Planner Demand Planner Supply Chain Consultant Project Manager Quality Control Manager Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Passed Graduation in any discipline from a recognised university with a minimum 50% marks with English as the medium of instruction NRI: For international applicants, a minimum of 50% in the qualifying examination is required, along with having studied the prerequisite subjects for admission to the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on the weighted score of CAT / XAT / NMAT / MAT / CMAT / GMAT / BU MAT/ CUET Percentile score & Graduation & Personal Interview. FMS, University of Delhi Dr. Ratan Kumar FPM - Management Development Institute, Gurgaon Mr. Krishan Ph.D. (Pursuing) - JNU, Delhi Dr. Vijay Prakash Mishra Ph.D. - Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, Pune Mr. Abhishek Saran Ph.D.(Pursuing) - IIM, Sambalpur Ms. Priya Mandleshwar Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIM, Indore Ms. Rimleena Boro Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIM, Indore OUR ASK MBA (Logistics & Supply Chain) Management Program Frequently Asked Questions What does the MBA in Logistics & Supply Chain Management focus on? The MBA in Logistics & Supply Chain Management focuses on managing and optimising end-to-end supply chain processes, including procurement, warehousing, transportation, multimodal logistics, and distribution. The program prepares students to manage complex, technology-driven, and global supply chains efficiently. What key areas will I study in the MBA Logistics & Supply Chain Management specialisation? Students learn core areas such as procurement, inventory management, warehousing operations, transportation planning, multimodal logistics, logistics documentation, and strategic supply chain planning, along with analytics and sustainable supply chain practices. How does this MBA specialisation prepare students for modern supply chain challenges? MBA in Logistics & Supply Chain Management integrates emerging technologies such as AI, IoT, automation, and blockchain, equipping students to tackle real-world supply chain challenges, including cost optimisation, efficiency improvement, resilience, and disruption management. What practical and analytical skills will I gain from the MBA in Logistics & Supply Chain Management program? MBA students develop strong forecasting, network design, simulation, analytics, and decision-making skills, complemented by hands-on training with logistics software and real-world problem-solving exercises. Is the MBA in Logistics & Supply Chain specialisation suitable for non-engineering or non-operations backgrounds? Yes, the curriculum is designed to build concepts progressively, focusing on managerial, analytical, and strategic aspects of supply chain and logistics management, making it suitable for students from diverse academic backgrounds. What tools and software will I learn during the MBA (Logistics & Supply Chain)? Students gain practical experience with tools such as MS Excel, Solver, LINGO, LINDO, SPSS, Power BI, and Tableau, along with Coursera certifications, to strengthen their technical and analytical skills for supply chain and logistics roles. What career opportunities are available after completing an MBA (Logistics & Supply Chain)? MBA Graduates of this specialisation can pursue high-demand careers in supply chain and logistics, including Supply Chain Manager, Logistics Manager, Procurement Specialist, Warehouse & Inventory Manager, Transportation Planner, Demand Planner, Supply Chain Consultant, Project Manager, and Quality Control Manager. Which industries hire graduates of Logistics & Supply Chain Management? These MBA graduates are recruited by manufacturing, FMCG, retail, e-commerce, logistics service providers, transportation companies, consulting firms, healthcare, and other global supply chain-driven industries. Post Graduate Diploma AI in Healthcare (in collaboration with Max) - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Post Graduate Diploma AI in Healthcare (in collaboration with Max) A unique, industry-aligned Post Graduate Diploma jointly offered by Bennett University and Max Healthcare combines academic depth with real-world clinical exposure. The one-year hybrid program is thoughtfully structured to offer a deep blend of academic learning and hands-on experience, ensuring that students gain not only theoretical knowledge but also practical insights to deploy AI effectively in clinical settings. With more than 50% of the curriculum dedicated to practical application, the program is a beacon for experiential and impactful learning in the rapidly evolving healthcare ecosystem. Duration 1 Yr. Credits 40 Mode Hybrid Start Date July 2026 Industry-Aligned Curriculum Co-designed with Max Healthcare doctors and AI experts. Covers AI-based medical imaging, predictive diagnostics, health data analytics, and more. Hospital Integration Guaranteed clinical training at Max Healthcare hospitals. Exposure to real-world clinical workflows, hospital AI systems, and patient data. Promising Career Prospects AI in Healthcare is projected to grow to $188 billion by 2030 (Statista). India ranks among the top 3 countries in AI healthcare startup growth (NASSCOM). Hospitals and health-tech firms are actively seeking AI talent with expertise in healthcare and operations. Dean’s Message Dr. Rajeev Tiwari Dean, School of Artificial Intelligence As Dean of the School of Artificial Intelligence and Head of Industry Connects at Bennett University, I am immensely happy to present our PG Diploma in AI in Healthcare, a truly pioneering program designed specifically for executive working professionals. Developed in close collaboration with Max Healthcare, our School of AI, and the School of Engineering-Biotechnology, this program uniquely blends our robust academic excellence with unparalleled hands-on clinical exposure within a leading hospital environment. My expectation for this program is that it will empower a new generation of healthcare leaders- professionals who can seamlessly integrate cutting-edge AI solutions into clinical practice, driving efficiency, improving patient outcomes, and fostering innovation across the industry. The dual mentorship model, combining academic and clinical expertise , along with practical, industry-relevant capstone projects, are meticulously crafted to ensure our graduates are not just knowledgeable, but also adept at real-world problem-solving. This is more than a diploma; it's a commitment to shaping the future of healthcare through intelligent design and application, further strengthening our ties and moving ahead with TIMES, The Times Group's. Prof. Latha Narayanan Head, Department of Biotechnology Dean, School of Engineering & Applied Sciences It gives me great pleasure to introduce the PG Diploma program in AI for Healthcare to aspiring participants. Recognizing the urgent need to define Artificial Intelligence (AI) ’s place in health & medicine, Bennett University has partnered with Max Healthcare, in truly preparing India to move with the Times. As a researcher in Bioinformatics & Computational Biology for over 20 years, I am aware of the role of computation in understanding disease biology and addressing unmet medical challenges. With the ever-increasing volume of data generated from advancement in technologies, integration of AI in healthcare holds promising potential in disease diagnosis, therapeutics & patient care. Bennett University in partnership with Max Healthcare aims to equip professionals with theoretical knowledge and tools to understand & apply Artificial Intelligence in clinical settings. By bringing together faculty experts in Artificial Intelligence, Computational Biology and Medical Sciences, we are uniquely positioned to highlight the transformative potential of AI. This course provides a valuable platform for participants from diverse backgrounds to explore and excel at the intersection of Artificial Intelligence and healthcare. As Dean, School of Engineering & Applied Sciences I encourage you to explore this program and thoughtfully consider advancing your career in AI applications for healthcare. Program Objectives This industry-aligned, premium program aims to equip science graduates and working healthcare professionals with advanced AI capabilities tailored to real-world medical challenges. Program Details Mode : Online + 8 On-Campus Immersions Schedule : Weekend Classes (Saturday & Sunday) Duration : 1 Year (2 Semesters) Curriculum : 2025-26 Intake : 20 Students/Year | Lecture Hours : 15 Hrs./Week Modules : 10 (5/Semester) + Capstone Project Certification : By Bennett University, jointly branded with Max Healthcare Program Highlights NEP-aligned Curriculum: Balances theory, labs, and clinical practice. Realworld Research & Projects: Tackle AI challenges in diagnostics, prognostics, hospital operations. Mentorship: Guidance from School of AI faculty and Max Healthcare experts. State-of-the-Art: Includes access to State-of-the-art healthcare & AI Labs. Research: 30-40 % research component in every course. Program Structure Semester 1: Bennett University Focus: AI foundations, data science, healthcare informatics. Delivery: Lectures, labs, doubt-clearing sessions. Faculty: Bennett University internal faculty (honorarium-based). Immersion: Four campus visits. Semester 2: Max Healthcare Focus: Clinical AI applications, hospital integration. Delivery: Simulation labs, patient environments, case studies. Faculty: Max-employed clinical professional. Immersion: Four hospital-based visits. Why PG Diploma AI in Healthcare First-of-its-Kind: India’s premier industry-linked Postgraduate Diploma in AI for Healthcare with a hospital brand. Dual Mentorship: Academic + Clinical guidance for practical learning and capstone support. Who can apply for PG Diploma AI in Healthcare? The PG Diploma AI in Healthcare is a premium, industry-aligned program for science graduates (B.Sc., B. Tech, B. Pharm…) and working healthcare professionals (doctors, nurses, pharmacists, radiographers, allied health professionals, and clinical researchers). Future Opportunities Foundation for long-format UG/PG programs with Max Healthcare. Unlock exciting career progression opportunities at the intersection of artificial intelligence and healthcare innovation. AI Models and Tools Covered As part of the PG Diploma AI in Healthcare, students will get hands-on experience with the following high-impact, free tools that are widely used in AI and medical research. These tools are selected to build strong, job-ready skills in data science, clinical AI, and digital health analytics. 1. Prompt Writing & Optimisation Tools Learn how to craft precise prompts for AI systems to extract reliable medical insights, generate patient summaries, or build interactive AI assistants. Practice with tools like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity AI for evidence-based medical querying and clinical text generation. 2. Machine Learning & Deep Learning Foundations Use Google Colab (free cloud GPU) to build models using: TensorFlow / Keras PyTorch Scikit-learn Develop AI models for disease prediction, classification, and prognosis. 3. AI Tools for Medical Imaging & Segmentation Gain hands-on skills with tools like: 3D Slicer – for CT/MRI/thermal image visualization and segmentation MITK (Medical Imaging Interaction Toolkit) – for interactive analysis ITK-SNAP – for anatomical structure annotation Learn to process DICOM files and perform tumor detection or tissue segmentation. 4. Healthcare Data Visualisation & Storytelling Create interactive dashboards and visual summaries using: Power BI (free desktop version) Tableau Public Image or 3D data visualization Ideal for analyzing hospital data , patient trends , and AI model outputs . 5. ICU & Hospital Dataset Handling Work with real-world clinical datasets. Learn to extract features from ECG, vitals, lab data , and build models for patient monitoring and outcome prediction . 6. Medical Data Annotation Tools Prepare training data for supervised learning models in healthcare. 7. Filtering & Denoising Medical Signals Learn how to clean noisy health data (ECG, EEG, thermal signals) using: Python SciPy , OpenCV , Matlab Image Denoising & Filtering Apply these techniques for accurate diagnosis and monitoring . Why These Tools Matter All tools are open-source, and used by top hospitals, health-tech firms, and researchers. Expertise in these tools gives you a practical edge in job roles like AI Engineer, Clinical Data Analyst, Medical Imaging Scientist, and Healthcare Product Designer among others. Tech Computer Science & Engineering - Global (2+2) or (3+1) with University of Essex, UK Empower your future with the B. Tech Computer Science Engineering - Global (2+2) or (3+1) Pathway Program at Bennett University in partnership with University of Essex. Designed for students with global aspirations ready to make an impact in the world of technology, the B. Tech Global Pathways program allows students to build a strong technical foundation with two years at Bennett University, India, before transferring to the renowned University of Essex, UK for the final two years. Upon successful completion, students earn a globally recognized Bachelor of Engineering – Computer Systems Engineering degree from University of Essex, UK. The B. Tech CSE – Global (2+2) or (3+1) is a perfect program for students seeking a world-class education, enhanced career opportunities, and a global academic experience without committing to an entire program abroad from the outset. Program Details Name Duration University Curriculum Highlights B. To develop critical thinking, leadership and entrepreneurship skills with global standards. To enhance career prospects in creative writing. A vibrant, student-centric environment with excellent support for international students. By completing initial years in India, students can save up to 40-50 lakhs, compared to completing all four years at University of Essex, UK. Eligible for up to 2 years of post-study work permit. Partner University University of Essex, UK University of Essex, established in 1964, is a distinguished public research university located in Colchester, United Kingdom. Renowned for its commitment to pushing boundaries and fostering curiosity, Essex has built a reputation for academic excellence and innovative research. The university is recognized for research excellence in computer science, sociology, economics and econometrics, politics and international studies, modern languages and linguistics. NIT, Warangal Ms. Shraddha Jain Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIT BHU, Varanasi ELIGIBILITY Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process Eligibility-Domestic & SAARC Domestic Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with Physics and Mathematics & any other third* subject with minimum 60% marks OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. Selection Process Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 Subjects Score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score). Personal Interview will be conducted with the panel during the admission process. OUR ASK Frequently Asked Questions What is the Global Pathways Programme at Bennett University? The Global Pathways Programme (GPP) is an academic collaboration that allows students to complete the first two years of their B. Tech in Computer Science Engineering (CSE) at Bennett University and transfer to University of Essex, UK, for the remaining academic years i.e. - 2+2 or 3+1 based on pathway chosen by the student. Upon successful completion, students earn a globally recognized Bachelor of Engineering – Computer Systems Engineering degree from University of Essex, UK. What are the benefits of enrolling in this program? Internationally recognized degree from a top UK university. Cost-effective compared to completing four years abroad. Seamless credit transfer with a structured curriculum. Global exposure and cultural diversity by studying in both India and the UK. Career and research opportunities in a global tech ecosystem. Post-study work permit in the UK for up to 2 years. What is the eligibility criterion for this program? Students must have: Class X & XII aggregate of 60% Minimum 55% aggregate in Physics + Mathematics + a third subject (e.g., Chemistry, CS, IT, Biology, etc.) in Class XII Student must comply with the English language proficiency requirements (IELTS/ TOEFL as per partner university guidelines). How does the 2+2 or 3+1 model work? Years 1 & 2 or 3 (based on pathway chosen): Study at Bennett University, India – Core CSE fundamentals, programming, mathematics, and basic engineering subjects. Years 3 & 4 or Year 4 (based on pathway chosen): Transfer to University of Essex, U.K. – Advanced CSE subjects, research, and specializations in AI, Data Science, Cybersecurity, etc. What degree will I receive after completing the program? You will be awarded a Bachelor of Engineering – Computer Systems Engineering degree from University of Essex, UK. How much cost savings can I expect? By following the 2+2 model or a 3+1 model, students save approximately ₹40-50 lakhs, compared to completing all four years at University of Essex, UK. What career opportunities are available after this program? Opportunities in global tech companies such as Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Facebook. Eligibility for a UK post-study work permit for up to 2 years. Research and internship opportunities at University of Essex, UK. How do I apply for the B. Tech CSE - Global (2+2/3+1) Program? Applying to B. Tech CSE – Global (2+2/3+1) program at Bennet University is quick and easy. To apply for the admission, all you need is to share your details (takes less than a minute) and our team will get back you Click here to apply: https://applications.bennett.edu.in/bu Alternatively, you can reach out to: Madhur Tandon Head, Global Pathways Program Mobile Phone: +91 9654 58 4594 Email: madhur.tandon@bennett.edu.in Download Brochure Enquire Now × SUBMIT YOUR ENQUIRY × Download Brochure WordPress Lightbox

ABOUT About B.Tech Computer Science Engineering The curriculum is forward-looking and designed for industry requirements. It focuses on futuristic technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Robotics, Machine Learning, IoT, Genetic Research, Bioinformatics, Big Data DevOps, Blockchain, Augmented & Virtual reality, Drones, Quantum Computing, Product Design Technology, Cyber Security, Full Stack, Cloud Computing, Automation, Gaming and more. At the School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology, the B.Tech course curriculum/syllabus & teaching pedagogy give equal importance to theory and hands-on training. The syllabus has been designed based on the latest market trends, industry needs and research outcomes relevant to different domains of CSE. Industry representatives on our Board of Studies include those from Google Research, SenRa, Terra Drone, REdBay, Lenovo, Automation Anywhere, Ui Path, AWS Academy, Unity Technologies, Simulanis, Yepper, White Bears Studio, Probability Gaming, Scientific Games, NVIDIA, Aindra Systems, IBM India, Sense AI, Here Tech, TCS , Towards Blockchain, Bankchainasset.com, DigiLedge, QoS Technologies Pvt ltd, Data Security Council of India, and Empass Learning. The School faculty comprises all Ph.D holders from premium institutions of the country, with wide international exposure. The curriculum itself is designed in consultation with industry experts from Google Research, NVIDIA, IBM India, TCS, AWS Academy, to name a few. The course offers 14 specialisations that help prepare students for the market. A detailed syllabus of B.Tech CSE will encourage students to choose their vision. The School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology offers flexible and modular choices of Minors, Honours, Open Electives, Audit courses, two internships of 8 and 16 weeks, and one year internship in the final year, besides opportunities to participate in competitions, hackathons, clubs, and societies, and in project-based experiential learning programs. Along with the Majors, there is a possibility in the curriculum to earn extra credits and additional knowledge through Honours and Minors. B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Curriculum 2025-29 B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Curriculum 2024-28 B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Curriculum 2023-27 B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Curriculum 2022-26 B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Curriculum 2021-25 B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Curriculum 2020-24 B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Curriculum 2019-23 B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Curriculum 2018-22 Specialisation Electives All specialisations have their lists/buckets of specialisation electives. Students may choose up to three Electives from their specialisation list. Minors Minors pack 4 courses that provides inter-disciplinary exposure to the students. The courses are offered between the 3rd and the 6th semester, with one credit to be won in each semester. (Total of 16 credits) Honours Honours pack 6 courses that provides additional knowledge on the same/other CSE specialisations. The courses are offered between the 4th and the 7th semesters, with two courses in the 5th & 6th semesters and one course each in the 4th and 7th semesters. (Total 18 credits) Open Electives These courses are University-wide courses from different schools. A student in CSE can choose a course from the School of Law or Media or Management or Engineering or CSET. Courses such as Mobile Journalism, Start-ups and Copyright Law are among the most popular of the Electives. (Total 12 credits) Specialisations Artificial Intelligence The CEOs and CXOs of NVIDIA, Aindra, Here Tech, Amazon, and other academicians have contributed to the curriculum design of the specialisation. The specialisation electives offered under AI are Cognitive Modelling, Advanced Computer Vision and Video Analytics, Reinforcement Learning, Image and Video Processing, and Natural Language Processing. These courses match the best of international standards in AI specialisation. The other high point is the arrangement the University has reached with NVIDIA, SAP, Hitachi and Times Internet to enable students to work on cutting-edge projects. Know More Cloud Computing Cloud Computing is in high market demand for its scalability, on-demand solutions, and flexible pricing. To match the demand, there are specialised electives and certification options available, that include Cloud Services Development and Operations, Git and GitHub for Version Control, AWS Cloud Support Associate, Developing Solutions for Microsoft Azure, Google Associate Cloud Engineer, among others. Know More Blockchain The curriculum for Blockchain specialisation has been developed in consultation with CEOs and CXOs of BankchainAsset.com, Digiledge, besides other subject matter experts. The specialisation electives offered as part of the B.Tech syllabus include Blockchain Technologies, Smart Contracts and Solidity Programming, Digital Currencies and Blockchain, Modern Cryptography, among others. * Samyak Jain (B.Tech CSE 2017-21) launched ‘Instadapp,’ a start-up for managing blockchain-based assets, and received funding of 1.4 million USD in 2019. Know More Cyber Security With increased dependency on online transactions and other social activities, there is now high demand for a robust Cyber Security framework. Keeping that in mind, the Cyber Security curriculum has been designed by the CEOs and CXOs of the Data Security Council of India (DSCI), QoS Technologies, IBM, and other researchers. The courseware includes topics on Cyber Security with Blockchain, Malware Analysis for Mobile Devices, Security and Privacy for Big Data Analytics, Penetration Testing and Ethical Hacking, among others. Also, to have students work on cutting-edge projects, the University has tied-up with DSCI, Fortinet, CompTIA and CISCO. Know More Data Science Data scientists play a key role in the working of smart cities, automation industry, BFSI and Fintech. Given the importance of Data Science, Bennett University has put together a curriculum with the help of CEOs and CXOs of Microsoft, Google, TCS, and other researchers. The curriculum includes elective courses on Structural Equation Modelling; Security and Privacy for Big Data Analytics; Data Visualisation; Big Data Analytics and Business Intelligence; and Satellite Data Analytics. Know More Gaming As of 2022, the gaming industry is worth 200 billion dollars which encompasses dozens of job disciplines and thousands of exciting jobs worldwide. Due to the gaming industry’s significance to culture, entertainment and technological growth, it is one of the most interesting sectors in the contemporary technological landscape. Know More Robotic Process Automation The specialisation helps students learn about software-based tools that help to automate manual rule-based human tasks. CEOs and CXOs of Automation Anywhere, UIPath have contributed in designing its curriculum. Courses on Advanced Robotic Process Automation; Digital Process Automation Architecture; and Enterprise Process Automation Framework are offered to students as part of the specialisation electives. (Bot Lab by Automation Anywhere and Speech Devices Lab by Amazon are available to students.) Know More DevOps DevOps practices help merge development, quality assurance, and operations (deployment and integration) into a single, continuous set of processes. The key topics covered in this specialisation comprise Introduction to Advanced DevOps concepts, Cloud Services, Integration, Deployment and Testing, among others. Students get the opportunity to study advanced subjects such as Cloud Services Development and Operations, Software Craftsmanship in DevOps, Test Automation in DevOps, Agile Software Development, and many others from the choice of subjects available as specialisation electives. Know More Full Stack The specialisation helps students understand the industry trends on accelerating the processes of design, development, and deployment. The specialisation electives available for Full Stack are Front-End Web UI Frameworks and Tools: Bootstrap, Front-End Web UI Frameworks, and Tools: React, Mobile App Development with React Native, and Application Development with Cloud Platforms, among many others. Know More Drones The drone specialisation builds critical thinking and problem solving skills in addition to technical skills, which benefit students along any career path they choose. Students who pick up the specialisation can contribute to safe infrastructure maintenance; to law enforcement support; and to agriculture management. Know More Quantum Computing Today, industries, academia, private and government organisations around the world are spending billions of dollars on quantum research and development. In fact, leading companies such as Google, Microsoft, IBM, Air-Bus, and Intel are in race to achieve quantum supremacy. In line with it, Bennett University has designed a curriculum for Quantum Computing, covering topics such as Quantum Computing Foundations; Machine Learning with Quantum Computing; Quantum Computing for Data Analysis; Quantum Cryptography; Quantum Information Theory; Quantum Computer Architecture; Security and Privacy for Big Data Analytics; and Cloud Infrastructure and Services. Know More IoT and Robotics IoRT is not IoT aided by Robotics or Robotics driven by IoT- but rather an amalgamation of the disciplines. It was slowly formed as a result of realizations that the two fields had a lot of overlapping of purpose, and could greatly benefit from a focus on their combination. Because the subject of robotics contains both hardware and software components, understanding of both is particularly valuable. Robotics is a holistic combined process of designing an autonomously working structure which logically comprehends the stimulus around and responding to it. Know More Augmented & Virtual Reality The market wants professionals who can use Augmented Reality (AR), Virtual Reality (VR) and Extended Reality (ER) to superposition the virtual environment on the physical world. To prepare the students for it, Bennett University has got CEOs and CXOs of well-known organisations such as Simulanis, Yeppar, Whitebears Studio, Probability Gaming, Scientific Games to design a suitable curriculum. Courses on VR Gaming, VR and 360, Video Production, 3D Interaction Design in Virtual Reality and Game Mechanics are offered as part of the Specialisation Electives. For project development, resources such as Apple Mac Lab, Nao Robot V6, and other head-mounted devices are available within the campus. Students completing the course will become market ready for jobs such as, AR-VR game developer, UX/UI Designer, Mixed reality artist and technical 3D artist. Know More Product Design Technology The specialisation brings together the creativity and practicality needed to bridge the gap between design engineering and product design. Product design technology empowers the students to create and visualise innovative concepts and transform the concepts into working products. The specialisation focuses on imparting technical skills such as using computational analysis to design the structure of products. Know More Mobile Technologies Mobile Technologies specialization encompasses iOS technology. Swift is a powerful and intuitive programming language for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, and watchOS. Writing Swift code is interactive and fun, the syntax is concise yet expressive, and Swift includes modern features developers love. Swift code is safe by design and produces software that runs lightning-fast. Read More UI/UX Course Outcome Students are expected to develop the ability to apply the knowledge of mathematics, science and engineering appropriate to the discipline. Students are expected to apply mathematical foundations, algorithmic principles and computer science theory in modelling, design and conduct of experiments as well as in data interpretation and analysis. Students are expected to develop the ability to identify, formulate and solve real engineering problems and understand the global impact of engineering solutions. Projects In the 8th semester, students must take up either an Industrial Project, an R&D project or a start-up project worth 12 credits. Capstone Project of 5 credits in the 7th semester is an advanced course to prepare the students for the industry. Internships Two internships of 8 and 16 weeks are a part of the B.Tech CSE syllabus. The 8-week internship is available after the 3rd year, during summer vacation. The 16-week internship is available in the last semester. Students can optionally do additional internships at the end of the 1st year and the 2nd year during summer vacations. There is also an option for the students to spend one year in an Industry internship in the final year of study. B.Tech CSE students have interned at Microsoft India, Amazon, Adobe India, Here Maps, Innovaccer, and many more. Skills Some of the advanced skills CSE students will acquire: Strong sense of professionalism Leadership Soft-skill Group discussions Public speaking We often organize: Personal interview workshops CV and cover letter workshops Networking events Social-media workshops Corporate & professional mentoring sessions for the benefit of students through our career cell. Research groups for working on popular research-oriented problem statements. Research Paper Writing Workshops using Tools like Latex. Talks/Seminars delivered by industry experts from different domains of computer science. Our strategic collaborations ensure that our students get a glimpse of global education practices.
