NIT, Warangal Ms. Shraddha Jain Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIT BHU, Varanasi ELIGIBILITY B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process Eligibility Domestic Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with Physics and Mathematics & any other third* subject with minimum 55% marks OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI Pass with 55% aggrigate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third* subject with highest score) * Third subject should not be vocational- Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc OUR ASK B.Tech Computer Science Engineering Frequently Asked Questions How can a student get an Honors degree? Pursuing an honors B.Tech degree gives students a definite advantage over a general student in terms of additional knowledge. At Bennett University, we encourage students to strive for academic excellence and offer the flexibility to earn an additional 18 credits above a general B.Tech degree. Any student can earn credits towards an honors degree on top of meeting the regular degree requirements subject to qualifying criteria through academic performance. The selection of the students rests entirely with the Department subject to their assessment of the student’s strengths and capabilities. What are the eligibility and requirements for pursuing an honors degree? The criteria to be eligible to pursue an Honors Degree is reviewed every year. As of date, the student must possess a CGPA of 7.5 should have no backlog courses to pursue an honors degree. What are the eligibility and requirements to pursue minors? Minors credits are earned through the range of electives available to students through various departments across the University. The pre-requisites vary by each course and are decided by the department offering the course. How does a minor benefit in student’s career? A Minor is a combination of courses/ electives taken through the duration of University education in fields other than the student’s parent department. The courses taken as Minor allow students to gain a broader understanding of the world with exposure to a wide range of subjects and areas of practice. This broader understanding is a critical requirement to succeed in the world of the future. Can an undergraduate student take part in research activities? Bennett University focuses on research, experiential, and applied learning. Undergraduate students are encouraged to work with members of the faculty in their live research projects. Such exposure makes students understand the intricacies of the research process and deepens their knowledge. What are the options available to 8th-semester students? 8th-semester BTech students can undergo either internship in the industry or start their venture if they have a startup idea. Students are also free to do courses with partner universities for their Masters program. Such flexibility helps students prepare themselves for the future and carve a proper roadmap ahead of them. Do you assist students in placements, summer internships, and industry tours? Bennett University has a dedicated Career Services Centre that works closely with the industry to participate in guest lectures, curricular inputs, joint program development, internships, and placements. To which companies have students gone for summer internships? BTech students from various programs have gone for summer internships in companies like Microsoft, HP Enterprise, CommVault, Sourcefuse, Suzlon, Yamaha, BHEL, DMRC, CDAC, Google, Here Maps, Honda Motorcycle, Indigo, Reliance Jio, IBM, Saint Gobain, C&S Electric, Daikin, GAIL (India) Ltd., GMR, Gumption Labs, Hitachi, JK Paper Ltd. LG, Mobikon, Safexpress, iSMRITI (IIT Kanpur), JCB, Yamaha Motors, Xen.Ai, Hindustan Coca Cola Beverages, Irtish Consulting, Leadingindia.ai, Kennis, Tata Advanced System, Tata Power, Cloud Fabrica, and Tata Consultancy Download Brochure Enquire Now × SUBMIT YOUR ENQUIRY × Download Brochure WordPress Lightbox

B.A (Hons.) Philosophy - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login B.A (Hons.) Philosophy The Philosophy Major at the School of Liberal Arts, Bennett University offers students a rigorous, intellectually stimulating environment to explore fundamental questions about existence, knowledge, ethics, and society. Philosophy explores fundamental questions about existence, knowledge, ethics, and logic. It encourages rigorous reasoning, conceptual analysis, and the development of coherent arguments. The discipline spans areas such as metaphysics, epistemology, ethics, and political philosophy. The philosophy program is designed to: (i) introduce students to philosophical thought and argumentation in the first two semesters; (ii) develop analytical and ethical reasoning skills through courses in logic, ethics, and existentialism; and (iii) offer electives in applied philosophy, philosophy of science, and Eastern and Western traditions in later semesters. Theory courses – 3-4 lectures a week depending on course credits, each lasting 55 minutes. Discussion-based seminars – Textual analysis and debate-based learning. The curriculum is designed to offer a strong academic foundation through Major-Core courses , complemented by Major-Electives for added specialization. Major-Core At the heart of the program, the Major-Core (MJ) curriculum delivers essential and advanced coursework, with students required to complete a minimum of 45 credits, up to a maximum of 60 credits. Introduction to Indian Philosophy Discourses in Ethics Introduction to Western Philosophy Greek Philosophy Contemporary Indian Philosophy Formal Logic Philosophy of Mind Applied Ethics Continental Philosophy Philosophy of Language Indian Theories of Self Metaethics Indian Epistemology Feminist Philosophy Major-Elective To further enrich their learning experience, students can select from Major-Electives (MJ-Elec), offering additional flexibility and breadth, with credit options ranging from a minimum of 27 to a maximum of 36 credits. Yoga Philosophy Social and Political Philosophy Philosophy of Religion Ethics and Technology Philosophy of Film Care Ethics Philosophy of Law Philosophy of Science Bio-Ethics Admission Counsellors Connect with our counsellors for any admissions related queries. explore Fee Structure Know more about fee structure. explore Scholarship Know more about Scholarship. explore Fee Structure 2025 Major: Psychology | Political Science & IR | Economics | English | Business Studies | Philosophy | Sociology Fee Type Year 1 Year 2 Year 3 TOTAL Tuition fee (₹) 4,70,000 4,70,000 4,70,000 14,10,000 Registration Fees One Time (₹) 45,000 – – 45,000 Total Fees (₹) 5,15,000 4,70,000 4,70,000 14,55,000 * Fee is subject to revision with the new academic year as per Bennett University policy. The standard foundation courses are: The Art and Science of Inquiry Logic and Reasoning Bibliophile System Thinking and Environment Sciences for Liberal Arts Intro to Polity and International Relation Build A Start-Up (BASU) Understanding Love A Primer on Public Policy Introduction to Philosophy: Understanding India Introduction to Psychology Introduction to Economics and Community Engagement Start-Up Colloquium Medical Humanities Creative Writing Thinking with Numbers Public Speaking Sports Introduction to Sociology Financial and Risk Literacy Leadership Colloquium 23 Minor Disciplines Psychology | Political Science & IR | Economics | English | Business Studies | Philosophy | Sociology | Mathematics | Marketing | Finance | Advertising | Legal Studies | Artificial Intelligence (AI) | Genetics | Design | Data Science | Sciences for Liberal Arts | Causal Methods| Public Policy | Entrepreneurship | Creative Arts | Creative Writing | Media Studies International Immersion Program Semester Abroad Program Students have the option to spend one semester to a full year studying at prestigious international institutions, gaining exposure to diverse academic frameworks and cultural environments. Current partner institutions include: National Taiwan University Augustana University HSE, Russia Summer School Programs For those seeking shorter yet impactful global experiences, summer school programs provide specialized learning opportunities: University of Nebraska, Omaha: A 6-week program designed to offer a deep dive into liberal arts disciplines. Babson College, USA: A 1–2-week Babson Build Entrepreneurship Program exclusively for UG2 and UG3 students, focusing on entrepreneurial skills and innovation. University of British Columbia, Canada: A 4-week intensive program providing exposure to international teaching standards and diverse academic content. Short-Term Study Abroad Programs Students can also participate in short-term study programs, such as the 2-week immersive program at UCJC, Spain, which combines academic learning with cultural exploration. Tanvi BA Liberal Arts Batch: 2020-23 Events BA Honours Programmes Frequently Asked Questions When was the B.A Liberal Arts program initiated under the School of Liberal Arts at Bennett University? The Liberal Arts program started with its first batch in 2020. It offers the opportunity to learn from highly qualified and experienced teachers. The program's foundation rests on two pillars -- the development of all-around personality and life skills. What are the majors available in the BA Liberal Arts course at Bennett University? The students can choose Majors from seven domains: Psychology, Political Science & IR, Economics, English, Business Studies, Philosophy & Sociology How is the BA Liberal Arts curriculum structured? In the first semester of the program, the students will be introduced to all the majors to select the Major which suits them the most. The choice of Majors and Electives is given from the second semester onwards. How do electives benefit a student's career? An elective is a combination of courses taken through the duration of university education. The courses taken as electives allow students to understand the world with exposure to a wide range of subjects and areas of practice. This broader understanding is a critical requirement to succeed in the world of the future. Can an undergraduate student take part in research activities? Bennett University focuses on research, experiential, and applied learning. Undergraduate students are encouraged to work with members of the faculty in their live research projects. Such exposure makes students understand the intricacies of the research process and deepens their knowledge Do you assist students in placements, summer internships, and industry tours? Bennett University has a dedicated Career Services Centre that works closely with the industry to participate in guest lectures, curricular inputs, joint program development, internships, and placements. What are the future career options after pursuing BA Liberal Arts? BA Liberal Arts graduates can look for rewarding careers in advertising, public relations, financial analysis, design, journalism, publishing, corporate communications, marketing, etc., or start a successful entrepreneurial journey of their own. What kind of industry exposure do students have access to? This is one of the unique hallmarks of the BA Liberal Arts program as it benefits from faculty members teaching courses in management, which in turn, provides students an opportunity to continuously interact with industry experts from India and abroad. MBA (Production & Operations) | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login MBA Production Operations Apply Now MBA Production Operations Apply Now About Mba Production Operations Management The MBA (Production & Operations) is a two-year postgraduate program that equips students to manage manufacturing and service operations efficiently. It focuses on the planning, design, and management of production systems and business operations, and deals with optimising processes, improving productivity, managing supply chains, ensuring quality, and delivering products and services effectively. The program covers supply chain management, analytics, risk management, and global operations in the era of Industry 4.0. The program helps students understand how goods and services are planned, produced, and delivered seamlessly, while building practical skills to improve quality, reduce costs, and manage modern, technology-driven operations. Additionally, it prepares graduates to take on leadership roles in operations across diverse industries and global markets. Duration 2 Years Specialisations NA Credits - Start Date July 2026 Curriculum Highlights Electives Industry 4.0 and Beyond Service Operations Management Risk Analytics in Supply Chains Data-Driven Production and Operations Decisions Global Operations & Logistics Production Planning and Control Experiential Learning Industry projects with manufacturing and service organisations Simulation exercises on operations and supply chains Internships in production, logistics, and supply chain firms Case studies on operational challenges and real-world solutions Why MBA (Production & Operations) from Bennett University High Industry Demand: Growing demand for operations and supply chain experts Analytics-Driven Learning: Hands-on exposure to analytics-driven decision-making Industry 4.0 Focus: Learn advanced production, logistics, and risk management techniques Diverse Career Opportunities: Roles in manufacturing, logistics, supply chain management, and consulting. Practical Skill Development: Training in production planning, logistics, risk management, and quality control. Program Objectives The program aims to develop expertise in production planning, operations, and supply chains; use data analytics for production and operational decision-making; manage global operations and logistics efficiently; apply risk management and quality control techniques in operations; and build leadership and strategic problem-solving capabilities. Learning Outcomes The MBA in Production & Operations prepares students to: Make data-driven production and operational decisions Manage supply chains and global operations effectively Implement Industry 4.0 technologies in operational contexts, and plan, monitor Control production processes efficiently Career Pathways The MBA Production & Operations graduates are well-positioned for operational leadership and specialist roles across a wide range of sectors, including manufacturing, e-commerce, supply chain firms, consulting, and service operations, where efficient production and operations management are critical to organisational success. Opportunities/roles they may take up are: Operations Manager Supply Chain Analyst Production Planner Logistics Manager, among others. Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Passed Graduation in any discipline from a recognised university with a minimum 50% marks with English as the medium of instruction. NRI: For international applicants, a minimum of 50% in the qualifying examination is required, along with having studied the prerequisite subjects for admission to the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on the weighted score of CAT / XAT / NMAT / MAT / CMAT / GMAT / BU MAT/ CUET Percentile score & Graduation & Personal Interview. MBA (Production & Operations) Program Fee Get detailed information about the MBA (Production & Operations Management) program fee structure, including tuition, hostel fees, and other applicable charges. Click here for details. IIT, Delhi Dr. Laxmi Gupta Ph.D. - IIT, Delhi Dr. Neha Zaidi Ph.D. - Aligarh Muslim University Dr. Ahammed Junaid FPM - MICA, Ahmedabad Ms. Anu Mohta Ph.d.(Thesis Submitted) - BHU Dr. Saad Ashraf Ph.D. - FMS, University of Delhi Dr. Ratan Kumar FPM - Management Development Institute, Gurgaon Mr. Krishan Ph.D. (Pursuing) - JNU, Delhi Dr. Vijay Prakash Mishra Ph.D. - Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, Pune Mr. Abhishek Saran Ph.D.(Pursuing) - IIM, Sambalpur Ms. Priya Mandleshwar Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIM, Indore Ms. Rimleena Boro Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIM, Indore OUR ASK MBA (Production & Operations) Frequently Asked Questions Who should choose the Production & Operations specialisation? This MBA specialisation is ideal for students interested in manufacturing, supply chain, logistics, quality management, project management, and process optimisation, especially those who enjoy problem-solving and improving operational efficiency within an MBA (Production & Operations). What key skills will I develop in Production & Operations Management? Students who pursue an MBA in Production & Operations Management develop skills in process improvement, supply chain management, quality control, operations planning, project management, lean systems, and decision-making, along with managerial and leadership capabilities through the MBA (Production & Operations). Is this specialisation suitable for students without an engineering background? Yes, while engineering students may find it familiar, non-engineering graduates can also excel. The program is designed to build concepts progressively, focusing on managerial applications rather than only technical aspects in the MBA (Production & Operations). What kind of practical exposure does the Production & Operations specialisation offer? The specialisation includes case studies, live projects, simulations, industry interactions, and practical problem-solving exercises that expose students to real-world operational challenges across industries as part of the MBA (Production & Operations). What career opportunities are available after an MBA (Production & Operations)? MBA graduates can pursue roles such as Operations Manager, Production Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Quality Analyst, Process Improvement Consultant, Logistics Manager, Project Manager, and Operations Analyst after completing the MBA (Production & Operations). Which industries hire Production & Operations Management graduates? Graduates are recruited by manufacturing firms, FMCG companies, logistics and supply chain organisations, e-commerce firms, automotive companies, pharmaceuticals, infrastructure, consulting firms, and service organisations with large operations that value an MBA (Production & Operations). How does an MBA in Production & Operations Management help in long-term career growth? This specialisation builds a strong foundation for leadership roles, as efficient operations are critical to business success. With experience, graduates can move into senior management, plant leadership, supply chain strategy, and operations consulting roles after pursuing an MBA (Production & Operations). School of Engineering And Applied Sciences - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Engineering and Applied Sciences Bennett University Today’s engineers must be citizens of the world. In the School of Engineering & Applied Sciences (SEAS), we are committed to developing global, multi-cultural and multifaceted engineers, who can operate across borders and boundaries. This is demonstrated by our curriculum and in the faculty, staff, and students we are striving to attract. SEAS at Bennett University is creating future ready professionals to inspire and transform the world with the best solutions to the technical issues. The School of Engineering and Applied Sciences comprises four departments: Electronics and Communication Engineering, Biotechnology, Mechanical Engineering, and Physics. SEAS provides not only fundamental knowledge of science and engineering, but also a strong and diligent understanding of research and development to provide complete solutions to technical and scientific problems. Dean’s Message Prof. (Dr.) N. Latha Dean - School of Engineering & Applied Sciences (SEAS) On behalf of faculty, staff, and students, I welcome you to the School of Engineering & Applied Sciences (SEAS) at Bennett University. The School of Engineering & Applied Science houses four departments: Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE), Biotechnology (BIOTECH) Mechanical Engineering (ME) and Physics (PHY). Read More × Prof. (Dr.) N. Latha Dean - School of Engineering & Applied Sciences (SEAS) The School of Engineering & Applied Sciences is an exciting place to be as it provides an environment that encourages the holistic development of a student and help transform students into lifelong learners with the ability to thrive in an ever-changing world. The school offers undergraduate, post graduate and doctoral level programs. The programs aim to blend rigorous academic instruction with real-world experiential learning. Whether you are interested in Mechanical Engineering, Electronics & Communication Engineering, Electronics Engineering and Computing, Engineering Physics or Biotechnology, our programs equip students with the knowledge and skills needed to excel in their chosen field. We emphasize to integrate classroom learning, internships, and experiential projects, we emphasize learnability in the curriculum ensuring that our students are well-prepared to adapt to new challenges and opportunities in their chosen careers. We believe in imparting pedagogical method to cater to student inclusivity and diversity. Students are also provided with an option to choose specialization courses in all our undergraduate programs offered by the school. The specialization courses are designed to develop skill set as per the current research trends and Industry requirements. Curriculum is tailored and periodically reviewed based on student feedbacks, faculty, alumni and experts from both Academia and Industry. All faculty are PhD holders from reputed Institutes in India and abroad and possess post-doctoral experience from some of the best Universities abroad. The School of Engineering & Applied Sciences (SEAS) at Bennett University remains at the forefront of innovation and cutting-edge research. Our faculty, serve in prominent leadership and editorial positions, and pursue impactful research with an interdisciplinary focus to address the complex challenges facing society today. This spirit of inquiry and exploration translates directly into the classroom, enriching our students' learning experiences and preparing them to be ethical problem solvers. The faculty has been sanctioned extramural grants from funding agencies. Besides teaching & research, most of the faculty in SEAS also supervise and mentor PhD scholars registered at the University. The Centre of Excellence (CoE) in Nanosensors and Nanomedicine under SEAS is a collaborative effort by all four departments that aims to foster interdisciplinary, cutting-edge research towards developing diagnostic and therapeutics in nanoscale for the healthcare sector and nanosensors for industrial purposes. We are proud of our alumni who have moved to institutes of high repute both in India & abroad for their higher studies. To name a few like IITs, IISERs, CSIR-IGIB, IISER Kolkata, BITS Pilani, University of Heidelberg, New York University, NorthEastern University, University of Oxford, University of Glasgow, Monash University, University Leipzig, Albert-Ludwigs Universitat Frieberg, University of Cologne. We welcome you to be part of SEAS family as a prospective student, current student, an alumnus, academic partner, or industry collaborator. I encourage you to visit webpages of individual departments to learn more about the impressive work of our faculty, students, and alumni. Departments Electronics and Communications Engineering Mechanical Engineering Physics Biotechnology Electronics and Communications Engineering Electronics and Communication Engineering is one of the most diverse technological fields in the world. The Department of Engineering and Applied Sciences at Bennett University offers B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering, B.Tech Electronics and Computer Engineering, and Ph.D programs. The SEAS provides unique opportunities in the field of research and development. The school covers the broadest areas in research. In tune with the academic philosophy of the University, the Department's curricula for the undergraduate and postgraduate programs aim at producing high-quality engineers in the area of Electronics & Communication who can take up challenges in design, development, research and manufacturing in areas such as the Internet of Things, 5G & Beyond, and Information Processing & Analytics. Students shall undertake courses in Basic Sciences, Mathematics, Basic Engineering as well as in core subjects of Electronics & Communication Engineering (ECE). They would be supported by laboratory facilities. All laboratories are equipped with state of the art instruments and software tools to enable students to perform design-oriented experiments and test their designs by simulation in research areas of Nanotechnology, VLSI Design & VLSI Signal Processing, Signal Processing for Information, Antenna and Microwave for 5G and above. Know More Courses BTech Electronics and Communications Engineering B.Tech Electronics and Computer Engineering Ph.D. in ECE Mechanical Engineering Mechanical Engineering is one of the most diversified traditional engineering domains. These engineers are trained to design and build machines & devices, make manual working ways easy and enhance work productivity. These machines extend the work capability and raise the standard of living. These engineers design and build machines to make normal human work easy with improved efficiency. In a way, Mechanical Engineers are involved in creating the future. They are the driving force behind many of the technologies and industrial processes in our modern world. Mechanical Engineering will be offered to train manpower with quality education in manufacturing, design, automation and thermal sciences. Students will have adequate flexibility to specialise in areas of their choice through the selection of electives and projects. The course will be designed to provide students with adequate exposure for hands-on experience in practice and design. It will emphasise on subjects like Flexible Manufacturing Systems, Computer Integrated Manufacturing, Robotics, Tribology, Composites and Laser Materials, Energy Engineering, Finite Element Methods etc to provide them to take up positions in manufacturing, design, energy generation and conservation and R&D. Know More Courses BTech in Mechanical Engineering Ph.D. in Mechanical Mathematics The role and use of mathematics have increased manifold in the past decade with the onset of the information revolution that has impacted several other disciplines as well. This has put additional expectations on mathematics from the point of view of teaching, research and applications. Skills in computational mathematics are needed more than ever before. Keeping this in mind a number of new courses (core as well as elective) have been designed and introduced at the undergraduate and postgraduate levels. The Department will also cater to the basic needs of various undergraduate and postgraduate programs in Engineering. Know More Courses Ph.D. in Mathematics Physics Established in 2016, the department has acheeved excellence in teaching and research. The department currently offers both academic programs at the undergraduate and postgraduate level. The academic programs include Engineering Physics through JEE Main and Ph.D. In addition, the department offers basic and topical courses for the entire student community of Bennett University. With eight faculty members, and several post-doctoral researchers, the department has a very vibrant interdisciplinary research environment. The areas of research interests and expertise spans a wide range including Biophysics, Condensed Matter Physics, Non-linear Dynamics and Hydrodynamics, Optics and Photonics, and Statistical Physics, covering both theoretical and experimental aspects. The faculty members of the department are also involved in several sponsored research and industrial consultancy projects. Browse our website to know more about various academic programs, research interests of the individual faculty members and the ongoing research projects. Please feel free to contact us for more information. Know More Courses B.Tech in Engineering Physics Ph.D in Physics Chemistry The Department of Chemistry is currently involved in promoting education and research in the interface of chemistry, biology, and material science. This involves coursework such as introductory chemistry and biology for engineers (ECHE102L), chemistry of bio molecules (EBTY103L). The research involves providing cost effective methods of biological detection, such as pathogen detection, sequencing, and biomedical device engineering. The research and teaching involve heavy degree of collaboration and consultation with faculties from Physics, Biotechnology, and ECE. Know More Courses PhD in Chemistry Biotechnology The importance of studying Biotechnology reflects on myriad fields, namely, environment, healthcare, agriculture and so on. To lead to a scientific breakthrough, Biotechnology has an immense impact in the private and public sectors; and innovations are one such component that drives the industry The Department of Biotechnology in Bennett University stands out with varied specialisations which is imperative in today’s world of innovation. Know More Courses BTech in Biotechnology Integrated BTech + MTech Biotechnology Ph.D. in Biotechnology × Dr. Neelanchali Asija Bhalla HoD (In-charge) Mechanical Engineering Department Greeting from the Mechanical Engineering Department of Bennett University! We started our journey in the year 2016. Over the past five years we have grown our expertise and competence in the core Mechanical Engineering curriculum and research. We offer a strong undergraduate programme in Mechanical Engineering, whereas, at the post graduate level, we offer Ph.D degrees. Several sponsored candidates from industries carry out their postgraduate studies in our department. Our department offers science-based engineering curriculum, emphasises to impart technical knowledge to students to make them proficient in solving engineering problems. The department offers large number of elective courses for providing wide spectrum of options to the students to pursue their interest in aerospace engineering, biomechanics, robotics and automation, advanced materials and manufacturing techniques, computer aided modelling & simulation studies and energy systems engineering. The course contents are periodically updated for introducing new scientific and technological developments. Undergraduate students are encouraged to undertake various research projects, as well as live projects with industries, to give them exposure to industrial environment and working ethics. Since, industrial internships and R&D projects are an integral part of B. Tech ME curriculum. Our Department maintains active research groups for carrying out collaborative and interdisciplinary research. We have state-of-the-art research facilities to support our academic programmes and research. Our faculty members have excellent academic credentials; and are members of editorial boards of national and international journals. They review technical articles for journals on a regular basis and organise international symposia and conferences. We are extremely confident that in years to come, ME Department, with its industry curriculum, as well as active industrial and interdisciplinary research collaborations, will strengthen and enforce the reputation of itself as well as of Bennett University, as an enviable platform for higher education! Three country program - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login OUR ASK 3 Country Program – Your Passport to a Global Future Bennett University proudly introduces the 3-Country Program with top global universities. The 3-Country Program is an innovative and future-ready study abroad initiative designed to immerse students in the academic, cultural, and professional environments of three different countries. This pioneering program redefines international education by offering students an exciting opportunity to study across borders while earning a globally recognized degree and opening doors for a global career. Students begin their academic journey at Bennett University in India, where they build a strong foundation in their chosen field. In the next phase, they transition to a partner institution in a second country, gaining deeper academic insights and exposure to new ways of learning. Finally, students will complete their degree at a renowned global university in a third country. More than just academics, the 3-Country Program is a transformative global experience. It enables students to live, learn, and thrive in diverse cultural settings, helping them develop adaptability, cross-cultural communication skills, and an international mindset—highly valued in today's interconnected world. What makes the program especially appealing is its cost efficiency. By starting their 3-Country Program in India and strategically transitioning abroad, students save significantly on international tuition and living expenses compared to enrolling directly in a full-term overseas program. OUR ASK Programs Available B. Tech CSE Global (2+1+1) With – University of Birmingham, UK Duration – 4 Years India > Dubai > UK Know more BBA Global (1+1+1) With – University of Reading, UK Duration – 3 Years India > Malaysia > UK Know more Partner Universities University of Birmingham, UK Founded in 1900, the University of Birmingham is one of the world’s leading public research universities. It holds membership in the Russell Group and is ranked among the top 100 universities globally (QS World Rankings). With cutting-edge programs in engineering, computing, and sciences, the university combines academic rigor with real-world relevance. Its Dubai campus extends this excellence to the Middle East, offering identical curriculum, faculty standards, and global recognition. QS World Rankings 2026 - 76 University of Reading, UK The University of Reading is one of the UK’s most respected institutions, renowned for its business, economics, finance, and sustainability programs. With campuses in both the UK and Malaysia, it offers a rich, globally integrated education model. The Henley Business School (Top 50th Business School in the world), part of the university, is one of the oldest and most prestigious business schools in Europe, ranked for its impactful research and career-ready graduates. M.Tech (Artificial Intelligence) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) Eligibility criteria, Course, Fee, Curriculum | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login M.Tech (Artificial Intelligence) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) Apply Now M.Tech (Artificial Intelligence) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) Apply Now About M.Tech Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning About M.Tech + Ph.D. in Artificial Intelligence (AI) The Master of Technology (M.Tech) Artificial Intelligence + Ph.D. is a rigorous, five-year dual degree program designed to cultivate elite AI researchers and thought leaders. It combines an intensive two-year Master’s curriculum focused on core AI theory and practical application with an advanced three-year Doctoral phase dedicated to original, high-impact research. Students gain immediate exposure to cutting-edge research environments, culminating in the award of a combined M.Tech and Ph.D. degree. The program is structured to bridge the gap between academic theory and industry innovation, preparing graduates for top-tier research and development roles globally. Duration 5 Years Specializations 4 Credits 80 Start Date July 2026 Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering/Technology or equivalent of BCA/B.Sc with minimum 60% marks. NRI: A recognised bachelor’s degree or equivalent qualification from an accredited institution. Selection Process Selection will be based on Aggregate Graduation Percentage. M.Tech (Artificial Intelligence) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) Program Fee Get detailed information about the AI Integrated program fee structure, including tuition, hostel fees, and other applicable charges. Click here for details. Why M.Tech (AI) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) from Bennett University? Accelerated Research Career: The integrated structure fast-tracks the transition from coursework to doctoral research, saving time compared to pursuing the degrees separately. Mandatory Research Publication: The program emphasises practical research, requiring a high-impact publication during the Ph.D. phase to ensure original and significant contribution. Seamless Transition: A dedicated Comprehensive Examination serves as a gateway, ensuring academic rigour and a smooth, continuous flow from the M.Tech coursework to the Ph.D. research phase. Interdisciplinary Faculty: Learn and collaborate with leading experts across multiple domains, all focused on the frontier of AI research. Specialisations Students select a track during the M.Tech phase that aligns with their intended doctoral research focus: Signal Processing: Focuses on applying advanced AI/Deep Learning to time-series and sensor data. Robotics: Integrates Machine Learning and control systems for intelligent automation. Computer Vision: Deals with enabling machines to "see" and interpret visual data. Natural Language Processing (NLP): Focuses on the interaction between computers and human language. Program Objectives The program aims to train world-class researchers and engineers who can tackle fundamental AI challenges and drive innovation. The program aims to impart deep theoretical knowledge and practical expertise across core AI domains, enabling graduates to make significant, original contributions to their chosen specialisation track, and solve complex, real-world problems through ethical and advanced AI systems. Learning Outcomes Graduates will be able to design, implement, and evaluate novel AI systems; conduct high-impact, independent research culminating in peer-reviewed publications; possess deep specialisation in one core AI track; and demonstrate advanced professional skills in critical thinking, project management, and ethical AI deployment. Curriculum Highlights Research-focused curriculum with a full-semester internship and an M.Tech thesis aligned to the Ph.D. topic. Access to advanced infrastructure, including high-performance GPU clusters and specialised labs. Global exposure through international collaborations, short-term visits, and dual supervision opportunities. Strong publication-oriented mentoring for top conferences and journals. Regular research seminars and a rigorous proposal–defense process to ensure high-quality research. Emphasis on technical depth and ethical responsibility for responsible AI innovation. Career Pathways Graduates of this program are uniquely positioned for high-impact roles requiring both advanced engineering skill and deep research capability, opening doors in both academia and cutting-edge industry labs. Career opportunities for graduates include roles such as Research Scientist (AI/ML), Applied AI Scientist, Robotics Engineer or Scientist, NLP Engineer or LLM Architect, Computer Vision Engineer, AI Consultant or Strategist, AI Ethicist, Postdoctoral Researcher or Faculty, and even leadership positions such as CTO or Co-founder of AI-driven startups. The program prepares students for impactful careers in major technology companies, global AI research labs, leading academic and research institutions, robotics firms, and innovation-focused enterprises. Faculty Prof. (Dr.) Rajeev Tiwari Dean, School of Artificial Intelligence Dr. Manoj Sharma Ph.D. - IIT Delhi Dr. Pratyush Pranav Ph.D. - University of Groningen Dr. Dinesh Kumar Ph.D. - University of Coimbra, Portugal Dr. Shakshi Sharma Ph.D. - University of Tartu, Estonia Dr. Neelam Choudhary Ph.D. - IIT Guwahati Dr. Navneet Pratap Singh Ph.D. - IIT Indore Dr. Yajnaseni Dash Ph.D. - IIT Delhi Dr. Gaurav Sharma Ph.D. - NIT, Hamirpur Dr. Prateek Yadav Ph.D.- IIT Delhi OUR ASK M.Tech (AI) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) Frequently Asked Questions What are the eligibility criteria to pursue M.Tech (Artificial Intelligence) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree)? Bachelor’s degree in engineering/technology or equivalent of MCA/M.Sc. or related disciplines with minimum 60% marks. Is a GATE score mandatory for admission to the AI Dual Degree program? While not always mandatory, a valid score in GATE (CS, EC, EE, or DA) is generally required for institutional assistantships and often considered for shortlisting. How is the M.Tech + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) program different from a regular M.Tech and Ph.D. in AI? This program is a continuous, focused journey. The M.Tech thesis directly feeds into the Ph.D. research, and the entire program is designed for an accelerated research path, unlike two separate, sequential degrees. Is it possible to exit the program after the M.Tech phase? Yes, subject to meeting all M.Tech academic and credit requirements (including the comprehensive exam), a student may exit the program and be awarded a standalone M.Tech degree. What is the role of the Comprehensive Examination in M.Tech (AI) + Ph.D.? It is a mandatory gateway at the end of Year 2. Clearing this exam formally elevates the student from the M.Tech phase to the Ph.D. candidacy, confirming their readiness for independent, advanced research. What is the expected financial assistantship? Students typically receive financial assistantship as per the university norms. How is the Ph.D. research topic selected? The student's M.Tech thesis topic, chosen in consultation with their supervisor in Year 2, is designed to evolve into the full-scale Ph.D. research problem approved by the Research Advisory Committee (RAC). Are the specialisations flexible? The first year covers core AI fundamentals. Specialisation is done through track-specific electives and the thesis/dissertation. While the specialisation is deep, the foundational coursework ensures broad AI knowledge. What kind of computational resources are provided for Ph.D. research? Students get access to high-performance computing (HPC) resources, necessary for training large and complex AI models specific to their Signal Processing, Vision, Robotics, or NLP research. The curriculum includes electives such as Structural Equation Modelling Security and Privacy for Big Data Analytics Data Visualization Big Data Analytics and Business Intelligence Satellite Data Analytics Electives General Elective: Students are offered one general elective. The purpose of the general elective is to give them the flexibility of choosing a course from another specialisation. Honours An optional Honours consists of a set of 4 courses that provide additional knowledge in the same or related area of study in CSE. These Honours courses contribute an additional 12 credits to the students. Projects To foster PBL, M.Tech programme consists of a project in each semester as given in the following table as: Semester Project 1 Specialisation Project 2 Industry Research Project 3 Capstone Project/Venture Launch at Bennett Hatchery 4 Projects in Research Lab/Industry Internships Internships/Thesis Dissertation (20 Credits): Two internships are part of the curriculum. The first internship is available in the 3rd semester, which can be an alternative to the capstone project. Capstone Design, and Capstone Project are cutting edge courses that prepare the students for the industry, and for further research. The second internship is done in the final semester, after which a thesis dissertation report must be submitted. There is also an option for the students to spend one full year in Industry internship in the final year of study. Students must learn to engineer requirement/system to carry out systems integration. Students must learn to explore the latest technology and its applications. Students must learn to deploy appropriate technology for given tasks. Students must learn to build convincing technical cases for approval / demonstration of initial ideas by conducting experiments and forming examples. The NVIDIA DGX-1 V100 is the first system built with groundbreaking NVIDIA Pascal™-powered Tesla® V100 accelerators. It is NVIDIA NVLink™ implementation that delivers a massive increase in GPU memory capacity. This is the world’s first purpose-built system for deep learning and accelerated analytics, delivering performance equal to 500 conventional servers. It comes fully integrated with hardware, deep learning software, development tools, and runs popular accelerated analytics applications. This means you can immediately shorten data processing time, visualize more data, accelerate deep learning frameworks, and design more sophisticated neural networks. NVIDIA DGX-1 software stack includes major deep learning frameworks, the NVIDIA DIGITS™ GPU training system, the NVIDIA Deep Learning SDK (e.g. CuDNN, NCCL), NVIDIA Docker, GPU drivers, and NVIDIA CUDA® for rapidly designing deep neural networks (DNN). It’s the ideal stack for accelerating popular analytics and visualization software. This powerful system includes access to cloud management services for container creation and deployment, system updates, and an application repository. This combined software can run on Pascal-powered Tesla GPUs allowing applications to run 12X faster than with previous GPU-accelerated solutions. Due to our partnership with NVIDIA, we have access to Certified Expert Instructors, Worldwide Workshops, Online Courses, Epicenter of industry leaders, Onsite Training, Global Reach, NVIDIA Partner Network, OEMs and startups, Deep Learning Teaching Kit. Big Data and Machine Learning Lab This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Big Data Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence to analyze large datasets, identify trends, and develop predictive models to solve their problems. Where students build and train deep neural networks, build a CNN and apply it to detection and recognition tasks, Train test sets, analyze variance for DL applications, use standard techniques and optimization algorithms, and build neural networks in TensorFlow. Students can Build and train RNNs, work with NLP and Word Embeddings, and use HuggingFace tokenizers and transformer models to perform NER. Quantum Computing Labs This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Quantum Computing Lab, for research uses and quantum theory to develop computer technology. Using quantum simulations this lab provides hands-on exercises that execute on real quantum systems provided by IBM Quantum Experience. NIT, Warangal Ms. Shraddha Jain Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIT BHU, Varanasi ELIGIBILITY M.Tech Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process Eligibility Domestic Bachelor’s degree in Engineering/ Technology/ Architecture or equivalent of MCA/ MIT/ MSc with minimum 50% marks . NRI The eligibility criterion for all programs for international applicants is minimum 50% in the qualifying examination and having studied the pre-requisite subjects for admission into the desired program. Selection Selection will be based on Aggregate Graduation Percentage. Note : Third subject should not be vocational- Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc Student Testimonials The faculty & environment are very student-friendly, and with their support, I was able to get my dream job with Microsoft. CSE Batch: 2018-2022 OUR ASK MTech Frequently Asked Questions What are the different specialisations I can opt for in M.Tech? At Bennett University, you can pursue M. Tech in four cutting-edge Specializations: Artificial Intelligence Data Science Cyber Security Blockchain What is the eligibility criteria for M.Tech? A Bachelor’s degree in Engineering/ Technology/ Architecture or equivalent of MCA/ MIT/ MSc with minimum 50% marks. Why should a student pursue an M.Tech from Bennett University? There are numerous advantages to becoming a part of Bennett University’s M. Tech Program. 70+ High-End Labs for practical, hands-on experience. Ph.D. Faculty from globally renowned institutions guiding and mentoring students. Certification Options: Earn a Certificate in Computer Science and Engineering after 6 months, or a Post Graduate Diploma after 1 year if you choose to discontinue the MTech program. Specialisation Choice: Opt for a specialization tailored to your career goals in MTech. Live Projects: Gain real-world exposure by working on live, industry-driven projects. What are the prospects for students pursing MTech from BU? After completing your M. Tech at BU, endless opportunities awaits: Corporate Roles : Unlock various high-level positions in the corporate world. Academic Careers : Pursue a rewarding career in academia. Research Roles : Become a Research Assistant/Associate in prestigious, funded projects. Doctoral Pursuit : Continue your journey with a Doctorate in your field of interest. Entrepreneurship : Launch your venture and shape the future! What are the career opportunities after MTech? There are several career choices available to students after completing MTech. Students can become Project Managers, Machinery Managers, Senior Engineers, Maintenance Engineers, Software Developer Research Associates, Design Engineers, Solution Integrators, Network Administrators, Database Administrators, Software Testers, Domain Consultants, Industries Officer (Technical), System Engineers, Team Leads/ Architect, or Technology Analysts. Students can also apply for doctorate after completing MTech or become a researcher. Download Brochure Enquire Now × SUBMIT YOUR ENQUIRY × Download Brochure WordPress Lightbox

MBA (Human Resource) | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login MBA Human Resource Apply Now MBA Human Resource Apply Now About MBA (Human Resource) The MBA in Human Resource (HR) specialisation is designed for students who aspire to lead people, culture, and organisational change in an era shaped by digital transformation and evolving workforce expectations. This specialisation integrates strategic HRM, organisational behaviour insights, analytics, and modern talent practices to create well-rounded HR leaders. Students gain practical skills in talent acquisition, employee development, HR metrics, performance systems, and change management capabilities that are in high demand across industries. A unique blend of behavioural sciences and data-driven decision-making forms the foundation of this specialisation, preparing graduates to design high-performance workplaces. Through immersive learning and industry-aligned coursework, the program equips students for impactful HR roles in global and dynamic business environments. Duration 2 Years Specialisations NA Credits - Start Date July 2026 Curriculum Highlights The curriculum of HR specialisation offers a powerful mix of foundational HR knowledge, organisational behaviour concepts, and advanced electives that address emerging industry needs. Students gain exposure to HR strategy, people analytics, talent management, behavioural sciences, and organisational change, building strong conceptual, analytical, and managerial expertise. Core Courses Human Resource Management Organisational Behavior Electives Strategic Human Resource Management Employee Relations HR Metrics & Analytics Organisational Change & Transformation Performance Management System Talent Acquisition Learning & Development Compensation & Reward Management Experiential Learning Industry talks by HR leaders and alumni working in prominent HR roles Industry-relevant projects offering practical exposure to real HR challenges Role plays on interviewing, conflict resolution, and negotiation skills Assessment Centre simulations, including OB exercises, case analyses, and group tasks Participation in HR conclaves, business competitions, and HR case challenges Why an MBA (Human Resource) from Bennett University High Industry Relevance: Curriculum aligned with modern HR practices, including HR analytics, digital HR, and organisational transformation. Experiential Learning: Live projects, business simulations, case studies, and assessment-centre-based evaluations. Practical HR Exposure: Hands-on experience with HR tools, behavioural assessments, and analytical dashboards. Expert Faculty: Insights from experienced academicians and corporate HR leaders in talent management, OD, and HR strategy. Future-Ready Careers: Robust career pathways with opportunities in HR consulting, analytics, L&D, employee relations, organisational development, to name a few. Program Objectives The program aims to develop strong managerial, behavioural, and analytical skills that are needed for modern HR roles. It provides hands-on experience with industry-relevant HR and analytics tools for evidence-based decision-making. The program strengthens strategic thinking, performance management, and organisational design skills, prepares students for leadership roles in HR, talent management, and organisational development, and enhances interpersonal abilities such as conflict management, negotiation, coaching, and employee engagement. Learning Outcomes After completion of MBA in Human Resource Management, students are ready to: Design and implement effective HR strategies across talent acquisition, performance, and reward systems Apply HR metrics and analytics to support data-driven workforce decisions Manage employee relations, engagement initiatives, and workplace culture effectively Lead and support organisational change and transformation initiatives Analyse human behaviour in organisational settings Apply psychological principles to enhance performance Career Pathways Graduates of the MBA (Human Resource) program are prepared for specialist and managerial roles in Human Resource and Organisational Behaviour, such as: HR Business Partner Talent Acquisition Specialist Learning and Development Manager Compensation and Benefits Analyst Performance Management Specialist People or HR Analyst Employee Relations Manager Organisational Development Consultant Change Management Specialist HR Generalist Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Passed Graduation in any discipline from a recognised university with a minimum 50% marks with English as the medium of instruction. NRI: For international applicants, a minimum of 50% in the qualifying examination is required, along with having studied the prerequisite subjects for admission to the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on the weighted score of CAT / XAT / NMAT / MAT / CMAT / GMAT / BU MAT/ CUET Percentile score & Graduation & Personal Interview. FMS, University of Delhi Dr. Ratan Kumar FPM - Management Development Institute, Gurgaon Mr. Krishan Ph.D. (Pursuing) - JNU, Delhi Dr. Vijay Prakash Mishra Ph.D. - Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, Pune Mr. Abhishek Saran Ph.D.(Pursuing) - IIM, Sambalpur Ms. Priya Mandleshwar Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIM, Indore Ms. Rimleena Boro Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIM, Indore OUR ASK MBA (Human Resource) Frequently Asked Questions What makes the MBA in Human Resource (HR) at Bennett University different from a traditional HR MBA? The MBA in Human Resource (HR) at Bennett University integrates strategic human resource management with organisational behaviour, HR analytics, and organisational transformation. This program prepares students for modern leadership roles such as HR Business Partner, Talent Strategist, and Change Leader, rather than limiting them to traditional administrative HR functions. How does this MBA program prepare students for evolving Human Resource (HR) roles such as HRBP and People Analytics? The MBA in Human Resource curriculum includes courses such as Strategic HRM, HR Metrics & Analytics, Performance Management Systems, and Organisational Change. These are supported by live projects and case-based learning, ensuring alignment with contemporary HR business roles and people analytics-driven decision-making. What career roles can graduates expect after completing an MBA in Human Resource? MBA graduates of this program typically pursue roles such as HR Business Partner, Talent Acquisition Specialist, Learning & Development Manager, Performance & Rewards Analyst, Employee Relations Manager, Organisation Development Consultant, and People Analytics Executive across diverse industries. Does this specialisation of MBA (Human Resource) focus on leadership and behavioural skills? Yes, the organisational behaviour component of the MBA Human Resource specialiation focuses strongly on leadership development, emotional intelligence, negotiation, conflict management, organisational culture, and change leadership, which are essential for effective people management and leadership roles. How is organisational change and transformation addressed in MBA Human Resource program? The MBA program includes dedicated coverage of Organisational Change & Transformation, enabling students to manage restructuring, cultural change, mergers, digital transformation initiatives, and employee engagement during periods of growth and uncertainty. Can students from non-HR backgrounds opt for the MBA (Human Resource) specialisation? Yes, the MBA in Human Resource is designed for students from diverse academic backgrounds. The program gradually builds strong HR foundations before progressing to advanced strategic HRM, organisational behaviour, and people analytics concepts. How does the curriculum of this MBA specialisation align with industry and future workforce trends? The MBA in Human Resource curriculum is industry-aligned and regularly updated to reflect future workforce trends such as the future of work, hybrid and remote workplaces, diversity & inclusion, employer branding, and the use of AI and digital tools in HR, ensuring graduates remain future-ready. What is the overall structure of the MBA (Human Resource) specialisation at Bennett University? The MBA (Human Resource) specialisation is offered as part of the two-year MBA program and features a balanced blend of core HR courses, organisational behaviour modules, analytics-driven electives, live industry projects, internships, and a capstone project, delivered across four semesters. How does this MBA program balance theory with practical HR application? The MBA in Human Resource and Organisational Behaviour program balances theory and practice by combining conceptual frameworks with real-world HR challenges through case studies, simulations, role plays, live industry projects, and internships, ensuring strong application-oriented learning. What kind of industry exposure is built into the Human Resource MBA program? MBA students of this program gain extensive industry exposure through guest lectures by senior HR leaders, live HR consulting projects, corporate internships, industry-led workshops, and professional interactions facilitated through the Times Group ecosystem. The Department provides core electives in Phytopharmaceuticals: Engineering & Production, Drug Design, and Discovery, Microbiome in Health & Disease, Personalized Medicine and Therapeutics, Biorefinery and Bioeconomy, among others (total 21 core electives). Biotechnology Curriculum 2018-22 Key Highlights Minors Students can pursue Minor subjects from the 2nd year, i.e.,3rd semester onwards. Specialisations Industry-focused 05 Specialisations: Biomedical Technologies Drug Discovery & Protein Therapeutics Industrial Biotechnology Nanotechnology & Biosensors Genetic Counselling & Precision Medicine Core Electives The Department provides core electives in Phytopharmaceuticals: Engineering & Production, Drug Design, and Discovery, Microbiome in Health & Disease, Personalized Medicine and Therapeutics, Biorefinery and Bioeconomy, among others Open Elective Students can choose open electives from 2nd year onwards, i.e., from 4th semester, and will carry 15 credits. These are the interdisciplinary courses that students can opt from different schools. Open Electives available from other departments: Linux Apache MySQL PHP (LAMP), Software Engineering, Numerical Methods, Introduction to Communication, Launch Your Startup, Personal Branding, Project Management, Constitutional Governance & RTI, Theories of Mass Communication, and Inside China: An Indian Perspective. Internships Project based learning through mini projects, summer internship, and Industrial R&D projects. Apart from on-campus research projects, students participate in summer internships from the very first year onwards but internships are mandatory in third year. The organizations where students went for internships are Centre of Biomedical Research - Lucknow, National Institute of Cholera and Enteric Diseases – Kolkata, HCL Infosystems, IIT Kanpur, Rapture Biotech-Lucknow, Shrii AMM Murugappa Chettiar Research Centre – Chennai, SeqIndia Labs, IIT Chennai, IIT Roorkee, IIT Kanpur, Department of Botany, University of Delhi, University of Nebraska, Omaha, USA, IIT BHU, IIT Delhi, IIT Roorkee, CSIR-IGIB, ICGEB, CDRI Lucknow, NCL Pune, Dabur Research Foundation, University of Hyderabad, Panacea Biotech, to name a few. B.Tech. Biotech Specialisations Biomedical Technologies Biomedical Technology has provided invaluable tools for diagnosis and creating life-saving equipment. This specialization will have three courses: Biomedical Technologies in Diagnostics and Therapy: 3D Bioprinting, Artificial organs, electronic implants, biomaterials, biocompatibility, stem cells and tissue engineering, radiation oncology; biomedical devices: prefilled syringes, auto injectors, on body devices and pumps, skin patches, etc. Bioimaging: Magnetic resonance imaging, micro and nano tomography of biological tissues, image analysis, molecular imaging, data analytics towards diagnosis Medical Innovations: Ex-vivo immunotherapy, organs-on-chip, microbiome-based therapies, Case studies Drug Discovery & Protein Therapeutics This specialization focuses on understanding various drug discovery tools and methods used for finding, identifying, and designing a new drug and understanding the regulatory procedures for drug development. The three courses include following subjects: Drug Discovery Process and Molecular Basis: Natural products as pharmaceutical lead compounds, modern medicinal chemistry techniques, case studies on the development of a class of compounds, indicative areas are in antivirals, oncology and central nervous system diseases. Introduction to screening hits, and optimization of hits to reduce potential drug side effects (increasing affinity and selectivity), efficacy or potency, metabolic stability (half-life), and oral bioavailability Rational Drug Design and Structure Determination: Protein structure determination including crystallography, NMR and Cryo-EM, Structure visualization, homology modelling and docking, virtual screening for lead discovery, structure-based and ligand-based design methods and the use of computational techniques in library preparation and data handling Biotherapeutics: Modern therapeutic approaches, overview of antibody based therapeutics, biologics for autoimmunity and inflammation, genetically engineered vaccines, present and future biologics, principles of immunodiagnostic assay, development and clinical implementation of live biotherapeutic products and regulatory roadmaps Genetic Counselling & Precision Medicine Students interested in acquiring advanced expertise in applied genetics and genomics should opt for this specialization. Brief B.Tech Biotechnology syllabus for three courses include: Human Genetics: Human genes and genomes, Mendelian and Non-mendelian mode of inheritance, Mitochondrial inheritance, Genetics of complex and single gene disorders, genetic variations in health and disease, Hardy-Weinberg Equilibrium, Haplotypes and linkage studies, Diagnosis and Molecular basis of Cancer, Cardiovascular, Cardiometabolic diseases, Neurodegenerative disorders, Mitochondrial and rare disorders. Signal transduction and human diseases, Animal models of human diseases Genetic Counselling: Review of human genetics, pedigree analysis, Causes and factors for seeking counselling, risks evaluation, basics of embryology, dysmorphology, Risks associated with invasive procedures, Foetal sampling procedure, Analysing ultrasounds, therapeutic benefits of dietary management and various counselling approaches; Preconceptional counselling; Counselling recurrent pregnancy loss; pre – marital counselling. Ethical and legal issues in genetic counselling. Genomics and Personalized Medicine: Human Genome Mapping, Gene therapy with case studies and Gene editing tools, Genome wide association studies, Omics technologies in precision medicine, Nutrigenomics, Metabolomics, Microbiome fingerprinting, etc. Molecular Basis of Personalized Medicine, Polymorphisms of Drug Transporters, Drug Targets, Drug metabolizing enzymes, Ethical, Legal, and Social Issues related to Personalized Medicine, Personalized management of miscellaneous disorders. Industrial Biotechnology This specialization aims to provide advanced courses on green products ranging from enzymes and biocatalysts, biofuels, biobased platform chemicals, among others. The subjects covered under this specialization are: Enzyme Technology: Enzyme production, purification, and preservation. Enzyme immobilization techniques, Enzyme reactors and reaction kinetics, Improvement of enzyme activity and stability, Directed evolution of enzymes, Enzymes in food industries, Enzymes in pharmaceutical industries, Enzymes in bio-pharmaceutical industries, Enzymes in biochemical industries, Enzymes in textile and detergent industries, Enzymes in R&D, Case studies and current status of enzyme research, Global market of enzymes and opportunities for biotech professionals, Quality control of enzymes. Green Chemicals & Sustainable Products: Concept of sustainability and green chemicals, Carbon credit & carbon tax, Lifecycle analysis (LCA) & cost benefit analysis, Smart drop-in chemicals, Fossil based chemicals vs. biobased chemicals, Biobased bulk chemicals, Bio-platform chemicals, Specialty chemicals, Biobased fragrance and aroma compounds, Biobased active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) and high value products, Antibiotics production, Biobased steroids & derivatives, Bioplastics and polymers, Biodegradable vs. non-biodegradable bioplastics, Natural vs. synthetic biopolymers, Final products manufactured using green chemicals, Case studies of industrial production of green chemicals, and current status of green chemicals research. Biofuels & Biorefinery: Biomass & bioenergy, Production and purification of bioethanol, Biodiesel production, Biobutanol, Biogas & Bio-CNG, Biohydrogen, Microbial fuel cell, Bio-aviation fuels, Biofuel production under biorefinery framework, Algal biorefinery for high value compounds, Biorefinery for high value secondary metabolites, Extraction and purification of high value phytochemicals under biorefinery framework, Biofuel production and simultaneous waste management, Metabolic engineering for biofuel production, Biofuel production process simulation, Scale-up of biofuel production processes, Case studies of biofuel production, and current status of biofuel research and job opportunities. Nanotechnology & Biosensors This specialization is best suited for students who want to deep dive into subjects of nanotechnology, the uses of nanomaterial in biosensors, and wearable technology. BTech Biotechnology syllabus of 3 subjects which will be covered during this specialization are: Nanomedicine: Principles and concepts, Development of nanoparticles/ liposomes/ lipid nanoparticles/ micelles, Surface modification, Characterization, Encapsulation, Release kinetics, Cargo- Drug/ gene/ protein/ antibody etc. Cellular and physiological barriers. Clinical Applications in different disease Introduction to Biosensors and Bioelectronics: Thermal and electronic biosensors, Optical biosensors, Mechanical biosensors: microfluidics – basic introduction to basic microfluidic concepts. Fabrication, characterization and detection of bioanalyte. Live sensors, Applications- clinical and non-clinical (defense/ Agriculture/ Pollution). Regulatory aspects. Commercialization examples. Digital healthcare, Theranostics and Lab-on-a-chip: Digital and mobile healthcare: wearable devices – commercial needs and contexts. Principles, Design, Preparation, Delivery mechanism, Imaging modality, Metallic nanoparticles, Photothermal Therapy/ Radiation Therapy/ MRI/ CT/ PET, Clinical Applications. Lab-on-a-chip sensing - device type, sample preparation, and readouts; Organ-on-a-chip – principles and applications. Regulatory aspects. Commercialization examples. Latha Ph.D. - IIT, Delhi Dr. Saurabh Jyoti Sarma Ph.D. - Universite du Québec, Canada Dr. Deepa Khare Ph.D. - POSTECH, South Korea Dr. Sarika Chaudhary Ph.D. - Banasthali Vidyapith Dr. Vibhuti Joshi Ph.D. - IIT, Jodhpur Dr. Vaishali Verma Ph.D. - University of Delhi Dr. Garima Juyal Ph.D. - University of Delhi South Campus Dr. Atul Vashist Ph.D. - AIIMS, New Delhi Dr. Lopa Pattanaik Ph.D. - IIT, Delhi Dr. Varun Vyas Ph.D. - University of Milan, Italy Dr. Pradeep Pant Ph.D.- IIT Delhi Dr. Krishna Kant Ph.D. - Flinders University, Adelaide, Australia ELIGIBILITY B.Tech Biotech Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process Eligibility Domestic Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with Physics and Biology/Biotechnology/Botany/Zoology & any other third* subject with minimum 55% marks OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Biology / Biotechnology / Botany / Zoology as compulsory subjects and any other third subject with highest score excluding Hindi) . * Third subject should not be vocational- Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc. PROCESS B.Tech Biotechnology Admission Process for 2025 The selection of a student at Bennett University will be finalized after series of evaluative steps: Submission of the application form with required details by paying non-refundable application fees of Rs. 1200. Merit-based assessment of candidates and release of provisional offer letters Counseling by the Bennett University’s admissions team Start My Application Program Infra The Department of biotechnology has 11 laboratories. All labs are equipped with modern instruments and lab benches to implement research-enabled project-based learning. The labs infrastructure is aligned to futuristic trends in industry and academia. This makes Bennett University one of the best B. Tech Biotechnology colleges in India. Molecular Biology: The students are trained in Molecular Biology techniques such as isolation, purification and handling of DNA, RNA, and proteins; RT-PCR, etc. here. Cell & Tissue Culture Technologies: This state-of-the-art cell culture facility covers mammalian cell culture techniques, stem cells, production of recombinant therapeutics in cell cultures. rDNA Technology: Students are trained in techniques of gene cloning, generating transgenic GMOs, CRISPR genome editing, etc. Bioprocess Engineering: The students learn about bioprocess and fermentation techniques in this lab. Biotech students have produced bioplastic and biofuel as part of their training utilizing facilities in this lab. Microbiology & Immunology: In this lab, students learn how to handle microorganisms such as bacteria, fungi, and bacteriophages. They also get to know different immunological assays such as Western blot and ELISA High-End Instrumentation laboratory may be renamed as Biotechnology Central Instrumentation Facility-I (CIF-1): High-end equipment such as HPLC, GC, and fluorescent microscopes are housed here. The B. Tech students are trained how to operate this equipment to make them industry ready. Chemistry & Bioanalytical Techniques: Students learn to analyze the major biomolecules quantitatively and qualitatively such as carbohydrates, lipids, proteins and nucleic acids. BSL2: The state of art BSL-2 Facility is envisioned to advance infectious disease research and address the challenges posed by infectious agents which are a potential threat to public health. Nanotechnology: At Centre for Excellence in Nanosensors and Nanomedicine (CoENN) students learn about integration of biomolecules with advanced sensing technology for various biosensing applications. Here emphasis is on applying Nanotechnology-based characterization and detection techniques in MedTech/Biotech Industry. Undergraduate laboratories: Two undergraduate labs are used to conduct experiments for the first-year courses. OUR ASK B.Tech. Biotech Frequently Asked Questions What is the faculty profile at the Department of Biotechnology at Bennett University? All faculty members are Ph.D., Post-doctorates or have been affiliated from foreign universities and Indian Universities/institutes of High Repute: named as Université du Québec/INRS (Canada), University of California (USA), University of Central Florida (USA), POSTECH (South Korea), NIH (USA), Cornell University (USA), University of Milar Italy, University of Lorraine (France), University of Galway (Ireland), University of Connecticut (USA), University of Duisburg Essen (Germany), IITs (Delhi, Jodhpur and Kharagpur), AIIMS (Delhi) and THSTI (Faridabad). The B. Tech students pursue mini projects with faculties and Ph.D. students, enabling them to get hands-on exposure gained from these Universities of high repute. What is the research profile of faculties? Do they have any patents? Faculty of Biotechnology Department are involved in diverse area of research based on their expertise such as infectious disease, drug development, Immuno-diagnostics/therapeutics, nanotechnology, nanoscience and bioengineering, environment biotechnology, bioprocess engineering, plant biotechnology and genome engineering (CRISpe-Cas9), plant-based protein therapeutics, metabolic engineering, cancer biology, neuro-oncology and neurological disorders, Computational Biophysics, discovery genomics, structure biology and drug discovery, antimicrobial resistance, vaccinology, antibody discovery and engineering. Yes, faculty 11 patents and published 74 research articles in peer reviewed journals in the last 6 years with extramural funding of 7 crores from DBT, DST and other funding agencies. How can I go abroad after BTech Biotech for Masters (MS) or Ph.D.? It is strongly encouraged in the Department that B. Tech students do extensive project-based learning during their tenure at Bennett University. It is often found that research project outcomes (such as patents or papers or conferences) are extremely helpful than even high CGPA in getting into good institutions abroad for MS/Ph.D. that too with good scholarships. Since biotech is a major part of the economy in many western countries (e.g., as high as 40% of venture capital funding goes to Biotech in the UK and the USA), the number of scholarships is much higher than the mass opted discipline of computer science. Additionally, students are guided through the application process of Biotech specific scholarships. Will I learn technical as well as personal skills with a B.Tech Biotech course? Yes, there are courses to strengthen soft/ life skills in the curriculum for developing students' competencies in verbal and written communication, collaboration, teamwork, etc. Along with mandatory courses that teach soft skills, one can take minors from the humanities department or even the School of Management, where we have created a mechanism of choosing either open electives or minor specialization. Additionally, Bennett Finishing School assists students in developing soft skills that recruiters specifically look for in potential employees. The technical skills are honed to international standards through a range of core biotech courses and projects, enriched through research paper reading, frequent class/ project seminars and presentations. What is the infrastructure facility at Bennett university? The high-end instruments include RT-PCR machine, fluorescence microscope, EVOS, mammalian and plant tissue culture, HPLC, GC, plate reader, FT-IR, bio-reactor, FPLC, AKTA protein purification system . Several minor instruments, like gel docs, thermal cyclers, refrigerated centrifuges, 4 degrees freezer, -20 degrees and -80 freezers, spectrophotometers, autoclaves, shaker incubators are also available in the labs. The students also have access to instruments in other engineering departments. Can a BTech Biotech student participate in research at Bennett University? B.Tech Biotech program strongly emphasizes research for undergraduate students. At Bennett University, faculty has created a research ecosystem where faculties and Ph.D. students are actively participating in research. The students are given examples of research problems. Furthermore, every course has a hands-on research project component to it. Also, students must compulsorily take project credits from 6th semester onwards under the supervision of a faculty. This means they are mandatorily getting at least 18 months of extensive research exposure. Are there any computer-based learning programs in biotechnology? Yes, the curriculum has a core course on Computational Thinking & Programming in B.Tech Biotech 1st year, another course on Bioinformatics, and the option of minor in Artificial Intelligence with four courses from Computer Science & Engineering. Will students be supported for entrepreneurship in biotechnology? Bennett University has its incubation centre as Bennett Hatchery, which provides full support for entrepreneurship and teaches courses on it in the curriculum. The faculty also supports and mentors to take advantage of BIRAC (Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council) set up by the Ministry of Science & Technology, Govt of India to provide minimal funds to the tune of Rs. 50 lakhs for any student or faculty willing to set up a Biotech start-up.
