BA in Film,TV & Web Series,Check Eligibility,Fee,FAQ - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login BA Film, TV and Web Series Elevate your storytelling journey in a digital world Apply Now ABOUT About BA Film, TV & Web Series The BA Film, TV and Web Series program offered by the Times School of Media is a 3+1-year degree program. Students are awarded the BA Film, TV, and Web Series Degree on successful completion of all compulsory courses that total 120 credits. They also have the option to earn an Honours degree by completing a group of Honours courses that total 30 credits. The curriculum has been designed keeping in mind the requirements of the Choice-Based Credit System. It has been curated by the top Editors of the Times Group and its focus is to provide skill-based education. The students can choose their specialisation track by picking courses of their interest from the electives basket. The curriculum can be grouped under five heads or five well-defined layers that shape the learning, knowledge, and personality of a student as he/she proceeds from semester to semester. These five layers are: Understanding of Concepts including fiction and non-fiction storytelling Building of Technical Skills Knowledge of Liberal Arts Development of General Awareness and Critical Thinking Practical Learning through Projects Duration 3 + 1 Yrs. Specialisation 2 Credits 120 for BA Film, TV and Web Series Credits 160 for BA Film, TV & Web Series (Hons) Start Date August 2026 Multi-Focus BA Film, TV and Web Series INDUSTRY FOCUSSED 2 SPECIALISATIONS Film & TV (Production) TV & Web Series (Post Production) STRONG INDUSTRY CONNECT Times Group companies have brought in their domain expertise in developing the Mass Communication curriculum namely: Times Now, Radio Mirchi, Times Internet, Media Wire, and Junglee Pictures The Board of Studies of TSOM includes Editors and Senior Managers of the Times Group. They are drawn from three streams: Editorial, Marketing and Films STATE-OF-THE-ART-INFRASTRUCTURE Film/ TV Studio and Radio Studio built under the guidance of Times Now and Radio Mirchi Engineers respectively iMac & Windows Labs. Curriculum Development: The BA Film, Television & Webseries curriculum has been developed under the supervision of Junglee Pictures, the film production and distribution company of the Times Group. The other companies engaged in the development of the curriculum related to film publicity, social media marketing and PR and its subsequent avenues are: Junglee Pictures: Film & OTT curriculum Zoom TV: Television programming Mirchi: Audio streaming curriculum Times Now: Studio production Mediawire: Marketing curriculum Times Internet Limited: Social Media curriculum 2. Teaching Support: Many BAFTW courses are taught by the senior managers of the Times Group. These courses are unique because they have been developed entirely by Times Group and reflect the latest industry practices. These courses are: Business of Radio and Television: Radio Mirchi and Times Now teams Business of Films: Junglee Pictures Team Besides this, different arms of Times Group teach specialised modules. These include: Digital Marketing and Native Advertising: Times Internet Limited Digital PR: Medianet Streaming Music: Mirchi Website Planning: Times Internet Limited Cyber and Copyright Laws: Legal Team of Times Group 3. Field Visits and Guest Lectures: The students are taken to the film studios, film sets, television shoots, film festivals as part of their learning. Visit to studios of Mirchi, Times Now as part of their training. They are often addressed by top professionals at Junglee Pictures, award winning filmmakers, and mainstream film directors. Celebrities related to media and entertainment, fashion and film production based across the globe interact with students and faculty. Students are expected to visit professional studio spaces like film cities across India. Curriculum & Syllabus 2021-24 Read More Curriculum BA Film TV and Web Series Curriculum 2025-29 BA Film TV and Web Series Curriculum 2024-28 BA Film TV and Web Series Curriculum 2023-26 BA Film TV and Web Series Curriculum 2022-25 BA Film TV and Web Series Curriculum 2021-24 Curriculum Delivery Specialized modules are developed & delivered by the senior professionals of the Times Group: Times Internet: Digital Marketing, Native Advertising & Website Planning Times Now: Interviews & PTCs Mirchi: Introduction to Radio Junglee Films: Business of OTT, Introduction to Film Production CII: Advanced Animation, Interactive VFX, Modelling Techniques Specialisation The specialisation courses give the students an opportunity to hone their skills in a particular film discipline of their choice under the guidance of faculty mentors. Film & TV (Production) TV & Web Series (Post Production) Internship Students across all programmes are offered mandatory internships by the school. This is valuable learning for the students because they get hands-on experience and learn from the best in the media industry. Experiential Learning Influencer Marketing: Learn the latest trends in the dynamic world of marketing Industry Interactions: CEO masterclasses, workshops, and regular guest lectures Metaverse: Explore infinite possibilities of virtual and augmented reality AI in Media: Learn to use AI tools in audio and video production Live Events: Conceptualise, conduct, and manage events with live audience Mojo Videos: Creating multimedia stories for mobile-first audience TV Production: Producing bulletins and shows based on campus events from the TV studios Cinematic Production: Learn techniques of acting, lighting, direction from industry professionals TimesofBennett.Com: Curate and publish web content for real-time learning Student Work As part of the curriculum, students will create short films, produce television programs, create podcasts, video stories, infographics, photo-and video essays, documentaries, and more. The student work includes: Visual Diary: Students use their observation to create content that uses visuals in the form of illustrations / photographs etc and that documents daily activity. No Dialogue Film : Students make two films without using dialogue. Scriptwriting: Short and long format scripts will be compiled as a written dossier to build the writing skills associated with film and fiction based formats Music Video: Create a two to three minute music video. Multimedia Content: In the 3rd semester, the students work on multimedia storytelling and social media in short narrative form. Film Festival: In Semester IV the students will work with the Faculty to organise a Film Festival. Mobile filmmaking : One to three minute films shot and edited on mobile phones for being published on the student website from 3rd semester onwards. Web Series: Produce three episodes of 15 minutes each. Documentaries: 20-30 minute Documentaries are made in the 4th semester. Final degree film : Students will produce 60 mn fiction film as a group exercise which will be part of the final portfolio. Studios, Labs and Software Television Studio Built under the guidance of Times Now, the Television Studio is equipped with a three-camera setup and a state-of-the-art production control room (PCR). The PCR is equipped with a Vision Mixer to handle a three-camera feed, an Apple Mac for editing, and four LED screens for output. The Studio also has a teleprompter to help students anchor news bulletins and shows. Radio Studio The Radio Studio, built with the help of Radio Mirchi, is an acoustically designed and fully equipped studio that provides students ample space and atmosphere for Radio and audio production. The studio space consists of a recording room and a control room. The control room is equipped with a Multi-Channel Mixing Console, Speakers, Talkback, and Cubase recording software for recording, mixing, and monitoring sound. The Radio production facilities enable students to learn the art of recording and mixing sounds and voice-over techniques. MoJo Lab Mobile-first lab used by students to create multimedia content for smartphone users. iMac and Windows Labs The school has two iMac labs and one Windows PCs labs. Each lab has 30 computers with the required software installed. Merit-based assessment of candidates and release of provisional offer letters Counseling by the Bennett University’s admissions team Start My Application OUR ASK BA Film, TV and Web Series Frequently Asked Questions Q. What is the duration of the program BA Film, Television and Web Series? It is a three-year program spread across six semesters, with credit score of 120 for Pass Course and Credits score of 150 for Honours degree. Q. Why BA in Films, Television & Web Series Bennett University? BA Film, TV and Web Series course offered by Times School of Media is one of its kind in India. The program focuses on providing skill-based education to students who wish to pursue careers in Films, TV, and Web Series. BA film and television production caters to the huge demand created by the advent of the OTT platform in the last few years. Industry Relevant Curriculum: Developed by experts from Junglee Pictures, MX Player, Zoom TV Experienced Faculty: Faculty drawn from Industry familiar with latest technology Focus on Practical Exposure: 60% time dedicated to acquiring practical skills Learning by doing: Mandatory Internship, Making of Documentary and Feature Films and Creation of web series episodes Interaction with Industry Experts: Workshops by eminent Film Makers, Actors, and Film Production professionals Q. What are the key specialisation course? Film & TV (Production) TV & Web Series (Post Production) Q. Name few of the Industrial Collaborations? Learning from deep industry engagement at Bennett University hones the necessary skills required to shine in a competitive work environment Junglee Pictures : The curriculum is curated under the guidance of Junglee Pictures. Students will interact with Directors, Editors, and Producers of award-winning films as Raazi, Badhai Ho, and Bareilly ki Barfi. Mx Player: Partners in development and updating of curriculum w.r.t industry demand from short digital formats to web series. Femina: Femina Events will support the students to network & interact with top industry experts. Q. What is the eligibility criteria for admission? Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with minimum 60% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 Q. Are there any scholarships available for students? There are two kinds of scholarships that Bennett University offers to support and encourage meritorious students – Admissions Scholarships and Academic Scholarships. For detailed information regarding scholarships criteria, please visit https://www.bennett.edu.in/admission/scholarships/ Q. Does the School organize Internships for students of BA Film, Television and Web Series? Yes. The Times School of Media organizes a six-week internship to gain first-hand knowledge. The internship will be in various film production houses, media houses, and distributing agencies. This makes it one of the most sought-after programs in Film Making and Web Series, making BU the best college for bachelor's in film and television production, in Delhi/NCR. Q. What are the placement prospects after completing BA Films, Television, and Web series course? The BA film course will open placement possibilities in entertainment companies involved in the production of films and television serials. Some pf the positions at the starting level are Script Writer, Video Editor, Cinematographer, Audio Editor, Assistant Producer, Casting Assistant, Line Producer, Production Assistant, Locations Assistant, Floor Runner, Assistant Director, Prop Master, Art Department Trainee, Video Assist Trainee, Sound Mixer, Lighting Trainee, Post-production Trainee, Archivist, Editorial Trainee, Marketing Assistant etc. Download Brochure Enquire Now × SUBMIT YOUR ENQUIRY × Download Brochure WordPress Lightbox

BBA Global – 3 Country Program - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login BBA Global – 3 Country Program Study in India, Malaysia and the UK About BBA Global – 3 Country Program (1+1+1) BBA Global – 3 Country Program (1+1+1) The BBA Global – 3 Country Program at Bennett University is a unique global business education program in partnership with the prestigious University of Reading, UK. The program offers ambitious students an excellent opportunity to study across three culturally and economically diverse countries—India, Malaysia, and the United Kingdom—while gaining global exposure and a world-class degree. Start your global academic journey in India, where you build strong business fundamentals and prepare yourself for a global experience in the first year. Continue your second year at the University of Reading’s Malaysia campus, experiencing Southeast Asia’s dynamic business and cultural environment. And conclude your final phase at the University of Reading UK, one of the top institutions known for excellence in business, finance, economics, and sustainability in the United Kingdom. Students can significantly reduce their overall education expenses by choosing the BBA – 3-Country Program over a traditional full-term program abroad. Whether your goal is to build a career in global business, pursue entrepreneurship, or continue with advanced international studies, the BBA Global – 3 Country Program opens doors to limitless global opportunities. Program Details Name Duration University Degree BBA Global - 3 Country Program (1+1+1) 3 Years Year 1 @ Bennett University Year 2 @University of Reading, Malaysia Campus Year 3 @University of Reading, UK B.Sc. in Business and Management Admission Counsellors Connect with our counsellors for any admissions related queries. explore Fee Structure Know more about fee structure. explore Scholarship Know more about Scholarship. explore Program Objectives To offer an academically rigorous and internationally immersive educational experience across three countries Deliver high-quality education through collaboration with globally ranked universities Develop globally competent, industry-ready graduates Foster cross-cultural understanding and global perspectives through international mobility Enhance adaptability and problem-solving skills by exposing students to diverse academic systems and learning environments Equip students with global employability skills through industry-relevant curricula. Create confident, ethical leaders capable of contributing meaningfully to a dynamic and interconnected business world Learning Outcomes Master core business disciplines in a global context Build leadership, analytical, and problem-solving capabilities across cultures Gain readiness for international careers or higher education at top universities Understand global socio-economic systems, business ethics, and sustainability principles Students benefit from a multi-country curriculum designed to meet global education standards and benchmarked against world-class teaching methodologies Final degree from the University of Reading – one of the UK’s top institutions known for excellence in business, finance, economics, and sustainability ABOUT Why Choose BBA Global – 3 Country Pathway Program Globally Recognized Degree: Earn a globally recognized BBA degree from a top-ranked UK university with a strong industry linkage. Three World Class Campuses: Study across three international campuses – India, Malaysia and the UK. Cross-cultural Competencies: Develop cross-cultural competencies through international mobility, industry projects, and global peer internship and exposure to emerging global markets. World Class Education: Graduate from a Top 200-QS ranked global university with a strong business and management faculty. Employability and Industry Exposure: Gain a competitive edge through industry-integrated learning, and exposure to international job markets. Affordable and Simplified: Enjoy a simplified admission process, access to exclusive scholarships, and highly cost-effective education compared to full-term program in a reputed foreign university. Partner University University of Reading The University of Reading is one of the UK’s most respected institutions, renowned for its business, economics, finance, and sustainability programs. With campuses in both the UK and Malaysia, it offers a rich, globally integrated education model. The Henley Business School (Top 50th Business School in the world), part of the university, is one of the oldest and most prestigious business schools in Europe, ranked for its impactful research and career-ready graduates. QS World Rankings 2026 - 194 Key Benefits International Education at Reduced Cost : By studying in India for 1 year can significantly lower the cost of a full-term program abroad. International Exposure : Students gain international exposure and can take advantage of global business environments in ASEAN and Europe. Higher Education Ready : After completing the program students may be eligible for MBA/Masters in top global institutions. Global Career Opportunities : The program opens doors to international career paths, offering placement support and networking opportunities across three countries. Career Opportunities & Future Prospects After completing the BBA Global – 3 Country Program, students can seek and explore career opportunities as Business Analyst, International Marketing Executive, Financial Analyst, Product Manager, Management Consultant, Operations Coordinator, Brand Strategist, Digital Marketing Executive, Human Resource Specialist – in MNCs, startups, consulting firms, banks, and development organizations globally. Who Should Apply? Aspiring business leaders seeking global exposure in their undergraduate journey. Students aiming for international careers or postgraduate studies abroad. Learners looking for a cost-effective, high-quality, international business education. NRI Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR). Selection Criteria Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 Subjects Score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score). Note: Student must comply with the English language proficiency requirements (IELTS/ TOEFL as per partner university guidelines). OUR ASK Frequently Asked Questions What is the structure of the BBA Global – 3 Country program? Students study Year 1 at Bennett University (India), Year 2 at University of Reading, (Malaysia Campus), and Year 3/ final year at University of Reading, UK. What degree will I receive? On successful completion, students will be awarded a B.Sc. in Business and Management global degree from the University of Reading, UK. Is this program open to students of all streams? The program is primarily for students interested in Management, Business or related fields. Are scholarships available? Yes, merit-based scholarships may be offered by Bennett University and other partners. What career paths does this program offer? After completing the BBA Global – 3-Country Program, students will be eligible for roles in global business management, consulting, finance, marketing, and international trade, among others. Can I stay in the UK after graduation? Yes, under the UK’s Post Study Work Visa (Graduate Route), students can stay and work for up to two years after graduation. MBA (Healthcare) | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login MBA Healthcare Apply Now MBA Healthcare Apply Now About MBA Healthcare The MBA specialisation in Healthcare Management prepares students to lead hospitals, clinics, pharmaceutical companies, and healthcare organisations effectively. It integrates management principles, healthcare policy, digital technologies, and analytics to solve challenges in healthcare delivery, operations, and patient outcomes. Students gain expertise in finance, strategy, policy, and AI-driven healthcare solutions. Duration 2 Years Specialisations NA Credits 84 Start Date Aug/Sep. 2026 (Tentative) Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Passed Graduation in any discipline from a recognised university with a minimum 50% marks with English as the medium of instruction NRI: For international applicants, a minimum of 50% in the qualifying examination is required, along with having studied the prerequisite subjects for admission to the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on the weighted score of CAT / XAT / NMAT / MAT / CMAT / GMAT / BU MAT/ CUET Percentile score & Graduation & Personal Interview. MBA (Healthcare) Program Fee Get detailed information about the MBA (Healthcare) program fee structure, including tuition, hostel fees, and other applicable charges. Click here for details. Why MBA (Healthcare) from Bennett University High Industry Demand: Growing need for skilled healthcare managers across hospitals, pharma, and health-tech sectors. Technology-Driven Learning: Learn to leverage data and technology for better patient care and operational efficiency. Healthcare Systems Insight: Gain insights into healthcare systems, policy, and finance Industry Mentorship: Mentorship from industry experts and healthcare leaders. Diverse Career Opportunities: Career opportunities in hospital management, healthcare consulting, policymaking, and health-tech. Program Objectives The program aims to enable students to understand healthcare systems, policies, and regulations. It focuses on building strong strategic, financial, and operational management skills for healthcare organisations. Students learn to use analytics and AI tools to support better healthcare decisions. The program also helps improve patient care, operational efficiency, and healthcare delivery, while developing ethical leadership, practical problem-solving, and sound decision-making skills. Learning Outcomes After pursuing an MBA in Healthcare Management, one can effectively manage healthcare organisations, apply data analytics and AI for strategic decision-making, design policies and strategies for healthcare systems, and lead healthcare teams and projects successfully across diverse healthcare settings. Curriculum Highlights The Healthcare Management specialisation offers a comprehensive blend of management fundamentals and healthcare-specific knowledge. The curriculum covers healthcare systems, policy, finance, analytics, and AI-driven applications, enabling students to develop strategic and operational expertise. Through practical exposure and industry-relevant electives, students are prepared to address real-world healthcare challenges and drive innovation in healthcare delivery. Electives Healthcare Systems and Policy Health Information and Data Analytics Strategic Healthcare Management AI in Healthcare Management Healthcare Finance Managing Healthcare Enterprises Experiential Learning Hospital and clinic field visits Healthcare analytics projects Internships with healthcare providers and pharmaceutical companies Case studies on healthcare policy, management, and strategy Career Pathways Graduates are prepared for specialist and managerial roles in the healthcare sector, such as Hospital Administrator, Healthcare Consultant, Health Analyst, and Policy Advisor, with opportunities across hospitals, pharmaceutical companies, health-tech firms, insurance companies, and government healthcare agencies. FMS, University of Delhi Dr. Ratan Kumar FPM - Management Development Institute, Gurgaon Mr. Krishan Ph.D. (Pursuing) - JNU, Delhi Dr. Vijay Prakash Mishra Ph.D. - Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, Pune Mr. Abhishek Saran Ph.D.(Pursuing) - IIM, Sambalpur Ms. Priya Mandleshwar Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIM, Indore Ms. Rimleena Boro Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIM, Indore OUR ASK MBA (Healthcare) Frequently Asked Questions Is prior experience in healthcare mandatory to pursue an MBA in Healthcare? No, prior healthcare experience is not mandatorily required. However, a basic interest in healthcare systems, patient care, and health services management is beneficial. How does the MBA (Healthcare) program prepare students for evolving roles in the healthcare sector? The curriculum of the Healthcare Management program at Bennett University encompasses subjects such as Hospital Operations Management, Healthcare Strategy, Health Economics, Healthcare Analytics, Quality & Patient Safety, and Healthcare Laws & Ethics, complemented by case-based learning and live projects to address the dynamic and regulated nature of the healthcare industry. What kind of practical exposure does the MBA (Healthcare) program offer? Students gain practical exposure through internships, live projects, hospital visits, case studies, simulations, industry interactions, and guest lectures by healthcare professionals. What career roles can graduates expect after completing an MBA in Healthcare Management? Graduates typically pursue roles such as Hospital Administrator, Healthcare Operations Manager, Healthcare Consultant, Quality & Compliance Manager, Healthcare Project Manager, Practice Manager, and roles in healthcare analytics, insurance, and pharma management. Does the Healthcare Management specialisation focus on leadership and patient-centric skills? Yes, the program emphasises leadership in healthcare settings, patient-centric service design, ethical decision-making, stakeholder management, and communication skills essential for managing clinical and non-clinical teams effectively. Can students from non-medical or non-healthcare backgrounds opt for the MBA (Healthcare) specialisation? Yes, the program is designed for students from diverse academic backgrounds. It begins with foundational healthcare concepts and gradually progresses to advanced management, operational, and strategic healthcare topics. Are internships compulsory in the MBA (Healthcare) program? Yes, internships are a mandatory component of the program and provide hands-on experience in hospitals, healthcare organisations, pharmaceutical companies, and consulting firms. What is the overall structure of the MBA (Healthcare) at Bennett University? The specialisation is offered within the two-year MBA program and includes core management courses, healthcare-specific modules, analytics-driven electives, live projects, and internships spread across four semesters. What kind of placement support is provided to MBA (Healthcare) students? Universities offer dedicated placement support, industry partnerships, career mentoring, and networking opportunities with hospitals, healthcare organisations, consulting firms, insurance providers, and pharmaceutical companies. School of Artificial Intelligence - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login School of Artificial Intelligence ABOUT About School of Artificial Intelligence The School of Artificial Intelligence at Bennett University is committed to expanding knowledge boundaries and revolutionizing the technological landscape. The faculty members are recruited from academia and industry, ensuring that students are equipped to tackle real-world challenges. The programs with a holistic curriculum balance theoretical research with practical applications, enabling students to contribute to and use the latest AI developments. They can also use technology to tackle pressing issues in fields such as Data Science, Media, Fintech, Legal-tech, Healthcare, and Signal Processing. As we enter the 5th Industrial Generation, AI is no longer optional-it is the foundation of tomorrow's careers, industries, and innovations. Read More Dr. Manoj Sharma Assistant Dean Research, CSR Outreach and PG Programs Dr. Pratyush Pranav Assistant Dean UG and Academic Affairs × Dr. Rajeev Tiwari Dean, School of Artificial Intelligence Architecting the Future with Artificial Intelligence – Let’s Move Ahead with TIMES Welcome to the School of Artificial Intelligence at Bennett University, where we stand at the forefront of a revolution powered by AI. As we enter the 5th Industrial Generation, AI is no longer optional-it is the foundation of tomorrow's careers, industries, and innovations. To our aspirants, parents, and valued stakeholders—the real question today is not if AI is part of your future, but how. Will you be a passive user or an active architect of this transformative technology? Why Choose a Dedicated AI Degree? The traditional Computer Science degree is evolving. AI and Machine Learning now drive global innovation and demand deep, focused expertise. An AI degree equips you with a future-proof skill set and a distinct identity in the job market, with applications in every sector. Global and Industry Alignment Top institutions globally like IITs, MIT, and Stanford offer full-fledged AI programs recognizing the need for specialization. Roles like AI Engineer, GenAI Prompt Engineer, and AI Ethicist are growing 35–60% CAGR. Our B.Tech in AI places you at the forefront of this surge. Why Bennett’s School of AI? Cutting-Edge, NEP-Aligned Curriculum : Co-developed with global tech leaders like NVIDIA, Microsoft experts, AWS experts etc. covering 70–80% AI/ML/DeepTech with strong applied, and experiential learning focus. Industry into the Classroom : Engage with real-world projects, industry mentors, and practitioners through internships, labs, and capstones. This is our main objectivity to offer the right real time environment of learning to students. Entrepreneurship & Innovation : We foster an entrepreneurial mindset with incubation support and startup mentoring with well dedicated Hatchery at Bennett University. World-Class Infrastructure : Home to one of India’s one of the best NVIDIA AI Labs, Deep Learning Labs, with advanced computing for breakthrough projects and research. Expert Faculty : Learn from researchers and practitioners from leading institutions and industries. Mostly IITs, NITs or top tier faculty to guide in the learning environment. Holistic Development : We blend technical excellence with leadership, ethics, and interdisciplinary learning across law, media, and business. A Personal Note To aspirants: This is your launchpad. To learners: Explore, innovate, and lead. To parents: Invest in a career that’s impactful, relevant, and future-ready. Mandal Professor, IIT Delhi View More Dr. Gaurav Harit Professor, IIT Jodhpur View More Prof. Anurag Agrawal Dean, Biosciences & Health Research, Ashoka University View More × Prof. (Dr.) Bernard Jones Emeritus professor, University of Groningen Bernard Jones is Emeritus Professor at the Kapteyn Astronomical Institute of the University of Groningen in the Netherlands His research has covered many areas of astrophysics, both theoretical and observational, with a strong emphasis on cosmology, where he is widely published and cited. He is the author of the recent highly celebrated book Precision Cosmology: the first half million years (CUP 2017) and "The Reinvention of Science: Slaying the Dragons of Dogma and Ignorance", co-authored with Vicent Martinez and Virginia Trimble (World Scientific 2013). Bernard did his PhD at the Institute of Astronomy in Cambridge (UK) supervised by Dennis Sciama and Martin Rees. He then went on to work as a postdoctoral fellow at Princeton and Berkeley under James Peebles. There followed periods in Queensland, Paris, and Copenhagen before finally settling at the Kapteyn Institute in Groningen, the Netherlands. Bernard has supervised and mentored the research work of over 50 postgraduates from all over the world and is highly regarded as a teacher, lecturer, and presenter of both popular and academic talks. × Prof. (Dr.) Rien van de Weygaert University of Groningen Rien van de Weygaert studied astronomy and physics at the University of Leiden, where he obtained his PhD cum laude in 1991 on ‘Voids and the Geometry of Large Scale Structure' (supervised by prof. Bernard Jones, prof. Vincent Icke and prof. P.T. de Zeeuw). Subsequently, he worked as an NSERC fellow at the Canadian Institute of Astrophysics (CITA) in Toronto, Canada and as a research fellow at the Max Planck Institut for Astrophysik in Garching, Germany before taking up a KNAW fellowship (Royal Academy of Arts and Sciences) at the Kapteyn Astronomical Institute at the University of Groningen. Since 2004 he is professor of cosmological structure formation at the Kapteyn Astronomical Instiute. His research interests concern cosmology, the large-scale Universe and the formation of structure in the Universe, as well as computational geometry and topology and pattern recognition. Within this research interest he has had a particular interest in the formation and dynamics of the Cosmic Web, the complex network of interconnected filamentary galaxy associations that pervades our universe on scales of tens to hundreds of million lightyears, and the existence and evolution of voids, the large near empty regions in between these structures. For the analysis of this structure, he has been working on a diverse array of tools based on Voronoi and Delaunay tessellations and related geometric and topological concepts, finding that they provide a highly versatile means of tracing the complex structures seen in the Universe. Aside from these cosmological concepts, he has also been interested in the history of astronomy, via a research project on world's oldest astronomical and mechanical computer, the Antikythera mechanism from ancient Hellenistic times. × Prof. (Dr.) Sankar Kumar Pal Chair, Academic Advisory Board National Science Chair, Govt. of India President, Indian Statistical Institute National Science Chair, Government of India; President, Indian Statistical Institute Biography: Sankar K Pal is a National Science Chair, SERB, Govt. of India, and an Emeritus Professor and the President of Indian Statistical Institute (ISI). He is a Distinguished Scientist and former Director of the Indian Statistical Institute, a former Distinguished Professor of the Indian National Science Academy, and a former Chair Professor of the Indian National Academy of Engineering. Currently, he is also an AICTE Distinguished Chair Professor, and a Scholar-in-Residence of IIT Jodhpur, India. He founded the Machine Intelligence Unit and the Center for Soft Computing Research: A National Facility in the Institute in Calcutta. He received a Ph.D. in Radio Physics and Electronics from the University of Calcutta in 1979, and another Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering along with DIC from Imperial College, University of London in 1982. He joined ISI in 1975 as a CSIR Senior Research Fellow where he became a Full Professor in 1987, Distinguished Scientist in 1998, the Director for the term 2005-2010, and the President for the term 2022-2024. He was the first Computer Scientist outside Statistics and Mathematics to become the Director of the Indian Statistical Institute since its inception in 1931. He is also the first Ex-employee of ISI to get elected to hold the honorable Chair of President of ISI after its inception in 1931. He worked at the University of California, Berkeley, and the University of Maryland, College Park in 1986-87; the NASA Johnson Space Center, Houston, Texas in 1990-92 & 1994; and at US Naval Research Laboratory, Washington DC in 2004. Since 1997 he has served as a Distinguished Visitor of the IEEE Computer Society (USA) for the Asia-Pacific Region and held several visiting positions in Italy, Poland, Hong Kong, and Australian universities. Prof. Pal is a Fellow of the IEEE, the Academy of Sciences for the Developing World (TWAS), the International Association for Pattern Recognition, the International Association of Fuzzy Systems, the Asia-Pacific Artificial Intelligence Association, and all the four National Academies for Science/Engineering in India. He is a Foreign Member of the European Academy of Sciences and Arts. He has co-authored 21 books, about 500 research papers, and 2 US patents in the areas of Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning, Image/Video Processing, Data Mining, Web Intelligence, Soft Computing, Bioinformatics, and Cognitive machines. Prof. Pal has made fundamental contributions to machine intelligence research by developing various modern approaches. He pioneered the development of Fuzzy Set Theory, and Neuro-Fuzzy and Rough-Fuzzy Computing, in general, and their applications in Pattern Recognition, Image Processing, Machine Learning, Knowledge-based systems, and Data Mining, in particular. He is the one who introduced the Soft Computing concept and research in India. He visited forty-five countries as a Keynote/ Invited speaker or an academic visitor. He has received the 1990 S.S. Bhatnagar Prize, 2013 Padma by the President of India and many prestigious awards in India and abroad including the G.D. Birla Award in 1999, Om Bhasin Award in 1998, Jawaharlal Nehru Fellowship in 1993, Khwarizmi International Award in 2000 from the President of Iran, FICCI Award in 2000-2001, Vikram Sarabhai Research Award in 1993, NASA Tech Brief Award (USA) in 1993, IEEE Trans. Neural Networks Outstanding Paper Award in 1994, IETE-R.L. Wadhwa Gold Medal in 1997, INSA-S.H. Zaheer Medal in 2001, Indian Science Congress-P.C. Mahalanobis Birth Centenary Gold Medal from the Prime Minister of India for Lifetime Achievement in 2005-06, J.C. Bose Fellowship of the Govt. of India in 2007, INAE Chair Professorship in 2013, DAE Raja Ramanna Fellowship in 2015, INAE-S.N. Mitra Award in 2015, INSA-Jawaharlal Nehru Birth Centenary Lecture Award in 2017, INSA Distinguished Professorial Chair in 2018, National Science Chair, Govt. of India in 2020, and AICTE Distinguished Chair Professor in 2021. Prof. Pal has served/serving in editorial board of ~30 internationally well-known scientific journals in computer science and engineering including several IEEE Transactions. His Google Scholar h-index is 83 with 35,000+ total citations. × Prof. (Dr.) Vincenzo Piuri University of Milan, Italy National Science Chair, Government of India; President, Indian Statistical Institute Vincenzo Piuri has received his Ph.D. in Computer Engineering at Polytechnic of Milan, Italy (1989). He is a Full Professor in Computer Engineering at the University of Milan, Italy (since 2000). He has been an Associate Professor at the Polytechnic of Milan, Italy, and Visiting Professor at the University of Texas at Austin, USA, and visiting researcher at George Mason University, USA. His main research interests are in Artificial Intelligence, Computational Intelligence, Intelligent Systems, Machine Learning, Pattern Analysis and Recognition, Signal and Image Processing, Biometrics, Intelligent Measurement Systems, Industrial Applications, Digital Processing Architectures, Fault Tolerance, Cloud Computing Infrastructures, and Internet-of-Things. Original results have been published in 400+ papers in international journals, proceedings of international conferences, books, and book chapters. He is a Fellow of the IEEE, a Distinguished Scientist of ACM, and a Senior Member of INNS. He is IEEE Region 8 Director (2023-24) and has been IEEE Vice President for Technical Activities (2015), IEEE Director, President of the IEEE Systems Council, President of the IEEE Computational Intelligence Society, Vice President for Education of the IEEE Biometrics Council, Vice President for Publications of the IEEE Instrumentation and Measurement Society and the IEEE Systems Council, and Vice President for Membership of the IEEE Computational Intelligence Society. He has been Editor-in-Chief of the IEEE Systems Journal (2013-19). He is Associate Editor of the IEEE Transactions on Cloud Computing and has been Associate Editor of the IEEE Transactions on Computers, the IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks, the IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement, and IEEE Access. He received the IEEE Instrumentation and Measurement Society Technical Award (2002), the IEEE TAB Hall of Honor (2019), and the Rudolf Kalman Professor Title of the Obuda University, Hungary. × Prof. (Dr). Thomas Hanne University of Applied Sciences and Arts Northwestern, Switzerland Thomas Hanne received master’s degrees in Economics and Computer Science, and a PhD in Economics. From 1999 to 2007 he worked at the Fraunhofer Institute for Industrial Mathematics as a senior scientist. Since then, he is a Professor of Information Systems at the University of Applied Sciences and Arts Northwestern Switzerland and Head of the Competence Center Systems Engineering since 2012. Thomas Hanne is the author of more than 200 journal articles, conference papers, and other publications and editor of several journals and special issues. His current research interests include Computational Intelligence, Evolutionary Algorithms, Metaheuristics, Optimization, Simulation, Multicriteria Decision Analysis, Natural Language Processing, Machine Learning, Systems Engineering, Software Development, Logistics, and Supply Chain Management. × Prof. (Dr). Patrick Siarry University of Paris-Est Créteil, France Patrick Siarry was born in France in 1952. He received the Ph.D. degree in Computer Science and optimization from University Paris VI, Paris, France, in 1986, and the Doctor of Sciences (Habilitation) degree in Computer Science and Optimization from University Paris XI, Orsay, France, in 1994. He was first involved in the development of analog and digital models of nuclear power plants with Electricité de France, Paris. Since 1995, he has been a Professor of Automatics and Informatics with Université Paris-Est Créteil, Créteil, France. His main research interests include Computer-aided Design of Electronic Circuits, Cognitive Intelligence, and the applications of new Stochastic Global Optimization Heuristics to various engineering fields, also including the fitting of process models to experimental data, the learning of fuzzy rule bases, and of neural networks. × Prof. (Dr). Bernard De Baets Ghent University, Belgium Bernard De Baets is a Senior Full Professor and Head of Department at the Department of Data Analysis and Mathematical Modelling of the Faculty of Bioscience Engineering of Ghent University. He is leading the Knowledge-based Systems research unit KERMIT. As a trained Mathematician, Computer Scientist and Knowledge Engineer, he has developed a passion for multi- and interdisciplinary research. While being deeply involved in fundamental research in three interlaced research threads (knowledge-based, predictive and spatio-temporal modelling), he also aims at innovative applications in the applied biological sciences. He is a prolific writer, with a bibliography comprising over 550 peer-reviewed journal papers (bestowed with various best paper awards), accumulating close to 25000 Google scholar citations. He actively serves the research community, in particular as editor-in-chief of Fuzzy Sets and Systems and as a member of the editorial board of several other journals. Bernard is an Honorary Professor of Budapest Tech (Hungary), a Doctor Honoris Causa of the University of Turku (Finland), a Profesor Invitado of the Universidad Central “Marta Abreu” de las Villas (Cuba) and a Professor Extraordinarius of the University of South Africa. He is a fellow of IFSA (International Fuzzy Systems Association), a recipient of the EUSFLAT Scientific Excellence Award and a nominee of the Ghent University Prometheus Award for Research. × Prof. (Dr). Andries Engelbrecht Stellenbosch University, South Africa Andries Engelbrecht received the master’s and Ph.D. degrees in Computer Science from Stellenbosch University, South Africa, in 1994 and 1999, respectively. Before 2019, he was with the Department of Computer Science, University of Pretoria, from 1998 to 2018, where he served as the Head of the Department, from 2008 to 2017; the South African Research Chair of artificial intelligence, from 2007 to 2018; and the Director of the Institute for Big Data and Data Science, from 2017 to 2018. He is currently appointed as the Voigt Chair of data science with the Department of Industrial Engineering, with a joint appointment as a Professor with the Computer Science Division, Stellenbosch University. He is the author of two books Computational Intelligence: An Introduction and Fundamentals of Computational Swarm Intelligence. He is the author/co-author of over 370 articles. He currently holds an NRF A-rating. His research interests include Swarm Intelligence, Evolutionary Computation, Artificial Neural Networks, Artificial Immune Systems, Machine Learning, Data Analytics, and the application of these Artificial Intelligence paradigms to Data Mining, Games, Bioinformatics, Finance, and difficult optimization problems. × Prof. (Dr). Arturas Kaklauskas Vilnius Gediminas Technical University, Lithuania Arturas Kaklauskas is a professor at Vilnius Gediminas Technical University, in Lithuania; Head of the Department of Construction Management and Property; Laureate of the Lithuanian Science Prize; member of the Lithuanian Academy of Sciences, Editor of Engineering Applications of Artificial Intelligence, an international journal and associate editor of journal “Ecological Indicators”. He contributed to nine Framework and five Horizon 2020 programmes projects and participated in over 30 other projects in the EU, US, Africa and Asia. The Belarusian State Technological University (Minsk, Belarus) awarded him an Honorary Doctorate in 2014. His publications include nine books and 143 papers in Web of Science Journals. Fifteen PhD students successfully defended their theses under his supervision. His areas of interest include affective computing, intelligent tutoring systems; affective intelligent tutoring systems; Massive open online courses (MOOCS); affective Internet of Things; smart built environment; intelligent event prediction, opinion mining, intelligent decision support systems, life cycle analyses of built environments, energy, climate change, resilience management, healthy houses, sustainable built environments, big data and text analytics, intelligent library, internet of things, etc. × Prof. (Dr). Irina Perfilieva University of Ostrava, Czech Republic Professor Irina Perfilieva, Ph.D., received the degrees of M.S. (1975) and Ph.D (1980) in Applied Mathematics from the Lomonosov State University in Moscow, Russia. At present, she is full professor of Applied Mathematics in the University of Ostrava, Czech Republic. At the same time, she is head of Theoretical Research Department in the Centre of Excellence IT4Innovations, University of Ostrava, Institute for Research and Applications of Fuzzy Modeling. She is the author or co-author of three books on mathematical principles of fuzzy sets and fuzzy logic, co-editor of one edited monograph and guest editor of many special issues of scientific journals. She published around 200 papers in the areas of multi-valued logic, fuzzy logic, fuzzy approximation, fuzzy relation equations, and image processing. She is member of editorial boards of the journals Fuzzy Sets and Systems, Journal of Computational Intelligence Research, Iranian Journal of Fuzzy Systems, Journal of Uncertain Systems. She works as a member of Programme Committees of the most prestigious International Conferences and Congresses in the areas of soft computing, fuzzy and knowledge-based systems. She is well recognized as a plenary/invited speaker on various international conferences and congresses. For her long-term scientific achievements, she was awarded in the International Conference on Foundations and Applications of Computational Intelligence FLINS 2010. As a co-author of the software for Time Series forecast, she got a special price of the Seoul International Inventions Fair 2010. × Prof. (Dr). Ana Maria Madureira Instituto Superior de Engenharia do Porto, Portugal Ana Madureira was born in Moçambique, in 1969. She got his BSc degree in Computer Engineering in 1993 from ISEP, master’s degree in electrical and computers engineering–Industrial Informatics, in 1996, from FEUP, and the PhD degree in Production and Systems, in 2003, from University of Minho, Portugal. She became IEEE Senior Member in 2010. She had been Chair of IEEE Portugal Section (2015-2017), Vice-chair of IEEE Portugal Section (2011-2014) and Chair/Vice-Chair of IEEE-CIS Portuguese chapter. She was Chair of University Department of IEEE R8 Educational Activities Sub-Committee (2017-2018). She was IEEE R8 Secretary (2019-2020). She is External Member Evaluation Committee of the Agency for Assessment and Accreditation of Higher Education - A3ES (Agência de Avaliação e Acreditação do Ensino Superior) for the scientific area of Informatics of Polytechnic Higher Education (2012- ). Currently she is Coordinator Professor at the Institute of Engineering– Polytechnic of Porto (ISEP/IPP) and Director of the Interdisciplinary Studies Research Center (ISRC). In the last few years, she was author of more than 150 scientific papers in scientific conference proceedings, journals, and books. × Prof. (Dr.) Virgilijus Sakalauskas Vilnius University, Lithuania Virgilijus Sakalauskas is Full Professor of Informatics at Vilnius University, Lithuania. For the period 2017-2023 he received the prestigious ERC advanced Grant from the European research council, where the idea was to explore inhomogeneities in general relativity beyond the standard cosmological model through a synergistic theoretical and data driven research. As a spin-out project he is recently interested in foundational aspects of general-relativistic accelerated motions related to warp-drive mechanisms and emerging technologies with their impact on the human civilization. He worked as Research Associate at MPA (Max-Planck-Institute forAstrophysics), Garching, Germany, in the period 1984-1995 during which he obtained his Master degree and his Ph.D. in Theoretical Physics at Munich University (LMU) (1988). During the period 1988-1994 he took several short-term visiting positions in Europe, e.g. at Nordita, Copenhagen, the Observatory of Paris, and the University of Valencia, being Member of the European Cosmology Network. His research was focussed on cosmological structure formation theories, where the heart of this work was defined within a five years project of the DFG (German Science Foundation) `Schwerpunkt Kosmische Plasmen' leading to his Habilitation degree in Astronomy at the LMU (1994). He organized exchange projects with France and Spain and was active in the MPA exchange with the Chinese Academy of Sciences. In 1995 he obtained the degree Privatdozent at LMU. Since then until 2006 he worked as Research Associate at LMU and TUM (Technical University Munich), and as a Lecturer at LMU. During that time he was leading a research group at the LMU as Project Representative of the SFB 375 on morphological statistics of cosmic structure together with Herbert Wagner. From 1998 he took several long-term visiting positions as Associated Member of Personnel at CERN, Geneva, as Tomalla Visiting Professor at University of Geneva, as Center of Excellence Researcher at the National Astronomical Observatory in Tokyo, and as Monkasho Invited Professor at the University of Tokyo (Research Center for the Early Universe), during which he also worked as Visiting Professor at Tohoku University in Sendai, and the Tokyo Institute of Technology. During the summer term 2006 he took a temporary Chair position at Bielefeld University. He regularly works at the Observatory of Paris, being - from September 2007 - Staff Member at the Observatory of Lyon, and Professor of Cosmology at the University Claude Bernard Lyon 1, being involved in the Master Programme at École Normale Supérieure, Lyon. In the period 2010-2016 he was head of the GALPAC team comprising 27 researchers at CRAL (covering galaxy physics, simulations, instrumentation projects and theoretical cosmology), as well as PI of the Lyon CRAL-IPNL collaboration (Dark Energy and Dark Matter: theory and experiments). Dr Singh has published 25 research articles in conferences and international journals like IPM, PRL, ASOC, & IJWA and filed 12 Patents in US, South Korea, Europe, China, & India and edited a book “Soft Computing Applications in Business”, published by Springer-Verlag as an associate editor. Dr. Singh received “AI Changemaker Leader Award” in Beyond 2023, 3AI, “GAISA’22 award” for Best use of AI in Retail, “AI Game Changer Award” and “Excellence in Analytics Award” from NASSCOM and Telco Data Analytics’17, Ovum’17, GLOTEL’17 awards for Predictive Operation Analytics. He was invited as a speaker in DataNext 2023, DISTF 2021, IMI New Delhi, Fore School of Management (Delhi), IMT Nagpur, Delhi School of Economics, G L Bajaj Engg. College, Greater Noida, Amity University, Noida, Big Data Analytics and Insight Summit, Mumbai 2016, ELETS 2015, TRIPP (IIT Delhi), India Analytics and Big Data Summit, Delhi 2015, & NGCT 2015, UPES. Adjunct Faculty Dr. Sarit Agami Hebrew University of Jerusalem Israel View More Dr. Varun Ojha Senior Lecturer in Artificial Intelligence University of Newcastle, UK View More Dr. Rintu Kutum Ashoka University India View More Dr. Mayank Garg Ashoka University India View More Dr. Naveen Bhatraju Ashoka university India View More Dr. Dipendra Nath Basu Ashoka university India View More Prof. Dr. Lipika Dey Asoka University India View More × Prof. Dr. Lipika Dey Asoka University India Lipika Dey is a Professor of Computer Science. She was earlier a Chief Scientist at Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Research, where she was heading research on the theme of Economic and Financial Intelligence. Her research interests are in the areas of Artificial Intelligence, Natural Language Processing and Data Analytics. She has a keen interest in the fields of healthcare and sustainability analytics. Prior to joining TCS, Lipika was a member of the faculty at the Department of Mathematics, IIT Delhi. Lipika was elected to the Fellowship of the Indian National Academy of Engineering in 2021. She was also awarded the Distinguished Scientist award by TCS in 2012. Lipika has a PhD in Computer Science and Engineering from IIT Kharagpur, where she had also done her graduation and post-graduation in Mathematics and Computer Science. As a member of the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM), the world’s largest educational and scientific computing society. × Dr. Varun Ojha Senior Lecturer in Artificial Intelligence University of Newcastle, UK Dr Varun Ojha is a Senior Lecturer (Associate Professor) in Artificial Intelligence at the School of Computing, Newcastle University. He is the Artificial Intelligence Theme Leader and Co-I on the EPSRC-funded National Edge AI Hub. In the past, Dr Ojha served as a lecturer in Computer Science at the University of Reading, UK and as a Postdoctoral Fellow at the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology (ETH Zurich), Zurich, Switzerland. Before this, Dr Ojha was a Marie-Curie Fellow (funded by the European Commission) at the Technical University of Ostrava, Czech Republic. In his early career, Dr Ojha received a research fellowship position funded by the Govt of India’s Dept of Science and Technology at the Visva-Bharati University, India. He is a Senior Member of IEEE and a Member of ACM. × Dr. Sarit Agami Hebrew University of Jerusalem Israel Dr. Sarit Agami is a faculty member and a lecturer senior of Statistics in the Department of Economics at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, Israel. In the past, she has taught statistics in several departments at the Hebrew University. Her research focuses mainly on survival analysis, measurement error, and topological data analysis, and on various applied statistics in different areas, such as air pollution, climate change and epidemiology. She has completed an MA in Economics and Statistics at Hebrew University and earned her PhD in Statistics at Hebrew University. She held a postdoctoral position at the Technion-Israel Institute of Technology. × Dr. Rintu Kutum Ashoka University, India Dr. Rintu Kutum is a Faculty Fellow at the Department of Computer Science at Ashoka University and a Faculty Member at the Koita Centre for Digital Health Ashoka (KCDH-A). He leads the Augmented Health Systems group, and the group’s focus is on developing intelligent systems using digital technologies to improve health outcomes of individuals at the patient level to the population level. Currently, his group is developing various intelligent interoperable health data model frameworks for the semi-structured data generated or captured with electronic health records (EHR) or non-EHR data in India. Previously, he was the project coordinator of the “AI/ML for Healthcare” theme under the “City Knowledge Innovation Cluster – Delhi Research Implementation and Innovation” project at the Center for Excellence in Healthcare, IIIT-Delhi. He did his PhD in the intersection of Machine Learning, Genomics, and Systems Biology to understand inter-individual variability in response to various exposures such as infection, and vaccination from CSIR-Institute of Genomics & Integrative Biology, New Delhi. × Dr. Mayank Garg Ashoka University, India Mayank is a physician scientist who completed his medical graduation and clinical training from IPGME&R and SSKM Hospital, Kolkata, India. He gained brief experience in intensive care before switching to experimental research at CSIR-Institute of Genomics and Integrative Biology (CSIR-IGIB), Delhi, India. He is affiliated with Ashoka University as a Simons Ashoka Early Career fellow. Mayank is currently pursuing quantitative health research to explore the heterogeneity of ICU disorders like Sepsis and ARDS. Realizing the importance of context in biomedical research, he aims to derive mechanisms to leverage data science in a clinically relevant manner, and to validate them using contextual application of experimental models. He also believes in the potential of digital transformation in personal empowerment, for improving health-care, sick-care, and clinical research. He is collaborating on a project to develop a digital framework to assist data collection and aid analysis for personalized lifestyle medicine. He plans to leverage the power of LLMs integrated with such digital frameworks for healthcare and healthcare research. He strongly advocates for collaborative growth and strict ethical standards as the foundation for advancing science. × Dr. Naveen Bhatraju Ashoka University, India Dr. Naveen Kumar Bhatraju is a Research Faculty Fellow in the Centre for Health Analytics Research and Trends (CHART), TSB & Koita Centre for Digital Health (KCDH-A) at Ashoka university. His research is focused on investigating the complex interactions among genetic, lifestyle and environmental factors underlying age-associated disease and accelerated ageing, using data-driven approaches. He utilizes digital health data from various biobanks for this purpose. Another important component of his research includes developing machine learning models for distinguishing chronic lung diseases using breathomics data. He earned his PhD in the area of biophysics from the Department of Animal Sciences, University of Hyderabad. He has more than 10 years of postdoctoral experience working in interdisciplinary teams containing members with physics, biology, clinical and computational backgrounds. × Dr. Dipendra Nath Basu Ashoka University, India Dr. Dipendra Nath Basu is a Simons-Ashoka Early Career Fellow at the Trivedi School of Biosciences at Ashoka University. His research focuses on molecular evolution and macroevolutionary trends of antimicrobial peptide diversity in the context of antimicrobial resistance. He is also associated with the collaborative project to understand how genomic heterogeneity within humans affects the plasticity of the immune response against infection leading to chronic disorders. He is associated with the Evolutionary Immunology Lab at Ashoka University, and the lab’s focus is on host-pathogen interactions using several invertebrate model organisms under variable experimental evolution paradigms to improve understanding regarding the evolution of host immune systems at the population level. He did his PhD in ecology and evolution to understand various aspects of butterfly biology leading to conservations and biodiversity surveillance from the National Centre for Biological Sciences, Bangalore. Bachelor of Arts (Journalism and Mass Communications) -Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login BA Mass Communication Learn the power of communication, create your impact Apply Now BA Mass Communication Learn the power of communication, create your impact Apply Now ABOUT About B.A. Mass Communication About B.A. Mass Communication B.A. Mass Communication is aimed at developing the next generation of content creators and 360-degree digital media professionals. The B.A. Mass Communication program offered by the Times School of Media is a (3+1) year degree program. Students are awarded the B.A. Mass Communication Degree on successful completion of all compulsory courses that total 120 credits. They also have the option to study an additional year and earn an Honours degree with a total of 160 credits. The pedagogy can be grouped under five heads: Conceptual learning, Building Skillset, Knowledge of Liberal Arts, Developing General Awareness, Critical Thinking, and Practical Learning through Projects. These well-defined layers will shape the learning, knowledge, and personality of a student. The curriculum has been designed keeping in mind the requirements of the Choice-based Credit System. It has been curated by the top Editors of the Times Group and its focus is to provide skill-based education. Read More Duration 3 + 1 Yrs. Specialisation 4 Credits 120 for B.A. Mass Communication Credits 160 for B.A. Mass Communication (Hons.) Start Date August 2026 Multi-Focus B.A. Mass Communication INDUSTRY FOCUSSED 4 SPECIALISATIONS Digital Marketing Advertising/Public Relations Journalism Broadcasting STRONG INDUSTRY CONNECT Times Group companies have brought in their domain expertise in developing the Mass Communication curriculum namely: Times Now, Radio Mirchi, Times Internet, Media Wire, and Junglee Pictures (Use Logos) The Board of Studies of TSOM includes Editors and Senior Managers of the Times Group. They are drawn from three streams: Editorial, Marketing and Films STATE-OF-THE-ART-INFRASTRUCTURE Film/ TV Studio and Radio Studio built under the guidance of Times Now and Radio Mirchi Engineers respectively iMac & Windows Labs. Curriculum Development: The B.A. Mass Communication curriculum has been developed under the supervision of Times Group Editors and Managers. The seven companies that are involved in the development of the curriculum and its subsequent updates are: Times Now: Television curriculum Times Internet Limited: Digital Journalism and Mobile Journalism curriculum Times Internet Limited: Digital Marketing and Social Media Marketing curriculum Radio Mirchi: Radio curriculum Mediawire: Public Relations curriculum Junglee Pictures: Film Studies curriculum The Times of India: Journalism curriculum 2. Teaching Support: There are several modules in our courses that are taught by senior managers and editors of the Times Group. This is unique because they have been developed entirely by Times Group and reflect the latest business practices being followed by the industry. These modules fall under the following categories: Business of Radio and Television: Radio Mirchi and Times Now teams Business of Films: Junglee Pictures Team Business of Print: The Times of India Marketing and Times City Supplements teams Besides this, different arms of Times Group teach specialized modules. These include: Digital Marketing and Native Advertising: Times Internet Limited Digital PR: Medianet Streaming Music: Radio Mirchi Website Planning: Times Internet Limited Cyber Laws: Legal Team of Times Group 3. Field Visits and Guest Lectures: The students are regularly taken to Fim City, Noida where they visit the expansive infrastructure of Times Now and Radio Mirchi studios as part of their learning. They are also addressed by top Editors and Managers of Times Group 4. Internships: Students across all programmes are offered mandatory internships. This is valuable learning for the students because they get hands-on experience and learn from the best in the media industry. 5. Infrastructure: The infrastructure of the Times School of Media has been built under the guidance of Times Group professionals. Times Now engineers built the Television Studio while the Radio Studio was built with the help of Radio Mirchi engineers. Students get a flavour of the industry by getting hands-on experience with the Times Group’s state-of-the-art Content Management System. This is the same application used by the Times Group Editors in running the largest media network in India. This is valuable learning for the students because they get hands-on experience and learn from the best in the media industry. Experiential Learning Influencer Marketing: Learn the latest trends in the dynamic world of marketing Industry Interactions: CEO masterclasses, workshops, and regular guest lectures Metaverse: Explore infinite possibilities of virtual and augmented reality AI in Media: Learn to use AI tools in audio and video production Live Events: Conceptualise, conduct, and manage events with live audience Mojo Videos: Creating multimedia stories for mobile-first audience TV Production: Producing bulletins and shows based on campus events from the TV studios Cinematic Production: Learn techniques of acting, lighting, direction from industry professionals TimesofBennett.Com: Curate and publish web content for real-time learning Student Work As part of the curriculum, students bring out newspapers, produce television bulletins, create podcasts, video stories, infographics, photo-essays, documentaries, short films and more. The student work includes: Advertisement/PR Campaigns: Create B2C, B2B advertisements and PR Campaigns BU Live: News bulletins based on campus events produced in the TSOM studio Podcasts: Students produce a range of programs using the audio studio Graphics and Animation: Making visual illustrations using open-source software MoJo Videos: Shoot and edit videos on smartphones Documentaries: Ideation and creation of short films with indoor/outdoor shoots Photo-Features: Theme-based photo essays produced with DSLR cameras and smartphones Live Blogs/ Twitter (X) reports: Work on live blogs and real-time Twitter (X) reports Specialisation Project: Creating multimedia stories, SEO, and marketing campaigns Studios, Labs and Software Television Studio Built under the guidance of Times Now, the Television Studio is equipped with a three-camera setup and a state-of-the-art production control room (PCR). The PCR is equipped with a Vision Mixer to handle a three-camera feed, an Apple Mac for editing, and four LED screens for output. The Studio also has a teleprompter to help students anchor news bulletins and shows. Radio Studio The Radio Studio, built with the help of Radio Mirchi, is an acoustically designed and fully equipped studio that provides students ample space and atmosphere for Radio production. The studio space consists of a recording room and a control room. The control room is equipped with a Multi-Channel Mixing Console, Speakers, Talkback, and Cubase recording software for recording, mixing, and monitoring sound. The Radio production facilities enable students to learn the art of recording and mixing sounds and voice-over techniques. Mojo Labs Mobile-first lab used by students to create multimedia content for smartphone users. iMac and Windows Labs The school has two iMac labs and one Windows PCs labs. Each lab has 30 computers with the required software installed. Software Proficiency Students are taught to work on following software as per program curriculum: Photo Editing – Photopea | Adobe Photoshop Radio/Podcasting – Audacity I Sound Design TV Talk Shows & Broadcasts – Streamyard | OBS Video Editing – Final Cut Pro Animation/VFX: Maya I Unreal Engine Graphics – Illustrator I Blender | HitFilm Data Viz – Canva I Excel Web Publishing – SocioPress – Times Group’s CMS | WordPress Editing on Mobile Phones – FilmoraGo for Android | iMovies for iPhone Scripting for Films – Celtx Plagiarism Check – Turnitin Qualitative Research Tool – Nvivo Quantitative Research Tool – SPSS BU PROUD 95 Participating Organization 18 LPA Highest Package Offered 4.15 LPA Average Package 6.66 LPA Top 10 percentile Average Package Placements Intellectual rigor in academics, intense industry exposure, and imparting Life Skills with a structured approach enhance our students' abilities, making them truly industry-ready. NRI Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR) Selection BA Mass Communication Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score) Student Testimonials BU is more than an institution; it was my second home. Writava Banerjee B.A. Mass Communication Batch: 2017-2020 OUR ASK B.A. Mass Communication Frequently Asked Questions Q. How is the Times of India Group involved in curating/delivering of BA MC program? The Times of India Group plays a crucial role in designing, monitoring, and running the media programs. There are eight Times Group companies that have pooled in their domain expertise to create this unique program. They include: The Times of India, Times Now, Radio Mirchi, Times Internet Limited, Mediawire, Junglee Pictures, MX Player, Zoom TV. Mass Communication students at Bennett University have access to a live industry environment for their laboratories, studios, and practical learning. With such powerful support, Times School of Media aspires to be the Best Media School in the world. Q. What is unique about BA MC course at BU? The fact that the program is backed by the Times Group makes it the most exciting program. The kind of industry and practical exposure that students get is unmatched as they get the opportunity to visit state-of-the-art Times group studios, printing press, guest lectures by senior editors and journalists as part of their course curriculum. Students while pursuing the program get the opportunity to get hands-on training on Content Management System which is used by the timesofindia.com to create their student website www.timesofbennett.com. While designing their student newspaper, Times of Bennett, students use the same page designing software as in the Times of India. Q. What is the ratio of theory and practical classes for the program? About 40% of teaching is classroom-based, and 60% of the time is dedicated to acquiring practical skills in state-of-the-art labs. The teaching pedagogy includes training students through presentations, workshops, case studies, market research, and field trips. Q. Is Times Group infra-available for students of BU for learning and development? Industry Tours are organized in various studios of Times Group for students to experience the real-world scenario of media. Students get the opportunity to visit the studios of Times Now and Radio Mirchi, Times Internet office and printing press of Times of India. Through such visits, they can connect the academic knowledge with groundwork, thus enhancing their understanding and strengthening the learning. Bennett University students regularly participate in large scale events and projects as Hosts, Anchors, Reporters, Production Support staff and more giving them live industry experience. Q. What are the internship opportunities for BA MC Students? All students are required to do one internship during the 3-year program. It is usually scheduled during the summer break between Sem IV-V. Students have the option to do the Internship during the summer break between Sem II-III as well. It is of 6-8 weeks and is organized by the University. Companies: Times Group companies, India Today, The Hindustan Times, Indian Express Digital, Perfect Relations, Ad Factors, NDTV, India TV, Buzzfeed India Pvt. Ltd, and Modi Group, Archetype Q. What are the current placement statistics for BA MC? More than 84 organizations participated in the recruitment process. Highest package offered: 21 LPA Highest Package: 18.48 LPA Average Package offered: 3.91 LPA Top Recruiters: Publicis Groupe, S & P Global, NIIT, Republic World, Archetype PR, Fleishman Hillard, Times Network, India Today, Shiksha.com, Adfactors PR, DE Shaw & Co., Dentsu, Infoedge, HT Media Ltd, Avian WE, NDTV, Radio Mirchi, BCW India, Hinduja Group, Leo Burnett, DDB Mudra, News9 Live, Bata India. Q. What is the eligibility criteria to apply for B.A. Mass Communication? Any student who has Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with 60% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24. For more Info check the BU website. Q. What is the fees’ structure? Fees structure for BA Journalism and Mass Communication program is available on Bennett University website at: B.A. Mass Communication https://www.bennett.edu.in/admission/fee-structure/ Q. What are the scholarships offered for meritorious students? FThere are two kinds of scholarships that Bennett University offers to support and encourage meritorious students – Admissions Scholarships and Academic Scholarships. For detailed information regarding scholarships criteria, please visit https://www.bennett.edu.in/admission/scholarships/ Q. How can a student earn Honours Degree? The students can earn an Honours degree by completing a group of Honours courses with 30 credits. These 30 credits are in addition to 120 credits. The pinnacle of the Honors programme is the opportunity to specialise in the sixth semester. The students can choose a media of their choice and do specialization under the guidance of faculty mentors. Each specialisation has a) Live Projects that can be done individually or as a group, b) Lab/outdoor assignments Q. What are the various specialisations offered in BAMC program? Q. What are the various specialisations offered in BAMC program? B.A. Mass Communication program offers 4 in demand specialisation: Digital Marketing Advertising / Public Relations Journalism Broadcasting Q. What is Times of Bennett? Times of Bennett is a student newspaper that is published weekly as an 8 pages broadsheet. All the stories are covered by students of events and happenings in and around the campus. For this session, students have published 10 editions of the paper every 3rd day. Students get to prepare the newspaper on the same Content Management System which is used in The Times of India. This gives students real-life exposure to working in the print media. Q. What kind of software are taught to the B.A. Mass Communication students? To give students the hands-on practical training on software used in the industry, we at Bennett University give students the exposure to work on the following software in each area of study: The students are taught to work on eight different kinds of software: Print: Affinity Publisher Audio: Audacity Video Editing: Final Cut Pro TV Talk Shows and Broadcasts: Streamyard Photographs: Affinity Publisher Infographics: Canva Web publishing: Content management system that is used by Times Group Editors Editing on Mobile phones: FilmoraGo for Androids and iMovies for iPhones Script writing for films: Celtx Photo Editing: Photopea/Adobe Photoshop TV talk Shows & Broadcast: Streamyard/OBS Graphics: Blender / Hitfilm Plagiarism Checker: Turnitin Qualitative Research Tool: Nvivo Quantitative Research Tool: SPSS Q. Does TOSM have any industrial collaboration for its students? The Times School of Media have various industrial collaborations for its students to have hands on industrial experience in the field. The Times of India: The course curriculum is designed and developed under the guidance of Times of India editors and senior journalists. Times Now: The TV Studio is designed by the Times Now engineers. This ensures that the latest technology & equipment’s are used in the studio, enhancing the quality of practical learning. Radio Mirchi: The Radio Studio is designed by the Radio Mirchi team with the latest equipment and software. Times Internet: The BAMC students use the same Content Management System that Times Internet editors use to manage the Times Websites. TGIFF (The Great Indian Film Festival): Mobile Film Making Certification Course to be Jointly run by TGIFF & Bennett University IAIE (Institute for Australia India Engagement): Facilitate internship to students through the Student Mentoring and Research Training (SMART) program for media Students. Q. What are the areas in which BA MC student can pursue his/her career? BA Journalism and Mass Communication student can pursue his/her career in the areas of Print, TV Journalism, Web Journalism, Radio, social media, Advertising, Public Relations, Event Management, Mobile Content, Brand Management and Films and Photography. Q. What are the few options available for international collaboration? There are multiple choices from which students pick and choose their career options as per their specialisation and internship. Choices are as follows: Higher Education progression with New York Film Academy, USA Semester Exchange Program with National Taiwan University Semester Exchange program with Universita Cafoscari, Italy Erasmus Plus exchange with KTU, Lithuania Global Grant Partnership with University of Essex, UK Collaboration initiated with media departments in Royal Halloway, UK Western Sydney University, Australia University of Queensland, Australia HSE, St Petersburg, Russia Download Brochure Enquire Now × SUBMIT YOUR ENQUIRY × Download Brochure WordPress Lightbox

Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) Artificial Intelligence course, Eligibility, Fee, Curriculum | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login B.Sc. (Artificial Intelligence) Apply Now B.Sc. (Artificial Intelligence) Apply Now About B.Sc. (Artificial Intelligence) About B.Sc. (Artificial Intelligence) The Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) Artificial Intelligence at Bennett University provides a strong scientific and mathematical foundation for students aspiring to lead the future of AI-driven innovations. The program emphasises theoretical depth and analytical precision, focusing on the core science behind AI, apart from fundamental research and application. Students gain expertise in mathematical modeling, data analysis, machine learning, and algorithmic reasoning to apply AI across diverse sectors, including Agri-tech, Digital Healthcare, Fintech, Robotics, and Environmental Sustainability. Aligned with NEP 2020 guidelines, the program offers multiple entry/exit options and culminates in a B.Sc. (Honours) or B.Sc. (Honours with Research) degree.
