Why B.Sc. (Artificial Intelligence) from Bennett University Our program emphasises a core scientific approach to Artificial Intelligence, empowering students with the theoretical, mathematical, and computational skills to design, analyse, and innovate intelligent systems. Scientific Foundation: Comprehensive understanding of mathematical, statistical, and computational principles underlying AI. Applied Learning: Practical exposure to AI tools, technologies, and real-world projects. Innovation Focus: Encourages innovation through Hackathons, AI Competitions, and Research Projects. (Honours with Research) students with a 12-credit dissertation project. Global Exposure: Our strategic collaborations ensure that our students get a glimpse of global education practices. More International Tie-Ups Specialisations The curriculum of B.Sc. (Artificial Intelligence) allows students to explore multiple emerging domains within AI and related sciences, encouraging interdisciplinary thinking and industry-ready skill development. Second year onwards, students can specialise in an applied domain from a range of specialisations across various fields. These include Data Science, Signal Processing , and Next-Generation Networks , which strengthen analytical, mathematical, and system-level understanding. Applied domains such as Digital Healthcare, AgriTech, and Environment & Sustainability focus on scientific problem-solving using AI for societal impact. Innovation-led fields like Robotics, FinTech, MediaTech, LegalTech , and Quantum Information Processing allow students to explore intelligent systems, automation, domain-specific analytics, and next-generation computing. Together, these pathways empower students to build expertise across both scientific fundamentals and cutting-edge AI applications. Program Objectives To provide a strong scientific and mathematical foundation for students. To nurture the next generation of experts with the analytical and theoretical underpinnings of AI to lead AI-driven innovations through rigorous training in fundamental research and application. Learning Outcomes Upon completion of the B.Sc. in Artificial Intelligence, students will master core AI concepts like Machine Learning, Computer Vision, and Robotics. They will develop a deeper understanding of the mathematical and statistical aspects to create responsible AI frameworks and possess the expertise to drive research toward next-generation AI tools and design scientifically sound, holistic AI systems. Curriculum Details The B.Sc. (AI) curriculum integrates scientific rigour with practical exposure, ensuring students develop both theoretical understanding and hands-on expertise. Core Subjects: Stochastic Processes, Mathematics for AI, Python Programming, Probability, Statistics, Signal Processing, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Generative AI & others. Specialisation Options: Digital Healthcare, AgriTech, Data Science, Robotics, FinTech, MediaTech, LeagalTech, Quantum Information Processing, Environment & Sustainability, Signal Processing, Next Generation Networks & others. Research Component: 20% research-focused coursework, including industry certifications and projects. Career Pathways Graduates of the B.Sc. (AI) program are prepared for advanced careers that demand analytical, computational, and research-oriented skills. They can pursue dynamic and rewarding career roles AI Researcher, Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, Algorithm Developer in the Technology & Research domain; Fintech Specialist, Quantitative Analyst, in the Finance & Consulting domain, and a few more roles in Digital Healthcare, Biotech, Quantum Computing, and Environmental Sustainability in the Applied Science sector. (Artificial Intelligence) Program Frequently Asked Questions What is the course curriculum of the B.Sc. Artificial Intelligence program? The curriculum emphasises mathematical and statistical rigour, includes a 20% research component with industry and societal projects, and aligns with NEP 2020 through flexible entry/exit and credit options. What is the difference between B.Sc. Artificial Intelligence and B.Tech. Artificial Intelligence? B.Sc. AI emphasises theoretical and scientific foundations, while B.Tech. AI focuses on engineering, hardware, and system development. What are the career prospects after completing B.Sc. AI? High demand spans IT, Finance, Gaming, and VR industries, offering roles like Data Scientist, ML Engineer, and AI Researcher, with pathways to advanced research careers. What are the core subjects taught in the B.Sc. AI program? Core subjects include Mathematics for AI, Probability & Statistics, Stochastic Processes, Signal Processing, Machine Learning, and Deep Learning. What are the multiple entry/exit options in the B.Sc. program under NEP 2020? Exit after 1st Year (40 credits): UG Certificate Exit after 2nd Year (80 credits): UG Diploma Exit after 3rd Year (120 credits): Sc. Degree Continue for 4th Year: Sc. (Honours) / B.Sc. (Honours with Research) What are the eligibility criteria for B.Sc. Artificial Intelligence program? Passed Class XII with Physics, Mathematics/IT, and one other subject with at least 55%; selection based on best of three subjects (Physics, Maths/IT, and one non-vocational subject). How does the Honours with Research stream work in B.Sc. AI? Students securing 75% or above in the first six semesters can opt for the Honours with Research track, completing a 12-credit dissertation project in their final semester. What specialisations or minors can students choose? Students may pursue minors in applied domains like Agri-tech, Digital Healthcare, Fintech, Robotics, or Data Science to complement their AI foundation. NRI Pass with 60% aggrigate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third* subject with highest score) Note : * Third subject should not be vocational- Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc OUR ASK B.Tech Artificial Intelligence Frequently Asked Questions What is the course curriculum of the B.Tech Artificial Intelligence program? The course curriculum of B.Tech Artificial Intelligence has fine a balance of theoretical research and practical applications, equipping students to leverage and contribute to the state-of-the-art in AI development. The curriculum follows an interdisciplinary approach with the option to choose from 10 minors in different domains. Each subject in the curriculum has a 20% research-focused component, including industry certifications, research papers, self-learning, start-ups, and projects with societal impact. Overall, this comprehensive approach equips students with the skills and knowledge needed to succeed in the rapidly-evolving field of AI. What are the career prospects after completing B.Tech AI ? Graduates of B.Tech Artificial Intelligence are in high demand across different industries. AI graduates can be recruited in IT, Finance, Gaming, VR industries, to name a few. The demand for AI professionals is growing rapidly as industries are integrating AI in their processes. Some of the popular career choices are Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, Robotics Engineer, AI Researcher, Software Developer, and Business Intelligence Developer, Software Engineer, NLP Engineer, Big Data Scientist, Business Intelligence Developer, to name a few. What are the skills required to excel in the B. Tech AI program? To excel in the B. Tech Artificial Intelligence program, students should have strong analytical skills, problem-solving ability, critical thinking, and creativity. Additionally, proficiency in programming languages like Python and C++, knowledge of machine learning algorithms, data structures, and computer vision concepts are essential. Is it necessary to have a background in Computer Science to pursue B.Tech AI ? Having a background in Computer Science is not necessarily a requirement to pursue a B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence. However, it could be beneficial to have some foundational knowledge in programming, algorithms, and data structures. What is the difference between B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and B.Tech Computer Science? B.Tech AI and B.Tech Computer Science are two different academic programs with distinct focuses. B.Tech AI primarily deals with the study of intelligent agents, which includes computer programs that can perform tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as visual perception, speech recognition, decision-making, and language translation. On the other hand, B.Tech Computer Science (CS) is an interdisciplinary field that involves the study of computer systems, software engineering, programming languages, algorithms, and data structures. While both programs have some overlap in their coursework, B.Tech AI focuses more on specialized topics such as machine learning, robotics, natural language processing, and cognitive computing, while B.Tech CSE covers a broader range of topics that include software development, database systems, networking, and computer architecture. Ultimately, the choice between these two programs depends on student’s interests and career goals. Download Brochure Enquire Now × SUBMIT YOUR ENQUIRY × Download Brochure WordPress Lightbox

Constitutional and Human Rights Law This specialisation will be the correct amalgamation of theoretical and practical knowledge on contemporary and critical issues surrounding constitutional law and human rights law. B.A. (Hons.) Sociology - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login B.A. (Hons.) Sociology The Sociology major at the School of Liberal Arts Bennett University emphasizes critical thinking, interdisciplinary learning, and a strong foundation in sociological theories and research methods. Sociology examines social structures, human interactions, and cultural patterns. It investigates how societies function, addressing issues such as social inequality, globalization, gender dynamics, and urbanization. The discipline equips students with research skills to study societal transformations. The sociology program is designed to: (i) introduce core sociological theories and research methods in the first two semesters; (ii) explore areas like social stratification, culture, and globalization in the following semesters: and (iii) offer specialized courses in urban sociology, migration studies, and gender and society in the later years. Theory courses – Theory courses – 3-4 lectures a week depending on course credits, each lasting 55 minutes. Fieldwork projects – Community-based research and social impact assessments. The curriculum is designed to offer a strong academic foundation through Major-Core courses , complemented by Major-Electives for added specialization. Major-Core At the heart of the program, the Major-Core (MJ) curriculum delivers essential and advanced coursework, with students required to complete a minimum of 45 credits, up to a maximum of 60 credits. Tanvi BA Liberal Arts Batch: 2020-23 Events BA Honours Programmes Frequently Asked Questions When was the B.A Liberal Arts program initiated under the School of Liberal Arts at Bennett University? The Liberal Arts program started with its first batch in 2020. It offers the opportunity to learn from highly qualified and experienced teachers. The program's foundation rests on two pillars -- the development of all-around personality and life skills. What are the majors available in the BA Liberal Arts course at Bennett University? The students can choose Majors from seven domains: Psychology, Political Science & IR, Economics, English, Business Studies, Philosophy & Sociology How is the BA Liberal Arts curriculum structured? In the first semester of the program, the students will be introduced to all the majors to select the Major which suits them the most. The choice of Majors and Electives is given from the second semester onwards. How do electives benefit a student's career? An elective is a combination of courses taken through the duration of university education. The courses taken as electives allow students to understand the world with exposure to a wide range of subjects and areas of practice. This broader understanding is a critical requirement to succeed in the world of the future. Can an undergraduate student take part in research activities? Bennett University focuses on research, experiential, and applied learning. Undergraduate students are encouraged to work with members of the faculty in their live research projects. Such exposure makes students understand the intricacies of the research process and deepens their knowledge Do you assist students in placements, summer internships, and industry tours? Bennett University has a dedicated Career Services Centre that works closely with the industry to participate in guest lectures, curricular inputs, joint program development, internships, and placements. What are the future career options after pursuing BA Liberal Arts? BA Liberal Arts graduates can look for rewarding careers in advertising, public relations, financial analysis, design, journalism, publishing, corporate communications, marketing, etc., or start a successful entrepreneurial journey of their own. What kind of industry exposure do students have access to? This is one of the unique hallmarks of the BA Liberal Arts program as it benefits from faculty members teaching courses in management, which in turn, provides students an opportunity to continuously interact with industry experts from India and abroad. Tech Electronics and Computing Course - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login B.Tech Electronics & Computer Engineering Innovate diverse algorithms through electronics & computer Engineering Apply Now B.Tech Electronics & Computer Engineering Innovate diverse algorithms through electronics & computer Engineering Apply Now Why B.Tech Electronics & Computer Engineering at Bennett University Cutting-edge Software tools State-of-the-art software tools such as MATLAB, Vivado, OptiSim, Milling machine, FPGA Virtex/Kintex boards, MSO, VNA etc. Hands-on practical Experience Work on live projects funded by the Government of India and leading industries. Learn Future Technologies Exposure to work on Future Technologies (5G+AI, smart sensors, Cognitive IoT, End-to-end Systems, Quantum Communication, Signal Processing, 5G Antenna Design) Encourage entrepreneurship Bennett Hatchery ideated more than 100+ start-up 21cr funding for start-up projects ABOUT About B.Tech Electronics & Computer Engineering B.Tech in Electronics and Computer Engineering is an interdisciplinary program that combines electronics engineering with computer science to provide students with expertise in both hardware and software domains. Students study electronics core courses like Analog and Digital Circuits, VLSI Design, Embedded Systems, Communication Systems, Signal Processing, Internet of Things and computer courses like Programming, Data Structures, Algorithms, Operating Systems, AI & Machine Learning, and Cybersecurity. The curriculum inculcates hands-on training on hardware and software development. Students are prepared for jobs in Telecommunications, Robotics, Healthcare Tech, IT, and Semiconductor Industry. The various job roles are Electronics Design Engineer, Hardware Engineer, Embedded Systems Engineer, VLSI Designer, Telecommunications Engineer, AI/ML Engineer, Software Developer, Cybersecurity Analyst, etc. Duration 4 Years Credits 162 Specialisation 4 Start Date August 2026 Admission Counsellors Connect with our counsellors for any admissions related queries. explore Fee Structure Know more about fee structure. explore Scholarship Know more about Scholarship. explore Fee Structure 2025 Fee Type Year 1 Year 2 Year 3 Year 4 TOTAL Tuition fee (₹) 3,60,000 3,70,000 3,80,000 3,80,000 14,90,000 Registration Fees One Time (₹) 45,000 – – – 45,000 Total Fees (₹) 3,60,000 3,70,000 3,80,000 3,80,000 15,35,000 * Fee is subject to revision with the new academic year as per Bennett University policy. B.Tech EEC Curriculum 2024-28 B.Tech EEC Curriculum 2023-27 Key Highlights Specialisations B.Tech program in B.Tech Electronics & Computer Engineering is offered with 4 specialization Embedded Systems and IoT In the Embedded System, hardware design and software programming are used for innovative product development. The various embedded systems are interconnected using IoT based network. IoT is very crucial for the industry as well as other environments in achieving high productivity, efficiency, safe working environment, and low carbon emissions. IoT solutions are going to play a very important role in home automation, security, and various industrial problems with 5G communications. In Embedded Systems and IoT specialisation, students are imparted knowledge of hardware design, software development, and interconnection with network and cloud infrastructure. The students completing the course can opt for jobs in various companies such as Synopsys, Intel, NXP, Qualcomm, Analog Devices, Samsung, Microsoft, Siemens. The various job profiles in the industry are IoT lead Engineer, IP Design Lead Engineer, Hardware Architect, ASIC Design FPGA Engineer, Application Engineer. The courses addresses various topics on Hardware-Software Co-design, Real Time Operating System, Data Management and Cloud Computing, and IoT Networks and Protocols Data and Signal Analytics The Specialisation focuses design, development, and research areas such as Data Analytics, Machine and Deep Learning, Information Processing & Analytics, an Computer Vision. The specialisation is carefully designed to attain technical aspects that enable students to grow into competent Data Analytics professionals. Signal and Data analytics is about making conclusions from the information they contain. Data analytics with machine learning technologies are widely used in commercial industries, research communities. The specialisation focuses on building foundations in data analytics using machine learning, a deep understanding of the area of specialisation, and innovative ability to solve new problems. Interdisciplinary skill sets and knowledge to handle and analyse large amounts of data are now essential for many career paths. The use of data analytics is a growing trend spanning all industries. The specialisation offers Machine Learning, Exploratory Data Analytics, Big Data, Natural Language Processing, and Artificial Intelligence, along with basic courses in programming languages. VLSI Design VLSI (Very Large Scale Integration) Design is a field of electronics engineering that involves the design and development of integrated circuits (ICs) with a large number of transistors on a single chip. VLSI technology enables the integration of thousands to billions of transistors, allowing for the creation of complex and highly functional electronic systems on a small silicon chip. In India and around the world, VLSI design plays a crucial role in the semiconductor industry, contributing to the development of advanced electronic devices such as microprocessors, memory chips, and application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs). VLSI engineers are involved in various stages of the IC design process, including architecture design, logic design, physical design, and verification. In India, the demand for VLSI engineers is significant, driven by the growth of the electronics and semiconductor industry. Companies like Intel, Qualcomm, Broadcom, and Samsung have a strong presence and offer job opportunities in VLSI design. Additionally, numerous Indian companies and startups focus on VLSI design services and contribute to the global semiconductor supply chain. Globally, VLSI engineers find employment in leading semiconductor companies across the United States, Europe, and Asia. Silicon Valley in the U.S. is a hub for VLSI design, with companies like NVIDIA, AMD, and Apple actively involved in chip design. Job opportunities in VLSI design include roles such as VLSI design engineer, ASIC design engineer, physical design engineer, verification engineer, and more. With the advent of new technologies such as 5G, artificial intelligence, and the Internet of Things (IoT), the demand for VLSI professionals is expected to grow, as these technologies rely heavily on advanced semiconductor solutions. As the global electronics industry continues to evolve, VLSI engineers will play a crucial role in shaping the future of technology, making it a field with promising job prospects both in India and worldwide. Mechatronics Mechatronics is an interdisciplinary field that combines mechanical engineering, electronics, computer science, and control engineering to design and create intelligent systems and products. The goal of mechatronics is to integrate these diverse engineering disciplines to develop more efficient and intelligent systems with enhanced functionalities. In India and around the world, there is a growing demand for mechatronics professionals due to the increasing complexity of modern engineering systems. Mechatronics engineers are involved in the design, development, and maintenance of systems and products that involve a combination of mechanical and electronic components, often incorporating sensors, actuators, and control systems. They work in various industries, including manufacturing, automotive, aerospace, robotics, healthcare, and more. In India, the manufacturing sector, which is a significant contributor to the economy, has a strong demand for mechatronics engineers. The automotive industry relies heavily on mechatronics for the development of smart vehicles with advanced features like autonomous driving and intelligent control systems. Job opportunities for mechatronics engineers include roles such as mechatronics engineer, automation engineer, robotics engineer, control systems engineer, and product development engineer. The skill set required for mechatronics professionals spans mechanical design, electronics, programming, and system integration. The various companies which offer job opportunities to Mechatronics engineers are Toyota, General Motors, Tesla, Siemens, ABB, Boston Dynamics, iRobot, NVIDIA, Boeing, Lockheed Martin, Medtronic, Johnson & Johnson, Samsung, Sony, LG Electronics, Apple, Intel, IBM, Google, NASA, ISRO, Accenture, Deloitte, etc. As industries continue to adopt advanced technologies, the demand for mechatronics professionals is expected to rise, providing ample job opportunities in India and globally for individuals with expertise in this interdisciplinary field. Honors An Honours in B.Tech Electronics & Computer Engineering is obtained by earning 18 more credits from six advanced courses. Projects 12 credit Major Project in the 8th semester, five credits in the 7th semester, and one credit in the 6th semester are mandatory. Students have an opportunity to start the project in the 6th semester, continue to evolve during the summer after 6th and 7th semesters, and finally complete it in the final semester. Such a long association helps the student gain considerable knowledge and skills required for prospectus career opportunities. Internships One internship at the end of the 6th semester is a part of the curriculum. Students have interned at INRS EMT Lab (Montreal, Canada), IITs, IIITs ECE Research Labs, HERE Maps, UST Software, Airport Authority of India, and various other industries. NRI Pass with 55% aggrigate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third* subject with highest score) * Third subject should not be vocational- Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc Events & Gallery Guest Lectures Events Industrial visit at CEERI, Pilani, Rajasthan Teachers' Day celebration by Resonance Club MediaTek Noida Industrial Visit Student Testimonials My experience at Bennett was truly amazing. ECE Batch: 2018-2022 OUR ASK Frequently Asked Questions What do Electronic and Computing engineers do? Electronic and computer engineer apply hardware and software knowledge for design, and development of various products in Internet of Things (IoT), 5G and 6G communication, bio-medical devices, computer vision, artificial intelligence etc. How this programme has been developed? It has been developed in response to industry demands to develop students' hardware and software engineering skills in an integrated way. Is Electronics the same as Computer Engineering? Electronics engineers study the principles of electricity, current, microelectronics and how electricity interacts with hardware parts. Computer engineers study the function of computer hardware and how to design, build and use hardware with its software. Electronics and Computing Engineer studies a unification of Electronics Engineering & Computer Engineering to develop hardware and software technologies that enable ICT (information and communications technology) and IoT (Internet of Things) applications. Is Electronics & Computer Engineering hard? The course is easy for the student who is interested in design, development, and programming of embedded systems. With this course, students can start their own business in the electronics and software industry. This programme provides opportunities to excel in software, hardware, system engineer or project management. What is the future of Electronics & Computer Engineering? Semiconductor manufacturing (Chip design and manufacturing) has declared as a thrust area by Government of India. Electronics and Computer Engineering hence adds more jobs to the existing VLSI, Circuit and Chip Design, System Design, Embedded Systems, IoT wearables and Medical Electronics. What kind of career do Electronics and Computer Engineer graduates pursue? Graduates of this course pursue their career in areas like VLSI design, Embedded Systems and IoT, Data and Signal Analytics, Computer Network and Security, etc. Download Brochure Enquire Now × SUBMIT YOUR ENQUIRY × Download Brochure WordPress Lightbox

B.A. (Hons.) English - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login B.A. (Hons.) English The English major at the School of Liberal Arts, Bennett University, offers a captivating journey into the world of literary exploration and critical analysis. English as a discipline explores literature, language, and cultural studies. It encompasses the study of classical and contemporary works, literary criticism, and creative writing. The field encourages analytical thinking, effective communication, and an appreciation of diverse literary traditions. The English program is designed to: (i) introduce foundational texts and critical theories in the first two semesters; (ii) engage with literary analysis, language studies, and creative writing in the following semesters: and (iii) offer electives in world literature, digital humanities, and translation studies in the later years. Theory courses – 3-4 lectures a week depending on course credits, each lasting 55 minutes. Writing workshops – Creative and academic writing assignments. The curriculum is designed to offer a strong academic foundation through Major-Core courses , complemented by Major-Electives for added specialization. Major-Core At the heart of the program, the Major-Core (MJ) curriculum delivers essential and advanced coursework, with students required to complete a minimum of 45 credits, up to a maximum of 60 credits. Forms of Literature Introduction to Literary Theory Early British Literature Literature in the Age of Empire Post-Colonial Literature Indian Literature Authors in Search of Characters and Plots I Sci-Fi Literatures Children Literature Workshops on Female Writers International Economics Teaching English I Teaching English II Editing I Editing II Major-Elective To further enrich their learning experience, students can select from Major-Electives (MJ-Elec), offering additional flexibility and breadth, with credit options ranging from a minimum of 27 to a maximum of 36 credits. Tanvi BA Liberal Arts Batch: 2020-23 Events BA Honours Programmes Frequently Asked Questions When was the B.A Liberal Arts program initiated under the School of Liberal Arts at Bennett University? The Liberal Arts program started with its first batch in 2020. It offers the opportunity to learn from highly qualified and experienced teachers. The program's foundation rests on two pillars -- the development of all-around personality and life skills. What are the majors available in the BA Liberal Arts course at Bennett University? The students can choose Majors from seven domains: Psychology, Political Science & IR, Economics, English, Business Studies, Philosophy & Sociology How is the BA Liberal Arts curriculum structured? In the first semester of the program, the students will be introduced to all the majors to select the Major which suits them the most. The choice of Majors and Electives is given from the second semester onwards. How do electives benefit a student's career? An elective is a combination of courses taken through the duration of university education. The courses taken as electives allow students to understand the world with exposure to a wide range of subjects and areas of practice. This broader understanding is a critical requirement to succeed in the world of the future. Can an undergraduate student take part in research activities? Bennett University focuses on research, experiential, and applied learning. Undergraduate students are encouraged to work with members of the faculty in their live research projects. Such exposure makes students understand the intricacies of the research process and deepens their knowledge Do you assist students in placements, summer internships, and industry tours? Bennett University has a dedicated Career Services Centre that works closely with the industry to participate in guest lectures, curricular inputs, joint program development, internships, and placements. What are the future career options after pursuing BA Liberal Arts? BA Liberal Arts graduates can look for rewarding careers in advertising, public relations, financial analysis, design, journalism, publishing, corporate communications, marketing, etc., or start a successful entrepreneurial journey of their own. What kind of industry exposure do students have access to? This is one of the unique hallmarks of the BA Liberal Arts program as it benefits from faculty members teaching courses in management, which in turn, provides students an opportunity to continuously interact with industry experts from India and abroad. Tech Artificial Intelligence The M. Tech Program at the School of Artificial Intelligence prepares a vibrant community of students and researchers passionate about leveraging artificial intelligence to revolutionize the world we live in. Through state-of-the-art facilities, dynamic research prospects, robust industry collaborations, advanced curriculum, and hands-on learning experiences, Bennett University empowers students with the knowledge, expertise, and ethical grounding essential for thriving in today’s dynamic landscape. As AI transforms industries worldwide, M. Tech Artificial Intelligence program at Bennett University opens doors to dynamic careers in tech, healthcare, finance, and more—making it a smart choice for a future-ready, high-impact profession. Duration 2 Years Specializations 4 Credits 80 Start Date July 2026 Admission Counsellors Connect with our counsellors for any admissions related queries. explore Fee Structure Know more about fee structure. explore Scholarship Know more about Scholarship. explore Program Objectives The M. Tech program in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning will help students learn about AI technologies and their usage in the real world. The program combines theoretical knowledge with hands-on experience, preparing graduates for impactful careers in various industries and research domains. The students will be well-equipped for careers in both industry and academia. They will have the flexibility to delve into various domains such as Defence, Fintech, Healthcare, Agriculture/Food Processing, Education, Retail/Customer Engagement, Human and Robot Interaction/Intelligent Automation, Smart City development, and Aid for Differently Abled/Accessibility Technology. Learning Outcomes Master the essentials of Artificial Intelligence, including Machine Learning, Inference Engines, Speech, Vision, Natural Language Understanding, Robotics, and Human-Computer Interaction. Integrate knowledge of human cognition, AI, Machine Learning, and data engineering to design comprehensive systems. Apply hands-on expertise with cutting-edge AI tools for practical problem-solving. Develop real-time and resilient AI-based systems tailored to specific software, hardware, and data requirements. Execute projects utilising intelligent cognitive solutions provided by AI algorithms, gaining insights into stakeholder management, risk modelling, intelligent resource scheduling, and managing project constraints through intelligent use of data models. Post-program Skills Design, develop, and deploy machine learning-based applications utilising both structured and unstructured data (e.g., speech, text, images/videos). Design and develop AI applications tailored for resource-constrained environments. Grasp fundamental concepts and gain hands-on knowledge about the state-of-the-art cutting-edge AI methodologies. Understand and evaluate the reliability, dependability, and trustworthiness of AI-based systems. Design and construct real-world AI systems for intricate planning, decision-making, and learning. Adhere to evolving ethics and privacy laws in diverse domains and territories. Plan and manage technical projects effectively. Curriculum Highlights Strong blend of theory and practice aligned with the need of industry and academia Research Focused (20 % research component in every AI course) on real world problems for enhanced employability Diverse Professional Specialization (Computer Vision, NLP, Robotics, & Signal Processing) Personalised mentorship by leading academia and industry experts Curriculum Details The M. Tech Artificial Intelligence curriculum is NEP compliant and industry oriented. The curriculum focuses on the state-of-the-art subjects, experiential learning, and code-based learning. NRI The eligibility criterion for all programs for international applicants is minimum 50% in the qualifying examination and having studied the pre-requisite subjects for admission in to the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on Aggregate Graduation Percentage. OUR ASK M.Tech Artificial Intelligence Frequently Asked Questions What does the M. Tech program in Artificial Intelligence offer? This program is designed for individuals seeking to explore artificial intelligence at an advanced level. It combines strong theoretical foundations with practical, hands-on learning. Whether you're interested in developing intelligent systems, pursuing cutting-edge research, or building a career in industry, the curriculum equips you with the skills and mindset needed to thrive in today’s AI-driven world. Who can apply for the M. Tech Artificial Intellgence program at Bennett University? Applicants must hold a B.E., B. Tech, or an equivalent degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Information Technology, Electronics, or related fields. A GATE score is not mandatory; however, a good score may strengthen your application, particularly for scholarships or merit-based admissions. Why should I consider the M. Tech in Artificial Intellgence program? Here’s what sets this program apart: Work with real datasets, tools, and technologies used in the AI industry. Learn from experienced faculty, including industry professionals and active researchers. Access support for internships, placements, and research collaborations. Engage in paper publishing, conference participation, and professional networking. What career options are available after completing M. Tech Artificial Intelligence Program? After completing the program, you may be equipped to take a wide range of roles such as: AI/ML Engineer Data Scientist Robotics or NLP Engineer AI Product Manager Researcher or Ph.D. Scholar AI is now a key part of many sectors — from healthcare and finance to space research and autonomous systems — offering graduates a wide range of opportunities. Is the M. Tech Artificial Intelligence program suitable for research or a Ph. D. path? Absolutely. The curriculum includes research components that prepare you for academic writing, methodology, and publishing. Many students go on to pursue Ph.D. programs in AI, robotics, or interdisciplinary areas, both in India and internationally. Are internships and placements part of the program? Yes, Bennett University offers strong placement and internship support. Students get access to live industry projects, research labs, and opportunities to collaborate with leading companies and institutions. What is the duration of the M. Tech Artificial Intelligence program, and how is it delivered? This is a two-year, full-time program. Teaching includes a blend of classroom lectures, lab sessions, projects, seminars, and research presentations. Emphasis is placed on applying concepts to solve real-world problems. Does the M. Tech Artificial Intelligence program offer international exposure? Yes, through collaborations with global universities and institutions, students can participate in exchange programs, joint research projects, and, in some cases, international internships. Is AI Ethics included in the M. Tech Artificial Intelligence curriculum? Yes, a dedicated course on AI Ethics is part of the curriculum, covering crucial topics like algorithmic bias, fairness, accountability, privacy, and responsible AI development — all of which are essential in today’s technology landscape. How do I Apply for the M. Tech Artificial Intelligence program? Applying to M. Tech Artificial Intelligence program at Bennett University is quick and easy. To apply for the admission, all you need is to share your details (takes less than a minute) and our team will get back you. BBA Global - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Global Pathways Program - BBA Global Study at University of Essex, UK Apply Now Global Pathways Program - BBA Global Study at University of Essex, UK Apply Now Global Pathways Program – BBA Global (2+1) BBA Global (2+1) is an exciting study abroad program at Bennett University that provides students with the opportunity to complete the initial years at Bennett University, India and transfer to a globally renowned institution – University of Essex, UK for final completion. Students complete two years at Bennett University and one year at the University of Essex, receiving globally recognized BBA Business Administration degree* from University of Essex, UK. Graduates receive a globally recognized degree under the Global Pathways Program (GPP), equipping them with international business acumen, leadership skills, and cross-cultural competencies. Program Details Name Duration University Curriculum Highlights BBA Global (2+1) 3 Years Years 1 & 2 at Bennett University , India Business Fundamentals, Finance, Marketing, HR, and Strategy Year 3 at University of Essex , UK Advanced Business Studies, International Business Strategy, Electives Degree Based on specialization: BBA Business Administration, B.Sc. Marketing, B.Sc. International Business and Entrepreneurship, B.Sc. International Business and Finance, B.Sc. Business Administration and Supply Chain Management, B.Sc. Management and Marketing, B.Sc. Business Management, B.Sc. Accounting, B.Sc. Accounting and Finance, B.Sc. Accounting with Economics, B.Sc. Accounting and Management, B.Sc. Finance, B.Sc. Banking and Finance, and B.Sc. Finance and Management Program Objectives To provide students with an international business education with real-world exposure. To develop critical thinking, leadership and entrepreneurship skills with global standards. To enhance career prospects in global business, finance, marketing and management. Global Academic Experience Students gain international exposure by studying at globally recognized universities. Seamless Credit Transfer Academic programs are carefully mapped to ensure a smooth transition between institutions. Cost-Effective Pathway Students save on overall tuition and living costs compared to a full-length international program. Internationally Recognized Degree Graduates earn a degree from a reputed partner university, enhancing their global career prospects. Cultural and Professional Growth Exposure to diverse academic, cultural, and professional environments enriches personal and career development. ABOUT Why Choose BBA Global (2+1) Access to globally recognized business degree from top university. Internship & networking opportunities in India and the UK. Cultural & academic diversity, enriching students’ global perspectives. A vibrant, student-centric environment with excellent support for international students By completing initial years in India, students can save up to INR 40-50 lakhs compared to completing a full degree abroad. Eligible for up to 2 years of post-study work permit. Partner University University of Essex, UK University of Essex, established in 1964, is a distinguished public research university located in Colchester, United Kingdom. Renowned for its commitment to pushing boundaries and fostering curiosity, Essex has built a reputation for academic excellence and innovative research. The university is recognized for research excellence in computer science, sociology, economics and econometrics, politics and international studies, modern languages and linguistics. JNU, Delhi Mr. Abhishek Saran Ph.D.(Pursuing) - IIM, Sambalpur Ms. Priya Mandleshwar Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIM, Indore Ms. Rimleena Boro Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIM, Indore ELIGIBILITY BBA Global (2+1) Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process Eligibility-Domestic & SAARC Domestic Domestic: Passed XII standard from any recognized Education Board with minimum 60% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Process Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 Subjects Score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score). Personal Interview will be conducted with the panel during the admission process. Note : Student must comply with the English language proficiency requirements (IELTS/ TOEFL as per partner university guidelines). OUR ASK BBA Global (2+1) Frequently Asked Questions What is the Global Pathways Programme at Bennett University? The Global Pathways Programme (GPP) is an academic collaboration that allows students to complete the first two years of their BBA Global program at Bennett University and transfer to University of Essex, UK, for the remaining one year. Upon successful completion, students earn a globally recognized degree - BBA Business Administration from University of Essex, UK. What are the benefits of enrolling in this program? The BBA Global Program at Bennett University comes with many benefits including internationally recognized degree from a top U.K. university, cost-efficiency compared to completing full graduation abroad, seamless credit transfer with a structured curriculum, global exposure & cultural diversity by studying in both India and the UK, and post-study work permit in the UK for up to two years. What is the eligibility criterion for the BBA Global Program? Students must have: Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with minimum 60% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. Student must comply with the English language proficiency requirements (IELTS/ TOEFL as per partner university guidelines). How does the 2+1 model in the BBA Global Pathways Program work? Years 1 & 2: : Study at Bennett University, India – Foundations in Business fundamentals, finance, marketing, HR, and strategy Years 3: Transfer to University of Essex, UK – Advanced business studies, international business strategy, electives. What degree will I receive after completing the program? You will be awarded a globally recognized BBA Business Administration degree from University of Essex, UK. How much cost savings can I expect? Yes, Iowa State University offers scholarships based on CGPA: By following the 2+1 model, students save approximately ₹40-50 lakhs, compared to completing all three years at University of Essex, U.K. What career opportunities are available after this program? Opportunities in global companies such as Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Facebook etc. Eligibility for a U.K. post-study work permit for up to 1-2 years. Research & internship opportunities at University of Essex, UK. How do I apply for the BBA Global (2+1) program? Interested students can apply through Bennett University’s admissions portal Click here to apply. https://applications.bennett.edu.in/bu or contact. M. Tech (CSE) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) program - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login M. Tech (Computer Science & Engineering) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) Apply Now Why M. Tech (Computer Science & Engineering) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) at Bennett University Industry-oriented specialisations Four industry-oriented specialisations Artificial Intelligence Blockchain Cyber Security Data Science State-of-the-art Research Students participate in research projects for hands-on practical experience Industry-Academia collaboration 10+ industry tie-up with leading IT industries enables students to learn innovative techniques to become future ready. Get customised Degree Students join MTech + PhD 4 year program. ABOUT About M. Tech (Computer Science & Engineering) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) M. Tech (Computer Science & Engineering) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) is a five-year degree in computer science engineering that enable students to understand the principles and practices of science and technology, attain problem-solving skills, and constantly learn multidisciplinary concepts. At Bennett University, students are benefited from the combination of a high academic standard and cutting-edge technology. The most distinctive feature of this programme, which comes in partnership with leading global universities and industries, is to provide hands-on experience to the students. The 4 year integrated Programme is challenging, and it equips graduates for jobs in the industry. In addition, Bennett also provides a strong foundation for further studies at the doctoral level anywhere in the world. Duration 5 years Specialisation 4 Credits 80 Start Date July 2026 Admission Counsellors Connect with our counsellors for any admissions related queries. explore Fee Structure Know more about fee structure. explore Scholarship Know more about Scholarship. explore Curriculum Details School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology has developed a comprehensive curriculum for the M. Tech (Computer Science & Engineering) + Ph.D. (Dual Degree) programmes that equips the students with skills on complex problem solving, design of computing systems, critical thinking, empirical and quantitative analysis, communication, teamwork, social & personal responsibility. Program Core Courses: The core courses build a solid foundation of advanced concepts on data structures, algorithms, network systems, operating systems, software engineering, databases and their practical skills that are essential for technology building and implementation. Life Skills: Life skills is offered in the 1st semester to cultivate the lifelong learning skills. Humanity: It consists of research practices, seminar, and ethics course. Entrepreneurship: A course on entrepreneurship is offered to inculcate the entrepreneurial skills amongst the students Specialisations Every Specialisation comprises two core courses and three elective courses. It also has a specialisation-oriented capstone Course and an Internship. Specialisations are chosen at the time of admissions. Specialisation Core Courses: Students are offered two specialisation core courses. Specialisation Electives: Students must choose three specialisation electives. Artificial Intelligence The CEOs and CXOs of NVIDIA, Aindra, Here Tech, Amazon, and other academicians have contributed to the curriculum design of the specialisation. The specialisation electives offered under AI are Cognitive Modelling, Advanced Computer Vision and Video Analytics, Reinforcement Learning, Image and Video Processing, and Natural Language Processing. These courses match the best of international standards in AI specialisation. The other high point is the arrangement the University has reached with NVIDIA, SAP, Hitachi and Times Internet to enable students to work on cutting-edge projects. Blockchain The curriculum for Blockchain specialisation has been developed in consultation with CEOs and CXOs of BankchainAsset.com, Digiledge, besides other subject matter experts. The specialisation electives offered as part of the B.Tech syllabus include Blockchain Technologies, Smart Contracts and Solidity Programming, Digital Currencies and Blockchain, Modern Cryptography, among others. Cyber Security With increased dependency on online transactions and other social activities, there is now high demand for a robust Cyber Security framework. Keeping that in mind, the Cyber Security curriculum has been designed by the CEOs and CXOs of the Data Security Council of India (DSCI), QoS Technologies, IBM, and other researchers. The courseware includes topics on Cyber Security with Blockchain, Malware Analysis for Mobile Devices, Security and Privacy for Big Data Analytics, Penetration Testing and Ethical Hacking, among others. Also, to have students work on cutting-edge projects, the University has tied-up with DSCI, Fortinet, CompTIA and CISCO. Data Science Data scientists play a key role in the working of smart cities, automation industry, BFSI and Fintech. Given the importance of Data Science, Bennett University has put together a curriculum with the help of CEOs and CXOs of Microsoft, Google, TCS, and other researchers. The curriculum includes elective courses on Structural Equation Modelling; Security and Privacy for Big Data Analytics; Data Visualisation; Big Data Analytics and Business Intelligence; and Satellite Data Analytics. Electives General Elective: Students are offered one general elective, the purpose of which is to give them the flexibility to opt for a course of other specialisations. Honours An optional Honours is a pack of 4 courses that provides additional knowledge of the same or other CSE specialisations. Honours offer 12 additional credits to students. Projects Project Based Learning (PBL) (6 credits)–: In the 1st and 2nd semesters, the students must take up a specialisation and an Industrial Project or R&D project. Internships Internships/Thesis Dissertation (20 Credits)– Two internships are part of the curriculum. The first internship is available in the 3rd semester, which can be an alternative of the capstone project. Capstone Design, and Capstone Project are cutting edge courses to prepare the students for industry, and further research. The second internship is done in the final semester, the outcome of which is a thesis dissertation report. There is also one flexible option for the students to spend one full year in an Industry internship in the final year of study. Program Outcome Students are expected to apply mathematical foundations, algorithmic principles and computer science theory in modelling, design and conduct of experiments as well as in data interpretation and analysis. Students are expected to develop an ability to identify, formulate and solve real engineering problems and understand the global impact of engineering solutions. Students will acquire the ability to engineer requirement/system to carry out systems integration. Students will acquire the ability to explore the latest technology and its applications. Students will build the capability to deploy appropriate technology paradigm for given tasks. Students will acquire the ability to build convincing technical cases for approval / demonstration of initial ideas by conducting experiments and forming examples. Skills Skills Students will Learn: Some of the advanced skills CSE students will acquire: Foundational Skills in Core areas of Computing Specialised Skills in your chosen area of specialization Project development and Coding Skills Strong sense of professionalism Leadership Soft skill Ability to negotiate a group discussions Public speaking The program will have: Personal interview workshops CV and cover letter workshops Networking events Social-media workshops Corporate & professional mentoring sessions for the benefit of students through our career cell. Labs Dell-EMC – Lab The lab has 32 Dell machines with the latest configuration. This is further enriched with the support of Dell-EMC. (Dell supports in faculty training, collaborative projects, and exclusive workshops.) Specific areas of operation include Information Storage and Management (“ISM”), Cloud Infrastructure Services (“CIS”), Data Science and Big Data Analytics (“DSBDA”) and Data Protection and Management (“DPM”). These help the students compete for jobs in Fortune 500 companies. (Computer configuration: Dell OptiPlex 5050MT XCTO, Intel i5 processor, 8Gb DDR4 RAM.) Bennett - NVIDIA Centre for Artificial Intelligence The lab comprises 30 node top-end HP Z440 workstations. It is the best configuration for executing applications while dealing with zeta scale data. Powerful graphics cards from NVIDIA and lightening-fast data transfers with thunderbolt technologies make for a beast of a system. Researchers and scholars use this lab for AI applications in healthcare, security, cognition, text, image, and video analysis. The NVIDIA DGX-1 V100 is the first system built with groundbreaking NVIDIA Pascal™-powered Tesla® V100 accelerators. It is NVIDIA NVLink™ implementation that delivers a massive increase in GPU memory capacity. It is the world’s first purpose-built system for deep learning and accelerated analytics, delivering performance equal to 500 conventional servers. NVIDIA DGX-1 software stack includes major deep learning frameworks, the NVIDIA DIGITS™ GPU training system, the NVIDIA Deep Learning SDK (e.g. CuDNN, NCCL), NVIDIA Docker, GPU drivers, and NVIDIA CUDA® for rapidly designing deep neural networks (DNN). It is the ideal stack for accelerating popular analytics and visualisation software. The powerful system includes access to cloud management services for container creation and deployment, system updates, and an application repository. The combination of these software capabilities running on Pascal-powered Tesla GPUs allows applications to run 12X faster than with previous GPU-accelerated solutions. Due to the partnership with NVIDIA, students have access to Certified Expert Instructors, Worldwide Workshops, Online Courses, Industry leaders, Onsite Training, NVIDIA Partner Network, OEMs and start-ups and Deep Learning Teaching Kit. Apple iMac Design & Multi-Media Lab It is a futuristic 32 node high-end lab with state-of-the-art Apple machines. As per international standards, the 27-inch monitors, using the best retina display, are useful for any visual effect with immersive experience. Processor Speed, Magic Mouse and Magic keyboard make the experience unforgettable. The lab is used for running courses that require high-end graphics capabilities including 3D graphics and video editing capabilities. It is specifically used for activities and projects related to gaming, design, multimedia, modern art, and media production. High configuration of the machines help run resource-hungry applications related to research requiring large scale simulations and experimentation. Programming Lab The Lab is used to impart skills of Programming/Coding to first-year students. Computer configuration: HP ProDesk Micro tower PC, Inter®core™ 9500 processor,8Gb DDR4 RAM. Information Management System Lab The Lab takes care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Information Management, Data Base Management systems and Other Business processing applications. Computer configuration: Acer, Intel®core™ i5 6400,64 Bit processor,8Gb RAM. Cloud Computing Lab The Lab takes care of the curricular requirements for Cloud Computing and applications. Drone Labs The Drone Laboratory facilitates the development of various drone technologies and sensing equipment which could be applied to the drones using DJI Mavic AIR QUADCOPTER DRONE, Veerobot F450 AIR QUADCOPTER DRONE and such other. The laboratory is equipped with various drone platforms: from quad/hexacopters, to pipeline crawlers, to remotely operated underwater vehicles. Computer Network and Cyber Security Lab The Lab takes care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Computer Network and Cyber Security Lab on Simulator Tools, including hand on experience with Routers and latest CISCO Switches. Students use different wireless network components and features to provide network security and to set Firewall Security in windows/ Linux OS. AR and VR Gaming The Lab takes care of the curricular requirements in the domain of AR and VR Gaming Labs. Our Virtual Reality, Augmented Reality, and Media Lab contain some of the most cutting-edge head-mounted displays available today. Students have access to the technology for the development of VR and AR software using the Oculus Go Standalone Virtual Reality Headset, and many others. Big Data and Machine Learning Lab The Lab takes care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Big Data Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence to analyse large datasets, identify trends, and develop predictive models to solve problems. Students build and train deep neural networks, build a CNN and apply it to detection and recognition tasks, train test sets, analyse variance for DL applications, use standard techniques and optimisation algorithms, and build neural networks in TensorFlow. Students also build and train RNNs, work with NLP and Word Embeddings, and use HuggingFace tokenisers and transformer models to perform NER. BU PROUD 450+ Companies for Placements 1.37 Cr/Yr. MBA (Media)| Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login MBA Media Management Apply Now MBA Media Management Apply Now About MBA Media Media Management is an interdisciplinary MBA specialisation that integrates media studies, business management, marketing, and digital technologies. It prepares students to lead media organisations, digital platforms, and content-driven enterprises in a highly competitive environment. The program builds strong creative, analytical, and managerial capabilities, with focused training in digital marketing, branding, public relations, media planning, and strategy. Students also develop leadership, communication, and project management skills. In the context of rapid digital transformation, the specialisation enables learners to address industry challenges, leverage emerging technologies, and create innovative, ethical, and audience-centric media solutions. Duration 2 Years Specialisations - Credits - Start Date Aug/Sep. 2026 (Tentative) Curriculum Highlights The curriculum offers a balanced mix of strategy, creativity, and technology. It focuses on key domains such as media marketing and distribution, film and entertainment business, event marketing and branding, and media planning and buying. Students gain expertise in: Designing targeted media marketing strategies. Understanding film production, distribution, and monetisation. Building brand experiences for events and entertainment sectors. Developing media plans backed by research, analytics, and audience insights. Managing cross-platform content distribution and promotional strategies. Electives Offered Media Marketing and Distribution Films and the Entertainment Industry Marketing and Branding in the Event Industry Media Planning and Buying Hands-On Experiences Short film and documentary projects Social media campaign competitions Studio production and editing sessions Field visits to media houses Participation in university media festivals Why an MBA (Media) from Bennett University Media Management graduates are in high demand as organisations across industries increasingly depend on digital media, content-driven engagement, and strategic communication. The rise of social media, OTT platforms, digital advertising, and influencer ecosystems has created exciting opportunities for skilled media professionals. Students specialising in Media Management at Bennett University benefit from: Excellent employability prospects in rapidly growing media and communication sectors. Diverse career pathways across media houses, advertising agencies, film/OTT industries, digital marketing firms, and corporate communication departments. Strong academic and industry mentorship from faculty with experience at globally reputed organisations and universities such as BBC, CNN, Cardiff University, University of Colorado Boulder, and MICA Ahmedabad. A curriculum designed for future-ready careers in digital platforms, entertainment, branding, communication strategy, and media technology. Program Objectives The program builds a strong understanding of media industries by covering media economics, policy, regulation, audience behaviour, and digital transformation, while developing essential management, leadership, marketing, financial, and strategic planning skills needed to operate media organisations effectively. It enables students to create, manage, and evaluate multimedia content and production workflows across both digital and traditional platforms, and trains them to use digital tools, analytics, and platform strategies to enhance audience engagement, content monetisation, and digital marketing. Additionally, the program promotes ethical, legal, and research-based approaches, nurturing professionalism, critical thinking, and entrepreneurial capabilities. Learning Outcomes Students pursuing an MBA in Media Management learn to: Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of media industries, including their structure, economics, regulation, and audience trends, while applying management, leadership, and strategic planning principles to real-world media projects and organisations. Create, manage, and assess multimedia content across platforms using appropriate media technologies and efficient production workflows. Career Pathways MBA Media Management graduates are prepared for specialist and managerial roles in Media Management, with opportunities across Media houses (TV, radio, print) Digital Marketing Agencies Film and Production Companies OTT platforms (Netflix, Prime, etc.) Advertising Agencies Public Relations Firms Corporate Communication Departments E-commerce Companies Startups and Tech firms Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Passed Graduation in any discipline from a recognised university with a minimum 50% marks with English as the medium of instruction. NRI: For international applicants, a minimum of 50% in the qualifying examination is required, along with having studied the prerequisite subjects for admission to the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on the weighted score of CAT / XAT / NMAT / MAT / CMAT / GMAT / BU MAT/ CUET Percentile score & Graduation & Personal Interview. MBA Program Fee Get detailed information about the MBA (Media) program fee structure, including tuition, hostel fee, and other applicable charges. Click here for details. An MBA (Media) Management combines business management with media and communication studies. It focuses on managing media organisations, digital platforms, content production, marketing, branding, and audience engagement in today’s fast-changing media industry. Who should choose this specialisation? This specialisation is ideal for students interested in media, entertainment, advertising, digital marketing, branding, film, OTT platforms, and communication strategies. It suits those who want both creative exposure and strong management skills. What skills will I gain from the MBA (Media) program? Students develop skills in media planning, digital marketing, content management, branding, public relations, leadership, project management, and audience analysis. The course also builds strategic thinking and communication abilities needed in media-driven organisations. What kind of job opportunities are available after the MBA (Media) specialisation? Graduates can work in media houses, digital marketing agencies, advertising agencies, OTT platforms, film and production companies, PR firms, corporate communication teams, e-commerce companies, startups, and tech firms. What are some emerging career roles in Media Management? Emerging roles include digital marketing strategist, content strategist, social media manager, media planner and buyer, OTT platform analyst, event branding specialist, influencer marketing coordinator, programmatic advertising specialist, and virtual or hybrid event producer. How does the MBA (Media) program improve employability? The program focuses on practical learning through live projects, case studies, simulations, internships, and a capstone project. Combined with strong faculty experience and industry relevance, it prepares students for real-world media challenges and long-term career growth. Master of Design (M.Des) in Communication Design Course, Eligibility, Fee, Curriculum | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login M.Des (Communication Design) Apply Now M.Des (Communication Design) Apply Now About M.Des (Communication Design) The Master of Design (M.Des) in Communication Design at Bennett University is a 2-year postgraduate program designed to empower graduates from diverse backgrounds, including Science, Arts, Commerce, and Management, to become future-ready communication designers. The program blends visual storytelling, branding, UI/UX, motion design, and filmmaking into an industry-aligned learning path. Through immersive studios, collaborative projects, and internships, students will build a versatile portfolio and gain hands-on experience in solving real-world communication challenges. The program fosters creative thinking, digital fluency, and professional readiness, equipping students to lead in dynamic creative industries. Duration 2 Years Specialisations 3 Credits 82 Start Date Aug./Sep. 2026 (Tentative) Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Passed Graduation in any discipline from recognised university with minimum 50% marks with English as medium of instruction. NRI: The eligibility criterion for all programs for international applicants is minimum 50% in the qualifying examination and having studied the pre-requisite subjects for admission into the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on NID/NIFT/CEED/BUDAT Score & Personal Interview. M.Des (Communication Design) Program Fee Get detailed information about the M.Des Communication Design program fee structure, including tuition, hostel fee, and other applicable charges. Click here for details. Why M.Des (Communication Design) from Bennett University The program is open to graduates from non-design backgrounds. Emphasis on portfolio-focused learning with strong industry engagement. Integration of cutting-edge tools such as Adobe Creative Suite, Figma, After Effects, Blender, and emerging AI/3D tools Focuses on experiential learning with practical exposure through Internship and Capstone Project in the final semester. Studio-based culture with cross-disciplinary collaboration and live mentoring Specialisations Visual Branding: Master the art and strategy behind identity creation, packaging, campaigns, and cross-platform brand storytelling. UI/UX Design: Learn to craft seamless user experiences, wireframes, and prototypes across web and mobile platforms. Motion Design: Understand animation principles, motion storytelling, and kinetic typography to create impactful narratives for brand and social media storytelling. Program Objectives The program aims to nurture creative professionals capable of designing meaningful communication experiences across media. It focuses on developing a balance of conceptual thinking, visual storytelling, and technical proficiency. Students learn to translate ideas into effective brand, digital, and motion solutions while gaining strategic, ethical, and collaborative skills for real-world design practice. Learning Outcomes Students will apply design thinking and storytelling principles to develop impactful communication across print, digital, and motion media. They will create user-centered interfaces and brand experiences using contemporary tools such as Adobe Suite, Figma, and AI-driven platforms, while analysing audience behaviour, culture, and trends to inform creative and strategic design decisions. By integrating interdisciplinary knowledge from branding, UX, and motion design, students will craft coherent multi-platform narratives and demonstrate professional readiness through a strong design portfolio, collaboration, and ethical practice. Curriculum Details Experiential and studio-based learning Industry-embedded live projects and mentorship Cross-media specialisation across brand, UX, and motion AI, AR/VR, and future-focused electives Internship and Capstone-led outcomes Career Pathways Graduates of the M.Des (Communication Design) program are equipped for diverse roles across the design and creative industries. They can pursue careers as Graphic Designers, UI/UX Designers, Motion Graphics Artists, Creative Strategists, Visual Storytellers, Filmmakers or Editors, Design Researchers, Art Directors, and Digital Product Designers. The program also nurtures entrepreneurial skills, enabling graduates to establish their own ventures in the field of communication design. Faculty Prof. (Dr.) Manisha Mohan Animation, UI UX and Game Design Dr. Mukesh Singh Painting, Sculpture and Foundation Studies Ms. Anu Jain Fashion Design Mr. Wajahat Rather Fashion and Textile Design Ms. Snigdha Banerjee Animation and Game Design Dr. Om Singh Communication Design OUR ASK M.Des (Communication Design) Program Frequently Asked Questions Who can apply for this program? Any graduate from any stream (Arts, Science, Commerce, Management) can apply. No prior design experience is needed. Do I need a design portfolio to apply for Master in Design Communication course at Bennett University? No, we teach design from the ground up. Your curiosity and interest matter most. Will I get hands-on software training? Yes, you will work with Adobe Creative Suite, Figma, After Effects, and emerging AI/3D tools. Are there internships or industry projects? Yes, an 8 to10 weeks industry internship and live project exposure is included in the final year. What are the job prospects after graduation? The M.Des Communication Design graduates work in UI/UX, advertising, branding, motion graphics, tech design, and more. What specialisation can I pursue in Communication Design? You can specialise in Visual Branding, UI/UX Design, or Motion Design. Is placement assistance provided at Bennett University’s School of Design? Yes, through portfolio reviews, mentorship, mock interviews, and industry connects. Are scholarships or financial aid available? Merit-based scholarships are available. Please visit the admissions office for details. Can I pursue Ph.D. or further studies after M.Des? Yes, this is a UGC-recognised postgraduate program, eligible for higher education. Is the degree accepted globally? Yes, M.Des is recognised by UGC and accepted in most global design institutions for PhD. MBA (Marketing) | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login MBA Marketing Apply Now MBA Marketing Apply Now About MBA Marketing The MBA specialisation in Marketing equips the learners with a strong understanding of consumer behaviour, brand strategies, traditional and digital marketing ecosystems, and data-driven decision-making through analytics. It blends persuasive communication with AI-enabled frameworks to prepare graduates for dynamic business environments. Through practical learning, students develop skills in creating cost-effective, solution-oriented strategies and delivering superior multi-channel user experiences. The future-ready curriculum ensures industry readiness for roles in product management, branding, digital marketing, and other strategic functions across sectors such as FMCG, retail, e-commerce, and beyond. Duration 2 Years Specialisations NA Credits - Start Date July 2026 Curriculum Highlights The Marketing specialisation is thoughtfully curated with courses that are highly in-demand from an employment perspective. The courses help learners thoroughly understand the marketing ecosystem and help them resolve the challenges of today's dynamic business environment. The learners get to study a mix of core and general electives, domain-specific electives, and open electives. Core Courses Marketing Management Digital Marketing & Media Management Electives Consumer Behaviour Sales & Distribution Management Integrated Marketing Communications Marketing Analytics Strategic Brand Management Marketing of Services Social Media Marketing Sustainable Marketing Industrial Marketing Management Retail Management Why MBA (Marketing) from Bennett University Extensive industry focus and alignment with in-demand industries such as FMCG, retail, e-commerce, fintech, media, and other digital trends. Rich experiential learning with the help of live industry projects, Harvard and other highly sought-after engaging simulations, marketing competitions and bootcamps, and workshops. Hands-on engagements through modern analytical tools such as Google Analytics, HubSpot, Canva, CRM systems, and other contemporary analytics platforms. One-to-one sessions and mentorship from highly experienced faculty Ever-in-demand career opportunities across various sales and marketing roles worldwide. Program Objectives The MBA in Marketing program aims to build strong conceptual and practical marketing skills, enhance analytical capabilities using research-driven digital and analytical tools, build employable skills for practical business problem-solving and multichannel communication, equip students with integrated strategy design capabilities, and prepare them to become problem-solving leaders capable of navigating multicultural markets and driving sustainable business growth. Learning Outcomes By the end of MBA in Marketing, student will learn to: Apply marketing frameworks to address uncertain and dynamic markets Design effective and long-lasting communication strategies Leverage modern technologies and decision-making tools Conduct problem-specific market research to understand consumer insights Lead key marketing functions or verticals toward organisational objectives Career Pathways MBA Marketing graduates are prepared for specialist and managerial roles across FMCG, retail and e-commerce, banking and NBFCs, IT/ITES, consulting, media, telecom, EdTech, hospitality, and other consumer-driven and digital-first industries. Brand Manager Digital and Social Media Marketing Manager Content Strategist Product Manager SEO/SEM Specialist Sales Manager Business Development Manager Customer Experience Specialist Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Passed Graduation in any discipline from a recognised university with a minimum 50% marks with English as the medium of instruction. NRI: For international applicants, a minimum of 50% in the qualifying examination is required, along with having studied the prerequisite subjects for admission to the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on the weighted score of CAT / XAT / NMAT / MAT / CMAT / GMAT / BU MAT/ CUET Percentile score & Graduation & Personal Interview. MBA (Marketing) Program Fee Get detailed information about the MBA (Marketing) program fee structure, including tuition, hostel fees, and other applicable charges. Click here for details.
