FMS, University of Delhi Dr. Ratan Kumar FPM - Management Development Institute, Gurgaon Mr. Krishan Ph.D. (Pursuing) - JNU, Delhi Dr. Vijay Prakash Mishra Ph.D. - Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, Pune Mr. Abhishek Saran Ph.D.(Pursuing) - IIM, Sambalpur Ms. Priya Mandleshwar Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIM, Indore Ms. Rimleena Boro Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIM, Indore OUR ASK MBA in Marketing Frequently Asked Questions What unique practical exposure does the Marketing specialisation in MBA offers? The Marketing specialisation is equipped with real-time problem-based projects, distinguished guest lectures, some of the highly sought-after Harvard, CESIM simulations based on real-world business environments, and try-on activities with new age marketing and analytical tools to impart a complete theory to practical experience. Are internships tailored specifically for Marketing students? Yes, along with the mandatory 16-week internship, MBA (Marketing) students are supported for specialised roles related to brand management, digital marketing and analytics, market research, and sales strategy. What core competencies will I gain through an MBA in Marketing Management specialisation? MBA Marketing students will build competencies related to consumer behaviour understanding and dealing strategies, designing competitive marketing strategies, managing market offerings, and applying necessary analytics in order to support business decisions. The Marketing specialisation also helps in developing skills like digital and social media marketing, marketing research, and strategy formulation, supporting students in applying learned marketing concepts effectively. Does the Marketing Management specialisation include training in digital tools? Certainly, the MBA specialisation brings detailed exposure to specific new-age digital marketing and analytics tools as part of assignments and course activities, particularly related to digital marketing, social media marketing, customer relationship management and web analytics (including social media), enabling them to understand how digital tools support precise decision making. Can Marketing specialisation students participate in international exchange opportunities? Yes, all management students, including those specialising in MBA (Marketing), can participate in study abroad and international immersion programs with partner universities. How does the MBA (Marketing) specialisation support placements? The MBA specialisation (along with other specialisations) closely coordinates with the University’s Career Services team through associated institutional program to prepare students for desired marketing roles, supported by skill-based courses, internships, and industry exposure. Employment support is provided through campus recruitment drives. In addition, multiple corporate interactions and career counselling help students pursue competitive opportunities across marketing, sales, research, and other related interdisciplinary business functions. Are there elective courses specific to the MBA (Marketing) beyond core subjects? Yes, the Marketing specialisation offers electives in high employment-oriented areas like retail, services, advertising, analytics, and customer experience to enable students to customise their learning path as per their interests. What types of projects do Marketing students undertake? Marketing students of MBA undertake a wide range of course-specific projects concerning market research, competition and consumer analysis, campaign planning and management, case and simulation-based assignments based on contemporary business problems, both at the individual and group level. These projects are formulated to boost conceptual understanding while providing practical exposure to decision-making and evaluation in real organisational contexts. How does the MBA in Marketing embrace modern trends like digital and data-driven marketing? This specialisation, through various in-class and assignment-based activities, integrates modern frameworks related to digital transformation, Business to Business, Artificial Intelligence adoption (including AR, VR, and Metaverse), along with contemporary topics such as marketing analytics, influencer marketing, and omni-channel marketing practices. What career support is available for Marketing specialisation students? MBA (Marketing) students receive multifaceted mentorship from the domain experts for guided resume building, preparation for interviews, an opportunity to attend industry events for networking, along with specific hiring opportunities. Students gain essential tools for effective decision-making in navigating industry characteristics, strategic analysis, and utilizing frameworks like Porter's Five Forces, SWOT, and Pestle. This course elevates marketing acumen by deciphering trends through databases like CMIE, EBSCO, etc. Also designed to impart the capability of unraveling industry complexities, providing a strategic edge in solving business dilemmas. Know yourself Know Yourself course is the first step towards self-discovery and unlocking one's potential. Students will gain insights into the concepts and consequences of self-development, by learning to analyze, develop, and implement self-development techniques. This course empowers students to fortify resilience against external judgments, enhancing productivity, and fostering self-motivation in their daily lives. Decoding the Times The course "Decoding the Times” provides students with the necessary skills and knowledge to understand and analyse how the economy and current happenings impact our lives and our workplaces. Through a combination of theory and practical exercises, students develop the ability to analyse and interpret economic news articles, financial reports, and other relevant information found in economic e- newspapers. The course focuses on enhancing critical thinking and encouraging students to apply their learning to real-world scenarios and economic events. Through this course, students also understand how the economy, politics, environment, health, and education are deeply linked and interconnected. MBA Curriculum 2022-24 MBA Curriculum 2021-23 MBA Curriculum 2020-22 MBA Curriculum 2019-21 MBA Curriculum 2018-20 Specialisations The best MBA programs are essential in today’s dynamic business ecosystem. Bennett University offers the most contemporary MBA program for aspiring business leaders and global management professionals. The program is carefully curated with industry-relevant specialisations to help students thrive and grow in today’s interconnected world. Marketing It aims to develop in-depth knowledge about consumer behaviour, segmenting consumer and business markets, pricing, advertising, brand management, sales promotion, public relations, integrated marketing communications, application of marketing mix and growth strategy. Business Analytics This specialisation provides the knowledge needed to develop and inculcate descriptive, prescriptive, and predictive business modelling skills through the latest tools/techniques and software for analysing and solving problems, improving business, and implementing changes. Finance It aims to develop an in-depth understanding of the framework and concepts of financial management. The specialisation lays a strong emphasis on building problem-solving skills by applying financial analysis tools and techniques to decision-making. Human Resource Managing workforce is related to leveraging human capital for growth. It needs strategic decision-making skills and handling myriad challenging situations. Therefore, trained professionals are in demand to handle the process seamlessly. This specialisation focuses on recruitment, management and, showing the right path to employees. Media This specialisation focuses on providing the practical skills to plan and run an efficient media organisation. The curriculum is designed to make students understand the realities of the different media markets and challenges in securing sufficient financial means to operate independently and produce content of high professional standards Logistics & Supply Chain Management The curriculum has the capability to create domain expert in Logistics & Supply Chain Management (LSCM) sector. The LSCM specific subjects like supply chain management, logistics strategy & planning makes more sector oriented Production & Operations Content will be updated soon. Healthcare Content will be updated soon. Sports Content will be updated soon. Labs / Software As part of the curriculum, students are trained on the latest analytics software/tools to make them understand tracking and analysing user behaviour, developing appropriate models to help decision- making process and solving business problems very efficiently. Software for Analytics and Modelling : R, Python, Tableau, IBM SPSS & AMOS, MS Project, Analytic Solver Optimization + Simulation Business Simulation Simulations by Harvard Business Publishing Education, AIMA BIZLAB Communication Lab Cambridge Empower, Cambridge Impact Live projects Short-term value-added projects assigned by companies to students, shortlisted through a proper selection procedure Students undertaking the project work under direct supervision of industry mentor International Immersion Program Global Immersion programs are designed to provide a high-level understanding of the economic, cultural, and geopolitical drivers behind regions integral to the global economy. Global Immersion: Participation in an immersion program of 10 to 15 days in one premier University Study Abroad Program: Opportunity to study two credit-based courses over one to two months with a partner international university Additional Certification Programs in line with specialisations As part of the curriculum, students are provided with option to pursue various certifications. All certifications are optional, except the Cambridge university press certification. All the certificates mentioned are on payment basis. Certification Program in 1st Year Post Graduate Programme in Digital Marketing from Purdue University, co-created with Facebook and Harvard Publishing. Successful completion leads to membership in the Purdue Alumni Association Post Graduate Programme in Business Analysis by Purdue University, co-created with IBM. Successful completion leads to membership in the Purdue Alumni Association Certification Program in 2nd Year Media Management Post Graduate Program in Project Management from University of Massachusetts Amherst co-powered by Harvard Publishing Advanced Social Media Marketing Certificate, suitable for marketing managers, digital marketing executives, content writers, marketing and sales professionals and entrepreneurs Finance NSE Certification in Online Equity Research Analysis provides an understanding of capital market & Industry Analysis Banking and Financial Markets: A Risk Management Perspective by IIM Bangalore leading to Foundation in Financial Risk (FFR) certification (IIMBX) Business Analytics Data Visualisation Program leads to Tableau Certification. This certification leads to gain expertise in the field of analytics, as data analysts, data scientists, BI and reporting professionals, and project managers Post Graduate Program in Lean Six Sigma by Isenberg School of Management, University of Massachusetts Amherst Sales & Marketing Management Advanced Social Media Marketing Certificate, suitable for marketing managers, digital marketing executives, content writers, marketing and sales professionals and entrepreneurs Advanced Mobile Marketing Certificate suitable to build the skills required for mobile marketing including campaign management, app marketing, mobile advertising, content marketing, location-based marketing, responsive website designs, and mobile marketing analytics Industry Certifications Grant Thornton Bharat Campus Adoption Program, to bridge the skill gap between students and the industry Six Sigma Green Belt by KPMG Cambridge University Press and Assessment India (Mandatory Certification) Cambridge University Press offers the 'Empower' module-This module offers a variety of activities and resources to enhance students' communication and language skills. Language workshops focusing on English language skills, including listening, speaking, reading and writing. MBA Program Outcomes Upon completion of the MBA programme a student shall be able to: PO1 : Integration: Integrate functional knowledge and apply managerial skills in changing business environment PO2 : Problem Solving: Integrate theories and practice for analysing and solving problems of business, society and environment PO3 : Ethics: Take ethical decisions and effectively communicate to all stakeholders PO4 : Critical Thinking: Develop sustainable competitive edge through strategic planning, critical thinking and innovation PO5 : Leadership: Demonstrate leadership skills to build high-performing teams Bennett Finishing School Initiatives in the following three key domains Skill Assessment (Technical & Aptitude Assessments) Career Readiness Training (Power Skills, Domain Skills & Life Skills) Career Guidance (Trends in Technology, Industry Insights & Alumni Mentoring) Personality Development/Outbound Training Skill Assessment (Technical & Aptitude Assessments) Career Readiness Training (Power Skills, Domain Skills & Life Skills) Career Guidance (Trends in Technology, Industry Insights & Alumni Mentoring) Resume Writing Sessions Employability Enhancement Training Life Skills Training Sessions Outbound Training (during the orientation programme) BU PROUD Placements Intellectual rigor in academics, intense industry exposure, and imparting Life Skills with a structured approach enhance our students' abilities, making them truly industry-ready. Peehu Singhal MBA (2019-2021) OUR ASK MBA Frequently Asked Questions About the Program Why should I choose Bennett University for an MBA Program? Bennett University, powered by The Times Group, offers one of the best MBA programs in India, blending academic excellence with strong industry partnerships. The program emphasises experiential learning, leadership development, and global business exposure through live corporate projects, expert mentorship, and a vibrant campus ecosystem that nurtures future-ready leaders. What is the duration of the MBA course at Bennett University? The MBA program is a two-year, full-time course structured over four semesters. It combines academic learning with real-world exposure through internships, global immersion, and industry-integrated modules. What makes the MBA program at Bennett University unique? The MBA program at Bennett University stands out for its globally aligned curriculum, 90% Ph.D. -qualified faculty, CXO lecture series, and collaborations with global institutions like Purdue University (USA). Students also benefit from tools like Harvard’s Markstrat simulation and AIMA BizLab for experiential learning. What specialisations are offered in the MBA program? In its MBA program, Bennett University offers cutting-edge specialisations in Marketing, Business Analytics, Finance, Human Resource, Media, Logistics & Supply Chain Management, Production & Operations, Healthcare Management, and Sports Management. Each specialisation is industry-aligned and supported by global certifications. What skills does the MBA program develop? The MBA program at Bennett University helps students with strong communication, decision-making, strategic thinking, problem-solving, quantitative analysis, leadership, entrepreneurship, and behavioural skills. The program also emphasises situational awareness, execution, and personality development to create confident business leaders. What teaching methods are used in the MBA program? The MBA follows an experiential learning pedagogy combining case studies, live projects, capstone assignments, Harvard-developed simulations, and leadership labs. This approach builds analytical, managerial, and problem-solving capabilities. How is the MBA curriculum designed and updated? The curriculum is co-designed with The Economic Times, Global Business Summit , Times Now Summit, and international partners. Regular reviews ensure alignment with current industry trends, digital transformation, and global business standards. What is unique about MBA Program at Bennett University? Bennett University's MBA course is designed to bring together all the strengths of the Times Group. Right from the industry leaders who share their experiences in the classroom to the best of full time and visiting teachers from the industry. The group also supports placement and internship opportunities in the organisations which work closely with the various Times Group companies. What is the MBA in Entrepreneurship & Family Business, and how is it different from a regular MBA? The MBA in Entrepreneurship & Family Business focuses on venture creation, innovation, and family business management. Unlike a regular MBA, it blends entrepreneurship, succession planning, governance, and digital transformation to equip students to start new ventures or modernize and scale family enterprises. Who should apply to the MBA in Entrepreneurship & Family Business? This program is ideal for next-generation family business members, aspiring entrepreneurs, early-stage founders, and professionals who want to build, grow, or transform business ventures. Admission Process What is the admission process for MBA program at Bennett University? The process is merit-based. Applicants apply online via Bennett University’s official website. Shortlisting of candidates is based on academic performance, entrance exam scores, and personal interview. Final selection considers merit and overall profile. Which entrance exams are accepted for MBA admission at Bennett University? Bennett University accepts CAT, XAT, NMAT, MAT, CMAT, GMAT, and its in-house BU-MAT (Bennett University Management Aptitude Test). What is BU-MAT, and who should take it? BU-MAT is Bennett University’s in-house management aptitude test for candidates who haven’t taken national-level exams. It assesses analytical reasoning, communication, and business aptitude. When does the MBA Program admission cycle at Bennett University begin? Admissions typically begin in January each year and continue a rolling basis until seats are filled. Early applicants receive benefits such as preferred specialisations and scholarship consideration. What is the difference between pursuing an MBA from a college and a university? An MBA from a university offers greater academic depth, stronger research focus, and access to a wider ecosystem of multidisciplinary learning, global exposure, and advanced infrastructure. University MBAs also carry full academic recognition as a Master’s degree, which is important for higher studies. In contrast, students completing a PGDM from autonomous colleges are not eligible for a Ph.D. from state or central universities. Eligibility & Selection Criteria What are the eligibility criteria for the MBA program at Bennett University? Candidates must hold a bachelor’s degree with at least 50% marks (or equivalent CGPA) from a recognised university in any discipline. Can final-year undergraduate students apply for the MBA Program at Bennett University? Yes, final-year students can apply and will be granted provisional admission until they submit their final marksheet. What factors are considered during selection for the MBA Program at Bennett University? Selection is based on academic background, entrance score, interview performance, and leadership potential, ensuring fair and holistic evaluation. Is work experience required for MBA admission? Work experience is not mandatory , but applicants with prior experience are given preference, as it adds value to class discussions and leadership learning. Fee Structure & Scholarships What is the fee structure for the MBA program? The MBA fee is semester-based and transparent, covering tuition, academic resources, and modern facilities. For more information about MBA program fee click here: Does Bennett University offer scholarships? Yes, Merit-based scholarships are awarded based on graduation marks or performance in entrance exams such as CAT, XAT, NMAT, GMAT, or BU-MAT. For details about Bennett University scholarships click here: Internships, Placements & Career Opportunities What are the placement opportunities for MBA students? Bennett University’s Career Services Cell provides placement assistance with opportunities in various sectors including Consulting, Analytics & Financial Services, Consumer Goods, Information Technology (IT) & Software Services, Transportation, Telecommunications, IT, Retail & E-commerce, AI, and Fin-tech among others. Which companies recruit MBA graduates from Bennett University? Top recruiters for MBA graduates from Bennett University include KPMG, EY, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, PwC, Infosys, Aditya Birla Group, ITC Hotels, Adani Wilmar, Sutherland Global, Federal Bank, Evalueserve, Blackrock, Suzlon, TCS, Cred, Hero Corp, Tata Power, L&T, DDB Mudra, Ultratech Cement, Zee Media, Gartner, Kuehne Nagel and The Times Group, among others. What is the average and highest salary package for MBA graduates? Bennett MBA graduates receive competitive salary packages , with the highest CTC on par with top B-schools in India. For details on MBA placements click here Does Bennett University offer internship opportunities? Bennett University offers 16-week of internships as part of the program, giving students meaningful industry exposure and practical skills that enhance their employability. How does Bennett University prepare MBA students for placements? The university, through Bennett Finishing School, equips students with mock interviews, resume workshops, case study training, and corporate mentorship programs to ensure students are industry-ready. Are entrepreneurship opportunities available during the MBA program at Bennett University? Bennett Hatchery Foundation at Bennett University supports early-stage startups across ideation, validation, prototyping, and market growth. Experiential Learning & Global Exposure How does the MBA program at Bennett University incorporate experiential learning? The MBA program at Bennett University emphasises experiential learning through live business projects, consulting assignments, simulations, and leadership labs that help students develop strong strategic and analytical skills. What global exposure opportunities are available? Bennett University offers Global Immersion Programs across Singapore, Malaysia, Vietnam, and Dubai, lasting 5–7 days, depending on the academic calendar, destination, and partner university scheduling. What makes Bennett University’s MBA globally relevant? The university offers opportunities to MBA students for global immersion experience in select foreign universities, participating in international conferences, etc., where they are exposed to diverse cultures, business practices, and global challenges. What do students gain from joining the Global Immersion Program? By joining the Global Immersion Program, students get first-hand exposure to how businesses operate across Asia and the Middle East, allowing them to understand diverse markets, cultures, and industry practices. The experience helps them build strong cross-cultural communication skills, gain insights into global business ecosystems, and develop a broader, more adaptable mindset. Along the way, students also expand their professional network and strengthen their overall career readiness, making them better prepared for opportunities in an increasingly global workplace. Campus Life, Clubs & Extracurricular Activities What extracurricular opportunities are available for MBA students at Bennett University? Bennett University offers a vibrant campus life with numerous extracurricular opportunities, including student-run clubs, leadership positions, workshops, competitions, and cultural events. Students engage in activities beyond academics to build confidence, teamwork, communication skills, and industry exposure. What types of clubs are available for MBA students at Bennett University? MBA students can join a variety of academic and interest-based clubs, including Marketing Club, Human Resource Club, Finance Club, Analytics Club, Entrepreneurship Cell (E-Cell) and Media & Communications Club. These clubs conduct guest sessions, industry panel discussions, live projects, competitions, and certification workshops to enhance domain knowledge and real-world skills. Is there a student council in School of Management (SOM) at Bennett University? Yes, SOM has an active Student Council that represents the student body and works closely with faculty and administration. MBA students can participate in leadership roles within the council, gaining exposure to strategic planning, event management, and student community engagement. Do students get opportunities to participate in external competitions or events? Yes, our students regularly participate in national and international case study competitions, business hackathons, startup challenges, debate championships, and management conferences. Students also represent the university in events organized by leading B-schools, corporates, and institutions. Are there opportunities for public speaking and leadership development? Absolutely. Various activities such as Toastmasters-style speaking forums, club leadership roles, industry interaction sessions, and event-management responsibilities help MBA students develop strong communication, confidence, and leadership skills. Does Bennett University encourage networking opportunities for MBA students? Yes. Through industry meetups, CXO sessions, corporate networking events, alumni talk, and participation in external summits—including those hosted by The Times Group—students build meaningful professional connections that support internships, placements, and career growth. The comprehensive curriculum will provide a roadmap for navigating the program effectively. BBA Curriculum 2022-25 BBA Curriculum 2021-24 BBA Curriculum 2020-23 BBA Curriculum 2019-22 BBA Curriculum 2018-21 Specialisation The BBA Programme offers a wide scope of Specialisation. The Specialisation courses start from the fourth semester. Marketing Marketing Specialisation in BBA Programme acquaints student to gain complete knowledge of marketing management. The key courses canvassed in this Specialisation are Integrated Marketing Communication, Sales and Distribution, Services Marketing, Product and Brand Management, Consumer Behaviour, among others. Finance BBA in Finance imparts comprehensive understanding of money, accounting, and advanced concepts to settle complex monetary issues. The primary courses covered under Finance Specialisation are Financial Modeling and Analytics, Security Analysis & Portfolio Management, Financial Markets, Financial Services, and Insurance, to name a few. Entrepreneurship Entrepreneurship Specialisation focuses to encourage a sturdy entrepreneurial attitude among students. This Specialisation allows students to learn specific knowledge and abilities required to formulate an entrepreneurial venture. Further, it lends the firmness to transform their innovative thoughts into new organizations. Courses under this Specialisation are Venture Creation Strategy and Simulation, Family Businesses Essentials, Entrepreneurial Marketing & Finance, Global Entrepreneurship. International Business BBA degree with a Specialisation in International Business is the most ideal for a competitor who needs to seek after higher studies/profession abroad. The course prepares future leaders to know about the business administration in a global environment. The course list for this Specialisation incorporates Emerging Markets in International Business, International Business Environment, EXIM Procedures and Documentation, International Finance, Cross-Cultural Management, International Finance, among others. Business Analytics - Integrated with Institute of Analytics, UK Business Analytics evolves information and train students on prescriptive, descriptive, and predictive business modelling abilities. These are the most recent apparatuses/strategies and software are used to analyse and solve issues for the implementation of changes and business development. The course list for this Specialisation incorporates includes Statistics with R, Simulations and Heuristics, SaS and Tableau, Python Programming, SQL, Machine Learning & AI, Social Media Analytics, Neural Networks an Deep Learning. Human Resource Management BBA in Human Resource Management is a Specialisation which focuses on helping students understand the recruitment process, managing people, and providing direction and guidance to the workforce. The course list for this Specialisation includes HR Tools and Techniques, Industrial Relations and Labour Laws, Leadership and Change Management, Recruitment and Selection, and Organizational Development. International Finance and Accounting – Integrated with ACCA, UK This Specialisation is designed in collaboration with the Association of Certified Chartered Accountants (ACCA), UK. This is one-of-a-kind specializations offered with ACCA certification as it integrates the curriculum of ACCA with the BBA degree. A student thereby saves time spent on pursuing ACCA certification on their own. If a student pursues this Specialisation, they can directly get exemption of nine ACCA courses in the certification. The course list includes Advanced Management Accounting, International Law, Financial Reporting, International Financial Management, Performance Management, Audit and Assurance, among others. Honours Honours can be earned by completing additional 20 credit courses. This consist of a Dissertation and Managerial Effectiveness Workshop. Internships The industry reconciliation of the Bennett BBA program permits each student to complete of two months corporate internship between the second and third year. Some of the prominent recruiters for internship have been Outlook Group, Bajaj Capital, Jindal Poly Films, Tenhard India Pvt, India, Pragyata Foundation, Axis Bank, SMC Group, to name a few. Experience of Inclusive Environment Every BBA student experiences an opportunity for Discovery Walkthrough in which students can observe skilled and talented parts of the society that help them to get out-of-box thinking and applied innovation. Moreover, students go through a community service project that makes them aware of their social and ethical responsibilities and instils community values. NRI Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score) Student Testimonials BU has always given opportunities, be it academics or performing arts, which have helped me excel in both areas. Why should I pursue BBA degree from Bennett University? The BBA program at Bennett University provides an international perspective combined with industry exposure to its students. Ranked 3rd Best BBA Program in India, Times BBA Ranking 2020 Curriculum in sync with Top 10 Future Job Skills (as per World Economic Forum 2020) Strong Business and Industry Connect with Times Network Semester Exchange & Pathway Opportunity with partner foreign Universities Live projects’ exposure and Strong Business and Industry Connect with Times Network Mandatory two credit-based internships, i.e., an internship after the first year with a non-profit organization or a firm involved in social activities and an industry internship after the second year Foreign languages like German and French are taught in the program to introduce global languages and cultures to the students To help students develop an entrepreneurial mindset, there are compulsory and optional credit-based courses on Entrepreneurial Learning for all BBA students Collaborated with ACCA, UK, to start a new integrated BBA-ACCA specialization Q. What is the difference between BBA and B. Com? BBA, a three-year undergraduate program, focuses on developing vital business skills. Apart from classroom learnings, BBA is backed by practical experiences that include summer internships, industry visits, presentations, conferences, and case studies. Thus, it aims to prepare students for both possibilities, entering the job market after graduation or going for higher education in the respective domain. B. Com is a highly theoretical program, and the curriculum provides an intense look into the fundamental subjects of commerce like Accounts, Administration, Finance, Economics, and Industrial Policy. Q. Name the specializations which are offered in BBA? There are eight specializations which are available in BBA: Marketing Finance Human Resource Management Entrepreneurship International Business International Finance and Accounting – ACCA Certification Business Analytics Advertising & PR Q. How can a student earn a BBA Honours Degree? Bennett University provides a platform for students to earn an Honours degree along with a general BBA degree in addition of 20 credits. For BBA Honours: 120 Credits. This includes a Dissertation and Managerial Effectiveness Workshop. Q. Is internship mandatory part of the program and who will arrange the same? Internships are a mandatory part of the program. Students have to compulsorily do a corporate internship of two months between 2nd and 3rd year. Q. Does BU have a start-up culture? Thriving start-up Culture with Bennett Hatchery. 21 Crore funding received by students C-Safekey, by Zaid Naim, FIX Coffee by Aditya Jain, Skill Arena by Hardik Aggarwal, Gym Trainers by Nikhil Panwar, Chocolate Box by Krishnammagri Samhita, Reeyal by Reeya Sharma, and Meera Scrochet by Ayush Arora are the testimonies of entrepreneurial culture at the University. Q. What are the key highlights of Partnership with ACCA, UK? Bennett University has partnered with ACCA, UK, to start a new integrated BBA-ACCA program. This will give students a very lucrative opportunity to pursue ACCA certification along with their BBA studies. Bennett BBA students will get six papers exempted from the rigorous ACCA exams and have to clear only the remaining seven. Even for those seven papers, the syllabi are integrated with the curriculum and will be taught as complete courses. Benefits of BU BBA-ACCA: £650 waived off per student Enhanced enrolment opportunity for university admissions overseas Invite Speakers & Mentors from other countries and corporates 100% Placement Assistance Q. What are your current placements statistics for BBA? Average Packagae: 5.16 LPA Highest Package: 8.5 LPA Companies: Moody’s Analytics, To The New, Tres Vista Financial, Aditya Birla Fashion & Retail, Publicis, TCS, ITC, D E Shaw, Clairvolex, Byjus, Bata India, HFFC, Ebix, Grant Thornton. Q. What are the job prospects after completing BBA from Bennett University? A plethora of job opportunities is available to students after pursuing BBA. Depending upon the area of specialization, the student can choose to build their career in their respective fields. BBA from Bennett University will position the students to become leaders, managers, and entrepreneurs capable of developing and leading sustainable business organizations Q. Have BBA students able to secure their position at prestigious foreign universities? More than 30% BBA students take admission in reputed international universities. 2021 BBA batch students got selected for Masters in top 100 Global Universities like University of Texas (USA), University of Liverpool (UK), Singapore Management University (Singapore), University of Greenwich (UK), to name a few. MBA (Sports) | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login MBA Sports Apply Now MBA Sports Apply Now About MBA (Sports) The MBA in Sports specialisation at Bennett University at Bennett University prepares students to lead and manage sports organisations, events, and teams with expertise. The program blends business management, marketing, analytics, law, and technology to equip students with the skills required in the competitive sports industry. Duration 2 Years Specialisations NA Credits - Start Date July 2026 Curriculum Highlights The Sports Management specialisation combines management fundamentals with industry-specific knowledge in sports marketing, analytics, events, media, and law. Students develop operational and strategic capabilities to manage sports enterprises, improve engagement and performance, and deliver impactful sports experiences through practical exposure, projects, and case studies. Electives Sports Marketing and Sponsorship Sports Analytics & Technology Strategic Management of Sports Event & Media Management for Sports Legal Aspects of Sports Business Sports Products and Brand Management Experiential Learning Organise sports events and tournaments Internships with sports clubs, leagues, and agencies Sports analytics and technology projects Workshops on sponsorship, media, and athlete management Why MBA (Sports) from Bennett University Industry-Relevant Skills: Opportunities in sports marketing, analytics, and event management. Practical Exposure: Exposure to the business of professional sports, leagues, and athlete management. Brand & Media Skills: Develop skills in sponsorship, brand management, and media in sports. Career Opportunities: Career prospects in sports agencies, clubs, leagues, and media. Program Objectives The program aims to help students gain a clear understanding of sports management, marketing, and analytics. It focuses on developing skills for organising sporting events and managing teams. Students learn to apply legal, financial, and strategic principles in sports organisations, use analytics and technology to improve sports performance and fan engagement, and build leadership, teamwork, and entrepreneurial skills for the sports industry. Learning Outcomes On completion of MBA in Sports, students are equipped to: Plan and manage sports events and teams Design sports marketing and sponsorship strategies Apply data analytics for performance and business decisions Effectively navigate the legal and financial aspects of sports organisations Career Pathways MBA (Sports) graduates are prepared for specialist and managerial roles within sports clubs, professional leagues, media companies, sponsorship firms and event management organisations as: Sports Manager Sports Event Coordinator Sports Analyst Sports Brand Manager Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Passed Graduation in any discipline from a recognised university with a minimum 50% marks with English as the medium of instruction. NRI: For international applicants, a minimum of 50% in the qualifying examination is required, along with having studied the prerequisite subjects for admission to the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on the weighted score of CAT / XAT / NMAT / MAT / CMAT / GMAT / BU MAT/ CUET Percentile score & Graduation & Personal Interview. The MBA (Sports) at Bennett University is a specialised management program designed to prepare students for leadership and managerial roles in the sports industry. The program covers key areas such as sports marketing, event management, athlete and team management, sports analytics, governance, and sports business strategy. Who should opt for an MBA (Sports)? The MBA (Sports) is ideal for students who are passionate about sports and aspire to build careers in sports organisations, leagues, clubs, federations, sports marketing agencies, event management companies, fitness centres, and wellness enterprises. What career opportunities are available after an MBA in Sports Management? Graduates of the MBA (Sports) program can pursue roles such as Sports Marketing Manager, Event Manager, Team Operations Manager, Sponsorship and Partnerships Manager, Athlete Agent, Sports Analyst, Facility Manager, Sports Consultant, and Sports Development Manager. Does the Sports Management program include core MBA subjects? Yes, the program offers a strong foundation in core MBA subjects such as Marketing, Finance, Human Resources, Strategy, and Operations, along with specialised courses focused on the sports industry. What kind of practical exposure does the MBA Sports Management program offer? Students gain hands-on experience through live industry projects, case studies, internships, sports events, industry interactions, guest lectures by sports professionals, and experiential learning opportunities. Are internships mandatory in the MBA (Sports) program? Yes, internships are an integral part of the MBA (Sports) curriculum. They provide students with real-world exposure and practical experience in sports organisations and allied industries. Is prior experience or a sports background required to pursue MBA in Sports Management? No prior sports background or professional experience is mandatory. However, a keen interest in sports, sports business, or sports events is considered an added advantage. What kind of placement opportunities can I expect after an MBA in Sports from Bennett University? Bennett University provides dedicated placement support through industry tie-ups, career mentoring, internships, networking opportunities, and interactions with sports organisations, marketing agencies, and event management companies. How is an MBA (Sports) different from a general MBA? While a general MBA provides broad management exposure, the MBA (Sports) focuses on the unique dynamics of the sports industry, including fan engagement, sponsorship management, sports law, media rights, sports marketing, and event operations. PhD in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Civil & Law - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Ph.D. Excellence in Research Pursue Ph.D. Programs at Bennett University Apply Now Announcement Announcement of Results: Ph.D. Entrance Examination (Even Semester 2025–26) Click to view>> Why Ph.D. at Bennett University? MENTORSHIP BY WORLD CLASS FACULTY Faculties are gold medalists and academically sound with strong research background along with international exposure CUTTING EDGE RESEARCH THROUGH MODERN LABS Varsity has well-equipped laboratories, studios and moot court for Tech, media and law. In management & social sciences, faculties guide scholars through a wide spectrum of respective fields. CHALLENGING PROBLEM-CENTRIC RESEARCH Effective assessment, clear objectives and systematic approaches by the faculties helps overcoming challenges About PhD The Bennett University Ph.D. program offers global exposure, academic integrity and technological excellence to the engineering researchers, with an aim to make them career-ready. The broad objectives of the Ph.D. programs are: to keep pace with the expanding frontiers of knowledge, and to provide research training relevant to the current social and economic objectives of the country. The Ph.D. degree is awarded for high academic achievements, independent research and application of knowledge to social, managerial, technical and scientific situations. Creative and productive inquiry is be the concept behind research work. The academic program leading to the Ph.D. degree is broad-based. It entails specified course work and credit requirements for different programs, publications and research thesis. The thesis would be the report of research work characterised by either the discovery of new facts or the new interpretation of known facts and theory, or an independent design and development, or the development any new instrument or technology. The degree of Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) of the Bennett University is conferred on a scholar who fulfils all the requirements specified in the Ph.D. Regulations. Attractive stipend is available through Teaching Assistantship for Ph.D. students (to cover program fee, hostel & incidental expenses). Ph.D. Program Details School of Artificial Intelligence School of Artificial Intelligence offers a Ph.D. in AI to nurture a new generation of researchers and innovators. The program is designed to meet the growing global demand for deep, research-based expertise that can drive innovation and ethical development in AI… Read More... Computer Science Engineering & Technology Bennett University’s Ph.D. program offers global exposure, academic integrity and technological excellence to the engineering researchers, with a motive to make them career-ready. The broad objectives of the Ph.D. programs are: to keep pace with the expanding frontiers of knowledge and to provide research training relevant to the present social and economic objectives of the country. The award of Ph.D. degree shall be in recognition of high academic achievements, independent research and application of knowledge to the solution of social, managerial, technical and scientific problems. Creative and productive inquiry shall be the basic concept underlying the research work. The academic program leading to the Ph.D. degree is broad-based. It involves specified course work and credit requirements for different programs, publications and a research thesis. The thesis shall be the report of research work characterized by either discovery of new facts or a new interpretation of known facts and theory, or an independent design and development, or development of new instrument/technology. The degree of Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) of the Bennett University shall be conferred on a scholar who fulfill all the requirements specified in Ph.D. Ordinances and Regulations. The programs are available in the specialisations of Computer Science Engineering and Mathematics (Applied/Pure mathematics)... Read More... Engineering, Technology and Applied Sciences In line with the philosophy of being a research and innovation driven University, the University’s School of Engineering and Applied Sciences (SEAS), is offering Ph.D. programs in Engineering, Technology and Applied Sciences. The programs are available in the specialisations of Electronics and Communication Engineering, Mechanical & Aerospace Engineering, Physics and Biotechnology. Scholars are required to take up intensive research work under the guidance of a supervisor on a specific problem, for a minimum of three years. They are also required to take up advanced level course work as required under the Ph.D. rules. The research work is expected to result in new findings, contributing to the knowledge in the chosen field. Doctoral research program gives an opportunity to scholars to show their analytical, innovative and independent thinking, leading to creativity and application of knowledge. Scholars are required to deliver seminars on their research progress regularly, and to publish their work. Finally, they are required to submit the thesis embodying their research findings for the award of the Ph.D. degree. School of Law The objective of the Ph.D. program is to equip the scholars with skills necessary to pursue research in the front-line areas of law, social science and science subjects. The aims at creating an inspiring, supportive and interdisciplinary research environment. Scholars would be required to complete the coursework, deliver seminars on their research progress, and publish crucial components of their ongoing work. On completion of the mandated stages of the program, and based on the final submission of thesis/valuation and Viva, the degree is conferred in Law or in Interdisciplinary work in Law based on the subject. Read More School of Management The objective of the Ph.D. program is to promote it as a source of rigorous and inter-disciplinary research in all areas of business management, including marketing, finance and accounting, organisational behaviour and human resource management, production and operations management, information management and analytics, communications, business economics and strategy. The School is committed to training and guiding the Scholars to equip them to conduct research of International standards. The University has an impressive profile of faculty members who are accomplished academics and have published research in leading journals contributing to the field of management. All faculty members are invested in helping scholars in their research journey to enhance the latter’s intellectual development. Read More Times School of Media Media research is an important part of media studies and Times School of Media would like its researchers and scholars to create a body of work that is relevant to today’s media. The School is particularly keen to encourage and guide scholars who want to look beyond traditional qualitative and quantitative research. We would like our research scholars to come up with solutions that can be adopted by the industry. The last two decades have seen a quantum shift in the creation and distribution of news, and the tsunami of change triggered by technology is yet to run its course. Anyone can be a publisher and influencer today. More than that, anyone can change the course and direction of media. These are exciting times. Creation, dissemination and consumption of information is being reshaped at a bewildering pace. The need of the hour is new ideas, ideas that are the product of refreshing research; ideas that have potential to change the way we create and consume information. The School would therefore like its scholars to invest their thoughts and energies in applied research. They will be guided in their pursuit by the School’s Faculty most of whom have been drawn from the industry. The scholars will also have the opportunity to interact with top editors and media professionals working in India’s largest media conglomerate – The Times of India Group. So, if you want to give a new direction to media research and be in the forefront of change then you are the one we are looking for. School of Liberal Arts School of Liberal Arts is commencing Ph.D. programmes from 2022. Ph.Ds will be offered in English literature, Sociology, Psychology, Economics, Philosophy and Political Science. The main aim of the Ph.D. programme is to enable socially relevant research resulting in a better understanding of the prevailing circumstances. Another attempt will be to undertake an experimental study, wherein, research can be an accompanying action. Ph.D. from the School of Liberal Arts can be interdisciplinary. The spirit of the school is to gain a holistic understanding of social sciences. At the same time, strict theoretical rigour will be required for the pursuance of research. Students will be regularly exposed to international and national seminars to learn myriad ways to approach a problem. The school highly encourage students to undertake fieldwork for their research to remain in touch with the ground-level facts. All the facilities are there in the University like well-established lab, lecture rooms, canteen, cafeteria etc. Technical programs are collaborated with leading universities in the world like Georgia Institute of Technology, Johnson Cornell university etc. Smita Tiwari OUR ASK Ph.D Frequently Asked Questions General How long does it take to complete a Ph.D. program? The PhD programme shall have a minimum duration of three (3) years, including coursework and Open Defence, and a maximum duration of six (6) years from the date of admission to the PhD programme. An extension of up to two (2) additional years may be granted, provided that the total duration for completing the PhD programme does not exceed eight (8) years from the date of admission. What is required grades to compete course work PhD. All PhD students are required to complete the prescribed coursework credits within the stipulated duration specified for their respective programme, ensuring a structured foundation for their research journey. The minimum course credit requirement for Ph.D. shall be 12 credits. In addition to the 12 credits Research As applicable, these courses will cover key areas such as quantitative methods, research ethics, literature review, training, and fieldwork. The course work should be Completed within one year . A Ph.D. scholar must obtain a minimum Cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA) of 6.0 on a 10-point scale to successfully complete the coursework. Apply Now Why MCA at Bennett University Industry oriented Curriculum Curriculum is designed by industry professionals and experts Problem solving skills Learn problem-solving skills using exploratory and analytical approach acquired in the field of Computer Science and Engineering. Industry-Academia collaboration Our Industry collaboration enables students to learn from industry experts Innovation and Entrepreneurship Develop the skills to become an Entrepreneur ABOUT About MCA Bennett University’s MCA program is a two-year professional degree in computer science. It is designed to equip graduates with advanced tools, technologies, and applications for the IT industry. The industry-oriented curriculum enables students to master various programming languages and application tools, empowering them to develop efficient and cutting-edge applications. Duration 2 years Specialisation 4 Credits 80 Start Date July 2026 Admission Counsellors Connect with our counsellors for any admissions related queries. explore Fee Structure Know more about fee structure. explore Scholarship Know more about Scholarship. explore Fee Structure 2025 Fee Type Year 1 Year 2 TOTAL Tuition fee (₹) 2,00,000 2,00,000 4,00,000 Registration Fees One Time (₹) 10,000 – 10,000 Total Fees (₹) 2,10,000 2,00,000 4,10,000 * Fee is subject to revision with the new academic year as per Bennett University policy. View full Fee Structure >> Curriculum Details The School of Computer Science, Engineering, and Technology has developed a comprehensive curriculum for the MCA program to help students in business applications, critical thinking, empirical and quantitative analysis, communication, teamwork, and life skills. The program scheme of MCA gives aspirants an edge in building their careers in preferred domains. MCA Curriculum 2025-27 MCA Curriculum 2024-26 MCA Curriculum 2023-25 MCA Curriculum 2022-24 Program Core Courses The Core Courses build a solid foundation for students to learn concepts such as data structures, algorithms, network systems, operating systems, software engineering and databases, essential for any computing professional. The specialisation comprises two Core Courses and three Elective Courses. It also has a specialisation-oriented Capstone Project and an Internship. Specialisations are chosen at the time of admissions. Specialisation Core Courses: Students are offered two specialisation core courses. Specialisation Electives: Students must choose three specialisation electives. Specialisations Artificial Intelligence CEOs and CXOs from companies like NVIDIA, Aindra, Here Tech, Amazon, and other leading academicians have contributed to designing the specialisation curriculum. The AI specialisation electives include- Cognitive Modeling Advanced Computer Vision and Video Analytics Reinforcement Learning Image and Video Processing Natural Language Processing These courses are aligned with international standards in AI. Additionally, the University has partnered with NVIDIA, SAP, Hitachi, and Times Internet, offering students opportunities to work on cutting-edge projects. Blockchain The curriculum for the Blockchain specialisation has been developed in consultation with CEOs and CXOs from BankchainAsset.com, Digiledge, and other subject matter experts. Electives include- Blockchain Technologies Smart Contracts and Solidity Programming Digital Currencies and Blockchain Modern Cryptography Cyber Security The growing reliance on online transactions and digital engagements has resulted in an increased demand for a robust Cyber Security framework. To address this, the curriculum has been designed by leaders from the Data Security Council of India (DSCI), QoS Technologies, IBM, and other industry experts. The electives for the specialisations are - Cyber Security with Blockchain Malware Analysis for Mobile Devices Security and Privacy for Big Data Analytics Penetration Testing and Ethical Hacking Additionally, the University has partnered with DSCI, Fortinet, CompTIA, and CISCO to provide students with opportunities to work on innovative projects. Data Science Data scientists play a crucial role in the development of smart cities, the automation industry, BFSI, and Fintech sectors. Recognizing the significance of Data Science, Bennett University has developed a specialised curriculum in collaboration with top executives from Microsoft, Google, TCS, and other experts. The curriculum includes electives such as Structural Equation Modelling Security and Privacy for Big Data Analytics Data Visualization Big Data Analytics and Business Intelligence, and Satellite Data Analytics Electives These are University-wide courses offered by different schools. Students can select courses from any open elective list available across the University. Popular options include courses like Mobile Journalism, Start-ups, and Copyright Law. Honours An optional Honours consists of a set of 4 courses that provide additional knowledge in the same or related area of study. These Honours courses are offered in the 2nd and 3rd semesters, contributing an additional 12 credits to the students. Projects To promote Project-Based Learning (PBL), the MCA program includes a project in each semester, as outlined in the table below- Semester Project 1 Specialisation Project 2 Project on Product Design Technology 3 Capstone Project/Venture Launch at Bennett Hatchery 4 Projects during Industry Internship Internships Two internships are part of the curriculum. The first internship is available in the 3rd semester, which can be an alternative to the capstone project. The Capstone Design, and the Capstone Project are cutting edge courses to prepare the students for the industry. The second internship is done in the final semester. There is also one flexible option for the students to spend a year in an Industry internship during the final year of study. Internship options are available at many leading companies. Skills Life Skills: A course on life skills is offered in the 1st semester to cultivate lifelong learning skills. Humanity: Students are offered humanity-based courses, which consist of a 1-credit seminar and a 1-credit ethics course. Entrepreneurship: A course on entrepreneurship is offered to inculcate the necessary skills among students. Lab Dell-EMC The lab consists of 32 Dell machines with the latest configuration. Moreover, it has the support of Dell-EMC. Dell provides support in faculty training, collaborative projects, and exclusive workshops. Specific areas of operation will include Information Storage and Management (“ISM”), Cloud Infrastructure Services (“CIS”), Data Science and Big Data Analytics (“DSBDA”) and Data Protection and Management (“DPM”). It will help students acquire the skills required by Fortune 500 companies. The lab incorporates incorporates information, resources, expertise, training, and knowledge in the field of (i) Information Storage and Management, (ii) Cloud Infrastructure Services, (iii) Data Protection and Management and (iv) Data Science and Big Data Analytics to enable Bennett University to disseminate the information and knowledge. Computer configuration: Dell OptiPlex 5050MT XCTO, Intel i5 processor, 8Gb DDR4 RAM. Bennett - NVIDIA Centre for Artificial Intelligence The lab is equipped with 30 high-performance HP Z440 workstations, designed to enhance computational processing. These workstations are ideal for handling zeta-scale data and executing demanding applications. They feature powerful NVIDIA graphics cards and fast data transfers with Thunderbolt technology, making them efficient and reliable. The lab provides a world-class workstation experience, ensuring smooth performance. Researchers and scholars use the lab for AI applications in healthcare, security, cognition, text, image, and video analysis. The NVIDIA DGX-1 V100 is the first system built with ground-breaking NVIDIA Pascal™-powered Tesla® V100 accelerators. It is NVIDIA NVLink™ implementation that delivers a massive increase in GPU memory capacity. This is the world’s first purpose-built system for deep learning and accelerated analytics, delivering performance equal to 500 conventional servers. It comes fully integrated with hardware, deep learning software, development tools, and runs popular accelerated analytics applications. This means you can immediately shorten data processing time, visualize more data, accelerate deep learning frameworks, and design more sophisticated neural networks. NVIDIA DGX-1 software stack includes major deep learning frameworks, the NVIDIA DIGITS™ GPU training system, the NVIDIA Deep Learning SDK (e.g., CuDNN, NCCL), NVIDIA Docker, GPU drivers, and NVIDIA CUDA® for rapidly designing deep neural networks (DNN). It’s the ideal stack for accelerating popular analytics and visualization software. This powerful system includes access to cloud management services for container creation and deployment, system updates, and an application repository. This combined software can run on Pascal-powered Tesla GPUs allowing applications to run 12X faster than with previous GPU-accelerated solutions. Due to our partnership with NVIDIA, we have access to Certified Expert Instructors, Worldwide Workshops, Online Courses, Epicenter of industry leaders, Onsite Training, Global Reach, NVIDIA Partner Network, OEMs and startups, Deep Learning Teaching Kit. Apple iMac Design & Multi-Media Lab The lab is designed for running courses that require high-end graphics performance, including 3D graphics and video editing. It will be specifically used for activities and projects related to gaming, design, multimedia, modern art, and media production. The high configuration of the machines will also support resource-intensive applications for research involving large-scale simulations and experimentation. The Centre will focus on multimedia design and research. It will be equipped with Apple iMacs, featuring high-end graphics and components ideal for multimedia work. A total of 32 high-performance machines will be available to support the objectives of the lab. 10 Programming labs This lab is designed to teach programming and coding skills to first-year students. It is equipped with state-of-the-art systems that meet all the necessary requirements for effective programming instruction. Information Management System Lab This lab supports the curricular requirements of Information Management, Database Management Systems, and other business processing applications. It is equipped with relevant software and IT infrastructure to fulfill these requirements. Cloud Computing Lab This lab addresses and supports the curricular requirements of Cloud Computing processing and applications. Students get hands-on practical experience through lab work for Cloud-Based Computer Laboratory (CBCL) solutions, including certifications such as Microsoft Azure. It is equipped with relevant software and IT infrastructure to fulfill these requirements. Drone Labs This lab focuses on meeting the curricular requirements in the field of Drone Technology, including drone assembly and application implementation. The Drone Lab provides facilities for the development of various drone technologies and sensing equipment, which can be integrated into drones such as DJI Mavic AIR QUADCOPTER DRONE, Veerobot F450 AIR QUADCOPTER DRONE and other advanced platforms. Students use development platforms such as Jetson Nano Nvidia Development Kit, and Raspberry Pi for their projects. The lab is equipped with a wide range of drone platforms, including quadcopters, hexacopters, pipeline crawlers, and remotely operated underwater vehicles, supporting diverse research and development activities. IoT and Robotics Lab This lab supports the curricular requirements for IoT and Robotics, providing students with hands-on experience using the Robo NAO and implementing applications with the NAO V6 Humanoid Robot. In this lab, students also build an Artificial Being (ABe), a network of mobile robots that emulate the various parts and functions of a body, differentiating it from traditional humanoid robots. Computer Network and Cyber Security Lab This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements of Computer Network and Cyber Security Lab on Simulator Tools, including Hardware hand on experience with Routers and the latest CISCO Switches. Students use different wireless network components and features providing network security and to set Firewall Security in windows/ Linux OS for implementing different types of vulnerabilities for hacking websites /Web Applications. Data Structure Labs This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Data Structure Labs. We provide the students with knowledge about Data Structures, Applications of data structures, Problem-solving using data structure tools and Techniques. Students are made familiar with C programming and its applications. AR and VR Gaming This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements in the domain of AR and VR Gaming Labs. Our Virtual Reality, Augmented Reality, and Media Lab contains some of the most cutting-edge head- mounted displays available today. Students have access to the technology for the development of VR and AR software using Oculus Go Standalone Virtual Realty Headset and many other. Microprocessor Lab This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Microprocessing Lab to provide intensive practical exposure to learn the assembly and embedded C programming systematically for the different issues of microprocessors and implement their codes on INTEL ALTERA DE2 115 DEVELOPMENT AND EDUCATION Boards. Big Data and Machine Learning Lab This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Big Data Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence to analyze large datasets, identify trends, and develop predictive models to solve their problems. Where students build and train deep neural networks, build a CNN and apply it to detection and recognition tasks, Train test sets, analyze variance for DL applications, use standard techniques and optimization algorithms, and build neural networks in TensorFlow. Students can Build and train RNNs, work with NLP and Word Embeddings, and use HuggingFace tokenizers and transformer models to perform NER. Quantum Computing Labs This lab supports the curricular requirements in the domain of Quantum Computing, focusing on both research and the application of quantum theory to advance computer technology. By leveraging quantum simulations, the lab offers hands-on exercises that run on real quantum systems provided by IBM Quantum Experience. CSE Batch: 2018-2022 OUR ASK MCA Frequently Asked Questions Can I do MCA after B. Com? Yes, you can do MCA after B.com from Bennett University. However, the B.Com background is not directly linked to MCA. Therefore, you can bridge the gap with preparatory courses in Mathematics and Computer Science to strengthen your foundation. Additionally, you need to check the eligibility criteria for an MCA course to pursue it. What are the different specialisations I can opt for in MCA? Bennett University offers MCA in four specialisations: Artificial Intelligence Data Science Cyber Security BlockChain What is the eligibility criteria for MCA? A graduation in any discipline from a recognised university, with minimum 50% marks Why should a student pursue an MCA from Bennett University? There are numerous reasons for someone to join Bennett University: 40+ high-end labs to provide practical exposure to the students Highly qualified and experienced PhD faculty to teach and train students If any student leaves MCA after Six months, he/she will be awarded a Certificate in Computer Applications. And if any student leaves the course after one year, he/she will be awarded Post Graduate Diploma in Computer Applications. Students will have the choice to complete their application in any of the four specializations provided by the department. Students will get an opportunity to work on live projects. Is it necessary for me to have prior computer knowledge? No, it is not necessary, but prior computer knowledge can be an added advantage for the students. Tech Mechanical Engineering, Advantages of Mechanical Engineering - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login B.Tech Mechanical Engineering Design & develop mechanisms for ease of living Apply Now B.Tech Mechanical Engineering Design & develop mechanisms for ease of living Apply Now Why BTech Mechanical Engineering at Bennett University Research Oriented Faculty Expert Faculty from IIT’s and leading global universities Hands-on practical Exposure State-of the-art laboratories & world class research facility for UG/PG students. International level institutional & digital infrastructure Industry Based Curriculum Mechanical Engineering curriculum fine-tuned to Industry 4.0 Advanced and Industry tuned specialisations Promoting Research Culture Under-Graduate Research Culture International Collaboration for Curriculum development Sharing of experiences in innovative teaching methods ABOUT About Mechanical Engineering Mechanical Engineers are engaged in creating the future in Aerospace Engineering, Automotive Engineering, Biomechanics Technologies, Energy Technologies, Mechatronics & Robotics Technologies and Industrial Engineering. They are the driving force behind many technologies and industrial processes in our modern world. Mechanical Engineering at the University trains the student in manufacturing, design, automation, and thermal sciences. Students here have adequate flexibility to specialise in areas of their choice through electives and projects. The course emphasises on subjects such as Flexible Manufacturing Systems, Computer Integrated Manufacturing, Robotics, Tribology, Composites and Laser Materials, Energy Engineering, and Finite Element Methods so as the students can eventually take up positions in manufacturing, design, energy generation and conservation and R&D. The goal is to prepare the global players and professionals of the future. Each Core course is designed with 60% hands on experience in well-equipped laboratories and 40% classroom teaching. Moreover, students can select electives as per their individual interests based on specialisations they opt. The University curriculum covers the subjects from the basics of the mechanical course to the industry-oriented courses. Also, students can take courses from other departments, which opens the scope of working on interdisciplinary projects. ME Department offers a design project course, which runs in all the semester. Through this course the students develop skills on technical writing, oral presentation, data analysis, code writing and experimental research. The course ultimately helps in their final year dissertation and in their higher studies. B.Tech. ME Curriculum 2024-28 B.Tech. ME Curriculum 2023-27 B.Tech. ME Curriculum 2022-26 B.Tech. Elective Mechanical Engineering Department offers a rich list of elective courses from various domains to choose from B.Tech (ME) students have to complete 5 elective courses in their curriculum to fulfill the requirements of B.Tech (ME) Degree. Specialisation Aerospace Engineering E-Mobility Energy Technologies Industrial Engineering Mechatronics & Robotics AI & ML Program Specific Outcomes PSO1: Effectively build and communicate the solutions using learnings from basic mechanical engineering, basic sciences, mathematics, computational skills and tools. PSO2: Ability to conduct experiments and simulate real-life problems in engineering using computational techniques and instrumentation; and can work independently in research or industrial environments. PSO3: Capability to compete with the available employment opportunities and solve complex engineering problems related to production, design, thermal and allied industries using systematic tools, while giving due consideration to the environment protection and sustainability. Projects Some of the recent exciting projects by the ME students: Free vibration and stability analysis of functionally graded rectangular plate. Studies on tribological behaviour of solid lubricating coating. Numerical simulation and analysis of cavitating flows around hydrofoils and in injector nozzles. Vibro-acoustic and wear phenomena in rail-wheel contact. Experimental and modelling studies on the high strain rate characterization of shear thickening fluids for dynamic loading applications. Analysis of rotary cement kiln for failure diagnosis and prediction of failure life. Design and Analysis of Gas Turbine Inlet and Exhaust Structures, AAF International, UK, 2019 Design and Development of Test Rig for High Strain Rate Characterization of Materials for Dynamic Applications. Design & development of Foot categorization device. Kinematic & kinetic analyses of human movement & spine postures. Design & Development of low-cost hybrid ATV. A Biomechanical Analysis of Movements of Body Segments and Quantification of Performance Enhancement factors while Playing Badminton. Design and Analysis of an active prosthetic knee for trans-femoral amputees. Measuring and optimizing biomechanical risk factors of lifting load tasks in Industry through Wearable System. Biomechanical Loads in Running-Based Sports: Estimating Ground Reaction Forces from Segmental Accelerations using wearable sensors Skills Some of the advanced skills students will acquire: Computer Aided Engineering & Drawing Auto-CAD & CATIA Software ANSYS for solid modelling and analysis Finite Element Analysis Computational Fluid Dynamics Computer Aided Manufacturing using advanced CNCs, G-codes & M-codes. Renewable Energy Harvesting Techniques Material Testing & Characterization Techniques Human Motion & Posture Analysis using wearable sensor technologies. Design & Analysis applications in Aerospace Engineering. Singh Ph.D. - IIT, Delhi Dr. Shitanshu Arya Ph.D. - IIT Delhi, New Delhi ELIGIBILITY Btech ME Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process Eligibility Domestic Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with Physics and Mathematics & any other third* subject with minimum 55% marks OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI Pass with 55% aggrigate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third* subject with highest score) * Third subject should not be vocational- Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc PROCESS Mechanical Engineering Admission Process for 2025 The selection of a student at Bennett University will be finalized after series of evaluative steps: Submission of the application form with required details by paying non-refundable application fees of Rs. 1500. Merit-based assessment of candidates and release of provisional offer letters. Selected Students to pay advance fee within deadline given. Submit documents and pending fee payment to complete registration as per timelines shared. Waseem Sheikh B.Tech. ME Batch: 2018- 22 OUR ASK Mechanical Engineering Frequently Asked Questions What is the current scope of Mechanical Engineers? Mechanical Engineers are focusing on new arenas of research such as nanotechnology, development of composite materials, biomedical applications, environmental conservation, and IT Setups etc. Name Specialisation that Bennett University offers under BTech Mechanical Engineering? Bennett University offers six Specialisations under BTech Mechanical Engineering - Aerospace Engineering E-Mobility Energy Technologies Industrial Engineering Mechatronics & Robotics AI & ML What are the career opportunities after completing BTech Mechanical Engineering? Students can opt for various job opportunities after the B. Tech degree in Mechanical Engineering. Students can become Materials Engineer, Maintenance Engineer, Automotive Engineer, Control and Instrumentation Engineer and many more. Apart from this, students can also apply for govenment jobs and can join as teachers or professors in government schools, colleges, and universities. In addition to that, students can also appear for government exams such as UPSC, Railways, Banks, etc. Is there any industrial interaction program for students? Yes, throughout the year students will get opportunity to work on live projects under industry experts, industry visits for hands-on experience, and internship opportunities with the leading industries. Also, students can apply for global immersion programs that expands student career dimension. What kind of Infrastructure is available in the BU’s ME department? The Mechanical department at Bennett University is equipped with all the state-of-the-art equipment. Students get training under highly trained faculty. Bennett University has – Vibration, Metrology & Measurement Lab Kinematics & Dynamics of Machine Lab Material Testing Lab Computer Aided Design and Simulation Lab Is there any research lab for BTech Mechanical Engineers? There are two hi-tech research labs for mechanical engineers. Computational Mechanics Lab Computational Fluids Dynamics Lab How many shops are included under Mechanical Engineering Workshop?
