Best BCA Course, Subjects, Syllabus and Fee - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Bachelor of Computer Application (BCA) Be a leader in multi-facated career Apply Now Bachelor of Computer Application (BCA) Be a leader in multi-facated career Apply Now Why BCA at Bennett University Quality Faculty The all Ph.D faculty come from IITs and other acclaimed institutions, bringing the best experience to class Industry-relevant Certifications In Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, Cyber Security, Data Science, DevOps, Full Stack, Drones and Gaming Access to high-end Labs 40+ high-end laboratories in association with Dell EMC, Amazon, Apple, NVIDIA Industry & Corporate Tie-ups With Amazon, Microsoft, IBM, NVIDIA, Apple, UIPath, Automation Anywhere, Fortinet, Transorg, Empass, SAP, and others ABOUT About BCA The BCA course offered by the Computer Science Engineering Department is designed for students who are enthusiastic about pursuing their careers in computer-related fields. A BCA degree with strong practical exposure provides a foundation for a career in Computer Applications, Information Technology (IT), and IT-enabled services. With a focus on hands-on training and practical exposure, the curriculum is designed to make the students industry ready. Bennett University offers BCA in nine specialisations. Duration 3 Years Specialisation 10 Credits BCA 120 & BCA (Hons.) 135 Start Date August 2026 Admission Counsellors Connect with our counsellors for any admissions related queries. explore Fee Structure Know more about fee structure. explore Scholarship Know more about Scholarship. explore Fee Structure 2025 Fee Type Year 1 Year 2 Year 3 TOTAL Tuition fee (₹) 1,80,000 1,90,000 2,00,000 5,70,000 Registration Fees One Time (₹) 45,000 – – 45,000 Total Fees (₹) 2,25,000 1,90,000 2,00,000 6,15,000 * Fee is subject to revision with the new academic year as per Bennett University policy. View full Fee Structure >> Scholarship 2025 Scholarship Range XII (Best of 3)*/ CUET IB SAT 50% >=95 >=40 >=1451 30% 85-94.99 37-39 1351-1450 20% 80-84.99 34-36 1301-1350 10% 75-79.99 31-33 1201-1300 View full Scholarship >> Curriculum Details The BCA curriculum , created by industry experts, focuses on hands-on and practical components so as the students are ready for the industry, without the companies having to invest in further coaching. The Board of Studies of School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology, comprises eminent personalities from the Industry and Academia. The program requires students to complete 120 credits to receive a BCA degree, and an additional 15 credits to acquire a BCA Hons degree. The program is enriched with relevant industry certifications on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, Cyber Security, Data Science, DevOps, Full Stack, Drones and Gaming. An aspirant can go through a detailed syllabus for redefining career. BCA Curriculum 2025-29 BCA Curriculum 2024-28 BCA Curriculum 2023-26 BCA Curriculum 2022-25 BCA Curriculum 2021-24 BCA Curriculum 2020-23 Projects The BCA is a completely project-driven program. The students must go through at least one project in each semester, apart from an Industrial Project in the 6th semester. Besides, there is a mandatory Summer Internship program at the end of the 4th semester, and a Capstone project in the 5th Semester. The University believes project-based learning will help the students develop critical thinking, experimental design, data collection, data analysis skills. Honours To get a BCA (Hons) degree, students must earn an additional 15 credits, starting from the 3rd semester to the 5th semester. Specialisation Electives These are a bucket of subjects under each specialisation to be taken up at the start of the 4th semester. Internships The BCA course carries one mandatory credit internship in the summer break coming at the end of the 2nd year of the program. Specialisations The department offers specialisations in Cloud Computing | Cyber Security | Data Science | Artificial Intelligence | DevOps | Full Stack | Drones | Blockchain | Gaming | Mobile Technologies. Data Science Data science is a popular field that examines how digital data reshapes businesses, and how it assists in making crucial decisions. The specialised electives offered under Data Science in BCA are E-commerce and Social Media Analytics, Time Series Analysis and Applications, Human Computer Interaction, Big Data Analytics and Applications, Deep Learning, Data Mining, Predictive Analysis, among other subjects. The BCA syllabus is at par with industry standards. Cloud Computing Cloud Computing is the new way of storing data that provides flexibility, data recovery, minimal maintenance, simple access, and enhanced security. The specialised electives offered under Cloud Computing & Cyber Security are System and Network Security Maintenance, Penetration Testing and Ethical Hacking, Application of Cryptography and Network Security, Application of Blockchain with Cyber Security, Automation Testing, among other subjects. Artificial Intelligence AI plays a big role in determining the way humans and machines interact. Apart from Computer Application industries, the technology plays a significant role in sectors including healthcare, banking and automobiles. A few of the specialised electives for AI are Computational Thinking and Model Design, Application of NLP in Indian Languages, Automated Recognition in Data, Integration of AI models and functional robots, Artificial Intelligence in Gaming: Past, Present, and Future. DevOps The DevOps method hastens the development process, improves quality, and cuts down on unscheduled tasks. The bucket of specialised electives in DevOps include subjects such as DevOps Engineering Practice and Applications, Microservices Architecture in DevOp, Applied DevOps, and Fundamentals of Cloud for Full Stack. Full Stack Full-stack developers are masters of both the back-end and front-end of a process, and are familiar with a variety of technologies. Such professionals are in demand these days as they can maintain every part of the system effectively. Some of the specialised electives offered with Full Stack specialisation are Front End Development, Fundamentals of Cloud for Full Stack, DevOps Engineering Practice and Applications, Server-Side Development using Spring Framework, and many others. Cyber Security With greater dependency on online transactions and other social activities, the demand for a robust Cyber Security framework is at its peak. To prepare students for jobs in the sphere, the CEOs and CXOs of the Data Security Council of India (DSCI), QoS Technologies, IBM, besides other researchers, have helped design a sharp curriculum. Some of the courses in this specialisation are Cyber Security with Blockchain, Malware Analysis for Mobile Devices, Security and Privacy for Big Data Analytics, Penetration Testing, and Ethical Hacking, among others. (To help students work on cutting-edge projects, tie-ups are in place with DSCI, Fortinet, CompTIA and CISCO.) Blockchain The curriculum of Blockchain specialisation is developed in consultation with CEOs and CXOs of BankchainAsset.com, Digiledge, and other subject matter experts. The specialisation electives offered as part of the B.Tech syllabus include Blockchain Technologies, Smart Contracts and Solidity Programming, Digital Currencies and Blockchain, and Modern Cryptography. Gaming TThere is much work happening to bring Augmented Reality (AG) and Virtual Reality (VR) to add to the experience of gaming. In keeping with the trend, Bennett University offers a specialisation elective designed by the CEOs and CXOs of Simulanis, Yeppar,Whitebears Studio, Probability Gaming and Scientific Games. Courses on VR Gaming; VR and 360 Video Production; Animation and Rendering Techniques; Game Mechanics and Game Physics. (Apple Mac Lab, Nao Robot V6 and other head-mounted devices are available to students for their project work.) Drones The drone specialisation builds critical thinking and problem solving skills in addition to technical skills, which benefit students along any career path they choose. Students who pick up the specialisation can contribute to safe infrastructure maintenance, to law enforcement support; and to agricultural management. Mobile Technologies Mobile Technologies specialization encompasses iOS technology. Swift is a powerful and intuitive programming language for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, and watchOS. Writing Swift code is interactive and fun, the syntax is concise yet expressive, and Swift includes modern features developers love. Swift code is safe by design and produces software that runs lightning-fast. Course Outcome After completion of the course: Students will be able to use the basic principles in problem-solving, inference, perception, knowledge representation, and learning solutions. Students will be acquire exceptional skills in leadership, problem solving and communication. Students will be able to apply solid technological foundations, algorithmic concepts and theories of Computer Science in designing, modelling and conducting experiments, and in data analysis and representation. Skills The BCA Students will acquire some of the following advanced skills: Strong sense of professionalism Leadership Soft skill Ability to negotiate group discussions Public speaking The School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology often organise: Personal interview workshops CV and cover letter workshops Networking events Social-media workshops Corporate & professional mentoring sessions for the benefit of students through our career cell Lab Dell-EMC The lab consists of 32 Dell machines with the latest configuration. Moreover, it has the support of Dell-EMC. Dell provides support in faculty training, collaborative projects, and exclusive workshops. Specific areas of operation will include Information Storage and Management (“ISM”), Cloud Infrastructure Services (“CIS”), Data Science and Big Data Analytics (“DSBDA”) and Data Protection and Management (“DPM”). It will help the students to compete for the skills required by the fortune 500 companies. It incorporates information, resources, expertise, training, and knowledge in the field of (i) Information Storage and Management, (ii) Cloud Infrastructure Services, (iii) Data Protection and Management and (iv) Data Science and Big Data Analytics to enable Bennett University to disseminate the information and knowledge. Computer configuration: Dell OptiPlex 5050MT XCTO, Intel i5 processor, 8Gb DDR4 RAM. Bennett - NVIDIA Centre for Artificial Intelligence The Lab consists of 30 node Top-end HP Z440 Workstations. If you want to take your performance in computational processing to the next level, then this is the right choice. This is the right mix for executing your applications which are dealing with zeta scale data. Powerful graphics cards from NVIDIA and lightning-fast data transfers with thunderbolt technologies makes it a wonderful monster to work with. The lab gives you a world-class workstation experience that never slows you down. Our researchers and scholars are using this lab for AI applications in healthcare, security, cognition, text, image and video analysis. In the time to come, this centre will position itself as a leading centre of excellence for bringing a positive addition to the quality of life using AI. The NVIDIA DGX-1 V100 is the first system built with groundbreaking NVIDIA Pascal™-powered Tesla® V100 accelerators. It is NVIDIA NVLink™ implementation that delivers a massive increase in GPU memory capacity. This is the world’s first purpose-built system for deep learning and accelerated analytics, delivering performance equal to 500 conventional servers. It comes fully integrated with hardware, deep learning software, development tools, and runs popular accelerated analytics applications. This means you can immediately shorten data processing time, visualize more data, accelerate deep learning frameworks, and design more sophisticated neural networks. NVIDIA DGX-1 software stack includes major deep learning frameworks, the NVIDIA DIGITS™ GPU training system, the NVIDIA Deep Learning SDK (e.g. CuDNN, NCCL), NVIDIA Docker, GPU drivers, and NVIDIA CUDA® for rapidly designing deep neural networks (DNN). It’s the ideal stack for accelerating popular analytics and visualization software. This powerful system includes access to cloud management services for container creation and deployment, system updates, and an application repository. This combined software can run on Pascal-powered Tesla GPUs allowing applications to run 12X faster than with previous GPU-accelerated solutions. Due to our partnership with NVIDIA, we have access to Certified Expert Instructors, Worldwide Workshops, Online Courses, Epicenter of industry leaders, Onsite Training, Global Reach, NVIDIA Partner Network, OEMs and startups, Deep Learning Teaching Kit. The scope, capacity and potential of this are unlimited. Bennett is striving for cutting-edge research. It’s the ideal stack for accelerating popular analytics and visualization software. Access to cloud management services for container creation and deployment, system updates, and an application repository. Apple iMac Design & Multi-Media Lab It is a futuristic 32 node high-end lab with state-of-the-art Apple machines. As per the International Standards the 27-inch monitors are useful for any visual effects with immersive experience by using most colorful retina display. Processor Speed, Magic Mouse and Magic keyboard makes the experience unforgettable. Lab is for running courses which require high end graphics capabilities including 3D graphics and video editing capabilities. it will be specifically used for activities and projects related to gaming, design, multimedia, modern art and media production. High configuration of the machines will also help in running resource-hungry applications related to research requiring large scale simulations and experimentation. This Centre will work in multimedia design and research. This Centre will be made of Apple iMacs which have very high-end graphics and other components suitable for multimedia research. We will have 32 machines of high configuration to serve the objectives of this lab. It has state of the art systems that are equipped with the requirements for programming instructions to be given to students. Information Management System Lab This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements of Information Management, Data Base Management systems and Other Business processing applications. It is equipped with relevant software and IT infrastructure to fulfil these requirements. Computer configuration: Acer, Intel®core™ i5 6400,64 Bit processor,8Gb RAM. Cloud Computing Lab This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements of Cloud Computing processing and applications. Students get hands-on practical experience through lab work for novel Cloud-Based Computer Laboratory (CBCL) solutions, including certifications like Microsoft Azure etc. It is equipped with relevant software and IT infrastructure to fulfil these requirements. Drone Labs This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Drone base Lab, assembling Drones and implementing the applications. Our Drone Laboratory provides facilities for the development of various drone technologies and sensing equipment which could be applied to the drones using DJI Mavic AIR QUADCOPTER DRONE, Veerobot F450 AIR QUADCOPTER DRONE and many more different drones and technologies. Students use Jetson Nano Nvidia Development Kit, Raspberry Pi etc. The laboratory is equipped with various drone platforms: from quad/hexacopters to pipeline crawlers, to remotely operated underwater vehicles. IoT and Robotics Lab This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements of IoT and Robotics Lab, hands-on experience with our Robo NAO and implementing the applications with NAO V6 HUMANOID ROBOT. Our students build an Artificial Being (ABe), which, unlike humanoid robots, is a congregation of networked mobile robots emulating the various parts/organs of a body in this lab. Computer Network and Cyber Security Lab This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements of Computer Network and Cyber Security Lab on Simulator Tools, including Hardware hand on experience with Routers and the latest CISCO Switches. Students use different wireless network components and features providing network security and to set Firewall Security in windows/ Linux OS for implementing different types of vulnerabilities for hacking websites /Web Applications. Data Structure Labs This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Data Structure Labs. We provide the students with knowledge about Data Structures, Applications of data structures, Problem-solving using data structure tools and Techniques. Students are made familiar with C programming and its applications. AR and VR Gaming This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements in the domain of AR and VR Gaming Labs. Our Virtual Reality, Augmented Reality, and Media Lab contains some of the most cutting-edge head- mounted displays available today. Students have access to the technology for the development of VR and AR software using Oculus Go Standalone Virtual Realty Headset and many other. Microprocessor Lab This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Microprocessing Lab to provide intensive practical exposure to learn the assembly and embedded C programming systematically for the different issues of microprocessors and implement their codes on INTEL ALTERA DE2 115 DEVELOPMENT AND EDUCATION Boards. Big Data and Machine Learning Lab This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Big Data Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence to analyze large datasets, identify trends, and develop predictive models to solve their problems. Where students build and train deep neural networks, build a CNN and apply it to detection and recognition tasks, Train test sets, analyze variance for DL applications, use standard techniques and optimization algorithms, and build neural networks in TensorFlow. Students can Build and train RNNs, work with NLP and Word Embeddings, and use HuggingFace tokenizers and transformer models to perform NER. Quantum Computing Labs This Lab is for taking care of the curricular requirements in the domain of Quantum Computing Lab, for research uses and quantum theory to develop computer technology. Using quantum simulations this lab provides hands-on exercises that execute on real quantum systems provided by IBM Quantum Experience. NIT, Warangal Ms. Shraddha Jain Ph.D. (Pursuing) - IIT BHU, Varanasi ELIGIBILITY BCA Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process Eligibility Domestic Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with minimum 55% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score excluding Hindi ). PROCESS BCA Admission Process for 2025 The selection of a student at Bennett University will be finalized after series of evaluative steps: Submission of the application form with required details by paying non-refundable application fees of Rs. 1500. Merit-based assessment of candidates and release of provisional offer letters. Selected Students to pay advance fee within deadline given. Submit documents and pending fee payment to complete registration as per timelines shared. Start My Application Student Testimonials Aaviral Agarwal BCA (2020-23) Aaviral Agarwal’s Father OUR ASK BCA Frequently Asked Questions Why should I pursue a BCA degree from Bennett University? The BCA degree at Bennett University provides practical learning to students. During the three years of the program, the students will get three relevant industry certifications. Certificates would be related to Cloud Computing, Cybersecurity, Robotic Process Automation, Data Science, and Web Technologies. Besides, the BCA course would include an internship at the end of the second year of the program, and a project in the last semester, with an option to do it in the industry for six months. Name the specialisations offered in BCA? There are nine specialisations available in BCA: Cloud Computing Cyber Security Data Science Artificial Intelligence DevOps Full Stack Drones Blockchain Gaming Mobile Technologies How is the BCA course structured? Top industry professionals and academicians have designed the BCA course. To complete the program, a student would need to earn 120 credits to get a BCA degree and 15 additional credits to earn a BCA (Hons) degree. There is a further division of credits into Core, Electives, and Humanities, as per regulatory guidelines. Students study 4-7 courses related to the specialisations, including the project courses. Master of Computer Applications (MCA) Artificial Intelligence Courses, Syllabus,Fees | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Master of Computer Applications (Artificial Intelligence) Apply Now Master of Computer Applications (Artificial Intelligence) Apply Now About M.Tech Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning About MCA (Artificial Intelligence) The Master of Computer Applications (Artificial Intelligence) at Bennett University is a 2-year postgraduate program designed to equip students with advanced technical and analytical expertise in AI and software development. The curriculum integrates core subjects such as Machine Learning, Agentic AI, Data Analytics, and Intelligent Systems, aligned with NEP 2020 and NCrF guidelines. With a strong focus on experiential learning, it includes a 20-credit Academic/Industry Project and Dissertation to build hands-on, industry-ready skills. Graduates are prepared for high-demand roles like AI Developer, Machine Learning Engineer, Data Scientist, and AI Architect across global technology sectors. Duration 2 Years Specializations 4 Credits 80 Start Date July 2026 Eligibility Criteria Indian National: Passed Graduation in any discipline from recognised university with minimum 50% marks with English as medium of instruction. NRI: The eligibility criterion for all programs for international applicants is minimum 50% in the qualifying examination and having studied the pre-requisite subjects for admission in to the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on Aggregate Graduation Percentage. MCA (Artificial Intelligence) Program Fee Get detailed information about the MCA (Artificial Intelligence) program fee, including tuition, hostel charges, and other applicable costs. Click here for details. Why Choose MCA (Artificial Intelligence) from Bennett University Our MCA (AI) program is designed to bridge the gap between core computer science knowledge and modern AI applications, enabling students to deploy intelligent systems at scale. Applied AI & Development: Focus on developing and deploying scalable, AI-powered software applications. Advanced Programming Core: Rigorous training in Advanced Machine Learning, Agentic AI, and Data/Signal Analytics. Industry-Ready Capstone: 20-credit Academic/Industry Project with Dissertation for real-world problem-solving. NEP 2020 & NCrF Aligned: Flexible structure ensuring outcome-based learning and skill-based progression. Application-Focused: Emphasises development and deployment of AI systems rather than only theoretical research. Specialisations Students can choose specialised electives to develop deep expertise in key AI domains: Signal Processing: Learn Adaptive Signal Processing, AI for Biomedical Signals, and Statistical Signal Processing. Robotics: Explore Deep Learning for Robotics, Bio-Inspired Robotics, and Probabilistic Robotics. Computer Vision: Gain skills in Deep Learning for Computer Vision, Augmented and Virtual Reality (AR/VR). Natural Language Processing (NLP): Master systems for language understanding with courses like Foundation of Large Language Models and Advanced NLU Techniques. Program Objectives The program aims to prepare graduates with practical AI development skills, advanced programming expertise, and strong theoretical grounding to design, develop, and deploy complex AI systems. It transforms learners into industry-ready professionals capable of managing end-to-end AI projects across domains such as Fintech, Robotics, Computer Vision, and Natural Language Processing. Learning Outcomes Upon completing the MCA in AI, students will be able to: Develop End-to-End AI Systems: Implement scalable and efficient AI-driven software solutions. Master Advanced Programming Tools: Gain proficiency in Agentic AI, Advanced ML, and Deep Learning. Integrate AI in Real-World Systems: Build intelligent systems adaptable to business and research environments. Lead AI Projects: Manage multidisciplinary teams and oversee AI project lifecycles. Conduct Applied Research: Undertake real-world projects and dissertations integrating scientific and business insights. Curriculum Details The curriculum is designed with a meticulous and interdisciplinary approach with a focus on core subjects. By taking a holistic approach, it covers not only the technical aspects but also the practical applications of AI technologies. Core Subjects: Mathematics for Computing, Advanced Machine Learning, Advanced Deep Learning, Agentic AI, AI Tools & Applications, Image Processing & Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing & Generative AI. Majors: Master of Computer Applications (MCA) in Artificial Intelligence, emphasising applied learning, coding, and domain specialisation. Minors: Not applicable for this program. Electives: Offered in Signal Processing, Robotics, Computer Vision, and NLP. Research/Dissertation Component: Mandatory 20-credit Capstone Project/Dissertation, contributing to 40 Research/Dissertation Credits out of 80 total. Career Pathways Graduates of the MCA (Artificial Intelligence) program are highly sought after in both technical and managerial roles across industries, leveraging AI technologies. Career Opportunities include AI Developer / Architect, Machine Learning Engineer, Data Scientist / Analyst, AI Solutions Consultant, Software Development Manager (AI Specialisation). Faculty Prof. (Dr.) Rajeev Tiwari Dean, School of Artificial Intelligence Dr. Manoj Sharma Ph.D. - IIT Delhi Dr. Pratyush Pranav Ph.D. - University of Groningen Dr. Dinesh Kumar Ph.D. - University of Coimbra, Portugal Dr. Shakshi Sharma Ph.D. - University of Tartu, Estonia Dr. Neelam Choudhary Ph.D. - IIT Guwahati Dr. Navneet Pratap Singh Ph.D. - IIT Indore Dr. Yajnaseni Dash Ph.D. - IIT Delhi Dr. Gaurav Sharma Ph.D. - NIT, Hamirpur Dr. Prateek Yadav Ph.D.- IIT Delhi OUR ASK MCA (Artificial Intelligence) Frequently Asked Questions What is the primary focus of the MCA (AI) program? The MCA (AI) program is application-oriented, focusing on the implementation, development, and deployment of AI systems using advanced programming, software engineering, and data management practices. What is the difference between MCA (AI) and M.Tech AI? MCA (AI) focuses on the software and application layer of AI—coding, design, and deployment, while M.Tech AI emphasises engineering, algorithms, and hardware optimisation. Is a background in Mathematics mandatory? Yes, candidates should have Mathematics at the 10+2 or undergraduate level to handle algorithmic and analytical coursework. What is the Capstone/Dissertation requirement? Students must complete a 20-credit Academic/Industry Project with a Dissertation, developing a complex, end-to-end AI application. What specialisation tracks are available? Students can specialise in Signal Processing, Robotics, Computer Vision, or NLP through dedicated electives. Sales and Marketing Management It aims to develop in-depth knowledge about: Consumer behavior, Segmenting consumer and business markets, Pricing, Advertising, Brand management, Sales promotion, Public relations, Integrated marketing communications, Application of marketing mix and growth strategy. Finance It aims to develop an in-depth understanding of the framework and concepts of finance, laying a strong emphasis on building problem-solving skills by applying financial analysis tools and techniques to decision-making. Business Analytics It provides the knowledge needed to develop and inculcate descriptive, prescriptive, and predictive business modelling skills through the latest tools/techniques and software for analysing and solving problems, improving business, and implementing changes. Product Management It equips students with the strategic and tactical skills needed to oversee the entire lifecycle of a product. This specialization integrates business, technology, and design, emphasizing the ability to identify market opportunities, create innovative solutions, and bring products successfully to market. MBA students focusing on product management learn key aspects such as market research, product development, launch strategies, and post-launch analysis. School of Law - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login ABOUT Law at Bennett University The School of Law of Bennett University being one of the best Law colleges in India approved by Bar Council of India always thrives to deliver and outstanding academic, research and industry-focused education to make law students ready for facing courtroom challenges, drafting of lawsuit/cases from respondent or complainant’s side. Besides, the varsity also trains students to handle corporate and commercial law related issues and passing resolution for the same. The school offers enriching programs such as B.A. LL.B (Hons.), BBA LL.B (Hons.), LL.M. and Ph.D. The curriculum of the program offered in the school are designed in such a way that it stives to create awareness about the 17 Sustainable Development Goals among the students. The mapping of the courses, research and other activities at the school level may be accessed in the attached document. Click Here Being a top private law college in India, the university offers world class infrastructure comprising amphitheatre classrooms, dedicated law library, Moot Court Hall to name a few to get the industry set up. The school is promoted by The Times Group that has more than 180 years of media legacy, and been the clearest and most representative voice for people of India and for their rights. Read More × Prof. (Dr.) Pradeep Kulshreshtha Dean, School of Law Dear Students, In the era marked by rapid economic growth, technological advancements, and evolving global affairs, the role of legal education stands at the forefront. The swift transitions not only present challenges but also create opportunities for continuous learning and adaptation, shaping legal professionals capable of meeting contemporary demands. At Bennett School of Law, our mission is to nurture future-ready professionals by providing robust exposure to interdisciplinary education, project-based learning, clinical legal education, and critical thinking, all while striving for excellence. Our School has made significant strides through experiential learning opportunities such as legal aid, mooting, internships, debating, and more. These platforms immerse students in real-world legal challenges, allowing them to refine their advocacy skills while upholding the values of empathy, justice, and service. Backed by the 187-year-old Times Group legacy, Bennett University, with NAAC A+ accreditation, remains committed to academic excellence, innovation, and research, continuing its mission to shape socially conscious changemakers. The School of Law promotes a robust research culture through its twenty specialised research cells addressing contemporary legal and societal issues and engaging faculty and students in legal and policy research. There is a robust faculty mentoring system in place ensuring a smooth and enriching transition from school to university and thereafter to the professional world. Thank you for choosing Bennett University in your pursuit of higher education with a purpose of positive social impact. Believe in yourself and remain a lifelong learner. B.A. (Hons.) Economics - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login B.A. (Hons.) Economics The economics major at the School of Liberal Arts trains students in the field of economics in an interdisciplinary setting. Economics is the study of how societies allocate scarce resources and make decisions. It is a discipline that provides insights into a wide range of real-world issues, including inflation, unemployment, economic growth, trade policies, and market structures. Economics combines theoretical models with empirical analysis to help understand human behaviour, markets, and institutions. The economics program is designed to: (i) provide students with a strong foundation in microeconomics and macroeconomics in the first two semesters; (ii) develop analytical skills through rigorous coursework in econometrics, mathematical economics, and applied economics in the following semesters: and (iii) offer advanced electives and research opportunities in areas such as development economics, public policy, and financial economics in the final year. Theory courses – 3-4 lectures a week depending on course credits, each lasting 55 minutes. Research courses – Data-driven assignments and projects. The curriculum is designed to offer a strong academic foundation through Major-Core courses, complemented by Major-Electives for added specialization. Major-Core At the heart of the program, the Major-Core (MJ) curriculum delivers essential and advanced coursework, with students required to complete a minimum of 45 credits, up to a maximum of 60 credits. Mathematics for Economics Microeconomics I Statistics for Economics Microeconomics II Macroeconomics I Game Theory History and Economics Econometrics I Macroeconomics II Development Economics International Economics Advanced Econometrics Complexity Economics Cost Effectiveness Analysis Development Economics Major-Elective To further enrich their learning experience, students can select from Major-Electives (MJ-Elec), offering additional flexibility and breadth, with credit options ranging from a minimum of 27 to a maximum of 36 credits. Industrial Organization Economics of Culture Mechanism Design Environment Economics Economics of AI Behavioral Economics Labor Economics Economics of Organizations Time Series & Financial Econometrics International Finance Money and Banking Casual Methods Survey Designs Business Cycles Economics of Tourism Economics of Sports Self-Designed Course Admission Counsellors Connect with our counsellors for any admissions related queries. explore Fee Structure Know more about fee structure. explore Scholarship Know more about Scholarship. explore Fee Structure 2025 Major: Psychology | Political Science & IR | Economics | English | Business Studies | Philosophy | Sociology Fee Type Year 1 Year 2 Year 3 TOTAL Tuition fee (₹) 4,70,000 4,70,000 4,70,000 14,10,000 Registration Fees One Time (₹) 45,000 – – 45,000 Total Fees (₹) 5,15,000 4,70,000 4,70,000 14,55,000 * Fee is subject to revision with the new academic year as per Bennett University policy. NRI Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score). Student Testimonials This course has taught me many things, like the society we live in, whereas subjects like Sociology and Psychology have helped me grasp various concepts Priyansha BA Liberal Arts Batch: 2020-23 The faculty and coordinators of Bennett University have given it all to make the virtual transition a more effortless process. Tanvi BA Liberal Arts Batch: 2020-23 Events BA Honours Programmes Frequently Asked Questions When was the B.A Liberal Arts program initiated under the School of Liberal Arts at Bennett University? The Liberal Arts program started with its first batch in 2020. It offers the opportunity to learn from highly qualified and experienced teachers. The program's foundation rests on two pillars -- the development of all-around personality and life skills. What are the majors available in the BA Liberal Arts course at Bennett University? The students can choose Majors from seven domains: Psychology, Political Science & IR, Economics, English, Business Studies, Philosophy & Sociology How is the BA Liberal Arts curriculum structured? In the first semester of the program, the students will be introduced to all the majors to select the Major which suits them the most. The choice of Majors and Electives is given from the second semester onwards. How do electives benefit a student's career? An elective is a combination of courses taken through the duration of university education. The courses taken as electives allow students to understand the world with exposure to a wide range of subjects and areas of practice. This broader understanding is a critical requirement to succeed in the world of the future. Can an undergraduate student take part in research activities? Bennett University focuses on research, experiential, and applied learning. Undergraduate students are encouraged to work with members of the faculty in their live research projects. Such exposure makes students understand the intricacies of the research process and deepens their knowledge Do you assist students in placements, summer internships, and industry tours? Bennett University has a dedicated Career Services Centre that works closely with the industry to participate in guest lectures, curricular inputs, joint program development, internships, and placements. What are the future career options after pursuing BA Liberal Arts? BA Liberal Arts graduates can look for rewarding careers in advertising, public relations, financial analysis, design, journalism, publishing, corporate communications, marketing, etc., or start a successful entrepreneurial journey of their own. What kind of industry exposure do students have access to? This is one of the unique hallmarks of the BA Liberal Arts program as it benefits from faculty members teaching courses in management, which in turn, provides students an opportunity to continuously interact with industry experts from India and abroad. Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA) Artificial Intelligence Course, eligibility, Syllabus, Fees International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Bachelor of Computer Applications (Artificial Intelligence) Apply Now Bachelor of Computer Applications (Artificial Intelligence) Apply Now About M.Tech Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning About BCA (Artificial Intelligence) The Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA) Artificial Intelligence at Bennett University nurtures the next generation of students with the potential to revolutionise the AI landscape through rigorous training in fundamental research and application. The program focuses on both theoretical and practical applications of AI, preparing students to leverage the latest developments and technology to address pressing issues across diverse fields. The curriculum is interdisciplinary and innovation-driven, equipping students with the skills for high-demand roles like Data Scientist and Machine Learning Engineer. Key domains where students apply their knowledge include Agri-tech, Digital Healthcare, Fintech, Robotics, Media, and Environment Sustainability. The program includes a 20% research-focused component, such as industry certifications and projects with societal impact, ensuring students are industry-ready upon graduation. Duration 3 Years/4 Years (Hons. /Hons. with Research) Specialisations 14 Credits 120 (3-Year) 160 (4-Year) Start Date August 2026 Eligibility Criteria Indian National: Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with English and Mathematics & any other thrid * subject with minimum 60% marks OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI: Pass with 55% aggrigate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR) *Third subject should not be vocational: Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc. Selection Process Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third* subject with highest score) *Third subject should not be vocational: Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc. BCA (Artificial Intelligence) Program Fee Get detailed information about the BCA (Artificial Intelligence) program fee structure, including tuition, hostel fee, and other applicable charges Click here for details. Why BCA (Artificial Intelligence) from Bennett University Our program is designed to equip students with the necessary knowledge and tools to excel in the rapidly evolving field of Artificial Intelligence. Comprehensive Knowledge: The program equips students with comprehensive knowledge of the foundational mathematical and computational aspects of AI. Practical & Innovation-Driven: Involves practical learning with state-of-the-art AI tools, technologies, and projects. Fosters an innovation-driven environment through Hackathons and AI Competitions. Interdisciplinary Focus: Promotes interdisciplinary research and provides a comprehensive understanding of real-world challenges. Industry Relevance: Curriculum is tailored to meet the industry’s demands and prepares students for a successful career. Specialisations The program emphasises an interdisciplinary approach with a focus on core AI/ML subjects. Students also have the option to choose from the following specialisations. Digital Healthcare: Students gain expertise in applying AI to clinical decision support, medical imaging, and patient-centric healthcare systems. AgriTech: Focuses on leveraging AI and machine learning for precision farming, crop monitoring, yield prediction, and sustainable agriculture. Data Science: Emphasis on advanced analytical and modelling skills to extract insights from structured and unstructured datasets. Robotics: Enables students to design and build intelligent autonomous systems with capabilities in perception, control, and real-time decision-making. FinTech: Students explore AI-driven financial technologies, including automated decision systems, risk modelling, and digital financial services. MediaTech: Develops an understanding of AI-enabled content intelligence, personalised recommendations, and audience analytics in digital media. LegalTech: Students gain exposure to NLP-based tools that automate legal research, contract analysis, and document review processes. Quantum Information Processing: Explores the intersection of AI and quantum mechanics to build next-generation algorithms and computational frameworks. Environment & Sustainability: Focuses on applying AI for climate modelling, pollution monitoring, and smart resource management to promote sustainable development. Signal Processing: Students learn core techniques for analysing and interpreting audio, image, and sensor signals for advanced AI applications. Next-Generation Networks: Enables students to apply AI/ML for designing, optimising, and securing future communication systems, including 5G and 6G networks. Program Objectives To train students in the foundational mathematical and computational aspects of AI and empower them to develop and apply intelligent systems. The program aims to cultivate strong analytical skills, problem-solving abilities, and creativity to tackle real-world challenges through research and practical projects. Learning Outcomes Upon completing the BCA in AI, students will be able to: Develop Responsible AI: Possess a deeper understanding of the mathematical and statistical aspects that form the fundamental pillars of Artificial Intelligence to develop responsible AI frameworks. Master AI Concepts: Master essential AI concepts, including Machine Learning, Inference Engines, Speech Recognition, Computer Vision, Natural Language Understanding, Robotics, and Human-Computer Interaction. Design Systems: Integrate human cognition, AI, and data engineering knowledge to design holistic systems. Drive Research: Possess an understanding of fundamental aspects of AI to drive research towards next-generation AI tools. Build Robust Applications: Develop real-time, robust AI-based systems tailored to specific software, hardware, and data constraints. Curriculum Details The curriculum is designed with a meticulous and interdisciplinary approach with a focus on core AI/ML subjects. By taking a holistic approach, it covers not only the technical aspects but also the practical applications of AI technologies. Students also have the option to choose minor options in diverse areas, which is part of the curriculum's structure. The BCA (Artificial Intelligence) curriculum follows the NEP 2020 guidelines with multiple entry/exit options and a credit framework. Core Subjects: Programming in Python and C++, Machine Learning & Deep Learning Algorithms, Cyber Security, Cloud, Data Structures, Computer Vision, LLMs, Generative AI & others. Specialisation Options: Digital Healthcare, AgriTech, Data Science, Robotics, FinTech, MediaTech, LeagalTech, Quantum Information Processing, Environment & Sustainability, Signal Processing, Next Generation Networks & others. Research Component: 20% research-focused coursework, including industry certifications and projects. Career Pathways Graduates of the BCA (Artificial Intelligence) program are highly sought after across diverse industries such as Information Technology, Fin-tech, Gaming, and Virtual Reality (VR). They can pursue dynamic and rewarding career roles, including Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, AI Researcher, AI Consultant, Big Data Analyst and Natural Language Processing (NLP) Engineer, among others. Faculty Prof. (Dr.) Rajeev Tiwari Dean, School of Artificial Intelligence Dr. Manoj Sharma Ph.D. - IIT Delhi Dr. Pratyush Pranav Ph.D. - University of Groningen Dr. Dinesh Kumar Ph.D. - University of Coimbra, Portugal Dr. Shakshi Sharma Ph.D. - University of Tartu, Estonia Dr. Neelam Choudhary Ph.D. - IIT Guwahati Dr. Navneet Pratap Singh Ph.D. - IIT Indore Dr. Yajnaseni Dash Ph.D. - IIT Delhi Dr. Gaurav Sharma Ph.D. - NIT, Hamirpur Dr. Prateek Yadav Ph.D.- IIT Delhi OUR ASK BCA (Artificial Intelligence) Program Frequently Asked Questions What is the course curriculum of the BCA (Artificial Intelligence) program? The curriculum balances theoretical research and practical applications, with an interdisciplinary approach and minor options. It includes a 20% research-focused component, like industry certifications and projects with societal impact. It aligns with NEP 2020, offering multiple entry/exit options and various credit categories. What are the career prospects after completing BCA AI? There is high demand across IT, Finance, Gaming, and VR industries. Roles include Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, AI Researcher, etc. What are the skills required to excel in the BCA AI program? Strong analytical skills, problem-solving, critical thinking, and creativity are essential. Proficiency in Python, C++, machine learning algorithms, data structures, and computer vision is also beneficial. Is it necessary to have a background in Computer Science to pursue BCA in Artificial Intelligence? It is not necessary, but foundational knowledge in programming, algorithms, and data structures will be beneficial. What is the difference between BCA (Artificial Intelligence) and BCA Computer Science? BCA (AI) focuses on intelligent agents (e.g., machine learning, robotics, NLP), while BCA (CS) covers broader topics like software development, database systems, networking, and computer architecture. Are there hands-on projects and industry certifications integrated into the course? Yes, the curriculum involves practical learning with state-of-the-art AI tools, Hackathons, and includes a 20% research-focused component for industry certifications and projects. Does the BCA (Artificial Intelligence) program include a research component? Yes, the program has a 20% research-focused component that includes industry certifications and projects with societal impact. Which programming languages are taught in the first year? Foundational programming is typically taught in the first year, including languages like C/C++ and Python, which are crucial for AI and Machine Learning applications. M.Tech. in Biotechnology Course, Curriculum, Eligibility, Fee | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Master of Technology ( Biotechnology ) Apply Now Master of Technology ( Biotechnology ) Apply Now About M.Tech. (Biotechnology) The M.Tech.(Biotechnology) program at Bennett University is a two-year, research-intensive program designed to develop professionals who can apply biotechnology innovations to healthcare, industry, and the environment. With four unique specialisations, the program integrates molecular biology, genetic engineering, and computational sciences to build interdisciplinary expertise. Graduates emerge with advanced research, analytical, and problem-solving skills, ready to lead in biotechnology, pharmaceuticals, and the life sciences. Duration 2 Years Specialisations 4 Credits 80 Start Date July 2026 Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: A bachelor’s degree in Biotechnology/Bioinformatics/Medical Biotechnology, OR an equivalent of an M.Sc. degree in Biotechnology OR any life science–related course, with a minimum of 50% marks (or equivalent), with English as the medium of instruction. NRIs: Applicants must have a minimum 50% in the qualifying examination and have studied the pre-requisite subjects for admission into the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on the Aggregate Graduation Percentage followed by interview. M.Tech. (Biotechnology) Program Fee Get complete details of the M.Tech. Biotechnology program fee, including tuition, hostel, and other applicable charges. Click here for details. Why M.Tech. Biotechnology from Bennett University? Interdisciplinary Expertise: Combines biology, engineering, and computational sciences to develop advanced problem-solving and innovation skills. Research-Driven Learning: Emphasises hands-on research, molecular techniques, and cutting-edge lab experience. Industry Integration: Strong linkage with the biopharma, healthcare, and biotech industries for internships and placements. High Growth Potential: Expanding global demand in healthcare, agriculture, bioinformatics, and sustainable technologies. Career Versatility: Opens pathways to R&D, academia, entrepreneurship, and international research opportunities. Specialisations Biomanufacturing: Focuses on large-scale biologics production using cell cultures and bioprocess engineering. Students learn quality control and GMP standards, preparing for roles in biopharma and sustainable biotechnology. Nanotechnology: Explores nanoscale materials and biosensors for diagnostics, drug delivery, and environmental use. Integrates nanofabrication and surface chemistry, equipping students for biomedical and nano-biotech research careers. Computational Biology: Merges biology and computer science to analyse genomic and proteomic data using AI and modelling tools. Prepares students for roles in drug design, genomics, and personalised medicine. Drug Discovery: Focuses on developing therapeutic compounds through molecular biology, genomics, and protein engineering. Trains students in target identification and preclinical research for the pharmaceutical and precision medicine sectors. Program Objectives The M.Tech in Biotechnology program aims to develop professionals with a strong foundation in biotechnology concepts, research methodologies, and technological skills to innovate in healthcare and industrial applications. Graduates will be equipped to design and develop novel bioproducts, address emerging biological challenges, and integrate socio-economic insights to become successful bioentrepreneurs and leaders in the biotechnology sector. Learning Outcomes Graduates will demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of biotechnology principles and research methodologies, enabling them to solve real-world healthcare and industrial challenges. They will possess the technical, analytical, and innovative skills to design novel bioproducts, uphold bioethical standards, communicate effectively, work collaboratively in multidisciplinary teams, and exhibit entrepreneurial and leadership abilities for sustainable biotechnological advancement. Curriculum Highlights Core Courses: Molecular Biology, Genetic Engineering, Bioprocess Technology, Bioinformatics Specialisations: Drug Discovery, Biomanufacturing, Nanotechnology & Biosensors, Computational Biology Laboratory Training: Hands-on experience with advanced instruments and biotech tools Research Project: One-year industry or academic project to foster innovation and R&D skills For detailed curriculum, click here Career Pathways Graduates of the M.Tech in Biotechnology program can pursue careers in research, academia, industry, or government sectors. Employment opportunities include pharma, healthcare, food, and environmental industries. Graduates may also develop entrepreneur skillset or pursue a Ph.D. in emerging biotechnological fields. Faculty Prof. N. Latha Ph.D. - IIT, Delhi Dr. Saurabh Jyoti Sarma Ph.D. - Universite du Québec, Canada Dr. Deepa Khare Ph.D. - POSTECH, South Korea Dr. Sarika Chaudhary Ph.D. - Banasthali Vidyapith Dr. Vibhuti Joshi Ph.D. - IIT, Jodhpur Dr. Vaishali Verma Ph.D. - University of Delhi Dr. Garima Juyal Ph.D. - University of Delhi South Campus Dr. Atul Vashist Ph.D. - AIIMS, New Delhi Dr. Lopa Pattanaik Ph.D. - IIT, Delhi Dr. Varun Vyas Ph.D. - University of Milan, Italy Dr. Pradeep Pant Ph.D.- IIT Delhi Dr. Krishna Kant Ph.D. - Flinders University, Adelaide, Australia OUR ASK M.Tech (Biotechnology) Program Frequently Asked Questions What are the specialisations offered? The program offers four specialisations: Biomanufacturing, Nanotechnology, Computational Biology and Drug Discovery with opportunities to choose electives or projects in these fields. What skills will I gain through M.Tech Biotech program? With M.Tech Biotechnology, students will develop skills in molecular biology, genetic engineering, bioinformatics, bioprocess design, and computational analysis, along with critical thinking, scientific writing, teamwork, and entrepreneurship. What are the career prospects after M.Tech. Biotechnology? Graduates can work in biopharma, healthcare, agriculture, and environmental sectors as R&D scientists, bioprocess engineers, bioinformatics analysts, or quality control experts. Opportunities also exist in academia, research institutes, and biotech startups. Does the program include practical or research training? Yes, the Master of Technology in Biotech emphasises hands-on laboratory work, dissertation projects, internships, and industry collaborations, ensuring experiential learning and research competence in emerging biotechnological areas. What companies recruit M.Tech Biotechnology graduates? Leading recruiters of M.Tech Biotechnology graduates include Biocon, Serum Institute, Syngene, Dr. Reddy’s, Thermo Fisher Scientific, Pfizer, Novozymes, and IQVIA, as well as reputed research institutes. What facilities are available for students who pursue Master degree in Biotechnology from Bennett University? Students have access to state-of-the-art laboratories equipped for molecular biology, cell culture, genomics, proteomics, nanotechnology, and computational biology, supported by high-end analytical instruments and software tools. Are there opportunities to collaborate with industry or research institutes? Yes, the program encourages industry internships and projects with national and international biotech organisations and universities. What kind of research projects can I work on? Projects cover areas like drug discovery, genetic engineering, molecular diagnostics, tissue engineering, bioinformatics, nanobiotechnology, and sustainable bioprocesses, under faculty or industry mentorship. How is M.Tech different from M.Sc. in Biotechnology? M.Sc. (Biotechnology) focuses more on theoretical and research aspects, meanwhile, M.Tech (Biotechnology) emphasises the engineering and technological applications of biotechnology, including process design, product development, and industrial applications. BBA Global - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Global Pathways Program - BBA Global Study at University of Essex, UK Apply Now Global Pathways Program - BBA Global Study at University of Essex, UK Apply Now Global Pathways Program – BBA Global (2+1) BBA Global (2+1) is an exciting study abroad program at Bennett University that provides students with the opportunity to complete the initial years at Bennett University, India and transfer to a globally renowned institution – University of Essex, UK for final completion. Students complete two years at Bennett University and one year at the University of Essex, receiving globally recognized BBA Business Administration degree* from University of Essex, UK. Graduates receive a globally recognized degree under the Global Pathways Program (GPP), equipping them with international business acumen, leadership skills, and cross-cultural competencies. Program Details Name Duration University Curriculum Highlights BBA Global (2+1) 3 Years Years 1 & 2 at Bennett University , India Business Fundamentals, Finance, Marketing, HR, and Strategy Year 3 at University of Essex , UK Advanced Business Studies, International Business Strategy, Electives Degree Based on specialization: BBA Business Administration, B.Sc. Marketing, B.Sc. International Business and Entrepreneurship, B.Sc. International Business and Finance, B.Sc. Business Administration and Supply Chain Management, B.Sc. Management and Marketing, B.Sc. Business Management, B.Sc. Accounting, B.Sc. Accounting and Finance, B.Sc. Accounting with Economics, B.Sc. Accounting and Management, B.Sc. Finance, B.Sc. Banking and Finance, and B.Sc. Finance and Management Program Objectives To provide students with an international business education with real-world exposure. To develop critical thinking, leadership and entrepreneurship skills with global standards. To enhance career prospects in global business, finance, marketing and management. Global Academic Experience Students gain international exposure by studying at globally recognized universities. Seamless Credit Transfer Academic programs are carefully mapped to ensure a smooth transition between institutions. Cost-Effective Pathway Students save on overall tuition and living costs compared to a full-length international program. Internationally Recognized Degree Graduates earn a degree from a reputed partner university, enhancing their global career prospects. Cultural and Professional Growth Exposure to diverse academic, cultural, and professional environments enriches personal and career development. ABOUT Why Choose BBA Global (2+1) Access to globally recognized business degree from top university. Internship & networking opportunities in India and the UK. Cultural & academic diversity, enriching students’ global perspectives. A vibrant, student-centric environment with excellent support for international students By completing initial years in India, students can save up to INR 40-50 lakhs compared to completing a full degree abroad. Eligible for up to 2 years of post-study work permit. Partner University University of Essex, UK University of Essex, established in 1964, is a distinguished public research university located in Colchester, United Kingdom. Renowned for its commitment to pushing boundaries and fostering curiosity, Essex has built a reputation for academic excellence and innovative research. The university is recognized for research excellence in computer science, sociology, economics and econometrics, politics and international studies, modern languages and linguistics. OUR ASK BBA Global (2+1) Frequently Asked Questions What is the Global Pathways Programme at Bennett University? The Global Pathways Programme (GPP) is an academic collaboration that allows students to complete the first two years of their BBA Global program at Bennett University and transfer to University of Essex, UK, for the remaining one year. Upon successful completion, students earn a globally recognized degree - BBA Business Administration from University of Essex, UK. What are the benefits of enrolling in this program? The BBA Global Program at Bennett University comes with many benefits including internationally recognized degree from a top U.K. university, cost-efficiency compared to completing full graduation abroad, seamless credit transfer with a structured curriculum, global exposure & cultural diversity by studying in both India and the UK, and post-study work permit in the UK for up to two years. What is the eligibility criterion for the BBA Global Program? Students must have: Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with minimum 60% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. Student must comply with the English language proficiency requirements (IELTS/ TOEFL as per partner university guidelines). How does the 2+1 model in the BBA Global Pathways Program work? Years 1 & 2: : Study at Bennett University, India – Foundations in Business fundamentals, finance, marketing, HR, and strategy Years 3: Transfer to University of Essex, UK – Advanced business studies, international business strategy, electives. What degree will I receive after completing the program? You will be awarded a globally recognized BBA Business Administration degree from University of Essex, UK. How much cost savings can I expect? Yes, Iowa State University offers scholarships based on CGPA: By following the 2+1 model, students save approximately ₹40-50 lakhs, compared to completing all three years at University of Essex, U.K. What career opportunities are available after this program? Opportunities in global companies such as Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Facebook etc. Eligibility for a U.K. post-study work permit for up to 1-2 years. Research & internship opportunities at University of Essex, UK. How do I apply for the BBA Global (2+1) program? MBA (Business Analytics) | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login MBA Business Analytics Apply Now MBA Business Analytics Apply Now About MBA Business Analytics The MBA specialisation in Business Analytics at Bennett University integrates analytical intelligence with operational excellence to prepare students for data-driven decision-making and business optimisation. The program equips students with strong quantitative, analytical, and managerial skills, enabling them to interpret complex data and improve organisational performance across industries. Students gain hands-on exposure to advanced analytical tools, emerging technologies, and modern process frameworks, along with real-world applications through projects, simulations, and case studies. International exposure programs in Malaysia and Singapore provide insights into global business environments and innovative operational strategies. The Business Analytics Club “Statopia” further enriches learning through industry interactions, guest lectures, and exposure to emerging analytics trends. Duration 2 Years Specialisations NA Credits - Start Date July 2026 Curriculum Highlights The MBA (Business Analytics) specialisation equips students with skills to leverage data and technology for optimising business processes. The core course in Python Programming builds foundational coding and data cleaning skills. Electives cover a range of topics, including Business Analytics, Machine Learning & AI, Deep Learning, and Data Mining & Visualisation to enable advanced analytical capabilities. Courses like Analytics for Service Operation and Analytics for Sustainable Supply Chain focus on applying analytics to improve operational efficiency and sustainability. Additional electives such as Project Management, Digital Business Modelling, and AI Techniques in Analytics prepare students for strategic decision-making and leadership in data-driven environments. This curriculum integrates technical proficiency, analytical thinking, and operational expertise, readying students for high-impact roles in modern businesses. Core Courses Introduction to Python Programming Electives Business Analytics Project Management Analytics for Service Operation Machine Learning & AI Deep Learning Data Mining & Visualisation Text Mining and Sentiment Analysis Programming for Analytics Analytics for Sustainable Supply Chain Digital Business Modelling AI Techniques in Analytics Tools, Software & Certifications Offered Power BI Tableau MS Excel Python SQL R SPSS Why MBA (Business Analytics) from Bennett University High Industry Relevance: Analytics skills for modern business challenges. Practical Exposure: Real-world projects, case studies, and simulations. Technical Readiness: Hands-on with Python, R, SQL, Tableau, and Power BI. World-Class Faculty: Insights from global analytics and operations experts. Future-Ready Careers: Opportunities in IT and technology-driven firms. Program Objectives The program aims to build strong analytical and managerial problem-solving skills, gain hands-on exposure to industry-relevant analytical tools and techniques, strengthen evidence-based decision-making and strategic thinking, equip themselves for leadership roles requiring both analytical and operational expertise, and prepare to handle real-world business challenges using advanced analytical frameworks. Learning Outcomes On completing their MBA in Business Analytics, students will learn to: Focus on building analytical and managerial skills to tackle complex operational challenges Gain practical experience with industry-relevant technologies and tools Strengthen strategic thinking and evidence-based decision-making Apply real-world insights to optimise business operations Develop the ability to lead data-driven and operational initiatives Prepare for careers requiring both analytics and operational expertise Career Pathways With an MBA in Business Analytics, graduates are well prepared for specialist and managerial roles across Analytics and Operations, enabling them to work as Business or Data Analysts Operations and Supply Chain Analysts or Managers Operations Research Specialists Data Analysts Data Visualisation Specialists ML Analysts Data Scientists Project Managers (Analytics/IT Projects) Analytics Consultants Operations or Service Operations Analysts Text Mining Specialists Process Improvement Consultants Digital Transformation Analysts These professionals are highly sought after across hiring sectors such as technology services, consulting, e-commerce, FMCG, logistics, manufacturing, healthcare, banking and financial services, retail, and other industries, increasingly adopting data-driven operational systems. Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Passed Graduation in any discipline from a recognised university with a minimum 50% marks with English as the medium of instruction NRI: For international applicants, a minimum of 50% in the qualifying examination is required, along with having studied the prerequisite subjects for admission to the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on the weighted score of CAT / XAT / NMAT / MAT / CMAT / GMAT / BU MAT/ CUET Percentile score & Graduation & Personal Interview. The MBA in Business Analytics focuses on building strong data-driven decision-making capabilities combined with operational excellence. Students learn how to analyse business data, optimise processes, and apply analytical insights to real-world managerial and operational challenges. Do I need a technical or programming background to pursue an MBA in Business Analytics? No prior technical background is mandatory. The program starts with foundational courses like Introduction to Python Programming, gradually building analytical and technical skills through hands-on learning, ensuring students from diverse academic backgrounds can succeed. What analytical tools and technologies will I learn during this specialisation? During an MBA in Business Analytics, students gain practical exposure to industry-relevant tools such as Python, R, SQL, Tableau, Power BI, MS Excel, and SPSS, along with advanced concepts in Machine Learning, AI, Deep Learning, and Data Visualisation. How is the curriculum structured for Business Analytics? The curriculum includes a strong core in programming and analytics fundamentals, followed by electives such as Business Analytics, Machine Learning & AI, Data Mining & Visualisation, Analytics for Service Operation, and Analytics for Sustainable Supply Chain, integrating technical expertise with business applications. What kind of practical exposure will I get in this specialisation? Students work on real-world projects, case studies, simulations, and industry-oriented assignments. The Business Analytics Club “Statopia” further enhances exposure through guest lectures, industry interactions, and discussions on emerging trends. What career roles can I expect after completing an MBA in Business Analytics? Graduates can pursue roles such as Business Analyst, Data Analyst, Operations Analyst, Data Scientist, ML Analyst, Analytics Consultant, Project Manager (Analytics/IT), Digital Transformation Analyst, and other analytics-driven leadership roles across industries. School of Computer Science and Engineering - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login School of Computer Science Engineering & Technology ABOUT School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology - Bennett University The School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology, Bennet University, offers a B.Tech. course that gives equal importance to theory and hands-on training. Download Brochure Dean’s Message Prof.(Dr.) Abhay Bansal Dean, School of Computer Science Engineering & TechnologyDean, International Affairs and Corporate Outreach (IRCO) On behalf of our faculty, staff and students, I welcome you to the School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology at Bennett University. Read More × Prof.(Dr.) Abhay Bansal Dean, School of Computer Science Engineering & Technology Dean, International Affairs and Corporate Outreach (IRCO) On behalf of our faculty, staff and students, I welcome you to the School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology at Bennett University. The School of Computer Science Engineering & Technology is an exciting place to be, providing an environment that fosters the holistic development of students. Our aim is to transform students into lifelong learners, equipping them with the skills and adaptability needed to thrive in an ever-changing world. With a focus on innovation and leading-edge technology the University is successfully moulding young minds into becoming fortified, proficient professionals who are “life and industry ready.” A niche curriculum designed by Industry leaders, delivered by experienced and highly qualified faculty and a deep industry interaction through the Times Group Connect proves to be a great benefit to the students. The School of Computer Science Engineering at Bennett University aims to achieve academic excellence by ensuring that the students gain conceptual knowledge and hands-on/practical training using Super Computing Facilities, Apple iMac Design and Multimedia Lab, High End AR VR & Gaming Lab, Drone Labs, IoT and Robotics Lab, Computer Network & Cyber Security Lab to name few. The inclusion of Multiple Seminar/ Guest Lectures/Internship/Project components in the curriculum makes it even better as it enhances the chances of interactions with the experts from different domains and familiarizes the student with the trend being followed in real life across the international markets. Maximum faculty are PhD holders from reputed Institutes(IIT/NIT/IIIT) in India and abroad and possess post-doctoral experience from some of the best Universities abroad. Our faculty members are not only distinguished academicians but also industry experts who bring real-world insights into the classroom. They are dedicated to mentoring and guiding you through your academic journey, ensuring that you are well-prepared to tackle the challenges of the future. The school offers Undergraduate, Post graduate and Doctoral level programs. The programs aim to blend rigorous academic instruction with real-world experiential learning. The school offers 16 specializations, i.e., AI, Blockchain, Cyber Security, Data Science, Gaming, Robotic Process Automation, Augmented & Virtual Reality, IoT and Robotics, Robotics Process Automation, DevOps, Full Stack, Cloud Computing, Quantum Computing, Drones, Product Design Technology, Mobile Technologies, and UI/UX. The knowledge of specialization helps the students to dive deep into the domain of their interest, which further leads to measurable outcomes in terms of getting offers from reputed industries. The School of Computer Science Engineering and Technology has over 80 collaborations with some of the top Universities of the world. Further, the International collaborations also provide you with opportunities for Exchange programs, Joint research projects, and exposure to diverse educational methodologies, aimed at enhancing your academic and professional development. Regularly our students are going to World Top Ranked universities like Georgia Tech, USA, University of Berkeley, USA, Babson College, USA, University of British Columbia, Canada, National University of Singapore, Singapore, University of Florida, USA for Research Internship, Semester Abroad, Summer Program etc School has implemented best practices of New Education Policy and offers open electives, Minor, Honor, and Audit courses. Students choose their pathway based on their interests and are ready to be the world citizen to contribute to society's growth. We are committed to delivering quality education through superior learning infrastructure, experienced faculty, a focus on innovation, intensive industry interaction and an emphasis on cultivating life skills. We believe that every student's needs and learning abilities are unique. Therefore, the flexible and individualized pedagogical approach employed by our faculty facilitates ease of learning, allowing each student to progress at their own pace and in their own style. Bennett University is a knowledge space to acquire a professional degree and shape the compassionate individual under the professional robe. As you embark on this exciting journey, I encourage you to take full advantage of the numerous opportunities available to you. Engage actively in your studies, participate in extracurricular activities, join student organizations, and seek out internships and research projects. These experiences will enrich your education and help you develop a well-rounded skill set. Remember, your time at Bennett University is not just about earning a degree; it is about growing as a person, building lifelong friendships, and preparing yourself for a successful career. We are here to support you every step of the way, and we are confident that your time with us will be both rewarding and transformative. Programs B. Tech (Computer Science Engineering) - 4 Yrs. Career Support for students: Internship and certification opportunities for students × School of CSET is a research-intensive school that aims to create and share technical advancements for complex problems. The school comprises of a complete spectrum of a research ecosystem. The school has faculty members with diverse research interests and state of the art facilities. The research at SCSET can be quantified in terms of research publications, research fundings, patents, PhD scholars, MoUs, awards, recognitions, and consultancy projects.
