to shine in a competitive work environment. BU has partnered with ClassAvatar to impart education in the Metaverse using Virtual Reality with proprietary software. This collaboration includes joint research, workshops, webinars, short-term academic programs, training of students, academics, and staff and the setting up of the Centre of Excellence. BU has collaborated with VMWare for providing trainings on new VMware technologies, international certification and faculty development programs to our students and faculties. This collaboration allows VMware to conduct various trainings on VMware & allied Technologies to enhance employability skills and provide placement support to successful candidates both at national and international level. BU has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Sales force. This MoU will help in enhancing the training and job placements of students in the Salesforce ecosystem in an endeavor named “Salesforce Student Partnership Program”. Xebia works on DevOps with industries. The Collaboration of BU and Xebia allows the faculties at BU to enhance the skills in DevOps for labs and workshops. The students training on the DevOps may help them to get an internship opportunity. Dhiaum Lab provides the algorithmic solutions to industries. The collaboration of BU and Dhiaum Lab allows the short-term student-staff exchange programs, collaborative projects, research publication, and research datasets. Unity is the well-known name in the game development world. The partnership between BU and the Unity has provide the access to exclusive content for faculty which in turn helps students. The partnership between BU and TalentSprint has opened the door for Pega platform. This collaboration has enables students to get training on Pega platform that is in demand in the top organizations. The collaboration between BU and Times Internet the university to opt for projects. The BU students and faculty members are working together on live projects. So far, three projects are already completed and successfully delivered. Empass is an AI-empowered platform for the active learners and job seekers. Empass collaboration with BU has opened the opportunity of internship for students. Code zinger is the coding platform on cloud that eliminate dependency on physical machines where students can login from any PC and work on their tasks with auto grading system that gives feedback right away post submission. The BU collaboration with Tezos tends to educate the students and faculty on Blockchain via hands on lab sessions. The students can get the opportunity for the internship as well. The BU is engaged in imparting education to students across various domains with CodeChef. The collaboration has provided the BU access to CodeChef platform to the students and faculty. OCF aims to collaborate with the IoT ecosystem to deploy and evolve the OCF ISO/IEC specifications, including the Secure IP Device Framework, its open-source reference implementation, and an industry-recognized certification program. BU is the official adopter of this program. BU has been recognized by Bharat Electronics as official AI design partner which enables to bid for different projects in BEL. One project has been successfully completed and delivered by the BU. BU has collaborated with ICT to enhance the sills of the faculty members. 23 FDP certification has been completed by faculty members in last few months in various courses such as flutter and UI design, emotional intelligence, and creative thinking. EC-Council recognizes BU as an authorized EC-Council academia partner. The EC-Council academia partner network is the premier partnership for delivering high-quality authorized EC-Council cyber security related academic courses. The BU collaboration with the CompTIA provided access to faculty training and certifications, course curriculum mapping, and student Industry readiness upgradation. DSCI is the well-known name in the cyber security field. The BU tie-up with DSCI have resulted in many ongoing research projects by BU faculty. The BU collaboration with SAP enables the training of faculty and students in SAP technology for the better job opportunity in IT Industry. The BU has collaborated with UiPath for undergraduate program in Robotic & Process Automation. 30+ students and 3 faculty members are now advance level certified by Ui Path. Focus of the alliance is developing AI Models, Solutions, Case Studies, and Joint Research. Drishya AI will provide projects & internships to students. The Times School of Media - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login ABOUT The Times School of Media The Times School of Media More Videos >> The Times School of Media is India’s number one destination for mass communication students aspiring to become top notch multimedia professionals. Promoted by India’s biggest media conglomerate, Times Group, the school has a Digital First approach. The Times School of Media imparts hands-on training on high-end equipment and provides exposure to new age AI tools equipping students to meet the current demands of the media industry. The school aims to create the next generation of multimedia content creators and brand managers to succeed in today’s highly competitive and rapidly changing media landscape. Download Brochure Dean's Message Dr. Sumit Narula Dean, Times School of Media Welcome to the Times School of Media, where tradition meets innovation, and learning transforms into real-world impact. As part of the prestigious Times Group—India’s most influential and trusted media conglomerate, our students gain access to a legacy of credibility, excellence, and potent industry connections. Read More Prof. Dhiraj Singh Deputy Dean, Times School of Media Head, Centre for Media & Technology, Bennett University My dear future media professionals, Let me begin by telling you that your search for the ideal media & communication school ends here. And I say this with great conviction because at Times School of Media, we don’t just teach you what’s in the curriculum, but we ensure that by the time you graduate, you have a well-rounded understanding of what it means to be a top communicator in the 21st century. Read More × Dr. Sumit Narula Dean, Times School of Media Welcome to the Times School of Media, where tradition meets innovation, and learning transforms into real-world impact. As part of the prestigious Times Group—India’s most influential and trusted media conglomerate, our students gain access to a legacy of credibility, excellence, and potent industry connections. In an age when the media landscape is constantly evolving, we are dedicated to preparing students to meet the demands of a dynamic and disruptive environment. Our curriculum is continually updated to reflect the most recent trends and technologies, including the expanding role of artificial intelligence in media operations. Students learn how to use new tools ethically and creatively to improve narrative, journalism, advertising, and digital content. We encourage innovative teaching practices that go beyond textbooks, such as simulations, real-time newsroom projects, podcasts, studio productions, and interactive assignments. Our cutting-edge studios, production labs, and digital editing suites enable students to gain hands-on experience in content creation, which is now a viable professional path. From YouTube to Instagram, branded content to independent digital platforms, our students are taught to create, produce, and distribute powerful content in a variety of formats and channels. Recognizing the changing media environment, in which the distinctions between journalism, entertainment, marketing, and technology are becoming increasingly blurred, we provide our students with a diverse skill set. This includes data-driven storytelling, mobile journalism, influencer techniques, media entrepreneurship, and audience analytics, all of which are critical tools for success in today's media landscape. Our highly trained faculty—an unparalleled blend of scholars and industry experts—guides students on a voyage of creative discovery and critical thinking. Our students graduate with not only knowledge, but also confidence, clarity, and a competitive advantage, thanks to regular masterclasses, guest lectures, internships, and industry immersions provided by our strong Times Group network. At Times School of Media, we are shaping the next generation of media leaders, content creators, and changemakers. I invite you to be part of this exciting journey. Warm regards, Prof (Dr) Sumit Narula Dean, Times School of Media Bennett University Greater Noida × Prof. Dhiraj Singh Deputy Dean, Times School of Media Head, Centre for Media & Technology, Bennett University My dear future media professionals, Let me begin by telling you that your search for the ideal media & communication school ends here. And I say this with great conviction because at Times School of Media, we don’t just teach you what’s in the curriculum, but we ensure that by the time you graduate, you have a well-rounded understanding of what it means to be a top communicator in the 21st century. Read More We all know that technology and new AI-enabled software have created many disruptions across disciplines and fields, yet what has remained unchanged is the human interface that brings creativity, critical thinking and compassion in communication. While much of the heavy-lifting and repetitive tasks can be left to machines, humans will continue to make all aspects of communication—be it advertising or filmmaking or journalism – memorable and value-laden. This is exactly why we at Times School of Media strive to unlock the creativity of our students. I say ‘unlock’ because we strongly believe that there is creative potential in each one of us. And it is the job of the teachers to unlock and reveal to each student his or her hidden potential. This doesn’t mean we shun technology—on the contrary, we embrace it as a friend and a helper in our journey towards becoming excellent communication professionals. Our extremely talented and qualified faculty ensure that each student is given the benefit of hands-on and personalised mentoring so that they become a better, more sparkling version of themselves. I am extremely happy to say that at Times School of Media, we use the latest technology and AI and pair it with the most rewarding experiences provided by the many platforms of the Times Group to create professionals that are known for their excellence. Plus, being part of the nearly 190-year-old Times Group legacy gives us an edge no other media school in the world can claim to provide. So, come join us in this exciting adventure of learning and discovery! Warm regards, Prof. Dhiraj Singh Deputy Dean, Times School of Media Head, Centre for Media & Technology, Bennett University Programs BA Mass Communication BA in Films, Television & Web Series MA Mass Communication PGD in TV and Digital Journalism PhD in Journalism and Mass Communication Times Group Advantage Co-Design India’s largest media conglomerate, the Times Group, has developed the Times School of Media as the premier institution in Asia which boasts of skill- based curriculum, exposure to latest technologies, and industry-relevant programs. 1. Curriculum Development: As a best college in India for Mass Communications, media professionals from The Times Group play an important role in defining the curriculum of the different programs run by the Times School of Media. The curriculum developed by the faculty members is reviewed, discussed, and suggestions are incorporated in the final curriculum. The goal is to make the students industry ready. 2. Curriculum Updation: The curriculum is reviewed regularly by the Board of Studies of the Times School of Media which includes senior editors and media mangers of The Times Group to upkeep the industry-aligned BA programmes in Mass Communication and Film, TV, Web Series along with the MA programme in Mass Communication. The members on the Board of Studies are drawn from various teams like TV, Radio, Internet, OTT, Films, Advertising, and Marketing. Faculty and student feedback also plays an essential role in bringing changes to the curriculum. This is important to stay in tune with the needs of the industry. 3. Times Group companies involved in curriculum development: BA Mass Communication Times Internet Limited: Digital Marketing/Social Media Marketing curriculum Times Now: TV curriculum Radio Mirchi: Radio curriculum Mediawire: Public Relations curriculum The Times of India: Journalism curriculum BA Film, Television & Web Series Junglee Pictures: Film curriculum MX Player: OTT curriculum Zoom TV: TV curriculum MA Mass Communication Times Internet Limited: Digital Marketing/Social Media Marketing curriculum Times Now: TV curriculum Radio Mirchi: Radio curriculum Mediawire: Public Relations curriculum The Times of India: Journalism curriculum Masterclass: Top corporate honchos, media managers, and social media influencers visit the School regularly to share their insights in their respective domains. Internships: Students across all programmes are offered mandatory internships by the School. This is valuable learning for the students because they get hands-on experience and learn from the best in the media industry. Infrastructure: The infrastructure of the Times School of Media has been built under the guidance of Times Group professionals. Times Now engineers built the Television Studio while the Radio Studio was built with the help of Radio Mirchi engineers. Students get a flavour of the industry by getting hands-on experience with the Times Group’s state-of-the-art Content Management System. This is the same application used by the Times Group Editors in running the largest media network in India. Program Highlights Curriculum co-designed & co-delivered by The Times Group Collaboration with Confederation of Indian Industries (CII) for skill-based courses Interdisciplinary programs that cater to specialised industry demands Strong Faculty: Blend of faculty from media industry and academia Focus on building industry ready skills and analytical mindset 60% of time devoted to attaining practical skills through projects and industry immersion Outcome Based Education: Students are encouraged to conduct research & focus on content-driven results Curriculum Delivery Specialized modules are developed & delivered by the senior professionals of the Times Group: Times Internet: Digital Marketing, Native Advertising & Website Planning Times Now: Interviews & PTCs Mirchi: Introduction to Radio Junglee Films: Business of OTT, Introduction to Film Production CII: Advanced Animation, Interactive VFX, Modelling Techniques Students’ Work Advertisement/PR Campaigns: Create B2C, B2B advertisements and PR Campaigns BU Live: News bulletins based on campus events produced in the TSOM studio. Podcasts: Students produce a range of programs using the audio studio. Graphics and Animation: Making visual illustrations using open-source software. MoJo Videos: Shoot and edit videos on smartphones. Documentaries: Ideation and creation of short films with indoor/outdoor shoots. Photo-Features: Theme-based photo essays produced with DSLR cameras and smartphones. Live Blogs/ Twitter (X) reports: Work on live blogs and real-time Twitter (X) reports. Specialisation Project: Creating multimedia stories, SEO, and marketing campaigns. Studios, Labs and Software Television Studio Built under the guidance of Times Now, the Television Studio is equipped with a three-camera setup and a state-of-the-art production control room (PCR). The PCR is equipped with a Vision Mixer to handle a three-camera feed, an Apple Mac for editing, and four LED screens for output. The Studio also has a teleprompter to help students anchor news bulletins and shows. Radio Studio The Radio Studio, built with the help of Radio Mirchi, is an acoustically designed and fully equipped studio that provides students ample space and atmosphere for Radio production. The studio space consists of a recording room and a control room. The control room is equipped with a Multi-Channel Mixing Console, Speakers, Talkback, and Cubase recording software for recording, mixing, and monitoring sound. The Radio production facilities enable students to learn the art of recording and mixing sounds and voice-over techniques MoJo Lab Mobile-first lab used by students to create multimedia content for smartphone users. iMac and Windows Labs The school has two iMac labs and one Windows PCs labs. Each lab has 30 computers with the required software installed. Siddhartha is based in Evanston, Illinois in the Midwest of the United States. He has taught at Medill School of Journalism at Northwestern University and at Ashoka University. Read More × Mr. Siddhartha Dubey Siddhartha spent several decades as a working journalist in newsrooms in New York City, London, Delhi, Singapore, and Mumbai. Siddhartha is based in Evanston, Illinois in the Midwest of the United States. He has taught at Medill School of Journalism at Northwestern University and at Ashoka University. He has worked for Dow Jones Television, CNBC Asia, Reuters Television and ET Now and has covered stories ranging from financial markets to conflict. His first job straight out of college in 1989 was as an 'errand boy' at a small community newspaper in greater London. Siddhartha did an MS in journalism at Columbia University's Graduate School of Journalism in New York and his bachelors at St. Stephen's College in Delhi University. Siddhartha is interested in the changing nature of journalism in the world's biggest media market; how young Indians report the news differently; what they consume and what they expect from their news and information providers. (Hons.) at Bennett University Apply Now Why B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) at Bennett University Law education in India and worldwide has added several feathers in terms of restructuring BA LLB (Hons.) syllabus and B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) course details. Bennett University being regarded as a top LLB colleges in India makes houses best in-house and guest faculties to impart best legal education to students those are keen for societal development. Overview To suit the requirement of a competitive world, and to execute effortless law mechanism, Bennett University is imparting five-year integrated B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) while referring to the emerging challenges and its prospects in legal fraternity Faculty The world-class faculty members embrace outcome-based learning; they are professionally networked to mentor and offer career guidance. Practical Focus on providing hands-on experience through moot court competitions, legal aid clinic, international & national seminars, workshops BCI recognition The five-year integrated B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) program is recognised by the Bar Council of India; Law School got recognition from the Bar Council of India in July 2017, vide BCI: D: 1114/2017/(LE/Std.1/2.7.2017) National Linkages The School of Law has memberships with prestigious law schools & societies, international bridges to justice, Indian society for international law, NLSIU Bangalore and Women Indian Chamber for Commerce & Industry Internship All the students have to undergo mandatory 05 credit-based internships while studying BA LLB (Hons.) subjects ABOUT Leap in legal education with B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) Bennett University’s five-year integrated B.A LL.B. (Hons.) aims to nurture legal professionals skilled in practical and procedural laws with critical thinking and analytical aptitude among BA LLB (Hons.) colleges in India This program is best suited for students with an inclination in social sciences, judiciary, UPSC, and other such competitive exams. At BU, legal education is meant for the development of society, solidarity and strengthening of rule of law as well as individual freedom. Learn by doing and by interacting with students from diverse backgrounds. The program structure will aid law aspirants to dive deep into the fundamentals of legal education which consists of building critical analytical abilities, advocacy skills and various laws of national and international importance. BA LL.B (Hons.) course details consists of law and humanities courses; the first degree under the integrated program will have 20 non-law papers. The Law courses beginning right from the first semester covers 20 compulsory papers, 4 clinic papers, 6 optional papers and 8 papers of specialisation. It includes Clinical, Electives, Honours courses, and Legal Internships Subjects in BA LL.B (Hons.) program covers History, Political Science, Economics, and Sociology, along with the in-depth study of law and legislature The curriculum is designed to impart advocacy skills through clinical legal education essential for courtroom litigation, alternative dispute resolutions, advisory and consultancy services The curriculum of the Law papers has been designed to incorporate emerging areas of Artificial Intelligence & Law, Entertainment Law, Advanced Intellectual Property Law, Internet Law & Governance, Privacy Law and other laws. B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2025-30 B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2024-29 B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2023-28 B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2022-27 B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2021-26 B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2020-25 B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2019-24 B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) Curriculum 2018-23 Core Law Papers Humanities Paper Clinical Papers Mooting Interdisciplinary work It offers 26 core law papers, including Constitutional Law, Family Law, Civil Procedure Code & Law of Limitation, Administrative Law, Public International Law, Labour & Industrial Law and many others. The first degree papers offered as a part of B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) include Political Science, Sociology, History and Economics. To impart the practical knowledge of court craft and necessary legal skills, the course curriculum has four clinical papers, namely, Moot Court Clinic, Alternate Dispute Resolution, Professional Ethics, and Drafting, Pleading and Conveyance Gain from the faculty and student- administered Moot Court Committee, which selects in- house teams for external participation. Moot court is a compulsory 1 credit course which begins from 1st academic year of the Law program. Gain knowledge and experience in an inter- disciplinary university environment e.g. artificial intelligence, entertainment, Internet, surveillance, air and space and privacy. Resources Avail a state-of-the-art library, Moot Court Hall and Legal Aid Cell. Workshops, Socratic Club and Guest Lectures Invite thinkers to deliberate on public policy issues. Research BU-SOL have own research journal (Bennett Journal of Legal Studies). Five major research centres will allow students to work outside the classroom. Also, it will allow to participate in legal conferences and competitions. Internships & Placements Gain hands-on experience through the mandatory 4-week internship every year. BU-SOL has constituted an Internship and Placement Committee (IPC) which oversee the responsibility of the internships and placement for the students of law. IPC enhances the prospects of internship in various legal institutions and is oriented towards placing the students in institutions of repute. BU-SOL has set in motion of establishing a network of law firms, corporates, policy think-tanks and other stakeholders of legal fraternity to facilitate the internships spread across geographical zones. Such internships will shape the students’ aspirations of the type of practice and connect with potential partners for final placement. Avenues of Placement Law Firms, Senior Law Practitioners, Judicial Offices, Civil Society Organisations, Parliament and State Legislature committees Government and other Local Bodies, Market Regulatory Agencies, Legal Regulatory Authorities, Corporate, International Organisations Partnership Participate in activities and hone that unique idea at the Centre for Innovation & Entrepreneurship at Bennett University. International Collaboration Avail international exposure to leading law schools in North America, Europe, Australia, Asia, and Africa. Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process Eligibility Domestic Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with minimum 60% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score) + Personal interview. PROCESS B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) Admission Process for 2025 The selection of a student at Bennett University will be finalized after series of evaluative steps: Submission of the application form with required details by paying non-refundable application fees of Rs. 1500. Merit-based assessment of candidates and release of provisional offer letters Counseling by the Bennett University’s admissions team Start My Application Student Testimonials My journey at university has taught me about who I am and where I need to go. Batch: 2019-24 OUR ASK B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) Frequently Asked Questions Are law programs approved by the Bar Council of India? The law programs, including BBA LL.B (Hons.), B.A. LL.B. (Hons.), and LL.B (Hons.), are approved by the Bar Council of India (BCI). What is the difference between BBA LL.B (Hons.) and B.A. LL.B. (Hons.)? The difference between both the programs lies in the BBA and BA components. BBA LL.B. (Hons.) focuses mainly on business and law, while B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) focuses more on the interface of law and society to build requisite capacity in litigation. The law components for both the programs are the same. Also, both streams will have elective courses for students that complement their BA or BBA components. Are law-specific subjects taught in the first year? If yes, how many? Yes, law-specific subjects are taught from the first year to both B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) and BBA LL.B (Hons.) programs. These include Law of Torts, Criminal Law, Law of Contracts, Legal Method, Law and Economics, and Moot Court Clinic. Apart from this, students are involved in Legal Aid activities from their first year through the legal aid clinic. What kind of legal publications and books are available in the Law Library? The School of Law library is equipped with a contemporary collection of books, journals, and reports. It has subscribed to online databases like Manupatra.com, Supreme Court Case Online (SCC ONLINE), All India Reporter (AIR Online), HeinOnline, LexisNexis, and West Law. The library is fully automated using KOHA v17 – Integrated Library Management System and Online and Mobile Public Access Catalogue (M/OPAC), which enable students to search the library’s collection. The Law Library is equipped with a collection of 3542 books and Legal language lab software by Clarity English. What are the skills a law graduate must possess? A graduate of law must reflect the broad spectrum of skills and ethics imparted through the rigorous 5-year program. A lawyer must be proficient in communication and language – both in English and the vernacular. Also, an acutely logical mind helps lawyers understand the law, analyze the factual matrix, and frame strong arguments. Most importantly, while advocating the cause of their client, the lawyer must ensure that this noble profession's legal and ethical mandates are upheld. Is the university responsible for arranging internships? University has a dedicated career services team that facilitates summer internships and final placements. The team works closely with senior faculty members and industry to help to arrange the right opportunities for students. School of Liberal Arts - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login ABOUT School of Liberal Arts The School of Liberal Arts at Bennett University is committed to creating robust momentum for spreading Liberal Arts education in India and worldwide. We are reimagining liberal arts education by making it more practice-oriented and embedding an entrepreneurial leadership mindset into the curricula. The goal is to inspire students with a sense of purpose, encouraging them to lead sustainable, healthy, and impactful lives. By nurturing healthier individuals, the school lays the foundation for a better world. The Liberal Arts program is curated in close consultation with global academic and industry experts. The school provides students with transformative experiences, through the Global Pathway Program, which connects them with universities and colleges worldwide. Additionally, students are encouraged to launch their ventures while at Bennett. The unique program, Enterprise First: Building Resilience, is one such initiative. 9 Distinguishing Features of Bennett School of Liberal Arts (SoLA) Practice-Oriented Holistic Development Enterprise First: Building Resilience Rootedness and Reflexivity Interdisciplinary Learning International Immersion Global and Diverse Perspective Adaptability Leadership and Innovation Community Engagement System Thinking & Sustainability Self-Designed Majors Download Brochure Dean's Message Prof. (Dr.) Sanjeev Kumar Dean, School of Liberal Arts At Bennett, we are reimagining the very essence of a liberal arts education. At the School of Liberal Arts, we are crafting a practice-oriented curriculum with a profound focus on nurturing leadership and entrepreneurial spirits. As our world undergoes deep transformation driven by technological advancements, and as the various intended and unintended consequences of these changes begin to unfold in our lives, a truly multidisciplinary education-the heart of liberal arts-has become more essential than ever. This blend of multi- and inter-disciplinary learning not only heightens our awareness of externalities but also cultivates adaptability and empathy-qualities that are crucial in a world increasingly fractured by political and ethnic divisions. Read More Prof. (Dr.) Sanjeev Kumar Dean, School of Liberal Arts Dear Students, At Bennett, we are reimagining the very essence of a liberal arts education. At the School of Liberal Arts, we are crafting a practice-oriented curriculum with a profound focus on nurturing leadership and entrepreneurial spirits. As our world undergoes deep transformation driven by technological advancements, and as the various intended and unintended consequences of these changes begin to unfold in our lives, a truly multidisciplinary education the heart of liberal arts has become more essential than ever. This blend of multi- and inter-disciplinary learning not only heightens our awareness of externalities but also cultivates adaptability and empathy qualities that are crucial in a world increasingly fractured by political and ethnic divisions. At the School of Liberal Arts here at Bennett, we inspire our students to master the art and science of inquiry, creating a psychologically safe environment that nurtures their innate curiosity and capacity for wonder. While we sharpen our students' ability to communicate with clarity, we also impart the tacit knowledge that emerges through experiential, team-based, and community-centered learning an understanding that transcends the limitations of language. Drawing inspiration from the Wright Brothers, we have transformed the Bennett campus into a living pedagogical tool. This dynamic environment allows us to impart cutting-edge knowledge on topics such as the workings of platform economies, the intricacies of systems thinking, the interconnectedness of all life forms, and the critical importance of physical, mental, and spiritual well-being. Read More Ms. Mitali Nikore Master of Economics London School of Economics and Political Science Ms. Mitali Nikore, an esteemed economist, brings forth a wealth of experience spanning over 12 years in the realms of gender-mainstreaming, development finance, policy advisory, and social entrepreneurship. Her advisory roles extend to prominent multilateral organizations such as the Asian Development Bank (ADB), UN Women, the World Bank, and various governmental agencies within India. With a global perspective, Ms. Nikore has contributed significantly across diverse geographies including Africa, South Asia, the United States, and the United Kingdom. Recognized for her outstanding contributions, Ms. Nikore has been honored with prestigious accolades, including the India UK Achievers Honours, which celebrates the achievements of 75 young UK Indian professionals who have made remarkable strides in their respective fields. She has been selected as a part of 'She is: 75 Indian women in STEAM' initiative by the Government of India (2022) and recognized as one of the 22For2022 industry leaders by Forbes magazine. Her expertise has been showcased in various high-profile platforms, including W20, U20, and B20 events during India's G20 presidency, as well as the Global Solutions Summit 2022 by Think20 and Think7. As a TEDx speaker and a noted contributor to discussions on gender equality, Ms. Nikore continues to advocate for meaningful change. She recently presented her research paper titled Gender Budgeting in India: Pre and Post Covid-19 Analysis at the 30th Annual International Association for Feminist Economics Conference in Geneva. Additionally, she is a prolific writer with contributions to esteemed publications such as the Times of India, Hindustan Times, Indian Express, SheThePeople, and Financial Express. Ms. Nikore holds a Master of Economics from the London School of Economics and Political Science , complemented by a Bachelor of Economics from the University of Delhi . Her multifaceted expertise and dedication to advancing economic and social progress make her a valuable asset to the academic community and beyond. Medical Humanities Lab Dr. Angeliki Kerasidou View More Clare Barker View More Dr. Eram Alam View More Dr. Jacob Stegenga View More Janet M. Wilson (D. Phil Oxon) View More Prof. Sangeetha Menon View More Professor Sebastian Groes View More Dr. Stella Bolaki View More Tamara L. Falicov View More x Dr. Angeliki Kerasidou Designation: Academician, Visual Artist & Writer Dr Angeliki Kerasidou is an Associate Professor in Bioethics at the Ethox Centre and Research Fellow at the Wellcome Centre for Ethics and Humanities, University of Oxford, UK. She hold an NDPH Senior Fellowship and is also Fellow at Reuben College where she co-leads the Ethics and Values Theme. She studied theology and philosophy in Greece, Germany and the UK, and received her DPhil in 2009 from Oxford University. Dr Kerasidou is the Vice-Chair of the Independent Ethics Committee of International Agency for Research on Cancer (IEC IARC), Member of the Central University Research Ethics Committee (CUREC), and Member of the TORCH Medical Humanities Steering Committee at Oxford University. x Clare Barker Clare Barker is Associate Professor of English Literature (Medical Humanities) at the University of Leeds, UK. Her research focuses on representations of health, disability and biomedical research in contemporary, postcolonial and Indigenous literature and film. She is the author of Postcolonial Fiction and Disability: Exceptional Children, Metaphor and Materiality (2011) and co-editor of The Cambridge Companion to Literature and Disability (2017). She has co-edited special issues of the Journal of Literary and Cultural Disability Studies on 'Disabling Postcolonialism' (2010) and 'Disability and Indigeneity' (2013), guest edited an issue of Moving Worlds: A Journal of Transcultural Writing on 'Literature, Medicine, Health' (2019), and guest edited an issue of BMJ Medical Humanities on 'Global Genetic Fictions' (2021). Clare is currently a Co-PI on the Wellcome-funded University of Leeds project LivingBodiesObjects, which centres on the development of a medical humanities 'lab' and explores ways of working that seek to make academic research culture more accessible, equitable and inclusive. Her ongoing project focuses on representations of genetics and biocolonialism in contemporary literature and film. x Dr. Eram Alam Dr Eram Alam is an Assistant Professor in the Department of the History of Science at Harvard University, USA. She specializes in the history of medicine, with a particular emphasis on globalization, race, migration, and health during the twentieth century. Her first book, The Care of Foreigners, explores the enduring consequences of post-colonial physician migration from Asia to the United States. x Dr. Jacob Stegenga Dr Jacob Stegenga is a Professor in the Department of History and Philosophy of Science at the University of Cambridge, UK. He has published widely on fundamental topics in reasoning and rationality and philosophical problems in medicine and biology. Prior to joining Cambridge he taught in the United States and Canada, and he received his PhD from the University of California San Diego. He is the author of Medical Nihilism and Care and Cure: An Introduction to Philosophy of Medicine. x Michiel Baas Designation: Ph.D. in Anthropology, University of Amsterdam, Netherlands University: Academic positions with the National University of Singapore, Nalanda University (Rajgir), the International Institute for Asian Studies (Leiden) & the University of Amsterdam. Michiel Baas is PhD in Anthropology from the University of Amsterdam, and has held various academic positions with the National University of Singapore, Nalanda University (Rajgir), the International Institute for Asian Studies (Leiden) and the University of Amsterdam. His latest publication is Muscular India. Masculinity, Mobility and the Middle Class in Urban India. Presently, he is working on a project on Craitivity: Towards an Anthropology of Artificial Intelligence x Janet M. Wilson (D. Phil Oxon) Janet M. Wilson (D. Phil Oxon) is Emerita Professor of English and Postcolonial Studies at the University of Northampton. Relevant publications include Fleur Adcock (2007), the coedited The Diaspora Studies Reader (2017), and Planetary Precarity and the Pandemic (2020), a special issue of the Journal of Postcolonial Writing. She co-edits the book series, Studies in World Literature (Ibidem-Verlag), is editor in chief of the Journal of Postcolonial Writing, and co-founded and chairs Challenging Precarity: A Global Network . In 2022-23 she is Fellow-in-Residence at the Rothermere American Institute, Oxford, UK. x Dr. Stella Bolaki Dr Stella Bolaki is Reader in American Literature and Medical Humanities in the School of English and Co-Director of the Centre for Health and Medical Humanities in the Division of Arts and Humanities at the University of Kent (UK). She is the author of Illness as Many Narratives: Arts, Medicine and Culture (Edinburgh University Press, 2016), and her medical humanities research has appeared in Life Writing, Mosaic, Literature and Medicine, Medical Humanities, the Journal of Medical Humanities, and the Journal of Literary & Cultural Disability Studies. Stella's research on contemporary artistic practice in the context of health, illness and disability has a collaborative and public engagement dimension and has received funding from the Wellcome Trust, the British Academy and Research England. Her interdisciplinary project Artists Books and the Medical Humanities (2016-2019) established the Prescriptions: Artists Books special collection that has informed public exhibitions, creative workshops for people living with illness and health professionals, and teaching provision. Stella is on the editorial board of the Contemporary Cultural Studies in Illness, Health and Medicine series (Edinburgh University Press) and together with colleagues at the Universities of Essex and East Anglia leads the Eastern ARC Medical Humanities Network. She is the Director of Kent's MA in Medical Humanities and contributes to health and medical humanities teaching for the Kent and Medway Medical School in the UK. x Professor Sebastian Groes Sebastian is is Professor of English Literature at the University of Wolverhampton (UK), where he leads a major research project on inclusivity and diversity, the Arts and Humanities Research Council-funded Novel Perceptions: towards an inclusive canon. His research for BBC's engagement project, Novel That Shaped Our World is included in the BBC podcast series Turn Up for the Books. His research often features on the BBC: he has written, for instance, about the need to rethink literary genre, fake news during the Corona crisis, and gendered responses to the pandemic. He is fascinated by the ways in which human behaviour, memory and thinking are changing in the twenty-first century and how these impact on health and wellbeing. Examples of this interest are his essays on information overload and the impact of the pandemic on mental and physical health in the works of Kazuo Ishiguro. His next academic book is The Prosthetic God: Transactive Cognition in the Age of Connectivity, and he is preparing a collection of essays on the way in which transcultural perspectives on digital inequality can be used to understand health and wellbeing issues. His most recent book, the genre-bending stroke memoir Right in the Head (London: Jetstone, 2023) explores the relationship between human beings and their brains. x Tamara L. Falicov Tamara L. Falicov is the inaugural Dean of the University of Missouri Kansas City's School of Humanities and Social Sciences, USA. Prof. Falicov comes to UMKC from the University of Kansas, where she was an associate dean in Arts, Humanities and Area Studies in the College of Liberal Arts and Sciences and a professor in the Department of Film and Media Studies and the Center of Latin American and Caribbean Studies. x Prof. Sangeetha Menon Prof. Sangeetha Menon is Professor and Head of the Consciousness Studies Programme, and the Dean of the School of Humanities at NIAS. She joined National Institute of Advanced Studies, in January 1996, in the Indian Institute of Science campus, Bangalore (India). Her interview and appointment were led by an illustrious group of scientists. Subsequently she conceptualised and commenced the NIAS Consciousness Studies Programme along with BV Sreekantan (former Director, TIFR and Honorary Professor, NIAS). This was the first academic group that started in India to study consciousness with an interdisciplinary and a multidisciplinary mandate. She currently heads the NIAS Consciousness Studies Programme and is the Professor and Dean of the School of Humanities at NIAS. Prof. Menon is the Member, National Task Force for Cognitive Science Research Initiative, DST, India. She is the Advisor for NAC (National Advisory Committee) IIT Kanpur for the Interdisciplinary Program (IDP) in Cognitive Science. Prof. Menon is also the Advisor Council member of METI International, San Francisco (US), and a Fellow, International Society for Science & Religion (ISSR) Cambridge. x Dr. Dhananjay Singh Dr. Dhananjay Singh is Professor of English Literature at JNU, New Delhi. Presently, he is the Member Secretary, Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR), Ministry of Education, Government of India. He headed the centre of English Studies, JNU as its Chairperson from January 2019 to January 2023. He served as the Associate Dean of the School of Language, Literature and Culture Studies from May 2021 to January 23, 2023. Prof. Singh was Visiting Professor at University of Bergamo, Italy in 2014; International Visiting Fellow at Grinnell College, Grinnell, IOWA, USA, in 2015; and Visiting Research Fellow at Trinity Long Room Hub, Trinity College Dublin in 2017. For his research on Seamus Heaney and Buddhist epistemology in a comparative framework, he was awarded Research Secondment by the European Union's Marie Curie International Research Exchange Scheme to the Project Social Performance, Cultural Trauma and Reestablishing Solid Sovereignties (SPECTRESS), at Trinity College Dublin in May-July 2017. x Aleksandr Andreas Wansbrough Designation: Academician, Visual Artist & Writer Aleksandr Andreas Wansbrough is an academic, cultural theorist and writer on cinema, art, and digital screen culture. His book, Capitalism and the Enchanted Screen (Bloomsbury, 2021), encapsulates his current research, exploring the relevance of class and power to technology. He is an editor for the Journal of Asia-Pacific Pop Culture, published by Penn State Press and an editor for the Journal of Alterity Studies and World Literature, the cofounder and convener of the annual Inhuman Screens conference, and the secretary of the Popular Culture Association of Australia and New Zealand (PopCAANZ). Wansbrough is also a visual artist and filmmaker. x Feroza Jussawalla Designation: Professor Emerita University: University of New Mexico, Abuquerque, NM Feroza Jussawalla is Professor Emerita t the University of New Mexico in Abuquerque, NM. She has taught English Literature, specifically Postcolonial Literatures, for over forty years, both at the University of Texas at El Paso, and the University of New Mexico. She is the author of Family Quarrels: towards a Criticism of Indian Writing in English; and editor of Conversations with V.S. Naipaul, and co-editor of Interviews with Writers of the Postcolonial World; Emerging South Asian Women Writers; Memory, Voice and Identity: Muslim Women's Writing from Across the Middle East. Her collection of poetry is entitled, Chiffon Saris. x Janet M. Wilson Designation: Emerita Professor, English and Postcolonial Studies, University of Northampton, UK University: Visiting Professor, English, Birmingham City University, UK Janet M. Wilson is Emerita Professor in English and Postcolonial Studies at the University of Northampton, UK and also Visiting Professor in English at Birmingham City University. She specialises in postcolonial and diaspora writing of the white settler societies of New Zealand and Australia, in particular migration, transnationalism, and refugee writing. She is currently chair of the Challenging Precarity Network and President of the National Conference of University Professors in the UK. She is also Principal Investigator of the AHRC-funded Diaspora Screen Media Network and is editor- in-chief of the Journal of Postcolonial Writing (London: Routledge) x Lenart kof Designation: Head, Institute for Philosophical Studies, Science and Research Centre of Koper, Slovenia University: Dean, Institutum Studiorum Humanitatis of Alma Mater Europaea, Slovenia Lenart kof is Head of the Institute for Philosophical Studies at the Science and Research Centre Koper and Dean of the Institutum Studiorum Humanitatis of Alma Mater Europaea (both in Slovenia). Dr kof received KAAD grant (Universit Tbingen), Fulbright grant (Stanford University) and Humboldt grant (Max Weber Kolleg, Universitt Erfurt). He is a member of European Academy of Sciences and Arts (EASA, Salzburg) and is the president of Slovenian Society for Comparative Religion. He co-edited Shame, Gender Violence and Ethics: Terrors of Injustice (Lexington Books, 2021), Atmospheres of Breathing (SUNY Press 2018), The Poesis of Peace (Routledge, 2017) and Breathing with Luce Irigaray (Bloomsbury, 2013). Dr kof is an author of several books, among them Antigone's Sisters: On the Matrix of Love (SUNY Press, 2021) and Breath of Proximity: Intersubjectivity, Ethics and Peace (Springer, 2015). His current research is dedicated to environmental respiratory philosophy, philosophical theology, and ethics of vulnerability. His books were translated into German, Russian, Persian and Bengali (forthcoming). Professor kof delivered lectures at Australian Catholic University, Deakin University, University of Vienna, University of Delhi, Jadavpur University, Link pings Universitet, University of Erfurt, Institute of Philosophy of the Russian Academy of Sciences, and others. He is editor-in-chief (with Magdalena Grska) of Routledge Critical Perspectives on Breath and Breathing book series. x Michiel Baas Designation: Ph.D. in Anthropology, University of Amsterdam, Netherlands University: Academic positions with the National University of Singapore, Nalanda University (Rajgir), the International Institute for Asian Studies (Leiden) & the University of Amsterdam. Michiel Baas is PhD in Anthropology from the University of Amsterdam, and has held various academic positions with the National University of Singapore, Nalanda University (Rajgir), the International Institute for Asian Studies (Leiden) and the University of Amsterdam. His latest publication is Muscular India. Masculinity, Mobility and the Middle Class in Urban India. Presently, he is working on a project on Craitivity: Towards an Anthropology of Artificial Intelligence x Peter Arnds Designation: Directs Comparative Literature program & Teaches German and Italian Literature University: Trinity College Dublin, Ireland Peter Arnds directs the Comparative Literature program and teaches German and Italian literature at Trinity College Dublin, where he is also a Fellow. He has held visiting positions in Kabul, Delhi, Adelaide and Salamanca, and is a member of the PEN Centre for German-Speaking Writers Abroad and of Academia Europaea. His publications include books on Wilhelm Raabe, Charles Dickens, Gnter Grass, as well as his recent monographs Lycanthropy in German Literature (Palgrave Macmillan 2015), Translating Holocaust Literature (Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, 2015), and Wolves at the Door: Migration, Dehumanization, Rewilding the World (Bloomsbury, 2021). Dr. Arnds has also translated Patrick Boltshauser's novel Stromschnellen (Rapids, Dalkey Archive Press, 2014, nominated for the IMPAC, Dublin International Literary Award) and published a novel, Searching for Alice (Dalkey Archive Press, 2019). He has published widely in the fields of Comparative Literature, German and Italian Studies, Holocaust Studies, and the Environmental Humanities. His current research examines the links between cultural production and species politics as well as the philosophy of walking in world literature. He contributes to several international research networks, including Challenging Precarity: A Global Network and Narratives of Resilience. x Scott Slovic Designation: Distinguished Professor, Environmental Humanities University: University of Idaho, USA Scott Slovic is University Distinguished Professor of Environmental Humanities at the University of Idaho, USA. He served as founding president of the Association for the study of Literature and Environment in the early 1990s, and completed a twenty-five-year term as Editor-in-Chief of ISLE: Interdisciplinary Studies in Literature and Environment, the central journal in the field of ecocriticism, in 2020. He has worked for many years, as a teacher and writer, on topics related to ecological literary studies, environmental communication, and interdisciplinary approaches to the environmental humanities. Much of his current research and teaching focuses on critical data studies (how information is collected, communicated, and received) in the contexts of humanitarian and environmental crises. x Sbastien Doubinsky Designation: Associate Professor, French Department University: University of Aarhus, Denmark Sbastien Doubinsky is Associate Professor in the French department of the University of Aarhus, Denmark. His research fields cover translation theory, comparative literature, work-reading theory and creative writing. He co-authored Reading literature today with Professor Tabish Khair, published by SAGE in 2011. He is also an established bilingual novelist and poet, published and translated in various countries. Master's in Economics | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Master's in Economics Apply Now Master's in Economics Apply Now About Master's in Economics The Master’s in Economics at Bennett University is a rigorous 2-year postgraduate program designed to cultivate exceptional analytical, quantitative, and research skills. The curriculum blends core economic theory, advanced mathematical modelling, and intensive data analysis using tools like R, Python, and Matlab . Students benefit from global exchange programs ,research grants , and internship opportunities , preparing them for high-impact careers in policy, finance, research, academia, and quantitative fields. The program outcome is a strong theoretical and quantitative foundation ideal for both professional roles and further studies, including Ph.D. programs. *The Master’s in Economics program allows students with a B.A. background to receive an M.A. degree, and students with a B.Sc. background to receive an M.Sc. degree upon completion. Duration 2 Years Specialisations 6 Credits 80 Start Date July 2026 Specialisations Environmental Economics Experimental Economics Health Economics Labor Economics International Economics and Finance Applied Econometrics Curriculum Highlights Experiential and research-driven learning Independent study and dissertation in the final year Internship opportunities Global exposure through university tie-ups Flexibility to choose electives across specialisations Why Master's in Economics from Bennett University Be part of a vibrant academic community of students and researchers driven to apply economics in shaping and transforming the world. Access a wide range of datasets and advanced software essential for economic research and analysis. Gain hands-on experience with leading statistical and computing tools, including MATLAB, Python, R, and other industry-relevant platforms. Develop strong analytical, quantitative, and research skills to address complex, real-world economic challenges. Learn in a dynamic environment that fosters curiosity, innovation, and the practical application of economic concepts. Program Objectives The Master's program in Economics from the School of Liberal Arts equips students with a rigorous understanding of fundamental economic concepts and their real-world applications. It builds a strong theoretical and mathematical framework that enables learners to model various aspects of the economy effectively. The program also helps students develop a clear understanding of the rationale behind designing and implementing public policies, preparing them to analyse, evaluate, and contribute to policy decisions. Learning Outcomes The Economics program ensures comprehensive learning outcomes. Graduates will: Gain the ability to distinguish between causation and correlation. Develop proficiency in analysing datasets to estimate relationships between variables of interest. Build a deep understanding of how markets function, including the behaviour of market participants and their impact on overall market structures. Be equipped to differentiate between private and public goods, identify market failures, and design mechanisms to address and reduce these failures. Career Pathways M.A. Economics or M.Sc. Economics degree opens a wide range of meaningful career paths. Graduates can pursue Ph.D. in Economics, Public Policy, or Development Studies, leading to roles in teaching, research, and policy analysis. Career opportunities span: Government Institutions: IES, RBI, NITI Aayog, SEBI, and NABARD International Bodies: World Bank, UNDP, and IMF Renowned Research Think Tanks: NCAER, ICRIER, CPR, and J-PAL Careers in the corporate and financial sectors: Financial Analysts Data Analysts Risk Analysts/Consultants Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Passed Graduation in any discipline from recognised university with minimum 50% marks with English as the medium of instruction. NRI: A recognised bachelor’s degree or equivalent qualification from a recognised institution. Selection Process Selection will be based on Aggregate Graduation Percentage & Personal Interview. Master's in Economics Program Fee Get detailed information about the M.A./M.Sc. Economics program fee structure, including tuition, hostel fee, and other applicable charges. Click here for details. Faculty Prof. (Dr.) Sanjeev Kumar Postdoctoral Fellow - Yale University Dr. Pooja Kapoor Doctor of Philosophy- Jamia Millia Islamia University, New Delhi Dr. Koyal Verma Ph.D. - Jawaharlal Nehru University Dr. Shalini Mittal Ph.D. - BHU Dr. Preeti Bala Mishra Ph.D.- University of Lucknow Dr. Shamindra Nath Sanyal Ph.D. - University of Calcutta Dr. Priyanka Chatterjee Ph.D. - JNU, New Delhi Dr. Kundan Kumar Ph.D. - IIT Patna Dr. Ujjawal Sawarn Fellowship program in Management (2023) Raipur Dr. Pratibha Biswas Ph.D. - Jamia Millia Islamia Dr. Ankita Das Ph.D. - IIT (ISM), Dhanbad Dr. Pallavi Kiran Ph.D. - IIT (ISM) Dhanbad Dr. Surbhi Uniyal Ph.D. - JNU, New Delhi Dr. Dhruvee Sinha Ph.D. - NIT Patna Dr. Tamoghna Bose Ph.D. - University of Birmingham Dr. Shivi Singh Ph.D. - University of Delhi Ms. Priya Harchandani Ph.D.(Pursuing) , Institute of Management, Nirma University Mr. Krishan Ph.D. (Pursuing) - JNU, Delhi Dr. Vaishali Sharma Ph.D - O.P. Jindal Global University, Sonipat OUR ASK Master's in Economics Frequently Asked Questions Who can apply for an M.A. Economics/ M.Sc. Economics? Anyone with a bachelor’s degree from a recognised university can apply. Students from Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, Commerce, or Engineering backgrounds commonly join, as long as they are comfortable with numbers and analysis. How does the admission process for Master's program in Economics work? The admission process for M.A./M.Sc. Economics at BU is primarily merit-based, relying on your Aggregate Graduation Percentage (minimum 50%). Selection involves a review of academic merit and a Personal Interview (PI) to assess motivation and analytical ability. What will I study during the program? In the Master’s in Economics program, you will study core courses such as Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Econometrics, and Mathematical Methods, along with electives in areas like Development, Behavioural, Environmental, or Financial Economics. Is the program good preparation for a Ph.D.? Yes, the Master’s in Economics provides a rigorous foundation in theory and quantitative methods, exactly what top Ph.D. programs look for. Many students use this degree as a springboard to doctoral study and academic research. Are there opportunities to research or internships during M.A. Economics/M.Sc. Economics? Yes, Bennett University encourages students to take up research projects, dissertations, or summer internships. You might work with a faculty mentor, assist on a policy project, or collaborate with a research institute. It is a great way to apply classroom learning to real-world questions. What kind of organisations hire Master’s in Economics graduates? Economists are in demand almost everywhere. Graduates work with government bodies (RBI, IES, NITI Aayog, Ministry of Finance), international agencies (World Bank, UNDP, IMF), research think tanks (NCAER, ICRIER, CPR, J-PAL), and private firms (Deloitte, EY, PWC, major banks, and fintech companies). What practical or technical skills will I gain while pursuing a Master’s in Economics? In M.A. Economics/ M.Sc. Economics, you will learn to analyse and interpret data, use software like R, Stata, Python, and Excel, and apply statistical methods to real economic problems. These skills make you job-ready for research, analytics, and consulting roles in both public and private sectors. What is Behavioural and Experimental Economics, and can I specialise in it? Behavioural and Experimental Economics is a field that studies how people actually make decisions. You can specialise through electives or your thesis, leading to roles in policy labs, development organisations, and consulting firms that design behavioural “nudges.” Download Brochure Enquire Now × SUBMIT YOUR ENQUIRY × Download Brochure WordPress Lightbox

School of Management - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Announcement Dr. Rajesh Chakrabarti, a renowned academician in Finance, Management, and Public Policy, will be joining as the Dean School of Management in the first week of October 2024. Click here >> × Dr. Rajesh Chakrabarti, Upcoming Dean, School of Management Dr. Rajesh Chakrabarti is a renowned academic and thought leader in the fields of Finance, Management, and Public Policy. His educational journey began at Presidency College, Calcutta, where he earned his B.Sc. in Economics (1991). He then pursued his Post Graduate Diploma in Management from the prestigious Indian Institute of Management, Ahmedabad (1993) before completing his Ph.D. in Management (Finance) from the University of California, Los Angeles (1999). Dr. Chakrabarti's academic career spans over two decades, during which he has held teaching positions at esteemed institutions worldwide. He has been a faculty member at the University of Alberta, Canada; Georgia Institute of Technology, USA; and the Indian School of Business (ISB). His expertise in Finance has been recognized globally, with visiting positions at institutions such as IIM Calcutta, Indian Statistical Institute Delhi, IIM Udaipur, ICN Nancy (France), and the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta. In addition to his teaching roles, Dr. Chakrabarti has held significant leadership positions in academia. He is currently serving as the Director of the Dera Bassi Campus, Dean of the LM Thapar School of Management, and Dean of the School of Social Sciences and Humanities at the Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology. His prior leadership roles include Director at MDI Gurgaon and Dean at the Jindal Global Business School, Jindal Global University. Dr. Chakrabarti's contributions to academia extend beyond the classroom. As the founding Executive Director of the Bharti Institute of Public Policy at ISB's Mohali campus, he played a pivotal role in designing and launching one of India's leading public policy programs. He has authored or edited ten books on subjects ranging from Finance and Public Policy to Artificial Intelligence. His work has been published in leading international scholarly journals, cementing his position as a thought leader in his field. Beyond academia, Dr. Chakrabarti has made significant contributions to the corporate and policy sectors. He served as the Executive Vice President for Research and Policy at the Wadhwani Foundation and cofounded Sunay Policy Advisory, a public policy consulting firm. His insights have been shared with a wider audience through his column in the Financial Express. ABOUT Management Program at Bennett University "Transpire, Transform and be transnational with Specialised MBA Program; Be a progressive business influencer!" ESG re-sketches workplace priorities; AI transforms the rules of work and thought, and the shifting sands of great-power equations play havoc with supply chains. The skills of tomorrow look increasingly different from those of yesterday. The search for solutions needs a much wider horizon. This is exactly what makes a B-School today more exciting than ever before. Particularly when the B-School part of a vibrant young university and is surrounded by faculties of AI, Law and Liberal Arts to provide one with the competitive edge that helps one make stand out. At the School of Management at Bennett University, our best-in-class faculty teaches, mentors and engages with young learners to prepare them for the world of tomorrow. Much of the learning in a business program happens outside the classroom. Industry visitors – from the NCR or afar – connect our classroom to the corporate world. The professional spirit of India’s leading media conglomerate infuses the program with a unique corporate preparedness. Our students not only master the concepts but get groomed all-round for a challenging corporate career. For the entrepreneurially inclined, BU’s incubator, Hatchery, provides the perfect nursery for new ideas. Our bustling fun-filled campus with its generous sprinkling of eateries and sports facilities – including pickleball courts – provides the perfect setting for student group discussion around projects and cases. Or just pure fun with friends. I invite you to share this excitement at the School of Management at Bennett University. To take your career forward, to step forward with us: to move with the Times. GAIP, NUS is a 4 week fully residential program based on Artificial Intelligence, Data Analytics, Big Data, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and IoT (Internet of Things) which enables student to explore the avenues for skill upliftment, networking with global students and faculty members of NUS. Corporate Immersion Programme BBA students, as part of Corporate Immersion Programme, went to Thailand for 10 days and to do project work from 1st July to 10th July 2022 at various properties and locations of MQDC, Thailand. Students were given a project to identify problems in the surrounding areas of Thailand (Bangkok). For the identified problems, students interacted with the Local stakeholders at Bangkok and submitted a report with proposed solutions for the identified problems to the Director and Project Head -MQDC. They were given Project Completion Certificate at the end of the immersion program and received excellent feedback from the management team. Learn Future Technologies Exposure to work on Future Technologies (5G+AI, smart sensors, Cognitive IoT, End-to-end Systems, Quantum Communication, Signal Processing, 5G Antenna Design) Encourage entrepreneurship 100 Cr+ funding for start-up projects Work under the supervision of Industry and Research Experts BU has Collaboration with industry and research institutes ABOUT About BTech ECE B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering focuses on developing a suitable academic environment to promote research attitude, employability readiness and entrepreneurial spirit among students. It is an intensely focused undergraduate program tailored to a student’s interests and real-world opportunities. The Electronics and Communication Engineers work in the areas of design, development, research, and manufacturing of Internet of Things, 5G & Beyond and Information Processing & Analytics. Students undertake courses in Basic Sciences, Mathematics, Basic Engineering, along with core subjects of Electronics & Communication Engineering (ECE) Electronic Communication Engineering is supported by a relevant, up-to-date curriculum, unique teaching pedagogy, fully equipped labs, and experienced faculty. B.Tech ECE Curriculum 2024-28 B.Tech ECE Curriculum 2023-27 Key Highlights Open Electives These courses are University-wide courses from different schools. A student in ECE can choose a course from an open list of electives provided by ECE department. Specialisations Embedded Systems and IoT In the Embedded System, hardware design and software programming are used for innovative product development. The various embedded systems are interconnected using IoT based network. IoT is very crucial for the industry as well as other environments in achieving high productivity, efficiency, safe working environment, and low carbon emissions. IoT solutions are going to play a very important role in home automation, security, and various industrial problems with 5G communications. In Embedded Systems and IoT specialisation, students are imparted knowledge of hardware design, software development, and interconnection with network and cloud infrastructure. The students completing the course can opt for jobs in various companies such as Synopsys, Intel, NXP, Qualcomm, Analog Devices, Samsung, Microsoft, Siemens. The various job profiles in the industry are IoT lead Engineer, IP Design Lead Engineer, Hardware Architect, ASIC Design FPGA Engineer, Application Engineer. The courses addresses various topics on Hardware-Software Co-design, Real Time Operating System, Data Management and Cloud Computing, and IoT Networks and Protocols Data and Signal Analytics The Specialisation focuses design, development, and research areas such as Data Analytics, Machine and Deep Learning, Information Processing & Analytics, an Computer Vision. The specialisation is carefully designed to attain technical aspects that enable students to grow into competent Data Analytics professionals. Signal and Data analytics is about making conclusions from the information they contain. Data analytics with machine learning technologies are widely used in commercial industries, research communities. The specialisation focuses on building foundations in data analytics using machine learning, a deep understanding of the area of specialisation, and innovative ability to solve new problems. Interdisciplinary skill sets and knowledge to handle and analyse large amounts of data are now essential for many career paths. The use of data analytics is a growing trend spanning all industries. The specialisation offers Machine Learning, Exploratory Data Analytics, Big Data, Natural Language Processing, and Artificial Intelligence, along with basic courses in programming languages. Mechatronics Mechatronics is an interdisciplinary field that combines mechanical engineering, electronics, computer science, and control engineering to design and create intelligent systems and products. The goal of mechatronics is to integrate these diverse engineering disciplines to develop more efficient and intelligent systems with enhanced functionalities. In India and around the world, there is a growing demand for mechatronics professionals due to the increasing complexity of modern engineering systems. Mechatronics engineers are involved in the design, development, and maintenance of systems and products that involve a combination of mechanical and electronic components, often incorporating sensors, actuators, and control systems. They work in various industries, including manufacturing, automotive, aerospace, robotics, healthcare, and more. In India, the manufacturing sector, which is a significant contributor to the economy, has a strong demand for mechatronics engineers. The automotive industry relies heavily on mechatronics for the development of smart vehicles with advanced features like autonomous driving and intelligent control systems. Job opportunities for mechatronics engineers include roles such as mechatronics engineer, automation engineer, robotics engineer, control systems engineer, and product development engineer. The skill set required for mechatronics professionals spans mechanical design, electronics, programming, and system integration. The various companies which offer job opportunities to Mechatronics engineers are Toyota, General Motors, Tesla, Siemens, ABB, Boston Dynamics, iRobot, NVIDIA, Boeing, Lockheed Martin, Medtronic, Johnson & Johnson, Samsung, Sony, LG Electronics, Apple, Intel, IBM, Google, NASA, ISRO, Accenture, Deloitte, etc. As industries continue to adopt advanced technologies, the demand for mechatronics professionals is expected to rise, providing ample job opportunities in India and globally for individuals with expertise in this interdisciplinary field. VLSI Design VLSI (Very Large Scale Integration) Design is a field of electronics engineering that involves the design and development of integrated circuits (ICs) with a large number of transistors on a single chip. VLSI technology enables the integration of thousands to billions of transistors, allowing for the creation of complex and highly functional electronic systems on a small silicon chip. In India and around the world, VLSI design plays a crucial role in the semiconductor industry, contributing to the development of advanced electronic devices such as microprocessors, memory chips, and application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs). VLSI engineers are involved in various stages of the IC design process, including architecture design, logic design, physical design, and verification. In India, the demand for VLSI engineers is significant, driven by the growth of the electronics and semiconductor industry. Companies like Intel, Qualcomm, Broadcom, and Samsung have a strong presence and offer job opportunities in VLSI design. Additionally, numerous Indian companies and startups focus on VLSI design services and contribute to the global semiconductor supply chain. Globally, VLSI engineers find employment in leading semiconductor companies across the United States, Europe, and Asia. Silicon Valley in the U.S. is a hub for VLSI design, with companies like NVIDIA, AMD, and Apple actively involved in chip design. Job opportunities in VLSI design include roles such as VLSI design engineer, ASIC design engineer, physical design engineer, verification engineer, and more. With the advent of new technologies such as 5G, artificial intelligence, and the Internet of Things (IoT), the demand for VLSI professionals is expected to grow, as these technologies rely heavily on advanced semiconductor solutions. As the global electronics industry continues to evolve, VLSI engineers will play a crucial role in shaping the future of technology, making it a field with promising job prospects both in India and worldwide. Specialisations Elective Data and Signal Analytics specialisation courses Data Analytics, Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing, Statistical Signal Processing, and Deep Learning. Embedded Systems and IoT specialisation courses Embedded System Hardware, Embedded System Hardware-Software Co-Design, Introduction to Real Time Operating System, IoT Networks and Protocols Minors Students are required to earn more credits in a field other than ECE through crediting a specific set of courses offered by other departments (16 credits from other departments). Minors offered by different engineering streams are open to B.Tech. ECE students. Mechatronics (with Mechanical Engineering Department at BU) Honours Honours in Electronics and Communication Engineering is obtained by earning 18 more credits than the regular degree by studying six advanced courses. Projects 12 credit Major Project in the 8th semester, five credits in the 7th semester, and one credit in the 6th semester are mandatory. Students have an opportunity to start the project in the 6th semester, continue to evolve during the summer after 6th and 7th semesters, and finally finish it in the final semester. Such a long association helps the student gain considerable knowledge and skills required for prospective jobs. Internships One internship at the end of the 6th semester is part of the curriculum. Students have interned at INRS EMT Lab (Montreal, Canada), IITs, IIITs ECE Research Labs, HERE Maps, UST Software, Airport Authority of India, and various other industries. NRI Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR). Selection Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third* subject with highest score). *Third subject should not be vocational- Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc Events & Gallery Guest Lectures Events Industrial visit at CEERI, Pilani, Rajasthan Teachers' Day celebration by Resonance Club MediaTek Noida Industrial Visit Student Testimonials My experience at Bennett was truly amazing. ECE Batch: 2018-2022 OUR ASK B.Tech. ECE Frequently Asked Questions Students of which career interest can pursue B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering? B.Tech Electronics and Communication Engineering is offered to students who are interested in public sector companies like Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL), ISRO, DRDO, IES and private sector companies as well. How can one choose career in IoT (Internet of Things)? Student pursuing B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering can become an expert in IoT, by learning courses related to Embedded Systems and IoT and improving skills by doing projects in this area. The different courses in this area are Embedded System Hardware, Embedded System Hardware-Software Co- Design, IoT Networks and Protocols, and Embedded Systems Design and Programming, IoT Systems and Software. What specializations do the programme offer? It offers three specializations Data and Signal Analytics Embedded Systems and IoT VLSI Design What will be a prime focus for the ECE aspirants this academic year and its future impression? TThe electronics industry will be a game-changer after the government had announced in its Union Budget 2022-2023. Government has taken various initiatives to make the electronics industry robust, of which electronics chip manufacturing has gained momentum. There will be around 1.3 Lacs jobs per year for ECE students in India by 2025 (Source: about ECE in Electronics for You). This is the right time to take admission in ECE. Even, All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) has issued a letter to various educational institutes/universities in India to start Chip design and manufacturing courses in Electronics.
