BA Global Liberal Arts - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Global Pathways Program – BA Global Liberal Arts Study at University of Essex, UK Apply Now Global Pathways Program - BA Global Liberal Arts (2+1) Unlock a world of possibilities with the BA Global - Liberal Arts (2+1) Program at Bennett University. This remarkable study abroad program allows students to build a strong foundation in creative expression, critical thinking, and storytelling. Students complete the first two years at Bennett University, India and one year at the University of Essex. Graduates receive a globally recognized degree based on the specialization under the Global Pathways Program (GPP), equipping them with international exposure, leadership skills, and cross-cultural competencies. The program equips students with the skills, knowledge, and global perspective needed to thrive in today’s dynamic world. This program not only broadens the academic horizons but also connects students to global networks and opportunities, preparing them for a future without limits. Program Details Name Duration University Curriculum Highlights BA Global Liberal Arts (2+1) 3 Years Years 1 & 2 at Bennett University , India Foundations in creative writing Year 3 at University of Essex , UK Advanced coursework in creative writing and interdisciplinary electives Final Program Degree Based on specialization: BA Business Economics, BA Economics, BA Financial Economics, BA Financial Economics and Accounting, BA Management Economics, B.Sc. Economics, B.Sc. Economics with Data Science, BA Financial Economics and Accounting, B.Sc. Management Economics, and B.Sc. Financial Economics Program Objectives To provide students with an exposure to international education with real-world exposure. To develop critical thinking, leadership and entrepreneurship skills with global standards. To enhance career prospects in creative writing. Global Academic Experience Students gain international exposure by studying at globally recognized universities. Seamless Credit Transfer Academic programs are carefully mapped to ensure a smooth transition between institutions. Cost-Effective Pathway Students save on overall tuition and living costs compared to a full-length international program. Internationally Recognized Degree Graduates earn a degree from a reputed partner university, enhancing their global career prospects. Cultural and Professional Growth Exposure to diverse academic, cultural, and professional environments enriches personal and career development. ABOUT Why Choose BA Global - Liberal Arts (2+1) Access to globally recognized business degrees from top university at UK. Internship & networking opportunities in India and the UK. Cultural & academic diversity, enriching students’ global perspectives. A vibrant, student-centric environment with excellent support for international students. By completing initial years in India, students can save up to INR 40-50 lakhs compared to completing a full degree abroad. Eligible for up to 2 years of post-study work permit. Partner University University of Essex, UK University of Essex, established in 1964, is a distinguished public research university located in Colchester, United Kingdom. Renowned for its commitment to pushing boundaries and fostering curiosity, Essex has built a reputation for academic excellence and innovative research. The university is recognized for research excellence in computer science, sociology, economics and econometrics, politics and international studies, modern languages and linguistics. Numerous opportunities exist in creative writing, drama, film production, literature, and interdisciplinary fields across London and other major cities. Extensive industry exposure, networking opportunities, and skill development through internships, practical coursework and collaborations with industry professionals. Who Should Apply High school graduates aspiring for a global career in creative writing. Students seeking affordable international education exposure in UK. Individuals aiming for higher studies or career opportunities abroad. Curriculum Details The BA Global Liberal Arts program curriculum at Bennett University is designed to provide a well-rounded education that nurtures critical thinking, creativity, and interdisciplinary knowledge. The curriculum is aimed at intellectually stimulating education, structured around three key tiers—Foundational Courses, Core Courses (Major), and Electives. It emphasizes analytical thinking, effective communication, and problem-solving skills through interactive classes, seminars, and projects. Students engage in intellectual exploration while developing a global perspective and an appreciation for diverse ideas and cultures. This versatile program prepares graduates for dynamic careers or advanced studies in various fields. Curriculum BA Global Liberal Arts @ Bennett University Read More... Jindal Global University, Sonipat ELIGIBILITY BA Global - Liberal Arts (2+1) Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process Eligibility-Domestic & SAARC Domestic Domestic: Passed XII standard from any recognized Education Board with minimum 60% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI Passing marks with 55% aggregate in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR). Note : Student must comply with the English language proficiency requirements (IELTS/ TOEFL as per partner university guidelines). Selection Process Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 Subjects Score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score). Personal Interview will be conducted with the panel during the admission process. OUR ASK Frequently Asked Questions What is the Global Pathways Programme at Bennett University? The Global Pathways Programme (GPP) is an academic collaboration that allows students to complete the first two years of their BA Liberal Arts at Bennett University and transfer to University of Essex, UK, for the remaining one year. Upon successful completion, students earn a globally recognized degree based on the specialization chosen from University of Essex, UK. What are the benefits of enrolling in this program? Internationally Recognized Degree from a top UK university. Cost-effective compared to completing full graduation abroad. Seamless Credit Transfer with a structured curriculum. Global exposure & cultural diversity by studying in both India and the UK. Post-study work permit in the UK for up to 1-2 years. What is the eligibility criterion for this program? Students must have: Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with minimum 60% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. Student must comply with the English language proficiency requirements (IELTS/ TOEFL as per partner university guidelines). How does the 2+1 model work? Years 1 & 2: : : Study at Bennett University, India–Foundations in creative writing. Years 3: Transfer to University of Essex, UK–Advanced coursework in creative writing and interdisciplinary electives. What degree will I receive after completing the program? You will be awarded a globally recognized degree in BA Liberal Arts based on specialization from University of Essex, UK. How much cost savings can I expect? By following the 2+1 model, students save approximately ₹40-50 lakhs, compared to completing all three years at University of Essex, UK. What career opportunities are available after this program? Opportunities in global companies. Eligibility for a UK post-study work permit for up to 1-2 years. Research & internship opportunities at University of Essex, UK How do I apply for the BA Global Liberal Arts (2+1) program? Applying to BA Global Liberal Arts (2+1) program at Bennet University is quick and easy. To apply for the admission, all you need is to share your details (takes less than a minute) and our team will get back you. BCA + MCA (Dual Degree) - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login BCA + MCA (Dual Degree) Apply Now BCA + MCA (Dual Degree) Apply Now About BCA + MCA (Dual Degree) BCA + MCA (Dual Degree) Program is a 4-year comprehensive program designed to develop strong foundations and advanced expertise in computer applications and emerging digital technologies. The curriculum offers a balanced blend of core computing subjects and hands-on practical learning, covering programming, data structures, databases, operating systems, computer networks, mobile programming, and modern software development practices. Students gain industry-relevant exposure through competitive programming, design thinking, innovation-driven learning, internships, and project-based training, enabling them to apply theoretical concepts to real-world challenges and graduate as job-ready technology professionals. Duration 4 Years Specialisations 10 Credits 160 (4 Years) Start Date August 2026 Specialisations Artificial Intelligence: This specialisation focuses on building intelligent systems that enhance human–machine interaction across key industries such as healthcare, banking, and automobiles. Blockchain: Designed with industry experts, this specialisation equips students with in-demand skills in blockchain technologies, smart contracts, cryptography, and digital currencies. Cyber Security: This specialisation equips students with practical skills in securing digital systems through ethical hacking, malware analysis, and advanced cyber defence techniques. Data Science: Focused on extracting insights from data, this specialisation trains students in analytics, visualisation, big data, and business intelligence for applications across smart cities, BFSI, and fintech. Cloud Computing: This specialisation develops expertise in scalable cloud solutions, DevOps tools, and industry-recognised cloud certifications across AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud. DevOps: Emphasising continuous integration and delivery, this specialisation enables students to streamline software development, testing, and deployment using modern DevOps practices. Drones: This specialisation combines technical and analytical skills to prepare students for drone-based applications in infrastructure monitoring, agriculture, public safety, and law enforcement. Gaming: This specialisation introduces students to one of the world’s fastest-growing industries, covering game development concepts that blend technology, creativity, and interactive entertainment. Full Stack: This specialisation equips students with end-to-end web and application development skills, covering front-end frameworks, backend systems, and cloud-based deployment. UI/UX: This specialisation focuses on creating intuitive and engaging digital experiences by teaching user research, interaction design, and modern interface design tools. Curriculum Details The BCA + MCA (Dual Degree) curriculum is NEP-compliant and industry-oriented, focusing on state-of-the-art subjects, experiential learning, and code-based education. Experiential and practical learning with a strong balance of theory, labs, and projects. Research-oriented approach emphasising analytical thinking, case studies, and problem-solving. Diverse specialisation pathways in high-demand technology domains. Comprehensive project exposure, including dissertation, capstone project, and industrial/R&D project. BCA + MCA curriculum 2026-30 Click Here>> Why BCA+ MCA (Dual Degree) at Bennett University? Single Integrated Pathway (BCA + MCA): Seamless undergraduate-to-postgraduate progression, saving time and effort. Career-Focused Curriculum: Aligned with modern IT job roles and industry expectations. Hands-on Learning with Projects & Labs: Strong emphasis on practical implementation and real-world problem solving. Future-Ready Skill Development: Exposure to Full Stack, Cloud, Data Analytics, Cybersecurity, DevOps, and AI applications. Enhanced Employability & Higher Study Support: Strong placement readiness with pathways to certifications, research, and higher education. Learning Outcomes By the end of the program, graduates will be equipped to: Build strong conceptual knowledge in computer applications and advanced computing domains. Apply programming and problem-solving skills to develop efficient, secure, and scalable software solutions. Gain practical expertise through labs and projects using real-world tools, frameworks, and platforms. Analyse, design, and manage data using modern database systems and computing environments. Design and implement end-to-end applications integrating front-end, back-end, and deployment practices. Adopt professional software engineering practices, including testing, debugging, documentation, and lifecycle management. Career Pathways The BCA + MCA (Dual Degree) program prepares graduates for diverse roles across the IT ecosystem. With strong technical foundations and project-based learning, graduates may also progress into advanced roles in Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Cloud Computing, and Information Security, across software companies, startups, MNCs, banking, healthcare, e-commerce, and government IT services. Career opportunities include roles such as: Software Developer Web Developer Full Stack Developer Mobile Application Developer Data Analyst Business Analyst Database Administrator System Administrator Network Engineer Cloud Support Engineer DevOps Associate Cybersecurity Analyst QA/Software Tester UI/UX Associate IT Support Engineer IT Consultant The program also prepares graduates to pursue higher studies, research (Ph.D.), and professional certifications, ensuring long-term career growth. Eligibility Criteria Indian National: Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with minimum 55% marks Aggregate OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects with English as medium of instruction. NRI: Pass with 55% aggrigate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Process Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score) Program Fee Get detailed information about the BCA + MCA (Degree Dual) program fee structure, including tuition, hostel fee, and other applicable charges Click here for details. MBA (Finance) Course, Eligibility, Fees, Curriculum | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login MBA Finance Apply Now MBA Finance Apply Now About MBA (Finance) The Finance specialisation of MBA program enables learners with a thorough understanding of financial markets, investment strategies, corporate financial management, and modern decision-making styles through quantitative analysis. It combines analytical rigour and strategic financial planning to prepare graduates for continuously changing economic landscapes. Through rigorous and practical learning activities, students develop competencies in drafting unique and solution-oriented valuation models, mastering fintech-enabled frameworks, and techniques, and delivering robust portfolio performance across asset classes. With real-time engagement towards the latest technologies and business world challenges, the specialisation offers high-level financial skills blended with innovation and leadership. With the help of a future-ready curriculum, the specialisation ensures the learner’s industry readiness for various roles in investment banking, corporate finance, wealth management, and other strategic functional roles across different industries such as Banking, Fintech, Consulting, and beyond. Duration 2 Years Specialisations - Credits - Start Date July 2026 Curriculum Highlights The curriculum is thoughtfully curated with courses that are in high demand from an employment perspective. It essentially helps learners in acquiring a deep understanding of global financial markets, frameworks of corporate finance, and contemporary financial challenges. Learners get to study a mix of core and domain-specific electives, aimed at the development of comprehensive financial capability. Core Courses Corporate Finance Managerial Accounting Electives Financial Modelling Business Valuation International Finance Security and Portfolio Management Banking Operations & Credit Management Infrastructure and the Private Sector Behavioural Finance Wealth Management Financial Derivatives & Risk Management Sustainable Finance & Reporting Financial Econometrics Why an MBA (Finance) from Bennett University Industry-Aligned Curriculum: Focus on banking, financial services, investment management, fintech, and corporate finance. Experiential Learning: Live trading simulations, financial case studies, valuation projects, and investment competitions. Advanced Analytical Exposure: Hands-on training in financial modelling, Excel, Python, and modern analytical tools. Expert Faculty & Industry Mentors: Learning from experienced academicians and finance professionals from leading financial institutions. Global Career Opportunities: Strong pathways into investment banking, asset management, consulting, corporate finance, and fintech roles. Program Objectives The program aims to develop strong conceptual and practical skills in corporate finance and investment management, while building robust quantitative and analytical capabilities through research-driven financial modelling and valuation tools. It equips students with employable skills in financial decision-making, risk assessment, and strategic capital allocation, and develops integrated competencies in financial strategy and portfolio management. Overall, the program aims to transform learners into analytical and strategic leaders capable of navigating complex financial markets and contributing effectively to value creation within organisations. Learning Outcomes Students will be able to apply established financial frameworks and valuation methodologies to make sound investment and corporate finance decisions. They will develop the capability to build full-scale financial models for business valuation and strategic planning, configure appropriate risk management and portfolio optimisation strategies, and make informed use of the latest financial technologies and analytical decision-making tools. Graduates will also be equipped to analyse financial instruments and markets to identify investment opportunities and manage systematic risks effectively. Career Pathways Graduates are prepared for specialist and managerial roles in Corporate Finance Manager, Portfolio Manager, Investment Analyst, Financial Consultant, Equity Research Analyst, Investment Banking Associate, Credit or Risk Analyst, Private Equity Associate, Wealth Management Advisor, Financial Planning and Analysis Manager, Insurance and Risk Manager, and FinTech Specialist. They are recruited across diverse sectors such as Banking and Financial Services, Insurance and Asset Management, Investment Banking, Private Equity and Venture Capital, FinTech and Digital Finance, Corporate Finance functions including Treasury and FP&A, Stock Exchanges and Capital Markets, Wealth Management and Mutual Funds, Financial Advisory Consulting, and Real Estate and Infrastructure Finance. Experiential Learning Hands-on portfolio management through investment clubs and live trading simulations Case-based learning using real market data and valuation scenarios Financial modelling workshops using Excel, Python, and advanced tools Investment pitch competitions and finance case challenges Guest lectures, masterclasses, and mentorship by senior finance professionals Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Passed Graduation in any discipline from a recognised university with a minimum 50% marks with English as the medium of instruction NRI: For international applicants, a minimum of 50% in the qualifying examination is required, along with having studied the prerequisite subjects for admission to the desired program. Selection Process Selection will be based on the weighted score of CAT / XAT / NMAT / MAT / CMAT / GMAT / BU MAT/ CUET Percentile score & Graduation & Personal Interview. MBA (Finance) Program Fee Get detailed information about the MBA (Finance) program fee structure, including tuition fees, hostel charges, and other applicable costs. Click here for details. MBA in Finance is a specialisation that focuses on financial management, investment analysis, banking, corporate finance, financial markets, and risk management, preparing students for strategic financial decision-making roles. What skills are essential for MBA Finance students? Key skills one attains include Analytical and quantitative ability, financial modelling and valuation, problem-solving and decision-making, Excel and financial software proficiency, and attention to detail and business acumen. Is MBA Finance suitable for non-commerce or non-math backgrounds? Yes, while a commerce or math background is helpful, concepts are taught from fundamentals, and students can succeed with consistent practice and conceptual clarity. What is the difference between Finance and Accounting specialisation? Finance focuses on decision-making, investments, and fund management Accounting focuses on recording, reporting, and compliance How can students improve their performance in finance subjects for an MBA? MBA students should practice numerical problems regularly, follow financial news and market trends, and work on case studies, use Excel for hands-on learning, and participate in simulations and live projects. What is the future scope of MBA Finance? With the growth of FinTech, AI-driven finance, ESG investing, and global financial markets, MBA Finance continues to offer strong career growth and leadership opportunities. Is MBA (Finance) stressful compared to other specialisations? MBA (Finance) can be academically rigorous due to numbers and analysis, but with proper planning, time management, and regular practice, it is manageable and rewarding. B.Com (International Accounting & Finance) Eligibility, Fee, Curriculum | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login B.Com (International Accounting & Finance) Integrated with ACCA* Apply Now B.Com (International Accounting & Finance) Integrated with ACCA* Apply Now About M.Tech Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning About B.Com (International Accounting & Finance) Integrated with ACCA* The Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com) International Accounting & Finance, integrated with ACCA–UK, is a four-year globally aligned program that prepares students for leadership roles in accounting, finance, and strategic financial management. It combines a strong commerce foundation with international professional competencies and offers up to nine ACCA paper exemptions. The program blends core subjects with advanced ACCA-aligned areas such as Financial Management, Audit & Assurance, Taxation, and Strategic Business Leadership. Through immersive learning, live projects, simulations, and case-based pedagogy, students develop strong analytical, ethical, and industry-relevant skills, graduating as globally competitive finance professionals. Duration 3 Years/4 Years (Hons./Hons. With Research) Specializations NA Credits 120 (3-Year) 160 (4-Year) Start Date Aug/Sep. 2026 (Tentative) Eligibility Criteria Indian Nationals: Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with English and Mathematics/Commerce & any other third subject with minimum 55% marks OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI: Pass with 55% aggrigate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Criteria Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English and Maths/Commerce as compulsory subjects and any other third subject with highest score) B.Com (International Accounting & Finance) Program Fee Get complete details of the B.Com (International Accounting & Finance) program fee, including tuition, hostel, and other applicable charges. Click here for details. Why B.Com (International Accounting & Finance) Integrated with ACCA* from Bennett University? Dual Advantage: Earn a globally recognised ACCA qualification alongside a full-time B.Com degree. Global Employability: Curriculum aligned with IFRS, GAAP, and international financial standards. Experiential Pedagogy: Harvard-style case studies, live simulations, and corporate mentorships. Technology-Enabled Finance: Training in FinTech, Data Analytics, and Digital Accounting Tools (Tally, SAP, Power BI). Leadership Orientation: Emphasis on professional ethics, communication, and global decision-making. Program Objectives The program aims to develop globally competent finance leaders with analytical precision, ethical grounding, and the agility to adapt to a rapidly changing financial ecosystem. By integrating ACCA’s professional benchmarks with academic rigour, it enables students to translate conceptual knowledge into impactful business insights and strategic financial decisions. Learning Outcomes Graduates will be able to demonstrate proficiency in global accounting standards such as IFRS, GAAP, and ESG reporting; integrate quantitative analysis and financial technology into decision-making; apply ACCA-aligned competencies in auditing, risk management, and business leadership; exhibit ethical and cross-cultural sensitivity in financial communication; and lead with a strategic, evidence-based approach to organisational finance. Curriculum Highlights Integrated ACCA Curriculum: Up to 9 paper exemptions; seamless progression to professional certification. Capstone Consulting Project: Solve real business problems in partnership with industry mentors. Leadership and Ethics Modules: Developing emotional intelligence and professional resilience. International Exposure: Study tours, global immersion modules, and cross-border internships. Career Pathways Graduates are prepared for a wide range of high-impact global roles, including Global Accountant, Financial Analyst, Investment Banker, Auditor, Risk Consultant, Corporate Finance Specialist, CFO Trainee, Business Analyst, Financial Planner, Treasury Consultant, and Entrepreneur in Financial Advisory or FinTech ventures. They can also pursue higher education pathways such as ACCA membership, MBA (Finance/Strategy), CFA, CPA, CMA (US), or MSc (Finance) in India or abroad. It is a full-time, four-year (eight-semester) undergraduate program. Does the program provide ACCA paper exemptions? Yes, students receive up to nine exemptions at the Applied Knowledge and Applied Skills levels. Is ACCA coaching integrated into the curriculum of the B.Com course? Yes, certified ACCA trainers conduct sessions aligned with the academic timetable. Can I pursue global career opportunities after graduation? Absolutely, the ACCA qualification is recognised in over 180 countries, opening doors to global career prospects. What practical learning opportunities are available? Students engage in consulting projects, internships, global immersion programs, and corporate simulations. Which firms typically recruit from this program? Graduates are recruited by Big 4 accounting firms, financial consultancies, multinational corporations, investment banks, and FinTech startups. Is B.Com (International Accounting & Finance) Integrated with ACCA* suitable for students without a strong math background? Yes, while quantitative aptitude helps, the first year includes courses that build essential analytical foundations. How is this program different from a traditional B.Com? It integrates ACCA’s global curriculum, advanced financial technology, and industry-oriented modules, making it future-ready. Can I pursue higher education after completing this course? Yes, you can pursue MBA, CFA, CPA, CMA, or MSc (Finance) in India or abroad. What kind of global exposure will I receive? Students participate in study tours, international expert lectures, global consulting projects, and cross-border collaborations. Btech Mtech CSE Dual Degree - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login Btech Mtech CSE Dual Degree Apply Now Btech Mtech CSE Dual Degree Apply Now About B.Tech + M.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Dual Degree) The B.Tech + M.Tech (Dual Degree) program in CSE is a 5-year dual-degree program designed for students who wish to gain a strong foundation as well as advanced expertise in computing. The program seamlessly integrates undergraduate fundamentals with postgraduate level expertise, helping students save an academic year compared to separate degrees. Program outcomes focus on developing strong problem-solving, design, research, and innovation skills to address complex computing challenges. Highlights of the program include industry-aligned curriculum, advanced laboratories, project-based learning, internships, and research exposure. Duration 5 Years Specialisations 16 Credits 200-210 (5 Years) Start Date August 2026 Specialisations Artificial Intelligence: An industry-designed AI specialisation aligned with global standards, offering advanced learning in vision, NLP, reinforcement learning, and real-world projects with leaders like NVIDIA and SAP. Cloud Computing: A high-demand specialisation focused on scalable cloud solutions, DevOps tools, and globally recognised certifications across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. Data Science: A data-driven specialisation preparing students for careers in analytics, AI, BFSI, and smart cities through advanced modelling, visualisation, and big data analytics. Cyber Security: An industry-driven cyber security specialisation addressing modern threats through ethical hacking, malware analysis, blockchain security, and global certifications. Blockchain: A future-ready specialisation designed with industry experts, covering blockchain technologies, smart contracts, cryptography, and digital currencies. Robotic Process Automation: A hands-on specialisation focused on automating enterprise processes using industry-standard RPA tools and platforms. Gaming: A creative-technology specialisation preparing students for the global gaming industry through immersive design, development, and interactive digital experiences. Augmented & Virtual Reality: An industry-aligned specialisation preparing students to design immersive AR, VR, and XR experiences for gaming, design, and digital interaction. Drones: A multidisciplinary specialisation enabling students to apply drone technologies to infrastructure, agriculture, surveillance, and public safety solutions. IoT and Robotics: A convergent specialisation combining hardware and software to build intelligent, autonomous systems using IoT-enabled robotics. Quantum Computing: A cutting-edge specialisation introducing quantum foundations, quantum machine learning, cryptography, and next-generation computing architectures. Product Design Technology: A design-engineering specialisation focused on transforming innovative concepts into functional products using computational and analytical tools. DevOps in collab with Xebia: A practice-oriented specialisation that integrates development, testing, and deployment to enable faster, scalable, and reliable software delivery. Full Stack in collab with Xebia: A comprehensive development specialisation covering front-end, back-end, mobile, and cloud technologies aligned with modern industry workflows. Mobile Technologies: A platform-focused specialisation centred on iOS development using Swift to build fast, secure, and high-performance mobile applications. UI/UX: A user-centric design specialisation focused on research-driven, accessible, and intuitive digital product experiences aligned with industry best practices. Curriculum Details Core Courses: Programming, Data Structures, Algorithms, Computer Architecture, Operating Systems, DBMS, Software Engineering Specialisations / Majors: The program offers 16 specialisations briefly outlined above in the Specialisations section. Minors & Electives: Interdisciplinary courses from Management, Mathematics, Electronics, and emerging technologies Research / Honours Component: Research methodology, guided research projects, publications, and a year-long M.Tech thesis Why B.Tech + M.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Dual Degree) at Bennett University? Advantages of Dual Degree: Earn both B.Tech and M.Tech degrees in just 5 years, saving time and gaining advanced qualifications. Initial expertise: Experience from undergraduate-level to postgraduate-level expertise. Industry and Research Focus: The curriculum is aligned with industry requirements, certifications, internships, and research-based projects. Strong career growth: Improved employability for higher-level roles, leadership positions, and doctoral studies. Holistic Skill Development: Emphasis is placed on innovation, problem-solving, and hands-on learning through labs and capstone projects. Learning Outcomes On completion of the B.Tech + M.Tech (Dual Degree) program in CSE, the graduates will demonstrate the basic strength required in computer science and advanced expertise in specialised areas like: Design, develop, and analyse complex computing systems effectively Apply research methods to solve real-world computing problems Use modern tools and techniques efficiently and responsibly Exhibit professional ethics, moral responsibility, and a commitment to lifelong learning Be well prepared for careers in industry, research, and higher studies Career Pathways Graduates of the B.Tech + M.Tech CSE (Dual Degree) program are well prepared for roles across software companies, startups, MNCs, banking, healthcare, e-commerce, and government IT services. Moreover, the program also provides a strong foundation for PhD studies, international higher education, research and development, and entrepreneurship. Career opportunities include roles such as: Software Engineer / Full Stack Developer AI & ML Engineer Data Scientist / Data Analyst Cyber Security Analyst Network Architect / Cloud Engineer Research Engineer / R&D Professional Entrepreneur / Startup Founder Higher Studies (Ph.D. in India or Abroad) Eligibility Criteria Indian National: Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with Physics and Mathematics & any other third* subject with minimum 55% marks OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI: Pass with 55% aggrigate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent (with physics & mathematics) and English language requirement (ELR). *Third subject should not be vocational- Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc Selection Process Selection will be based on merit of Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of Physics and Maths as compulsory subjects and any other third subject with highest score) *Third subject should not be vocational- Music, Dance, Painting, Home Science, Arts, etc Program Fee Get detailed information about the B.Tech + M.Tech (Dual Degree) program fee structure, including tuition, hostel fee, and other applicable charges Click here for details. The program spans 5 years and awards both B.Tech and M.Tech degrees. When do students choose a specialisation in B.Tech + M.Tech CSE (Dual Degree)? Specialisations are chosen at the start of the third semester of the program. Is there an option for early exit in B.Tech + M.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Dual Degree)? Yes, the option of early exit is there as per NEP-2020. Does the B.Tech + M.Tech CSE (Dual Degree) program include internships? Yes, mandatory internships and industry projects are integrated into the curriculum. Are research opportunities available after B.Tech + M.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Dual Degree)? Yes, students undertake research projects and a full-year M.Tech thesis. How is the B.Tech + M.Tech CSE (Dual Degree) program different from regular B.Tech? It offers early specialisation, research exposure, and saves one academic year compared to a separate B.Tech and M.Tech programs. Are international opportunities available after B.Tech + M.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Dual Degree)? Yes, through student exchange programs, global certifications, and international collaborations. What career options are available after graduation in B.Tech + M.Tech CSE (Dual Degree)? Master of Science (M.Sc.) Artificial Intelligence Course, Eligibility, Fee, curriculum | Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login M.Sc. ( Artificial Intelligence ) Apply Now M.Sc. ( Artificial Intelligence ) Apply Now About M.Sc. (Artificial Intelligence) The Master of Science (M.Sc.) Artificial Intelligence at Bennett University is a 2-year postgraduate program crafted for students aspiring to become AI scientists, researchers, and advanced domain experts. This program deepens scientific and mathematical understanding of AI and emphasises research, innovation, and specialisation in next-generation AI. Students gain expertise in areas such as Advanced Deep Learning, Reinforcement Learning, and Generative AI, culminating in a 20-credit Academic/Industry Project with Dissertation. The program is aligned with NEP 2020 and the National Credit Framework (NCrF), preparing graduates for high-impact roles in R&D, AI innovation, and Ph.D. pathways globally. Duration 2 Years Specialisations 4 Credits 80 Start Date July 2026 Eligibility Criteria Indian National: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering/Technology or equivalent of BCA/B.Sc with minimum 60% marks. Selection Process Selection will be based on Aggregate Graduation Percentage. M.Sc. (Artificial Intelligence) Program Fee Get detailed information about the M.Sc. Artificial Intelligence program fee structure, including tuition, hostel fee, and other applicable charges. Click here for details. Why M.Sc. (Artificial Intelligence) from Bennett University Our M.Sc. (AI) program is designed for students who aspire to pursue advanced research, scientific inquiry, and innovation in Artificial Intelligence. Research Focus: Emphasises academic and industry projects with a 20-credit dissertation, building strong research and analytical capabilities. Advanced Core Curriculum: Covers Advanced Deep Learning, Reinforcement Learning, and Physics-Inspired Machine Learning, providing depth in algorithmic and mathematical understanding. Scientific Foundation: Develops strong foundations in mathematical modeling, algorithm design, and scientific problem-solving, preparing students for Ph.D. programs and high-level R&D roles. Specialisation Tracks: Offers domain-specific electives in Signal Processing, Robotics, Computer Vision, and Natural Language Processing (NLP). NEP 2020 Aligned: The curriculum follows NEP 2020 and National Credit Framework (NCrF), ensuring flexibility, credit recognition, and alignment with global standards. Specialisations Students choose specialised elective tracks to gain expertise in high-demand research and industrial areas. Signal Processing: Students gain expertise in analysing and manipulating signals for AI applications, with courses like Adaptive Signal Processing and AI for Bio-Medical Signal Processing. Robotics: Focuses on autonomous systems, decision-making, and deep learning integration, with elective courses such as Bio-Inspired Robotics and Probability Robotics. Computer Vision: Centres on visual intelligence applications, covering core techniques and advanced topics like Deep Learning for Computer Vision and 3D Computer Vision. Natural Language Processing (NLP): Specialises in language and text generation, covering Foundations of LLMs and Advanced NLU Techniques to build sophisticated NLP systems. Program Objectives To provide a rigorous scientific and mathematical foundation for students seeking to deepen their understanding of AI. The program aims to train future AI scientists, researchers, and specialised domain experts, preparing them for high-level research and development roles through advanced coursework and a substantial research dissertation. Learning Outcomes Students will gain mastery in complex mathematical modeling and advanced algorithm design (e.g., Deep Learning and Reinforcement Learning). They will possess specialised domain knowledge in areas like Generative AI, Computer Vision, and Robotics, along with scientific writing skills, positioning them for Ph.D. programs and roles requiring deep scientific knowledge. Curriculum Details The M.Sc. (AI) curriculum is structured to balance theoretical rigour with research application, ensuring graduates are prepared for both academia and industry. Core Subjects: Mathematics for Computing, Advanced Machine Learning, Advanced Deep Learning, Natural Language Processing & Generative AI, Reinforcement Learning, Research Methodology & Ethics in AI, etc. Specialisations: Signal Processing, Robotics, Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing (NLP). Minors: Not applicable. Specialisation is achieved through electives. Research Component: 20-credit Academic/Industry Project with Dissertation. The 2-Year M.Sc. track requires 40 Research / Dissertation Credits. Career Pathways Graduates of the M.Sc. Artificial Intelligence programs are prepared for careers in research, development, and advanced AI applications across academia, industry, and government sectors. Career opportunities include roles such as AI Scientist (R&D Labs), Machine Learning Researcher, Deep Learning Engineer, Quantitative Analyst (FinTech), Domain Specialist in areas like Robotics or Computational Biology, and opportunities to pursue Ph.D. programs globally. Faculty Prof. (Dr.) Rajeev Tiwari Dean, School of Artificial Intelligence Dr. Manoj Sharma Ph.D. - IIT Delhi Dr. Pratyush Pranav Ph.D. - University of Groningen Dr. Dinesh Kumar Ph.D. - University of Coimbra, Portugal Dr. Shakshi Sharma Ph.D. - University of Tartu, Estonia Dr. Neelam Choudhary Ph.D. - IIT Guwahati Dr. Navneet Pratap Singh Ph.D. - IIT Indore Dr. Yajnaseni Dash Ph.D. - IIT Delhi Dr. Gaurav Sharma Ph.D. - NIT, Hamirpur Dr. Prateek Yadav Ph.D.- IIT Delhi OUR ASK M.Sc. (Artificial Intelligence) Program Frequently Asked Questions What is the main difference between M.Sc AI and M.Tech AI? The M.Sc. AI focuses on AI theory, mathematics, and research (20-credit dissertation), while the M.Tech. emphasises engineering, system development, and industrial AI applications. What are the key skills I will gain with M.Sc. in Artificial Intelligence? You will gain mastery in complex mathematical modeling, advanced algorithm design (e.g., Deep Learning and Reinforcement Learning), scientific writing, and specialised domain knowledge in areas like Generative AI, Computer Vision, and Robotics. What are the eligibility criteria for the M.Sc. (AI) 2-year track? Candidates require a 3-Year UG Degree (Generic/Professional) in a relevant discipline (e.g., B.Sc., BCA, B.Tech.). Selection is based on the academic merit of the UG degree, a written test, and/or a personal interview. What is the duration and total credit requirement for M.Sc. AI? The M.Sc. Artificial Intelligence program is a 2-Year program for those with a 3-year UG entry, with a total credit requirement of 80 credits, aligned with the NCrF. What is the focus of the M.Sc. (AI) Program? The program is a rigorous postgraduate degree designed for students seeking to deepen their scientific and mathematical understanding of AI, leading to high-level research and specialised development roles, with a substantial research dissertation. What specialisation tracks are Available? Students can choose specialised elective tracks in Signal Processing, Robotics, Computer Vision, and Natural Language Processing (NLP) to gain expertise in high-demand research areas. What are some examples of core courses? Some of the core courses include Mathematics for Computing, Advanced Machine Learning, Advanced Deep Learning, Natural Language Processing & Generative AI, Reinforcement Learning, etc. What is the Dissertation Requirement? The program requires a major Academic/Industry Project with Dissertation (20 Credits), completed over Semesters III and IV, to solve complex, novel problems in a chosen specialisation domain. Does the program include global exposure? Yes, through international collaborations, students gain insights into global AI research practices and academic exchanges. Community initiatives like Swachh Bharat Abhiyaan promote practical applications of his values. CARYS – The School Cultural Club CARYS fosters creativity and intellectual engagement through literary competitions, Reading & Writing Roulette, and events like Girl Child Day. It provides a platform for artistic expression and social awareness, strengthening community and inclusivity. The standard foundation courses are: The Art and Science of Inquiry Logic and Reasoning Bibliophile System Thinking and Environment Sciences for Liberal Arts Intro to Polity and International Relation Build A Start-Up (BASU) Understanding Love A Primer on Public Policy Introduction to Philosophy: Understanding India Introduction to Psychology Introduction to Economics and Community Engagement Start-Up Colloquium Medical Humanities Creative Writing Thinking with Numbers Public Speaking Sports Introduction to Sociology Financial and Risk Literacy Leadership Colloquium Curriculum BA (Honours / Honours with Research) in Business Studies Program 2025-29 BA (Honours / Honours with Research) in Economics Program 2025-29 BA (Honours / Honours with Research) in English Program 2025-29 BA (Honours / Honours with Research) in Philosophy Program 2025-29 BA (Honours / Honours with Research) in B.A. Hons in Political Science & International Relations Program 2025-29 BA (Honours / Honours with Research) in Psychology Program 2025-29 BA (Honours / Honours with Research) in Sociology Program 2025-29 BA (Honours / Honours with Research) Curriculum 2024-28 BA Liberal Arts Curriculum 2023-26 BA Liberal Arts Curriculum 2022-25 BA Liberal Arts Curriculum 2021-24 BA Liberal Arts Curriculum 2020-23 Economics Major The economics major at SOLA trains students in the field of economics in an interdisciplinary setting. The purpose of turning the training in economics more practice-oriented is to help students internalize the core concepts curated from microeconomics, macroeconomics, game theory, public economics, and impact evaluation and econometrics to help students experience the beauty of comprehending the contemporary issues happening around them and know how to leverage them to make the world a better place for all. Major-Core Mathematics for Economics| Microeconomics I | Statistics for Economics| Microeconomics II | Macroeconomics I | Game Theory| History and Economics| Econometrics I | Macroeconomics II | Development Economics| International Economics| Advanced Econometrics| Complexity Economics| Cost Effectiveness Analysis| Development Economics Major-Elective Industrial Organization | Economics of Culture| Mechanism Design| Environment Economics | Economics of AI| Behavioral Economics| Labor Economics| Economics of Organizations| Time Series & Financial Econometrics | International Finance| Money and Banking| Casual Methods| Survey Designs| Business Cycles| Economics of Tourism| Economics of Sports| Self-Designed Course Psychology Major The Psychology major at the School of Liberal Arts, Bennett University offers an exceptional and transformative educational journey, blending interdisciplinary knowledge, global exposure, and a holistic understanding of psychology. The Psychology Lab, equipped with advanced test materials and research tools, allows students to conduct experiments, assessments, and research projects, bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application. The lab is equipped with several psychometric tests such as 16 Personality Factors Questionnaire, Adult Suicidal Ideation Questionnaire, Hare Psychopathy Checklist, Climate Change Perception Questionnaire, Depression Scale, Forgiveness Scale, Intrinsic Work Motivation Scale, etc. This experiential learning fosters strong research skills and deepens their understanding of psychological practices. As part of the B.A. Psychology curriculum, students are required to complete a mandatory internship in one of three modalities, namely Professional Internship, Research Internship, and Entrepreneurial Internship providing them with practical experience and professional exposure. Major- Core Personality Theory and Application | Bibliophile-Psychology| Statistics for Psychology| Cyberpsychology| Foundation of Positive Psychology| Forensic Psychology| Organizational Psychology I| Counselling Psychology for Change| Organizational Psychology II| Psychology of Ageing | Gender Psychology | Diaspora and Migration Psychology| Mandatory Internship Major-Elective Psychology of Parenting | Cross-Cultural Psychology | Adv Social Psychology| Biopsychology| Geriatric Psychology| Emotions and Implications| Trauma and Resilience in Marginalized Populations| Health Psychology | Psychometric Testing and Experimental Psychology Philosophy Major The Philosophy Major at the School of Liberal Arts, Bennett University offers students a rigorous, intellectually stimulating environment to explore fundamental questions about existence, knowledge, ethics, and society. The program provides an in-depth understanding of philosophical traditions across cultures, helping students to engage critically with complex ideas and to think independently about the world around them. Comprehensive Curriculum: The Philosophy Major at Bennett University is structured to provide a deep understanding of key philosophical concepts, theories, and figures across various philosophical traditions, including Western, Eastern, and contemporary schools of thought. Research and Dialogue: Students are encouraged to engage in independent research, debate, and dialogue through seminars, discussions, and research projects. Major- Core Introduction to Indian Philosophy |Discourses in Ethics| Introduction to Western Philosophy| Greek Philosophy| Contemporary Indian Philosophy| Formal Logic| Philosophy of Mind| Applied Ethics| Continental Philosophy| Philosophy of Language| Indian Theories of Self| Metaethics| Indian Epistemology| Feminist Philosophy Major-Elective Yoga Philosophy| Social and Political Philosophy| Philosophy of Religion| Ethics and Technology| Philosophy of Film| Care Ethics| Philosophy of Law| Philosophy of Science| Bio-Ethics Sociology Major The Sociology major at the School of Liberal Arts Bennett University emphasizes critical thinking, interdisciplinary learning, and a strong foundation in sociological theories and research methods. The course aims to provide students with a thorough understanding of social structures, inequalities, and cultural contexts, with distinct approaches and features in the Sociology curriculum. The Sociology curriculum in the School of Liberal Arts emphasizes social justice, and its curriculum reflects this commitment. The university encourages students to explore themes such as inequality, human rights, and social movements, fostering a sense of social responsibility. Students are often involved in community work or field-based research that links their academic studies with real-world social challenges. Major- Core Individual and the Social World| Economy and Society| Urban Space and Society| Media and Culture Studies | Gender and Society| Social Theory: Re-imagining Society| Race, Class and Gender| Globalization and Social Change| Sociology of Everyday Life| Development and Society| Migration and Diaspora Studies| Social, Cultural and Political Ecology| Social Inequalities and Public Policy| Qualitative Research and Ethnographic Methods| AI and Society: Thinking about Digital Revolutions Major-Elective Sociology of Love, Loss and Desire| Magic, Science, and Religion| Cybersociality| Work, Labour and Industry| Anthropology of Dreams| Sociology of Mental Health| Language, Society and Communication| Sociology of Music and Performing Arts| Ethnography of Travel and Identity Political Science & International Relations Major The Political Science & International Relations major at the School of Liberal Arts, Bennett University, offers an exceptional and transformative educational journey. It blends interdisciplinary knowledge, global exposure, and a comprehensive understanding of political systems, governance, and global affairs. The program emphasizes experiential learning through a mandatory dissertation, worth 16 credits in the final semester, hands-on projects, and field internships. The program is further enriched by the Gandhi Study Circle, a student-led initiative that fosters critical engagement with Gandhian philosophy and its relevance to contemporary issues. The Circle organizes impactful events such as lectures, debates, and workshops on peace, non-violence, and sustainable development. Courses like Political Theory, Indian Constitution, Western Political Thought, and International Relations form the cornerstone of the program, providing students with a robust understanding of political ideologies, constitutional frameworks, and the intricacies of global politics. In addition, core courses such as Human Rights: Theory and Practice, India’s Foreign Policy, and Political Parties and Changing Political Landscape facilitate a deeper understanding of contemporary political issues. Major- Core International Relations | Political Theory| Indian Constitution| Nationalism in India| Indian Political Thought| Human Rights: Theory and Indian Context |Western Political Thought| Political Parties and changing political landscape| Gender, Power and Politics| India’s Foreign Policy| Public Administration| India and World Politics| Peace and Conflict Resolution| Comparative Politics Major-Elective Democracy, Elections and Electoral Politics in India | Foreign Policy of Major Powers| Nationalism in India| Contemporary Political Economy | International Organisation| Local Self-Governance in India| State and society in South Asia| Politics of Developing Societies| News, Media and Politics| Political Sociology English Major The English major at the School of Liberal Arts, Bennett University, offers a captivating journey into the world of literary exploration and critical analysis. This program is designed to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of English literature while incorporating distinctive themes and courses that set it apart. From classic works to contemporary pieces, the program provides a holistic view of the literary landscape, fostering a deep appreciation for the power of language and storytelling. Through internships, literary events, and collaborative projects, students have the opportunity to apply theoretical knowledge to practical situations, honing their analytical and communication skills. This hands-on approach prepares them for a variety of careers where critical thinking and effective communication are essential. The program also establishes a unique link with medical humanities, providing students with insights into the intersection of literature and healthcare. Exploring narratives related to illness, healing, and the human condition, students gain a broader understanding of the impact of literature on well-being. Major- Core Forms of Literature| Introduction to Literary Theory| Early British Literature| Literature in the Age of Empire| Post-Colonial Literature| Indian Literature| Authors in Search of Characters and Plots I | Sci-Fi Literatures| Children Literature| Workshops on Female Writers | International Economics| Teaching English I| Teaching English II | Editing I | Editing II Major-Elective Politics and Literature| History and Literature| Autofiction | Biographies | Autobiographies| Storification of Everyday Life | Environment and Literature| Facts and Fiction| Self-help Literature| Utopian Writing| Science Writing| Social Media and Literature Business Studies Major The Business Studies major at Bennett University’s School of Liberal Arts offers a dynamic and interdisciplinary approach to understanding the complex realities of the Indian business world. Combining core business principles with insights from the humanities and social sciences, this program equips students with critical thinking, leadership, and problem-solving skills. With a strong emphasis on problem identification, ethics, innovation, and global perspectives, students gain hands-on experience through case studies, internships, industry interactions, and entrepreneurial initiatives. Graduates are turned future-ready, prepared for diverse career paths in management, entrepreneurship, and consulting, making them adaptable professionals ready to navigate the evolving business landscape with confidence and creativity. Major- Core Accounting Principles| Corporate Finance| Statistics for Business| Operation and Information Management| Legal Studies and Business Ethics| Intro to Org Behavior| Design Thinking and Innovation| Intro to Business Research and Methods| Introduction to Financial Management Major-Elective Product and Brand Management| Digital Marketing| Merger and Acquisitions| Financial Service and Insurance| Entrepreneurial Strategy| International Marketing| International Supply Chain and Logistics| Cross Cultural Management| Venture Creation Strategy and Simulation International Immersion Program Semester Abroad Program Students have the option to spend one semester to a full year studying at prestigious international institutions, gaining exposure to diverse academic frameworks and cultural environments. Current partner institutions include: National Taiwan University Augustana University HSE, Russia Summer School Programs For those seeking shorter yet impactful global experiences, summer school programs provide specialized learning opportunities: University of Nebraska, Omaha: A 6-week program designed to offer a deep dive into liberal arts disciplines. Babson College, USA: A 1–2-week Babson Build Entrepreneurship Program exclusively for UG2 and UG3 students, focusing on entrepreneurial skills and innovation. University of British Columbia, Canada: A 4-week intensive program providing exposure to international teaching standards and diverse academic content. Short-Term Study Abroad Programs Students can also participate in short-term study programs, such as the 2-week immersive program at UCJC, Spain, which combines academic learning with cultural exploration. University of Teesside, UK UCJC, Spain Augustana University, USA National Taiwan University, Taiwan University of Nebraska, Omaha The University of British Columbia, Canada University of Essex, UK King’s University College, Canada University of Teesside, UK UCJC, Spain Augustana University, USA ELIGIBILITY BA Honours Programs Eligibility Criteria & Selection Process Eligibility Domestic Passed XII standard from any recognised Education Board with minimum 60% marks (Class XII Best of 3 subjects score) and English as compulsory subject OR IB Board with minimum score of 24 credits, 3 Higher level and 3 standard level subjects. NRI Pass with 55% aggregate marks in grade 12th/ A level or equivalent and English language requirement (ELR) Selection Selection will be based on Class XII Best of 3 subjects score (comprising of English as compulsory subject and any other two subjects with highest score). Student Testimonials This course has taught me many things, like the society we live in, whereas subjects like Sociology and Psychology have helped me grasp various concepts Priyansha BA Liberal Arts Batch: 2020-23 The faculty and coordinators of Bennett University have given it all to make the virtual transition a more effortless process. Ranveer BA Liberal Arts Batch: 2020-23 Everything went smoothly online, from classrooms, libraries, resources, events, and even exams. My professors supported and guided me. Sri Guna BA Liberal Arts Batch: 2020-23 The Liberal Arts program is designed in a way that it provides not only holistic education but also overall personality development. Tanvi BA Liberal Arts Batch: 2020-23 Events BA Honours Programmes Frequently Asked Questions When was the B.A Liberal Arts program initiated under the School of Liberal Arts at Bennett University? The Liberal Arts program started with its first batch in 2020. It offers the opportunity to learn from highly qualified and experienced teachers. The program's foundation rests on two pillars -- the development of all-around personality and life skills. What are the majors available in the BA Liberal Arts course at Bennett University? The students can choose Majors from seven domains: Psychology, Political Science & IR, Economics, English, Business Studies, Philosophy & Sociology How is the BA Liberal Arts curriculum structured? In the first semester of the program, the students will be introduced to all the majors to select the Major which suits them the most. The choice of Majors and Electives is given from the second semester onwards. How do electives benefit a student's career? An elective is a combination of courses taken through the duration of university education. The courses taken as electives allow students to understand the world with exposure to a wide range of subjects and areas of practice. This broader understanding is a critical requirement to succeed in the world of the future. Can an undergraduate student take part in research activities? Bennett University focuses on research, experiential, and applied learning. Undergraduate students are encouraged to work with members of the faculty in their live research projects. Such exposure makes students understand the intricacies of the research process and deepens their knowledge Do you assist students in placements, summer internships, and industry tours? Bennett University has a dedicated Career Services Centre that works closely with the industry to participate in guest lectures, curricular inputs, joint program development, internships, and placements. What are the future career options after pursuing BA Liberal Arts? BA Liberal Arts graduates can look for rewarding careers in advertising, public relations, financial analysis, design, journalism, publishing, corporate communications, marketing, etc., or start a successful entrepreneurial journey of their own. What kind of industry exposure do students have access to? This is one of the unique hallmarks of the BA Liberal Arts program as it benefits from faculty members teaching courses in management, which in turn, provides students an opportunity to continuously interact with industry experts from India and abroad. Bachelor of Design (Hons.) - Bennett University International Admissions Research Alumni Career Online Payment Library Student-Faculty Login B.Des (Hons.) Future-Ready Designers, World-Ready Solutions Apply Now B.Des (Hons.) Future-Ready Designers, World-Ready Solutions Apply Now Bachelor of Design (Hons.) The School of Design at Bennett University is the space built for thinkers, makers, and dreamers—those who want to shape or colour the world with their ideas. With four exciting specialisations— Advanced Animation & VFX (in partnership with CII), Fashion Design, Game Design, and Communication Design —the B. Des (Hons.) opens a range of creative paths, each with its own unique magic. This four-year design program offers a fine blend of theoretical and experiential learning with industry alliances that facilitate the integration of live projects into the classroom. At its heart, design is about seeing possibilities—and Bachelor of Design (Hons.) is about helping you turn those possibilities into something real and fascinating. Unleash the creative genius within you. Give life to your design creativity with B. Des (Hons.) at Bennett University. BUDAT 2026 Examination Schedule School of Design, Bennett University BUDAT Exam 7 February 2026 Creative Development The B. Des (Hons.) program at Bennett nurtures creativity, critical thinking, and problem-solving abilities. It is designed to cultivate these essential skills to produce highly skilled design professionals ready to impact the world. Technical Proficiency Students acquire hands-on experience with advanced design tools, software, and techniques, ensuring they have the technical skills necessary to bring their ideas to life effectively and professionally. Industry Exposure Embedded internships and industry-driven projects provide students with valuable experience, networking opportunities, and a real-world understanding of the design industry. Diverse Career Paths The B. Des (Hons.) program paves the way for numerous career options, from graphic and interior design to product, fashion, and UI/UX design, reflecting the diverse landscape of the design field. Global Opportunities Recognizing design as a global industry, the program emphasizes international job prospects, global collaborations, and exposure to diverse perspectives, preparing students for worldwide success. About Why B. Des (Hons.) Top faculty from NID, NIFT & International Design Schools, with experience in Industry & Academia. Industry-aligned curriculum with focus on experiential learning, research and innovation. Industry readiness through apprenticeship, masterclasses and internships with the best in the industry. Industry alliances, such as with CII and others, pave the way for internships and placements, providing students with invaluable hands-on experience and a career path. School of Design The state-of-the-art workshops and labs provide students with the environment, tools and materials to experiment, prototype, and realize their ideas. The following are some of the spaces which will provide a platform for new ideas for our students. Metal Workshop A space equipped with tools and machinery for working with various metals, allowing students to explore processes like cutting, welding, forging, and casting. This supports students in product design, industrial design, and sculpture projects, fostering hands-on learning and craftsmanship. Wood Workshop A studio tailored for woodworking processes such as sawing, sanding, carving, and assembly. It aids students in creating prototypes, furniture, and models, encouraging precision and an appreciation for material properties. VR Lab A high-tech facility providing immersive virtual reality experiences and tools for designing VR environments. It supports interactive design, simulation projects, and storytelling, making it essential for gaming, fashion, product design, visual design, film, architectural visualization, and experimental interfaces. Gaming Studio A creative space equipped with hardware and software for game design and development. It focuses on creating engaging experiences, serious games for learning or health, and explorations into gamification across industries. Media Lab A versatile facility supporting video production, sound design, and digital media creation. It empowers students in communication design, advertising, and storytelling with the tools needed for content creation and post-production. Fashion – Pattern Making and Garment Construction Studio A specialized studio for designing and constructing garments, equipped with pattern-making tools, sewing machines, and drafting tables. It allows students to explore global trends, emerging technologies, and sustainable fashion design processes. Ceramic Studio A studio focusing on clay and ceramic-based projects, including pottery, sculpture, and functional design. Equipped with kilns, pottery wheels, and glazing stations, it nurtures an understanding of material manipulation and creative exploration. These studios provide hands-on, practical environments where students merge theory with practice, fostering creativity, technical skills, and an interdisciplinary approach to design. Career Linked Pathways/ Electives Advanced Animation & VFX (in partnership with CII) 2D and 3D Animation Character Animation VFX for Films & OTT Augmented Reality (AR) and Virtual Reality (VR) Educational Animation Communication Design Core Graphic Design Film & Video Interaction Design Game Design Game Design Game Design Game Programming 3D Development Serious Games Fashion Design Athleisurewear Fashion Styling and Communication Indian wear Fashion Technology Specialisations Fashion Design The fashion design program at Bennett School of Design provides a comprehensive curriculum that blends creativity, technical skills, and an understanding of emerging technologies. Students engage in designing clothing, accessories, and textiles with a focus on both traditional craftsmanship and modern technology. They learn design principles, sustainable practices, and global design trends, while gaining skills in textile design, garment construction, pattern making, and digital fashion tools. Students gain insights into the latest developments in fashion including smart textiles and wearables, while mastering digital tools such as 3D garment simulation, digital pattern making, and virtual fashion show platforms. The program fosters innovation and encourages students to create collections that reflect personal expression, societal values, and an awareness of ethical fashion. Emphasizing an entrepreneurial approach, this program prepares students for diverse roles in the fashion industry, from independent fashion designers to stylists, accessory designer, fashion illustrators, brand strategists, trend forecasters. Intelligent Textile Design Details to be updated soon. Game Design The game design program at Bennett University School of Design combines creativity, storytelling, and technical skills to develop engaging, immersive experiences for players across multiple platforms and formats. Students learn the art and science of game mechanics, level design, character development, and interactive storytelling, along with fundamental skills in programming, 3D modeling, and animation. The curriculum emphasizes both entertainment and serious game design, encouraging students to create games with purpose, including educational, health, and socially impactful themes. With a focus on emerging technologies such as augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), and artificial intelligence (AI), the program prepares students to innovate and adapt within a dynamic, rapidly evolving industry. Through hands-on projects and collaboration, students gain the skills needed to build meaningful and memorable gaming experiences. Through hands-on projects, industry collaborations, and a focus on player engagement, students graduate ready to excel in the dynamic world of game design. There are multiple roles this program prepares students for, including game designer, game programming, concept artist, serious game designer, 3D Artist, esports game designer, board game designer. Product Design with AI The Bachelor of Design (Hons) in Product Design with Artificial Intelligence is a modern undergraduate program that combines industrial design principles with new intelligent technologies. The curriculum is designed to develop strong skills in human-centered design, ergonomics, aesthetics, materials, manufacturing processes, and digital fabrication. It also improves knowledge in artificial intelligence, machine learning, and data-driven decision support systems. Advanced Animation & VFX (in partnership with CII) New The Advanced Animation and VFX program at Bennett University’s School of Design is tailored for students passionate about visual storytelling, digital artistry, and cinematic innovation. Co-developed with the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII), the B. Des (Hons.) in Advanced Animation and VFX bridges the gap between academic learning and real-world industry requirements, preparing students for the dynamic and fast-evolving animation and VFX landscape. The curriculum combines traditional design principles with emerging technologies, offering modules on motion graphics, generative AI, prompt engineering, AR/VR, and node-based compositing. The program focuses on hands-on learning through state-of-the-art labs, real-life industry projects, and internships facilitated by CII and The Times Group partners. With tools such as Autodesk Maya, Foundry Nuke, PFTrack, Adobe Suite, and Runway AI, students gain industry-standard proficiency and build a strong creative portfolio. Guest lectures, masterclasses, and mentoring by VFX professionals ensure that learners are constantly exposed to the latest trends, workflows, and expectations of top studios. Graduates are equipped to pursue careers in 3D Animation, Visual Effects (VFX) for film and OTT, Motion Design, Game Cinematics, AR/VR Storytelling, Digital Set Design and Simulation. Communication Design The communication design program at Bennett University School of Design centres on crafting impactful visual and digital communication across various media platforms. The program is rooted in fostering intellectual growth, conceptual thinking, and the art of storytelling. Students explore the power of visual and digital communication to connect with diverse audiences, while developing critical thinking and problem-solving skills to create meaningful, impactful designs. Students learn to convey messages effectively through typography, illustration, photography, motion graphics, and interactive design. The curriculum covers design fundamentals, user experience (UX), branding, storytelling, film making and digital media, equipping students to engage audiences creatively and meaningfully. Emphasizing emerging trends and technologies, including augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), and data visualization, the program prepares students to adapt and innovate in a rapidly changing landscape. Through hands-on projects, internships and collaboration, students develop skills to excel in fields like advertising, digital media, social media, and brand identity design. Internships Internships play a pivotal role in bridging the gap between academic learning and industry experience for students. Through internships, students develop a robust set of skills, including essential soft skills such as communication, teamwork, and problem-solving. These opportunities also provide students with valuable networking experiences, enabling them to forge connections that can pave the way for future career pathways in design. Furthermore, internships offer students the chance to bolster their resumes and portfolios with tangible experience, while also serving as brand ambassadors for their institution. From a recruitment perspective, internships provide companies with a unique opportunity to evaluate potential candidates before making a hiring decision, creating a potential pipeline for talent acquisition. Industry alliances Department-specific industry alliances play a vital role in enriching the academic experience at Bennett University. These alliances facilitate the integration of live projects into the classroom, particularly benefiting students in their third year and above. Additionally, these partnerships pave the way for internships and placements, providing students with invaluable hands-on experience. Industry experts also conduct masterclasses, sharing their expertise and insights with students. Furthermore, these alliances enable collaborative research initiatives, fostering innovation and knowledge creation. Ultimately, department-specific industry alliances contribute significantly to institutional branding, reinforcing Bennett University's reputation as a hub for industry-academia collaboration and excellence. Academic alliances – International Universities Bennett University has established academic alliances with renowned international universities, fostering a global learning environment that enriches the academic experience. Selection Process Selection Criteria for Students Intake Design Entrance Test –UCEED/NIFT/NID/BUDAT Personal Interview with Portfolio Round OUR ASK B. Des (Hons.) Frequently Asked Questions Is B. Des (Hons.) program only for students who are good at design or drawing? Not necessarily. While creativity is important, the program also focuses on developing critical thinking, problem-solving, and technical skills—you can build during the course of the program. Which specialisations can I choose from in the B. Des. (Hons.) program? You can choose from four exciting specialisations— Advanced Animation & VFX (in partnership with CII), Fashion Design, Game Design, and Communication Design. Will I get hands-on experience with industry tools and software? Yes. You will work extensively with advanced design tools such as Autodesk Maya, Foundry Nuke, PFTrack, Adobe Suite, and Runway AI among others to build strong technical skills and bring your ideas to life professionally. Does the program include internships or real-world projects? Yes, the curriculum includes embedded internships and industry-driven projects to offer you practical experience and networking opportunities. What kind of careers can I pursue after B. Des (Hons.) degree? You can explore careers in fashion, graphic design, animation, UI/UX, product design, game design, and more. The program prepares you for a wide range of creative roles. Is this program suitable for international career opportunities? Absolutely, the B. Des (Hons.) program is designed with a global perspective and includes exposure to international trends, collaborations, and career pathways. What does a CII partnership mean for students? A CII partnership connects students with industry insights, real-world learning, and professional networks. It ensures the curriculum is aligned with current market needs and can lead to enhanced internship, mentorship, and placement opportunities through CII’s wide network of member organisations. CII brings robust support as an industry partner with over 30 years of experience in Animation & VFX, along with by 9,000+ member companies and 300,000+ global connections. Who should apply for the B. Des (Hons.) in Advanced Animation & VFX? The B. Des (Hons.) in Advanced Animation & VFX is ideal for aspiring animators, visual storytellers, and technical artists looking to make a mark in the global media and entertainment industry. Are there internship opportunities available for Design students? Yes, the School of Design at Bennett University offers robust internship opportunities for students, connecting them with leading design firms and industry experts. These internships provide hands-on experience, allowing students to work on real-world projects, build professional networks, and develop job-ready skills essential for a successful design career.
